Filter rentals by aircraft category (the familiar 1–7 circles, with All / None / Invert), a draggable pilot-payout gradient that doubles as a minimum-payout slider (right-click to recolor it), aircraft type and aircraft owner multi-selects, and an “available now only” toggle. Every section collapses, the panel is draggable and resizable, and your layout preferences are remembered.
  • Results table. A sortable, groupable list of every matching aircraft — sort by type, owner, airport, payout or availability; group by owner, airport or type; and drag the dividers between columns to resize them (double-click to auto-fit). Click any row to fly to that airport. Aircraft blocked by a ground-operations timer show their countdown in red.
  • Search airports by their notes. The map search box now matches your Scenery Library Notes — type words or a “quoted phrase” and the airports whose notes contain them appear on the map.
  • Direct-launch URL. Add ?mode=owner, ?mode=map or ?mode=simbrief to the address to skip the start screen and open straight into that view.
v3.49.0 June 25, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library Store / Dev / Notes are now "click to edit" — immune to extension slowdowns. Some browser extensions (virtual-card tools like Privacy.com, password managers, Grammarly) scan every text field on a page and re-check them on each keystroke and scroll. With a few hundred airports the library held ~2,000 such fields, which is what made typing in Store and Dev crawl for anyone with one of those extensions. Now each row shows those values as plain text and only turns into an editable field when you click it — so at rest there are no fields for an extension to scan, and typing stays fast no matter what's installed. It looks and works almost exactly the same: click a Store/Dev cell to edit, the autocomplete suggestions and Tab-to-complete are unchanged, Tab hops to the next field, Enter saves, and Esc cancels. The Notes box is likewise built only while a row's notes are expanded.
v3.48.3 June 25, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery Library speed — the genuine fix this time, with the live autocomplete back. The real cause of the slowdown with a large library was a graphics setting on the scrolling list: it was being pushed onto the GPU as one single layer the full height of the list. With a few hundred airports that layer is over 40,000 pixels tall — far bigger than the graphics card can handle in one piece — so the browser had to laboriously redraw it for every little change, including each keystroke in a Store or Dev field. Removing that one setting lets the browser draw only the rows actually on screen, which is what makes the small (local-file) library feel instant. Scrolling stays smooth because the list already only renders visible rows.
  • Live Store / Dev autocomplete restored. The previous attempt removed the as-you-type suggestion dropdown on a hunch that it was the cause — it wasn't, and you correctly flagged the loss. The dropdown is back exactly as before (Tab-to-complete still works too), and the search box is live again.
v3.48.2 June 25, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery Library typing is fast again, even with a big library. Found the real cause: the Store and Dev fields used a built-in browser autocomplete dropdown, and on a large library loaded over the map that dropdown forced the whole page to re-lay-out and repaint on every keystroke — which is why it dragged badly online (where your full library and the map are loaded) but felt fine when opened from a local file (a separate, usually-empty library). The fields now use a lightweight approach: type a few letters and press Tab to autocomplete to the best match (the suggestions are unchanged), with no per-keystroke slowdown. The same change was applied to the right-click Quick-Rate popover's Store / Dev fields.
  • Library search no longer stutters. Typing in the Scenery Library search box used to rebuild the entire list on every keystroke; with a large library that made the box itself feel laggy. The rebuild now waits a fraction of a second after you stop typing, so keystrokes stay instant and results still appear effectively immediately.
v3.48.1 June 25, 2026 Fix
  • Typing in the Scenery Library is smooth now (the real fix). The animated rainbow page title and the slow radar sweep behind the map were still running every frame even while the map and the Scenery Library window covered them — and because those overlays are slightly see-through, the browser kept repainting the title on every frame. That constant repainting stole time from the keyboard, which is what made typing into the Store and Dev fields feel like it kept dropping characters. Both animations now pause automatically whenever the map or any window is open, freeing the browser to keep up with your typing. They still animate normally on the home screen where you can actually see them.
v3.48.0 June 25, 2026 Feature
  • Import your FlightSim.to scenery in one place. A new 🌐 Import from FlightSim.to item under the Scenery Library's 🛠 Manage menu opens a guided two-step window. Step 1 gives you a ready-to-run snippet (with a 📋 Copy snippet button) to paste into your browser console while signed in to FlightSim.to — it scans everything you've downloaded there and saves a simfly-flightsimto-scenery.json file to your Downloads. Step 2 loads that file straight into your Scenery Library, pulling in every airport you own scenery for — whether or not a SimFly player owns it.
  • Re-scraping is always safe. The import only ever fills in blanks: brand-new airports are added in full, but for airports already in your library it only sets a Store, Developer, or Rating that's currently empty. Ratings and attributions you've set by hand are never overwritten, so you can re-run the import any time to pick up new purchases. Imported airports float to the top of the list for review, and the result is summarised in a toast (added / filled / unchanged / skipped).
v3.47.1 June 25, 2026 Fix
  • Typing in the Scenery Library no longer feels laggy. The yellow “unsaved changes” bar at the top of the Scenery Library used to pulse continuously. That glow repainted the whole bar on every frame, and with the map drawing behind it the constant repaint competed with your keystrokes — so typing into the Store, Dev, or Notes fields felt like the input was being swallowed. The bar is now a steady, clearly-visible highlight (it fades in once when changes appear, then holds still), so editing is smooth.
v3.47.0 June 25, 2026 Feature
  • Chain several searches on one line with commas. Type a comma-separated list in the map search box — e.g. California, United States, Europe, Kyndra & Africa — and the dropdown previews what each piece resolves to. Press Enter (or click ▶ Show all) and every item is applied at once, exactly as if you'd searched each one and added it with a +. The map shows the union of all of them. Prefix the whole line with + to add the list on top of the current view, or - to remove every listed item.
  • Filter the map by scenery store. The search box now recognizes the marketplace your scenery came from — type SimMarket, Orbx Direct, MSFS Marketplace, etc. (any store you've recorded in the Scenery Library) to filter the map to airports bought there. Stores work just like the existing developer filter: combine several with +, exclude with -, and an active filter shows as a chip in the filter strip — hover it for a per-store count of visible airports.
  • Search results now include a 🛒 store lane. Alongside owner, country, region, continent, and developer (📦) matches, the dropdown shows matching scenery stores so you can pick one to filter by in a click.
v3.46.0 June 25, 2026 Feature
  • Drag the map panels wherever you want. The Pilot Payout legend, the Scenery Rating panel, the Payout Palette editor, the airport right-click rating popover, and the ⓘ airport-stats panel on the File-on-SimBrief chip can now all be dragged by their title bar (look for the ⠿ grip), exactly like the File-on-SimBrief chip itself. Move a panel out of the way of an airport you're trying to see and it stays where you put it for the rest of the session. Positions reset on reload — nothing is saved permanently.
  • Right-clicking Payout Colors… in the Display menu now works properly. Previously the palette editor opened tucked under the menu and the click you used to dismiss the menu also closed the editor, making it impossible to use. The menu now closes cleanly and the palette opens in its normal spot at the top-right of the map.
  • Customizing palette colors is smooth now. Dragging inside the color picker to set a new stop color used to feel laggy because the whole map repainted on every tiny change. The gradient still updates live, but the heavy map repaint is now batched to once per frame — so the picker tracks your cursor without the stutter.
  • A “…” on a menu row now means “right-click for more”. The four rows with a right-click alternate — Display New Airports…, Payout Colors…, SimBrief Flight Plan…, and Create Random Route… — end with an ellipsis so it's clear there's an extra options menu behind a right-click. To keep the cue unambiguous, the ellipsis was removed from rows that merely open a window with no right-click of their own — Layers, Aircraft Icon, Mobile Alerts, and Flight Demo Mode.
v3.45.0 June 25, 2026 Feature
  • Airport stats right on the map — no need to leave it. The floating File on SimBrief route chip now has a small ⓘ button. Click it and a panel pops up just above the chip showing the full departure and arrival airport cards side-by-side — owner, payout, SimFly category & level, rotations left, runways, weather, and the aircraft-suitability verdict — exactly the same cards you'd otherwise see at the bottom of the main planner screen. Click ⓘ again, click anywhere off the panel, or press Esc to close it.
  • Stays in sync as you plan. The panel tracks the chip if you drag it, and refreshes automatically if you swap the route or change endpoints while it's open. On narrow screens the two cards stack vertically.
v3.44.1 June 24, 2026 Fix
  • Additive "+ICAO" search no longer clears the map. Pinning an airport with a + prefix (e.g. +KSFO) is meant to add that airport on top of whatever is currently shown. When no filter was active, it was wrongly clearing the map and leaving only the pinned airport. Now +KSFO on the default view keeps every airport that was already visible and simply adds KSFO. A plain KSFO search (no +) still replaces the view and zooms to just that airport, as before.
  • …and it zooms to the airport you just added. When you +pin an airport onto the default view, the map now flies to that airport instead of zooming all the way out to fit every airport in the world. (When a filter is already active — e.g. Washington then +KSFO — the map still frames everything that's visible together, as before.)
v3.44.0 June 24, 2026 Feature
  • New "Layers" manager — control what draws on top. The 👁 Display menu has a new 🗂 Layers… item that opens a window listing every map layer — airport dots, airport tooltips, waypoint labels, the aircraft icon and trail, route line and labels, alert labels, and measurement/region overlays — each shown with a little graphic of the real element and its stacking value. Drag a layer up (or use the ▲▼ buttons) to lift it above the others; the top of the list draws on top of everything. Changes apply instantly and are saved in your browser, so the stacking order you pick sticks between sessions.
  • Reset to Default if you ever bork it. A ↺ Reset to Default button restores the sensible factory order in one click. By design no layer can ever be pushed below the base map or above the menus/windows, so you can experiment freely.
v3.43.4 June 24, 2026 Fix
  • Airport tooltips now sit above geofence and waypoint alert tags. When an alert label (a geofence ring tag or a waypoint-alarm tag) overlapped the airport you were inspecting, it could cover the airport's tooltip. The airport info tooltip now renders on top of those alert tags, so it's always fully readable. The flight-plan waypoint labels still sit beneath the alert tags as before.
v3.43.3 June 24, 2026 Fix
  • The "has scenery" dot now grows with the airport and stays on top. When you hover an airport you have scenery for, its black centre dot now enlarges in proportion to the growing airport dot and remains on top throughout. Previously the scenery dot stayed small and, after a hover, could end up hidden behind the airport dot.
v3.43.2 June 24, 2026 Fix
  • New-airport indicator now uses each airport's real activation date. The pulsing "new airport" ring was being driven by when our hourly data feed first noticed an airport, which could be reset by a brief hiccup in SimFly's data — so long-established airports you've owned for over a year (e.g. EGPN, active since January 2025) were wrongly showing as new. It now reads the airport's actual SimFly activation date, so only genuinely brand-new airports get the ring. This corrected a batch of about thirty airports that were falsely flagged.
  • Hovered dots grow in step with the tooltip. The hover-to-enlarge delay now matches the tooltip's, so the dot grows at the same moment its tooltip appears instead of a beat later.
v3.43.1 June 24, 2026 Fix
  • Hovered dots no longer leave "corona dust" behind. When an airport dot grew or shrank on hover, fragments of its level-corona spikes and rotation ring could be left painted on the map. The map now fully repaints on each resize, so the dot grows and shrinks cleanly with nothing left behind.
  • The rotation ring and corona spikes get bolder as the dot grows. While a hovered dot is enlarged, its rotation gauge ring and level-corona spikes now thicken in proportion (instead of staying hairline-thin on the bigger dot), so they're genuinely easier to read.
v3.43.0 June 24, 2026 Feature
  • Airport dots grow when you hover them. Linger the mouse over any airport dot for half a second and it doubles in size (and lifts above its neighbours), making its rotation gauge ring and level corona spikes much easier to read on the airport you're examining. The dot snaps back to normal as soon as you move away.
  • Tooltip no longer covers the level corona. The airport tooltip now sits a little higher above the dot so it clears the 12 o'clock corona spike instead of hiding it — you can read the tooltip and see the level marks at the same time.
  • Random Flight Reset now sticks. The ↺ Reset button in the Random Flight Filters window now also clears the saved settings, so the cleared state is remembered — closing and re-opening the window no longer brings the old filters back. Previously Reset only cleared the on-screen fields.
v3.42.0 June 24, 2026 Feature
  • Reset button on the Random Flight Filters window. A new ↺ Reset button in the bottom-left corner clears every filter back to its default in one click — categories, continents, countries, owners (on both the Departure and Arrival panels), the distance and flight-time ranges, and the "only airports shown on the map" toggle. Nothing is saved until you click Save & Generate, so you can reset, tweak, and still cancel if you change your mind.
v3.41.0 June 24, 2026 Feature
  • The File on SimBrief chip now stays clear of your airports. The floating chip still defaults to the bottom centre of the map, but if that spot would cover your departure or arrival airport it now automatically slides left or right to keep both airports visible — a frequent problem on smaller, lower-resolution screens.
  • You can drag the File on SimBrief chip anywhere. A new ⠿ grip handle on the left edge of the chip lets you move it to wherever suits you. Your chosen spot is remembered for the rest of your session; reloading the app returns it to the default bottom-centre placement.
  • The rotation gauge now starts off. When you first open the map, airport dots no longer show the rotation gauge ring by default — a cleaner first look. Turn it on any time from the 👁 Display menu (or middle-click the map), and your choice is remembered between sessions as before.
v3.40.3 June 23, 2026 Fix
  • No modal window closes unexpectedly while you select text inside it. Every pop-up window now uses the same press-origin guard the Random Flight Filters window got last release. Pressing inside a text or number box and drifting a few pixels onto the dimmed background before releasing — easy to do when selecting text — used to be misread as a click on the background and close the window, losing your work. Windows now close only when you both press and release on the dark area outside the panel. This covers the Scenery Library, Scenery Developers, Manage Values, Help, Release Notes, Map Annotations, all AFK Alert windows, and every other pop-up.
v3.40.2 June 23, 2026 Fix
  • Random flights stop repeating the same route. The random flight generator now avoids handing back the route — or, when you have a departure pinned, the destination — it just gave you, as long as another valid option exists. Previously a small pool of matching airports could land on the same pick twice in a row, making it look like the button hadn't done anything.
  • Random Flight Filters window no longer closes unexpectedly. Clicking and dragging inside the distance or flight-time boxes (for example, to select the text) could be misread as a click on the dimmed background and close the whole window. It now only closes when you actually click the dark area outside the panel.
v3.40.1 June 23, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery Library scrolling is smooth again. The library list now only renders the rows currently on screen, so scrolling a library of hundreds of airports no longer stutters. Previously every scroll repainted all rows at once — including their inputs and rating stars — which made the list feel slow and clunky on desktop.
  • Scenery Developers modal scrolls smoothly too. The developer summary table was given the same scroll-isolation treatment so its sticky header and rows no longer lag while scrolling.
  • Removed the live background blur behind these two modals. The blurred backdrop was being re-computed on every scroll frame, which was the main reason both windows felt heavy. It's replaced with a plain dim — the same blur still appears behind the Release Notes and Help windows.
v3.40.0 June 22, 2026 Feature
  • Keyboard-friendly Quick-Rate popover. The right-click airport popover now fully supports the keyboard for fast data entry. Opening it focuses the star rating immediately, and Tab walks through Rating → Store → Developer → Notes → links, cycling within the popover.
  • Arrow keys set the rating. With the stars focused, ←/→ (or ↑/↓) adjust by half a star, 1–5 set a rating directly, and 0 clears it — no mouse needed.
  • Tab-to-complete on Store and Developer. Type the start of a marketplace or developer name and press Tab to autocomplete to the top matching suggestion (the native dropdown with ↑/↓ still works too).
  • Escape still closes the popover from anywhere inside it; the map no longer pans or zooms from key presses while you're typing in a field.
v3.39.1 June 22, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery dot now shows on non-SimFly airports too. When you pin a non-SimFly-owned airport on the map (for example by searching its ICAO), its black scenery indicator dot now appears if you have scenery owned or installed for it. Previously the dot only showed on SimFly-owned airports, so a searched airport in your Scenery Library looked like it had no scenery.
v3.39.0 June 21, 2026 Fix
  • Auto-connect now reaches touchscreen PCs. The FSUIPC auto-connect that arms when you load a SimBrief flight plan was being skipped on any machine that reports touch support — which wrongly included touchscreen Windows laptops and desktops. It now only stands down on true touch-only devices (phones and tablets), so a touchscreen flight-sim PC auto-connects like any other desktop.
  • Auto-connect catches up when you return to the tab. Browsers throttle background timers, so the once-a-minute FSUIPC check could stall while you were alt-tabbed into the sim. Active Airports now re-checks the instant you switch back to its tab, so flipping over to confirm your flight connects on the spot instead of waiting out the timer.
  • FSUIPC button no longer shows a false “Tracking…”. When a live connection dropped (sim closed, FSUIPC restarted, a network blip), the button could stay stuck on FSUIPC Tracking… while nothing was actually connected. It now reads FSUIPC Reconnecting… while the background reconnect retries and flips back to FSUIPC Tracking… the instant it succeeds — so the button always reflects the real state (and still toggles off in one click).
v3.38.0 June 21, 2026 Feature
  • Per-airport scenery Notes. Each airport in your Scenery Library can now carry a free-text note (up to 1,024 characters) — handy for install quirks, version numbers, or reminders. Notes are saved with the library and round-trip through Save / Load Scenery Library.
  • Notes appear in two places. A ▶ Notes disclosure sits in each Scenery Library row and in the right-click Quick-Rate popover. Click the triangle to expand the field for entry or editing (it becomes ▼); a small ● next to the label marks airports that already have a note. The popover opens with the note already expanded when one exists, so saved notes are visible at a glance — library rows stay collapsed to keep the list compact.
v3.37.0 June 21, 2026 Feature
  • Default Alerts now has separate Load and Save buttons. The single ⭐ Set Default button in the Alerts modal footer was ambiguous about what it did. It's now two clearly-labelled buttons: ⭐ Save Defaults (snapshot the current alarm queue) and ⭐ Load Defaults (pull your saved set into the modal on demand).
  • Buttons disable when they can't do anything. Load Defaults is greyed out when no defaults are saved; Save Defaults is greyed out when there are no alerts configured to save.
  • Save now confirms before overwriting. If you already have a saved default set, Save Defaults asks for confirmation before replacing it.
  • Right-click ⭐ Load Defaults to clear your saved defaults — the clear shortcut moved from the old combined button.
v3.36.2 June 21, 2026 Improved
  • Live menu indicators now line up. The checkmarks, the FSUIPC live-signal dot, and the Mobile Alerts counter are all right-justified in a single consistent column on their menu rows, instead of sitting at slightly different positions.
  • Auto-load Default Alerts uses a checkmark. The Live-menu Auto-load Default Alerts row now shows a green ✓ when on (and nothing when off), matching every other toggle, in place of the old ON/OFF badge.
  • New Airports no longer double-signals. The ✦ Display New Airports item in the 👁 Display menu used to paint a colored highlight when active; now the green ✓ alone marks it on, consistent with the rest of the menu.
v3.36.1 June 21, 2026 Improved
  • The 🎲 Re-roll button can now be dismissed. Once you generate a random flight, the Re-roll button in the map header used to stay for the rest of the session with no way to remove it. It now has a small ✕ on its right — click it to hide the button. It reappears the next time you create a random flight from the 🛠 Tools menu.
v3.36.0 June 21, 2026 Improved
  • Payout and Rating moved into the 👁 Display menu. The standalone 💰 Payout and ⭐ Rating header buttons are gone on desktop — both now live inside the Display menu alongside the other map overlays and filters, decluttering the map header. On phones and tablets, where the Display menu is hidden, they stay available as direct buttons in the right-hand button band.
  • Display menu reorganised into Overlays and Filters. The menu is now split into two labelled groups separated by a divider: Overlays (Display Levels, Display Rotations, Display New Airports, Payout Colors) for things drawn onto the map, and Filters (For Sale Only, Rotations Left Only, Star Rating) for things that hide airports — so related settings sit together.
  • Active items now show a green check instead of a highlight. Across the Display, Tools, and Live menus, rows no longer light up with a filled cyan background when on. Instead a green ✓ appears on the right of each active row, right-justified and aligned in its own column — a cleaner, more legible on/off cue that's now consistent app-wide.
v3.35.4 June 20, 2026 Fix
  • Drawing a region that starts on an airport dot no longer opens the airport's rate popover. Beginning a region-circle drag on top of an airport used to be swallowed by the dot — the region never started and the dot's long-press popped its Quick-Rate card instead. A press on a dot during region drawing now drives the region draw as expected; normal long-press / right-click rating still works everywhere else.
v3.35.3 June 20, 2026 Improved
  • Region distance label restyled to match the route label. The center-to-center distance label shown between two selected region circles now uses the same Rajdhani font and neutral-text / gold-border styling as the dep → arr route label, instead of its old cyan monospace look — so the two on-map distance labels look consistent.
v3.35.2 June 20, 2026 Improved
  • Rotation gauge now has a filter-strip chip. While the rotation gauge is shown, a 🔄 Rotation Gauge chip appears in the map's filter strip — click its ✕ to hide the gauge. Hovering the chip reminds you that you can also middle-click the map to toggle the gauge on and off.
v3.35.1 June 20, 2026 Improved
  • For Sale menu item now hints at the sale-page shortcut. Hovering 🏷 For Sale Only in the 👁 Display menu now shows a tooltip reminding you that you can double right-click a for-sale airport dot to open its SimFly sale page.
v3.35.0 June 20, 2026 New
  • Rotation gauge — the dot outline flags airports running low. Each airport dot's outline is now a 3 px black fuel gauge that only depletes when an airport is getting low on flight rotations for the week — because that's what matters for planning. An airport with 10 or more rotations remaining shows a full 3 px ring; once it drops below 10 the ring shrinks anti-clockwise from 12 o'clock as a percentage of 10 (9 left ≈ 90% of the ring, 5 ≈ 50%, 1 ≈ 10%); and an airport with zero rotations left has no outline at all — so the airports to avoid this week are simply the ones with no ring. The ring sits clear of the level-corona spikes, which radiate outside the dot. Toggle it from 👁 Display → Display Rotations or by middle-clicking anywhere on the map (on by default).
  • Rotations remaining shown in tooltips and the Quick-Rate card. The dot hover tooltip now reads "ICAO  Cat n  Lvl n  (remaining / total)", the detailed airport tooltip carries a "Rotations: X / Y left this week" line, and the right-click Quick-Rate card shows the same — all colour-coded (green = 10+ left, amber = fewer than 10, red = none) so this planning number is available everywhere you look at an airport.
  • "Rotations Left Only" map filter. A new filter under 👁 Display hides airports that have exhausted their rotations for the week, leaving only those you can still fly into. Airports with no rotation data are left visible. A removable filter chip appears while it's active.
  • Low-rotation warning on the route distance label. When a departure/arrival pair is selected, the on-map distance label shows a red ⚠ Low rotations note if either airport has 3 or fewer rotations remaining this week — so a nearly-exhausted endpoint is obvious before you file.
  • Low-rotation badge on the File on SimBrief buttons. Whenever the selected departure or arrival has 3 or fewer rotations left, a yellow ⚠ warning badge appears on both the main File on SimBrief button and the floating map route pill — a glanceable cue even before you click.
  • Low-rotation confirm before filing on SimBrief. Clicking File on SimBrief — from either the main button or the floating map route pill — now pops a confirmation first if the departure or arrival has 3 or fewer rotations left, naming the airport and how many remain. It appears before the new-pilot-codes prompt; Cancel backs out, File Anyway → continues.
  • Renaming a geofence alert now updates its map label immediately. Previously, editing an alert's name refreshed the alarm card but left the on-map geofence label showing the old name until an unrelated event (a flight-plan change, a trip, or a page reload) redrew it. The label now updates the instant you rename.
  • Changing a geofence's trigger now updates its label immediately. Switching a geofence between Entry, Exit, and Entry & Exit now refreshes both the on-map label and the alarm-card subtitle right away, instead of leaving the old wording in place.
  • "For Sale" now uses a 🏷 tag icon instead of 💰. The For Sale filter, its filter chip, and the for-sale tooltip markers shared the same 💰 icon as Pilot Payout, which was confusing. They now use a distinct 🏷 price-tag icon.
  • Visible "View sale listing" link for for-sale airports. Right-click a for-sale airport dot and the Quick-Rate popover now shows a 🏷 View sale listing on SimFly → link that opens its marketplace page — a discoverable alternative to the existing double-right-click shortcut (which still works).
v3.34.2 June 20, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery dirty badge counts airports, not changes. The numeric badge on the Scenery button (and its tooltip) now shows how many distinct airports have unsaved changes, not the total number of individual edits. Three changes to the same airport shows 1, not 3. The banner message inside the Scenery panel is updated to match (N unsaved airport(s)).
v3.34.1 June 20, 2026 Fix
  • Quick-Rate popover — disk button moved inline with airport name. The 💾 save icon now sits as a flex item in the header row immediately after the airport name, rather than as an absolute-positioned element in the top-right corner. Uses display:contents on the SimFly link wrapper so ICAO and name remain direct flex participants while the disk button stays outside the <a> element (avoiding invalid nested interactive content).
v3.34.0 June 20, 2026 Feature
  • Quick-Rate popover — SimFly details link. When the airport under the cursor is owned by a SimFly player, the ICAO code and name in the popover header become a clickable link to that airport’s SimFly details page. Non-owned airports keep the plain header.
  • Quick-Rate popover — inline Store and Developer fields. Two text inputs now appear below the star rating: Store (the marketplace) and Developer (the publisher). Both autocomplete from the full list of known stores and developers — including your own previously-entered custom values — exactly as they do inside the Scenery Library panel. Committing a value (Tab, Enter, or clicking away) saves it immediately to the library entry; setting a value on an airport not yet in the library adds it automatically, the same as shift-clicking from the map.
  • Quick-Rate popover — save disk button. A small 💾 icon appears in the popover header whenever there are unsaved scenery changes (rating, store, developer, or anything else that has incremented the dirty count). Clicking it exports the full scenery database to a JSON backup file and clears the dirty count — identical to the Save button inside the Scenery Library panel. The button hides itself after saving.
  • Quick-Rate popover — external links. Two links appear at the bottom of every popover: View on flightsim.to and View on FS Addon Compare, each using the same external-link icon as the distance calculator. Both open in a new tab and search for the airport’s ICAO code on the respective site.
v3.33.3 June 19, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library footer reorganised. Three primary buttons are always visible: ⤓ Save Scenery, ⤒ Load Scenery (matching the Alerts modal's iconography), and ⚙ Developer Stats. Everything else — Add from map, Scan folder, Paste ICAOs, Stores & Developers, FS Addon Compare, Import scenery config, and Clear all — is consolidated under a 🛠 Manage ▾ dropdown with separator lines grouping related actions. Replaces the previous crowded inline layout and unlabelled ⋯ overflow.
v3.33.1 June 19, 2026 Improved
  • Menu icon and text alignment polished. All rows in the 🛠 Tools, 👁 Display, and 🔌 Live pulldown menus now use a consistent two-column flex layout — icons are fixed-width and centred in their column, text labels start at the same horizontal position on every row. Mixed-width emoji and symbol glyphs (📐 ⊙ 🎲 / ☼ ✦ 💰 / ✈ ⭐ 🔌 🛩 📍 📱 🎬) no longer cause the labels to stagger.
v3.33.0 June 19, 2026 Feature
  • Default Alerts — auto-load your standard alert set on every flight plan load. A new ⭐ Set Default button in the Alerts modal footer saves the current alarm queue (plus Mobile Alerts config) to localStorage as your default set. Enable ⭐ Auto-load Default Alerts in the 🔌 Live menu and the defaults load automatically whenever a SimBrief flight plan is plotted — so you step into every flight already armed. The chooser screen's Load SimBrief Flight Plan card shows a checkbox reflecting the same setting when defaults are saved, for quick toggling before you load. The same auto-apply fires at app startup if the alarm queue is empty.
  • Airborne safeguard. Auto-load is silently skipped if the aircraft is airborne (groundspeed > 30 kt, |VS| > 100 fpm, or wheels-up confirmed) so an in-progress alert setup is never overwritten mid-mission. Browser refresh is safe by design — the queue restores from localStorage on reload and the startup auto-apply only fires when the queue is empty.
  • Fingerprint guard. Re-fetching the same plan (same origin, destination, and waypoint count) after customising alerts above the defaults does not re-apply defaults, so per-flight additions are preserved across plan refreshes. Clearing the plan resets the guard so the next plan load (even the same route) starts fresh.
  • Right-click ⭐ Set Default to clear. Right-clicking the Set Default button removes the saved defaults and resets the Auto-load toggle. The Live menu row and chooser checkbox both hide when no defaults are saved.
  • Alerts footer buttons renamed. ⤓ Save to File is now ⤓ Save Alerts and ⤒ Load from File is now ⤒ Load Alerts — matching the system's user-facing "Alerts" terminology throughout.
v3.32.1 June 18, 2026 Fix
  • Follow pin now stays in sync after demo Disconnect / Reconnect. Simulating a telemetry disconnect in Flight Demo Mode called _liveAcClear() (which hid the 📍 Follow pin and Live-menu Follow button), then restored the demo source — but never called _liveAcUpdateFollowBtn() to re-show them. Both the disconnect and reconnect branches of _demoSimDisconnect now call _liveAcUpdateFollowBtn() immediately after the source is restored, so the pin and menu item reflect the correct state at all times.
  • Follow pin syncs on Live menu open and on cross-tab alert reload. Two additional defensive sync calls added: one fires when the Live pulldown is opened (so the menu item is always current at the moment the user looks at it), and one fires in the cross-tab alert-group reload path so loading alarms from another tab can never leave the pin in a stale state.
  • 🎲 Re-roll button moved left one slot. The quick Re-roll button now sits between the 🛠 Tools and 👁 Display pulldowns instead of after Display, keeping the random-flight controls adjacent to their source menu.
v3.32.0 June 16, 2026 Feature
  • Auto-connect to FSUIPC after a flight plan loads (desktop). Once you plot a SimBrief flight plan, Active Airports quietly checks for FSUIPC once a minute and connects automatically the moment FSUIPC7 + Paul Henty's WebSocket Server is reachable — so forgetting to click FSUIPC Live before alt-tabbing to the sim no longer leaves you untracked. The first time it doesn't find FSUIPC it shows a one-time heads-up, then keeps checking silently. Clicking FSUIPC Live yourself still connects immediately and keeps the faster 15-second reconnect behavior; an explicit Stop cancels the auto-checks. Desktop only — FSUIPC requires a PC sim.
v3.31.2 June 16, 2026 Improved
  • Alerts modal — group headers now line up with alarm cards. The drag handle, expand arrow, icon, and the right-side enable toggle and ✕ delete button on a group header now sit in exactly the same columns as the equivalent controls on standalone alarm cards, so the list reads as a clean grid instead of having the group row's controls drift left and right of the cards above it.
v3.31.1 June 16, 2026 Fix
  • Alert labels now draw above flight-plan waypoints. Geofence labels and waypoint-alarm rings now render above the SimBrief flight-plan waypoint dots and their label pills. The v3.31.0 change that lifted airport tooltips above the breadcrumb chips had also pushed the flight-plan waypoint labels on top of the alert labels; the alert annotations are now back on top of every flight-path element.
v3.31.0 June 16, 2026 Feature
  • Alert groups now have a drag handle. Each group in the Alerts panel gets a ⋮⋮ grip on the left of its header. Grab it to drag the whole group — members move together, in order — up or down the list (or onto the top/bottom drop zones). Previously only individual alarms could be reordered; a group could not be moved as a unit.
  • Aircraft icon now sits above the flight path. The live-aircraft marker was drawing under route labels and its own breadcrumb trail. It now renders in a dedicated layer above every flight-path element — route line, waypoint markers, route labels, and the trail — so the plane is always visible.
  • Airport tooltips no longer hide behind the breadcrumb chips. A dot tooltip near the top of the map used to be painted under the breadcrumb filter strip. Tooltips now sit above the breadcrumb bubbles.
  • Airport name moved above the “For sale” line in map tooltips. In the map dot tooltip the airport name now reads first, with the optional 💰 For sale flag beneath it.
v3.30.0 June 16, 2026 Feature
  • Double right-click a for-sale airport to open its marketplace listing. On the map, double right-clicking a 💰 for-sale airport opens the SimFly user marketplace filtered to that airport in a new tab. A single right-click still opens the scenery Quick-Rate popover as before — only for-sale airports add the second gesture, and only their rate popover is deferred a fraction of a second to tell the two apart, so right-click-to-rate stays instant on every other airport. (Desktop only, like the rest of the For Sale feature.)
v3.29.2 June 16, 2026 Fix
  • For-sale icon changed to 💰. The for-sale marker in tooltips, the Display-menu toggle, and the filter chip now use the widely-supported 💰 money-bag glyph instead of the 🏷 tag, which rendered as a blank box on some devices.
v3.29.1 June 16, 2026 Improved
  • For Sale is a desktop feature. The for-sale filter already lived in the desktop-only Display menu; now the 💰 Listed for sale tooltip marker is hidden on mobile too, so the For Sale feature stays out of the mobile version entirely. Airport level and category still show in mobile tooltips as before.
v3.29.0 June 16, 2026 Improved
  • New 👁 Display menu. The Tools menu was getting long, so the three visual-overlay toggles — ☼ Display Levels, ✦ Display New Airports, and 💰 For Sale Only — now live in their own 👁 Display pulldown in the map header. The 🛠 Tools menu keeps Measurement, Select Regions, and Create Random Route.
v3.28.2 June 16, 2026 Fix
  • Random Flight filter inputs no longer crowd the spinner. The distance and flight-time number fields are now left-aligned so the digits sit clear of the up/down spinner instead of being squashed against it.
v3.28.1 June 16, 2026 Improved
  • Airport level badges now on by default. The map now shows the level “corona” spikes the first time you open it, so airport levels are visible out of the box. Toggle them off any time from 🛠 Tools → ☼ Display Levels — your choice is still remembered between sessions, so anyone who previously set the toggle keeps their preference.
v3.28.0 June 16, 2026 Feature
  • Airport level now shown in every tooltip. Hover any airport — on the map, in the distance matrix, on the route inputs, or the info cards — and its SimFly level is shown right alongside its category (e.g. Category: 3 · Level: 2). Previously the level only appeared as the corona badge ring.
  • Airports for sale are flagged. When an airport is listed for sale, its tooltip now shows a 💰 Listed for sale line (and a 💰 For sale tag on the map dot tooltip).
  • Filter the map to airports for sale. A new 💰 For Sale Only toggle in the 👁 Display menu hides everything except airports currently for sale; it shows as a For Sale chip in the filter strip (✕ to clear), is swept up by ⊗ Clear All, and stacks with every other map filter (including the Random Flight “currently shown” option). For-sale status comes from the airport data feed; airports show as for sale once the feed marks them.
v3.27.0 June 16, 2026 Feature
  • Random Flight: filter the departure AND the arrival. In Create Random Route mode the filter panel now has a Departure / Arrival toggle, so you can set category, continent, country and owner independently for each end — e.g. depart a Cat 1 grass strip and arrive at a Cat 5 international. (In Choose Random Destination mode the departure is your pick, so only the destination is filterable.)
  • Live filter counts. The number beside every country and owner now updates as you change the other filters — pick a category or continent and each row shows how many airports still qualify. Tick Only airports currently shown on the map and the counts fold in your active map filters too.
  • Quick re-roll button. After your first random flight, a 🎲 Re-roll button appears in the map header so you can spin up another with the same filters in one click — no need to reopen the Tools menu. Right-click (or long-press) it to tweak the filters.
v3.26.0 June 16, 2026 Feature
  • 🎲 Random flight generator — in the 🛠 Tools menu. Suggested by a user who flies wherever the mood takes them: one click picks a random route to another pilot’s SimFly-owned airport, draws it on the map, and pops up the File on SimBrief button so you can fly it straight away. If you’ve already selected a departure, it switches to Choose Random Destination and keeps your departure fixed, re-rolling only the destination on each click. With no departure selected it’s Create Random Route and picks both ends fresh every time.
  • Filter your random flights — every filter stacks. Right-click the menu item (long-press on touch) to open the filter panel. Mix and match any combination: limit to airports currently shown on the map (respects all your active map filters); pick destination airport categories (colour-coded pills) and continents; multi-select destination countries and owners (searchable checklists with counts and All / None); set a distance range (nm); and — when the selected aircraft has flight-time calibration — a flight-time range (minutes). Category, continent and country pills only ever show values that actually have an owned airport, and the flight-time filter is hidden when the current aircraft has no calibration. Your filters are saved in the browser; the first time you use the feature it opens the filter dialog so you can dial it in, then every later click just generates a new flight.
  • Always a clean map. Any existing route is cleared before a new one is drawn, so there’s never more than one random route on screen, and the map re-centres to frame each new route.
v3.25.5 June 16, 2026 Improved
  • Startup choice wording. The Show airports by Owner card now reads “I already know the SimFly owners of the airports I want…” to make clear the owners are SimFly pilots.
v3.25.4 June 16, 2026 Improved
  • Startup choice wording. The Explore map card on the startup screen now reads “Explore SimFly airports based on…” to make clear the map covers the SimFly airport network.
v3.25.3 June 16, 2026 Fix
  • Switching aircraft now refreshes on-screen time estimates. Estimated en-route times already drawn on the map (measurements, the region hero line, and the route line) used to keep the previous aircraft's figures until the line was redrawn. They now re-estimate immediately when you change aircraft — and disappear entirely if the newly selected aircraft has no flight-time calibration.
v3.25.2 June 16, 2026 Improved
  • Flight-time estimate label reads "1:30 est." The estimated en-route time on the measurement, hero, and route lines now reads as e.g. 1:30 est. instead of the previous ≈1:30 — clearer at a glance and no squiggly-equals symbol. The calibration panel's live preview hint matches.
v3.25.1 June 16, 2026 Improved
  • Estimated flight time now updates live while you draw. The H:MM est. estimate (from the aircraft's flight-time calibration) previously appeared only on the finished line. It now also ticks along in real time as you drag — on the distance measurement label as you stretch the ruler, and on the region hero line's "… nm to arrival" readout as you reach toward the arrival region. As before, nothing is shown when the selected aircraft has no calibration (or none is selected).
v3.25.0 June 16, 2026 Improved
  • The ⊗ Clear All chip now also resets the flight plan and aircraft track. The circled ⊗ button at the front of the map breadcrumb strip used to clear only filters. It now also removes a plotted SimBrief flight plan and any recorded aircraft track in the same click — a single "reset the map" entry point. The chip now appears whenever any of those is present, not just when a filter is applied, so it shows up even if the only thing on the map is a flight plan or a track. The confirmation toast names exactly what was cleared (e.g. Cleared filters and flight plan).
v3.24.0 June 16, 2026 Feature
  • Estimated flight time on the map — calibrate any aircraft with two flights. The aircraft selector now has a Flight time calibration panel. Give it your shortest and longest flights in that airframe — each as a distance in nautical miles and an elapsed time in minutes, both read straight off the SimFly UI — and the app draws a straight line through the two points to estimate en-route time for any distance.
  • Estimates appear where you measure distance. Once an aircraft is calibrated, an H:MM est. time is appended to three places: the ruler/measurement tooltip, the region-to-region hero line in Region Mode, and the selected route line. If the current aircraft has no calibration (or none is selected), nothing is added — the tooltips look exactly as before.
  • Saved per aircraft, survives reloads. Each airframe keeps its own calibration in the browser, so once you've dialled in a type it stays dialled in. A live hint under the inputs confirms when estimates are switched on and shows a sample time so you can sanity-check the numbers.
v3.23.0 June 16, 2026 Feature
  • Filter the map by airport level — right-click any Map category circle. Each numbered category circle in the map header now opens a level filter on right-click (long-press on touch). Pick exactly which levels of that category stay on the map — tick individual levels for a custom mix, or use the ≥ button on any level to keep that level and everything above it in one tap. All / None clear or empty the category in one click. Each category keeps its own independent level selection.
  • The level choices come from what's actually on the map. The menu only lists levels that real airports of that category currently have under your other filters — if there are no Category 5 / Level 3 airports in view, Level 3 simply isn't offered. Each level shows a live count so you know how many airports it covers before you toggle it.
  • Wired into the filter status strip. A small dot appears on every category circle that has a level filter applied, and a Level Filtering chip joins the breadcrumb strip whenever any level filter is active — hover it to see each category's kept levels, click its ✕ to clear them all at once. The level filter is also swept up by ⊗ Clear All alongside every other filter, and it constrains the region distance matrix exactly the way the category filter does.
v3.22.0 June 15, 2026 Feature
  • ☼ Airport level badges — new "Display Levels" toggle. Each airport now carries a SimFly level (separate from its category) that climbs as the airport earns XP from traffic. With the new toggle on, every airport dot grows a ring of tick marks — a "corona" — showing its level: the first spike sits at 12 o'clock and each additional level adds another evenly-spaced spike around the dot (level 2 = top + bottom, level 3 = 12/4/8 o'clock, level 4 = 12/3/6/9, and so on). A white halo keeps the marks legible on both the default and satellite basemaps. To keep the map uncluttered, badges are off by default and only level 2 and above are drawn (a single level 1 tick on hundreds of dots was just noise). The toggle lives in the 🛠 Tools menu (desktop) and the right-hand button band (mobile), and your choice is remembered.
  • Level data now flows from the hourly payout feed. The shared payout-data.json dataset that powers owner colors, categories, and new-airport rings now also carries each airport's level, so the badges stay current automatically with no extra fetch.
v3.21.12 June 15, 2026 Fix
  • SimBrief startup mode now actually matches Explore Map speed. The previous implementation polled window._rgMap, which is never set because _rgMap is a let variable — the poll loop ran forever and the SimBrief plan never loaded on the no-cache path. Replaced all custom fetch/poll machinery with a direct call to _sbOfpPlot() once the map is ready, identical to the Live menu button that was already proven fast.
v3.21.11 June 15, 2026 Improved
  • SimBrief startup mode now matches Explore Map speed in all cases. The progress bar is removed entirely. With a cached plan the route appears the instant the map opens. Without a cached plan the map opens fully usable immediately, the SimBrief fetch runs silently in the background, and a toast confirms when the route lands — the same non-blocking experience as Explore Map.
v3.21.10 June 15, 2026 Improved
  • SimBrief startup mode is now instant when a cached plan exists. If a SimBrief flight plan was previously loaded, the route now appears the moment the map opens — identical in speed to the Explore Map path — with a background refresh running silently. A toast appears only if SimBrief returned a newer plan. The loader is shown only on first-ever use when no cached plan exists.
  • SimBrief fetch parallelised with map init. The API call now starts the instant the startup button is clicked, in parallel with the map opening. The misleading two-phase "Loading map & airport data…" progress bar is replaced with a single accurate "Fetching SimBrief flight plan…" label — the map and airport data were already loaded before the choice screen appeared.
v3.21.9 June 15, 2026 Improved
  • SimBrief startup mode loads significantly faster. The SimBrief API fetch now starts the moment the button is clicked — in parallel with the map opening — instead of waiting for the map to fully initialise first. The progress bar now shows a single accurate label ("Fetching SimBrief flight plan…") rather than the misleading "Loading map & airport data…" phase, which was never the actual bottleneck at that point in the startup sequence. On a typical connection the route now appears 3–6 seconds sooner.
v3.21.8 June 15, 2026 Fix
  • Waypoint alarm card header now updates immediately when waypoints are checked. Checking or unchecking a specific waypoint in the alarm editor now instantly reflects the selection in the card header (e.g. "MLP" instead of "(no waypoints picked)"). Previously the header only updated after closing and reopening the alarm list.
v3.21.7 June 14, 2026 Fix
  • Geofences redraw when departure and arrival are swapped. Dep/arr-anchored geofence circles now re-resolve and redraw immediately whenever the route direction is reversed — whether via the route pill on the map or the ⇄ button in the distance planner. The wasInside flag is also reset so the alarm re-arms cleanly against the new airport position. Previously the circles remained in their original locations until the next telemetry tick.
v3.21.6 June 14, 2026 Fix
  • Buy Me a Coffee button no longer overlaps the choice cards. The button was absolutely positioned relative to the screen edge, which caused it to collide with the taller three-card layout. It now flows naturally below the card grid, matching where the removed Skip link used to sit.
v3.21.5 June 14, 2026 Improved
  • Startup choice screen — Skip link removed. The “Skip — just take me to the app” link has been removed from the choice screen on both desktop and mobile. The three mode cards are now the only entry points. Pressing Esc still dismisses the screen.
v3.21.4 June 14, 2026 Improved
  • SimBrief startup mode — progress bar while map and flight plan load. A slim progress strip now appears between the map header and the map tiles while the two async stages complete. Stage 1 (“Loading map & airport data…”) animates 0→44 % while waiting for Leaflet to initialise; stage 2 (“Fetching SimBrief flight plan…”) picks up at 50 % once the map is ready and animates toward 84 %. On completion it snaps to 100 % (“Route ready”), holds briefly, then fades out. Colours, font, and bar style match the boot splash screen.
v3.21.3 June 14, 2026 Improved
  • SimBrief startup mode — route auto-populated for one-click filing. After the SimBrief OFP loads, the departure and arrival ICAOs from the flight plan are written into the Distance Planner fields. This lights up the File on SimBrief → button with the correct route pre-filled, so hovering shows the full filing preview and clicking files immediately — no manual ICAO entry needed.
v3.21.2 June 14, 2026 Fix
  • SimBrief startup mode — Alerts panel opens immediately on map open. v3.21.1 still gated the Alerts panel behind the Leaflet map initialising (which waits for the airport database to load from the Gist — several seconds on a cold start). The Alerts modal is a pure DOM overlay that needs no map; it now opens 60 ms after openRegionModal is called, regardless of map state. The SimBrief OFP fetch continues to poll for the map in the background and plots the route once ready.
v3.21.1 June 14, 2026 Fix
  • SimBrief startup mode — Alerts panel now opens immediately. In v3.21.0 the Alerts panel was deferred until the SimBrief API fetch completed, causing a 15–20 second wait before the modal appeared. The fetch and the panel open now happen concurrently: the Alerts modal opens as soon as the map is ready while the flight plan plots in the background.
v3.21.0 June 14, 2026 Feature
  • New startup mode — Load SimBrief Flight Plan (desktop only). A third card now appears centered above the two existing startup cards, forming a triangle layout. Clicking it imports your latest SimBrief OFP and opens the map with the Alerts panel already open, so you can configure waypoint, cruise, descent, or timer alarms immediately after loading the route. If your SimBrief Pilot ID isn’t saved yet you’re prompted for it once (same ID used by the in-map SimBrief button — no re-entry needed if already set). Cancelling the prompt returns you to the choice screen. The button is hidden on mobile since FSUIPC-based alerts require a desktop simulator connection.
v3.20.0 June 14, 2026 Improved
  • Alert groups — more visually distinct. Group containers now have a stronger cyan border (left side accented at 3 px, full perimeter at 70% opacity) and a more visible background tint so a group is immediately recognisable against standalone alert cards. The tripped state keeps its red accent; light-mode colours updated to match.
  • Drag an alert onto a group header to add it. Any standalone alert card can now be dragged directly onto the group header row (the row with the group name, member count, and mode pill) to add it to that group. The header highlights green with a “+ Add to group” label on hover. Previously the only drop target was an individual member card inside the group body, which was invisible when the group was collapsed. Right-click → Add to existing group… still works as before.
  • Load from File — alerts default to collapsed. Importing an alert file now collapses every imported card automatically. Previously all cards rendered expanded, flooding the modal when loading a large saved set. Cards can be expanded individually after load by clicking the chevron; the collapsed state is persisted across reloads as normal.
  • Group header — mode pill and Ungroup button no longer wrap. Added white-space: nowrap and flex-shrink: 0 to both buttons so “CASCADE” (and “CONCURRENT” / “SEQUENTIAL”) never break across two lines when the header is tight. The group name is now an overflow-ellipsis field that surrenders space instead, keeping all buttons single-row.
  • Alerts modal wider. The Alerts modal max-width increased from clamp(720px, 52vw, 1080px) to clamp(820px, 58vw, 1200px) so the group header row has more breathing room on larger screens.
  • SimBrief geofence auto-update — verified. Confirmed that dep/arr-anchored geofences (anchor = Departure airport or Arrival airport) re-resolve and redraw the instant a SimBrief flight plan is loaded or replaced. When the loaded plan changes (different origin/destination ICAO), the _afkTick fingerprint check resets wasInside and re-renders geofence circles so no stale state carries over to the new plan. No code change was required; this is documentation of confirmed existing behaviour.
v3.19.51 June 14, 2026 Improved
  • Explore map card — mobile description trimmed. On mobile viewports the “Explore map” startup card no longer lists flight tracking and in-flight alerts, since both features are hidden on mobile (they require a simulator connection). The desktop description is unchanged.
v3.19.50 June 14, 2026 Improved
  • Buy Me a Coffee link on the startup choice screen. A Buy Me a Coffee button now appears pinned to the bottom center of the “What would you like to do?” screen (absolutely positioned, 40% smaller than the default size). It sits clear of the choice cards and Skip link and does not affect the vertical centering of the main content.
v3.19.49 June 12, 2026 Fix
  • ✦ Display New Airports always visible once payout data loads. Previously the menu item was hidden when no airports fell within the current pulse-days window, making it impossible to right-click and widen the window to bring airports back. The button now stays visible as long as payout-data.json has loaded successfully, regardless of how many airports currently qualify.
v3.19.48 June 12, 2026 Fix
  • ✦ Display New Airports not appearing in Tools menu. _rgSyncNewAirportsButtons() was only toggling the .active class and never removing the button's inline display:none. Because _rgRenderDots() fires when the map first opens — before loadAirportDB() completes and sets the button visible — the button stayed hidden even when new airports were already showing pulse rings. Fixed by moving the style.display logic into _rgSyncNewAirportsButtons(), which is called on every dot re-render and always reflects the current newAirportsDB state.
v3.19.47 June 7, 2026 Improved
  • Button highlight matches chip visibility — The 💰 Payout and ⭐ Rating menu buttons now light up exactly when their respective filter panel (chip) is visible on screen. Closing a panel with its ✕ button dims the menu button; re-clicking the button to reopen the panel restores the highlight. Applies to both desktop and mobile.
v3.19.46 June 7, 2026 Feature
  • Payout palette editor now accessible on mobile. The editor was intentionally hidden on touch devices in v3.19.23 (considered desktop-only). It’s now re-enabled: long-press the 💰 Payout button for 600 ms to open it, or long-press anywhere within the payout legend panel. Three layers of blocking were removed: the display: none !important CSS in both the 768px and (hover: none) blocks, and the touch-action: manipulation that was suppressing contextmenu events on the legend container. The 60vh height cap with scrolling ensures it fits on a phone screen. The existing slider-drag guard (600 ms since last slider input) still prevents the editor from opening accidentally mid-drag.
v3.19.45 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Distance slider stacking now works on all phones. The stacking CSS was in @media (max-width: 480px) but some phones report a CSS pixel width above 480 px depending on their display density — meaning the 480 px block never matched on those devices. The layout rules (two-row stacking, order properties, “C750 Range” label hide) are now in the @media (max-width: 768px) block, which the 768 px category-circle dimming confirmed is matching correctly. The 480 px block is now a font-size-only override for the narrowest phones.
v3.19.44 June 7, 2026 Improved
  • Category circle dimming — much stronger off-state on mobile. The previous treatment (opacity: 0.25 + grayscale(0.55)) wasn’t visually distinct enough on real phone screens. Both the main-screen distance filter row and the Region Mode header now use opacity: 0.22 + grayscale(1) brightness(0.6) — fully desaturated and clearly darker — so inactive circles look unmistakably muted against the vivid, fully-coloured active ones. Light-mode override uses a lighter version of the same treatment so circles remain legible on the cream background.
v3.19.43 June 7, 2026 Improved
  • Distance slider stacking finally works on phones. The previous attempt added flex-wrap and justify-content: space-between but omitted the critical order properties. Because the HTML order is [min-val][range-wrap][max-val], flex-wrap produced three separate rows (min / track / max) instead of the intended two. Explicit order: 1 / 2 / 3 on min-val, max-val, and range-wrap now cause flex to place both value labels on row 1 (space-between, left–right) and the track full-width on row 2. The max value can no longer clip off the right edge.
  • Distance slider handles enlarged and colour-coded for touch. Touch thumbs grow from 28 px to 36 px with a drop shadow that lifts them off the track. The minimum thumb is cyan and the maximum thumb is orange, matching the app’s departure/arrival colour convention, so the two handles are distinguishable when close together.
  • Category circles no longer overflow onto a second row. The 2.55 rem size introduced in v3.19.39 was too wide for a 375 px phone: 7 circles + label totalled ~380 px against a ~359 px content area. Reduced to 2.2 rem (gap 0.5 rem), which totals ~333 px and fits with room to spare.
v3.19.42 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • New Airports button title uses desktop language. The mobile-only ✦ New button had title="Tap…Long-press…" — mobile language that is inconsistent with the rule that title attributes use desktop wording (they only render on hover, never on touch). Updated to "Toggle pulse indicators · Right-click for color / pulse-rate / days settings". The title was unreachable in practice (the button is hidden on desktop; titles don’t render on mobile) but is now consistent with the rest of the codebase.
v3.19.41 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Payout button tooltip uses correct language per device. The title attribute on the 💰 Payout button was changed to say “Tap…Long-press” in v3.19.40, but title tooltips only render on desktop hover — touch devices never show them. Reverted to desktop-appropriate wording: “Click to color airports by pilot payout · Right-click to customize the palette”.
v3.19.40 June 7, 2026 Improved
  • Payout palette editor opens on mobile via long-press. Previously the palette editor (gradient color-stop customiser) was inaccessible on touch devices because right-click is unavailable and the “bail on touch” guard blocked it entirely. Long-pressing the 💰 Payout button in the map header or the gradient bar in the Pilot Payout legend now opens the editor on iOS and Android. Two supporting fixes were needed: (1) the dismiss listener now uses touchstart instead of mousedown on touch, preventing the synthesised mouse event from closing the editor the moment you lift your finger; (2) a drag-detection guard suppresses accidental opens when the slider was recently dragged (versus an intentional still hold).
v3.19.39 June 7, 2026 Improved
  • Distance slider stacks cleanly on phones. Previously the min and max value labels, slider track, and absolutely-positioned aircraft-range code label all competed for the same horizontal row, clipping the right-side values against the screen edge. On phone the layout now stacks into two rows: the min value sits at the left and max value at the right of the first row, then the track spans full-width below. The aircraft-range code label is hidden on phone — the filter button already names the aircraft, so it was redundant information causing the overflow.
  • Category circles larger and better spaced on phones. The Dep and Arr numbered circles (1–7) on the main screen now render at 2.55 rem (up from 2.1 rem) with wider gaps between them, making them easier to tap. The change is scoped to the main-screen filter row — the smaller circles in the map header are unaffected.
v3.19.38 June 7, 2026 Improved
  • Opening the map no longer resets the owner filter. Previously, opening the map while departure and arrival owners were selected on the main screen would automatically narrow the map to show only those two owners — discarding any filter you had set during a previous map session. The map now opens with whatever owner filter was last active, leaving you in control. The Recip Tracker / RM Lite “Send to AA” handoff (which explicitly passes a pilot set via URL) is unaffected.
v3.19.37 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Filter airports button renders correctly on phone. The JS width-lock in _distShowAcFilterBtn measured offsetWidth while the parent .dist-filter-row was still display:none, locking the button to 0 px. The button border then rendered as a tiny circle and the text floated outside it. Fixed by skipping the width-lock on touch devices entirely (CSS width:100% !important handles sizing there). Also added !important to the existing width:100% rule so it wins over any inline style, and white-space:normal so long labels can wrap instead of overflowing.
  • Distance range slider no longer clips on a phone. The fixed 110 px value labels plus the 140 px track minimum totalled 384 px — 25 px wider than a 375 px phone’s 359 px content area, clipping the max-value label. Value labels now size to their text content and can shrink; track minimum reduced to 80 px. The labels and track are still centred with the rest of the Distance section.
  • Range slider thumbs enlarged for touch. The 18 × 18 px desktop thumbs are hard to grab with a finger. On touch devices (@media (hover:none)) the track height and thumbs are raised to 28 × 28 px, closer to the 44 px HIG ideal. The value-marker arrow below the track is repositioned to clear the taller track.
v3.19.36 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Airport Owners button shows space between “Airport” and “Owners” on desktop. The button uses display:inline-flex, which causes trailing whitespace at the end of an inline flex item to be stripped during line-box collapsing. The trailing space in the .aa-owner-prefix span was being dropped, rendering the label as “AirportOwners”. Changed the space to &nbsp;, which is not collapsible. Mobile behavior is unchanged — the whole span is hidden on narrow screens, taking the non-breaking space with it.
v3.19.35 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Payout Palette editor flips upward when near the map bottom. Previously the editor always expanded downward from the legend’s top edge; on a short browser window with many colour stops it could clip below the map. _rgPayoutPaletteOpen() now measures the editor’s rendered height after display and, if the bottom would exceed the map container boundary, repositions to open upward from the legend’s bottom edge instead.
  • Pilot Payout chip width matched to Scenery Rating chip. Added min-width:271px to the Pilot Payout legend to match the Scenery Rating legend, so both chips are the same width when centred on a phone screen.
v3.19.34 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Pilot Payout and Scenery Rating chips centred horizontally on phone. Both chips were right-aligned (right:8px inline) which looks fine on a wide desktop map but sits awkwardly on a narrow phone screen. @media (hover: none) now overrides to left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%) so both chips (and the palette editor, if it were ever visible) are centred over the map. The JS bottom-stacking from v3.19.33 is unaffected — it only sets top/bottom.
v3.19.33 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Payout and Scenery Rating chips move to the bottom of the map on touch devices. Both panels were anchored at top:50px, placing them over the breadcrumb filter row at the top of the screen. On touch devices _rgRepositionFilterPanels() now sets top:auto and positions each panel from the bottom (50 px above the Leaflet attribution bar). When both chips are visible simultaneously, the second stacks upward above the first with an 8 px gap. Desktop behaviour is unchanged.
v3.19.32 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Category filter circles fully touch-friendly on phones. “Dep” and “Arr” labels now appear above their respective circle rows (each label is full-width in its flex container, so it wraps onto its own line). Both “Inv” buttons removed to free horizontal space. All 7 circles now fit on a single row with no overflow. Gap between the Dep section, Arr section, and the two action buttons increased so accidental presses on adjacent targets are avoided.
v3.19.31 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Category filter circles and action buttons sized for finger taps. The 1–7 category circles were 1.55 rem (~25 px) with minimal gaps — too small and crowded for reliable touch use. Bumped to 2.5 rem (~40 px) with wider gaps and scaled the “Dep”/“Arr” labels and Inv button to match. The “Filter airports unsuitable for …” and “Hide filtered airports” buttons also got taller padding to reach ~44 px tap height.
v3.19.30 June 7, 2026 Fix
  • Filter sub-row buttons no longer overflow on a phone. The “Filter airports unsuitable for …” and “Hide filtered airports” buttons were sitting side-by-side in a flex row, clipping both labels on a 390 px screen. On touch devices they now stack vertically, each taking the full width, matching the treatment already given to other action buttons in the touch UX refactor.
v3.19.29 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Touch-UX refactor — iPad and other tablets now get the touch-friendly behaviours that v3.19.x built for phones. Up through v3.19.28, every “this is for touch users” rule was gated by @media (max-width: 768px) — which conflated small viewport (which iPhone has) with touch input (which iPad also has, in a wider viewport). Result: an iPad in portrait at 810 px got the desktop layout but missed every touch-friendly behaviour we built — the tap-to-pin tooltip was the only one that worked (because it uses a runtime _aaIsTouchDevice() check, not a media query).
  • The fix: a new @media (hover: none) block is added after the existing mobile block, duplicating the touch-only rules. Phone (which matches BOTH queries) behaves identically to v3.19.28. Tablets (which match hover: none but NOT max-width: 768px) inherit the same touch polish while keeping their desktop spatial layout. Desktop browsers with a mouse are unchanged — hover: none doesn’t match a hover-capable pointer.
  • What iPad / tablets now get for free:
    • OS text-selection menu no longer hijacks taps on dropdown items / chips / pills.
    • Sim-tied features hidden — AFK Alerts button, Live menu (FSUIPC / STKP / Mobile Alerts setup / Demo Mode / etc.), Tools menu’s mouse-drag-only items (Measurement, Select Regions), the FSUIPC telemetry data panel, and the Payout palette colour editor.
    • Map view’s left band (which held Tools / Live / Alerts) is hidden; center and right bands re-anchored via grid-column so the layout still reads as 3-column.
    • The ✦ New Airports direct toggle button appears in the right band’s display-toggle cluster (the Tools dropdown becomes a single-item dropdown otherwise, which is silly).
    • Display-toggle buttons (Payout / Rating / New Airports) get clear neutral-off / vivid-on contrast instead of the desktop cyan-tinted border that looked similar in both states.
    • Help modal’s PC-only feature sections are hidden, replaced with the explanatory note (text updated from “on mobile” to “on touch devices” to match).
    • ✕ close buttons (filter chip, SimBrief pill, modal headers) sized to finger-friendly 30-38 px tap targets.
    • The Payout slider’s contextmenu-from-long-press trap (which on touch would open the palette editor and swallow taps) is suppressed.
  • What stays at (max-width: 768px): all narrow-viewport layout work (column stacks, smaller fonts, removed Map/All/None labels, shrunken category circles, modal layout restructure, Scenery Library / Manage Values / Developers modal mobile layouts). iPad has enough horizontal room that those simplifications would feel cramped on the bigger screen.
  • Hybrid result on iPad: desktop spatial layout (Owners button reads “Airport Owners”, full-size category circles, side-by-side Store/Dev fields), but with phone-style touch input (large tap targets, no FSUIPC chrome, tap-to-pin tooltips, sim-tied features absent).
v3.19.28 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library row — mobile layout restructured. v3.19.27 just shrunk the stars; v3.19.28 properly restructures the row into two grid lines. Row 1: ICAO + airport name link on the left, delete ✕ on the right. Row 2: stars on the left, rating number (e.g. 3.0/5) on the right. Stars come back to a finger-friendlier 1.55 rem now that they have the full row width to spread. The meta sub-row (Store / Dev free-text fields) also restructures into a 2-column label/input grid so each input gets a full-width line, and the two field widths match.
  • Manage Store & Developer Values modal — usable on mobile. The two tables (Stores / Developers) used to sit side-by-side via grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr, which on a 390 px screen crushed the AIRPORTS column header to one letter per line and made the modal effectively unreadable. Stacked vertically on mobile, with table fonts + padding shrunk so the actual data fits each row.
  • Scenery Developers modal — phone-readable fonts. The Developers modal’s fonts were bumped +40% on desktop (1.29-1.37 rem) for laptop legibility. On a phone that’s too big — the columns don’t fit and headers run off the right. Reduced intro text to 0.92 rem, table header to 0.82 rem, table body to 0.95 rem, drill-down chips to 0.9 rem; stars in the rating column come down from 1.47 rem to 1 rem. Per-row filter 🔍 button also resized.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop sizing for all three surfaces is unchanged.
v3.19.27 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery Library list row — stars no longer overlap the ICAO on mobile. Each row’s interactive star widget is sized for desktop at 2.24 rem per star with 5 px h-padding (~46 px × 5 = 230 px total) and the widget is flex-shrink: 0. Combined with the 3.15-rem-min rating-number and the delete ✕, that’s ~340 px of fixed-width content before the ICAO + name link gets a slice — the link’s flex: 1 collapses below the 4.7 rem min-width of the ICAO span, and the stars wind up rendering on top of the ICAO text. The scenery library wasn’t designed for 390 px viewports.
  • The fix: on mobile, shrink the per-row stars to 1.4 rem / 2 px padding (~120 px total cluster), shrink the rating-number to 0.95 rem / 2.4-rem min-width, shrink the delete ✕, and tighten the row gap from 0.6 rem to 0.35 rem. ICAO + name fonts also come down a touch (1.27 rem → 1.05 rem and 1.21 rem → 1 rem respectively). The row fits one line cleanly with no overlap.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop still shows the larger 2.24-rem stars and the longer rating-number.
v3.19.26 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery dirty-changes badge no longer clipped to a 1/4 arc. The yellow unsaved-changes badge on the Scenery split button (positioned at top: -7px; right: -7px to “pop out” of the button’s top-right corner) was getting clipped because its parent .rg-btn.rg-sl-split had overflow: hidden. The original intent of the clip was to keep the inner split-button segments (the mode dot, separator, and gear icon) from poking past the 4-px rounded corners — but the current child layout fits cleanly inside the radius, so the clip is no longer needed. Flipped to overflow: visible; the badge now displays as a full circle.
  • Affects all viewports. Bug existed everywhere, not just mobile.
v3.19.25 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Rating breadcrumb ✕ now correctly clears the filter instead of re-opening the panel. Reported flow: open ⭐ Rating panel, click a star to set the threshold (breadcrumb chip appears), close the panel via its ✕ (panel hidden, mode still on), then click the ✕ on the breadcrumb chip — expected the filter to clear, but the panel popped back open instead. Root cause: the chip’s ✕ handler at line ~32410 called _rgToggleStarFilter(), whose first branch is the v3.12.0 “if mode is on but panel is hidden, treat a click as re-open the panel” UX (so the ⭐ button can bring the panel back without flipping mode). That early-return path intercepted the chip’s clear-the-filter intent.
  • The fix: new dedicated _rgClearStarFilter() helper that takes the “turn off” path unconditionally — sets mode false, clears the threshold, hides the panel, drops the button’s active class, repositions filter panels, applies the map filter, refreshes the breadcrumb strip. The chip’s ✕ handler now calls this helper directly, bypassing the toggle’s re-open special case. The ⭐ button still calls _rgToggleStarFilter() so its documented behaviour (“click button = reopen panel without flipping mode if the panel was hidden via its ✕”) is preserved.
  • Affects all viewports. This was a logic bug not a touch-input bug — it could be reproduced with a mouse on desktop just as easily as with a finger on mobile, the user just happened to be testing on mobile.
v3.19.24 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • New Airports pulse animation restored on mobile. v3.19.14 suppressed the .aa-new-ring CSS animation on mobile via animation: none !important as part of a map-pan smoothness pass — the rationale was that the per-frame compositor work could chunk up scroll on slower phones. After v3.19.22 restored long-press access to the New Airports settings modal, users reported that the pulse cycling stopped entirely on the map once that modal had been opened. v3.19.24 reverts the disable: the animation runs on mobile again, and animation-play-state: running !important is added as a belt against any browser heuristic (some iOS Safari versions, some Android low-power modes) that might pause animations while a heavy overlay covers the page. will-change: transform on the ring elements puts each one on its own compositor layer so the pan-smoothness cost the original v3.19.14 fix was trying to avoid is mitigated a different way.
  • The two bigger v3.19.14 perf fixes remain. #rgMapContainer still gets will-change: transform + transform: translateZ(0) for GPU compositor promotion, and #regionModal still has contain: layout paint for paint isolation. Those were the bigger wins for pan smoothness; only the pulse-suppression part is reverted.
v3.19.23 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Mobile screen taps no longer get “swallowed” after using the Payout or Rating filter panels. Root cause: the Payout panel’s gradient bar / slider have oncontextmenu="_rgPayoutPaletteOpen(event)" — an inline handler so desktop right-click opens the palette colour editor. On touch devices, the browser fires contextmenu after a ~500-700 ms hold, so a user dragging the Payout slider slowly was accidentally triggering it.
