The palette editor would open (often off-screen on a phone), attach a document-level mousedown capture-phase dismiss listener, and then the next few screen taps felt “swallowed” as the listener intercepted them.
  • Three-layer fix:
    • touch-action: manipulation on the Payout legend, gradient bar, slider, thumb, zero-dot, and the Rating panel + widget. This tells the browser to use only standard tap/pan/zoom gestures, suppressing the long-press contextmenu on most engines.
    • -webkit-touch-callout: none as a belt for older iOS Safari.
    • The palette editor is fully hidden on mobile via #rgPayoutPaletteEditor { display: none !important } — it’s a desktop power-user feature for customising the gradient colour stops, with no mobile use case.
    • JS: _rgPayoutPaletteOpen() now early-exits on touch devices via _aaIsTouchDevice(), so even if a contextmenu somehow fires the editor never opens and no document listener gets attached. Defence in depth.
  • Desktop behaviour unchanged. Right-clicking the Payout gradient bar on a laptop still opens the palette editor exactly as before. The mobile fix only changes touch behaviour.
v3.19.22 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Long-press on the mobile New Airports button now opens the color / pulse-rate / days settings. The desktop dropdown row has always supported right-click to open New Airport Display Settings (color, pulse rate, days threshold). When v3.19.17 promoted New Airports to a direct mobile toggle, the right-click handler wasn’t carried over — long-press is the standard mobile equivalent of right-click. v3.19.22 adds oncontextmenu="_rgNewAirportSettingsModal(event)" to the mobile button; both iOS Safari and Android Chrome fire contextmenu after a ~500–700 ms hold, so the settings modal opens on long-press just like right-click does on desktop.
  • Click-after-long-press is suppressed. After a long-press fires contextmenu and the modal opens, the user lifts their finger and the browser synthesises a click event — which would otherwise also fire _rgToggleNewAirports and invert visibility on top of opening settings. A small timestamp-based guard (_rgNewAirportsSettingsTs) is stamped inside _rgNewAirportSettingsModal and checked at the top of _rgToggleNewAirports: if the modal opened within the last 600 ms, the toggle bails out. Net effect: long-press = settings modal opens, visibility unchanged; tap = toggle on/off.
  • Button title updated to surface the new affordance: “Tap to toggle pulse indicators · Long-press for color / pulse-rate / days settings.” The title shows in browser hover-tip on devices that expose it.
  • Pure JS / HTML edit; no CSS change; affects mobile only. Desktop right-click behaviour on the dropdown row is unchanged.
v3.19.21 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • New Airports toggle now reflects the actual map state at startup. The map defaults to showing pulse rings on recently-added airports (_rgNewAirportsVisible = true at module load), but the toolbar toggle button was never given its .active class until the user clicked it. Result: on first opening the map, the pulse rings were clearly visible while the button looked off — a confusing state mismatch. v3.19.21 extracts the button-syncing logic from _rgToggleNewAirports() into a new _rgSyncNewAirportsButtons() helper and calls it from _rgRenderDots() right after the pulse layer is initialised. Both the desktop dropdown row and the mobile direct toggle now reflect the current value of _rgNewAirportsVisible at startup, on every dot re-render, and after every user toggle.
  • Pure JS fix; no CSS change; affects all viewports. The bug existed on desktop too — the dropdown menu row that says “Display New Airports” was equally not lit up at startup — just less obvious because the dropdown isn’t open by default.
v3.19.20 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • 💰 Payout and ⭐ Rating buttons now show on/off clearly — same visual language as ✦ New Airports. v3.19.18 fixed the ✦ New Airports toggle to use a clearly neutral off state (gray border, dim text, mostly-transparent background) and a vivid on state (saturated cyan background + bright border + glow). The sibling display toggles 💰 Payout and ⭐ Rating were still using the default .rg-btn / .rg-btn.active styling, where both states had a cyan-tinted border that made it nearly impossible to tell when they were active. v3.19.20 extends the same neutral-off / vivid-on rules to all three buttons so they share the same visual state language. Dark mode and light mode both get the matching treatment. The Scenery split button (⬤ + ⚙) keeps its own existing styling — it’s a different control shape (split button with cycling mode) and didn’t suffer from the same ambiguity.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop sees no change — on a wider screen the existing cyan-tinted border / brighter-cyan-active styling reads fine.
v3.19.19 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Light-mode accessibility — category circle neon palette swap (applies on desktop too). The category filter circles set --rg-cat-color inline from the dark-mode neon palette (#FF4040, #FF8800, #FFEE00, #44FF44, #00DDFF, #4466FF, #FF44FF) which is designed to glow against a dark background and washes out into pale outlines against the cream light-mode bg. A new global light-mode rule overrides --rg-cat-color per data-cat with the same accessible darker variants we already use for matrix-header ICAOs (#b8002a, #a64500, #7d6500, #1a7a2e, #005f87, #2433a3, #8a1875). Applies on both desktop and mobile light mode so the circles read with proper contrast either way.
  • Mobile light-mode font weights — the wispiness fix. The existing global light-mode rule bumps a handful of element classes from Rajdhani 500 to 600 for PC legibility, but several on-screen labels weren’t in that list and looked hazy on a small phone with light-mode glare. Mobile light-mode now also bumps: .subtitle (the “Route Distances Between Active Player-Owned Airports” line under the title), .version, the aircraft selector title and selected-label, and the Departure / Arrival Airport Owner labels. All go to 600.
  • Inactive category circles in mobile light mode — readable now. The dark-mode-tuned off state (opacity 0.25 + grayscale 0.55) was nearly invisible against the cream light-mode bg. Mobile light mode now uses opacity 0.4 + grayscale 0.35 — off-circles stay clearly muted but are still readable. The active state adds a slightly opaque white background fill (rgba(255,255,255,0.75)) so the colored border has more presence on the cream backdrop.
  • Same gate for the mobile-specific rules: @media (max-width: 768px). The light-mode --rg-cat-color palette swap is intentionally OUTSIDE the media query — it’s a desktop-and-mobile accessibility fix, not just a mobile fix. Dark mode is byte-identical to v3.19.18 on all screen sizes.
v3.19.18 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Inactive category circles — clearly off now. The shared .dist-cat-btn style uses opacity: 0.35 for the off state with the rainbow border colours still showing through. On desktop that’s a fine contrast; on phones it was easy to mistake a dim-but-still-colourful circle for an active one. Mobile now drops opacity to 0.25 and adds filter: grayscale(0.55) so off-circles read as visibly muted. Active circles retain full opacity and full saturation so the contrast is unambiguous.
  • ✦ New Airports toggle — obvious on/off contrast. The default .rg-btn style uses a cyan-tinted border (var(--border)) and the .active class swaps that for a brighter cyan border + cyan-tinted background. On a phone both states looked similar and the bright border made the OFF state look like ON. Mobile now forces a clearly neutral off state (gray border, dim text, mostly-transparent background) and a vivid on state (saturated background + bright border + glow). Light mode gets an equivalent neutral-vs-saturated treatment.
  • Close ✕ button now fits on the same row as the rest of the right band. The Airport Owners button label has been split: “Airport ” is now wrapped in a <span class="aa-owner-prefix">, and that span is hidden on mobile. The button reads “Owners ▾” on phones, “Airport Owners ▾” on desktop. The ~30-40 px saved is exactly enough to bring the ✕ close button onto the same row as Owners + 🎨 + 💰 + ⭐ + ✦ + sep + ● + ⚙, eliminating the standalone bottom row. Right band collapses from 2 rows to 1.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop sees no change — the Owner button still reads “Airport Owners”, the category circles use the original 0.35 / 1.0 opacity contrast, and the New Airports button (which only shows in the Tools dropdown on desktop) uses its existing active-class styling.
v3.19.17 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Map header on mobile — lone Tools row dropped, New Airports promoted to a direct right-band toggle. The 🛠 Tools dropdown was sitting alone on its own row at the very top of the map view, occupying ~50 px of vertical space for a single button. Inside it, Measurement and Select Regions are mouse-drag-only and hidden on mobile (v3.19.6), leaving Display New Airports as the only remaining menu item. A dropdown for one item is silly. v3.19.17 hides the entire left band on mobile and replaces the Tools dropdown with a direct ✦ toggle in the right band, alongside Payout / Rating / Scenery in the display-toggle cluster.
  • Implementation: new <button class="rg-btn rg-mobile-only" id="rgMobileNewAirportsBtn"> added to the right-band HTML between Rating and the Scenery separator. .rg-mobile-only is display: none by default; display: inline-flex inside @media (max-width: 768px). The existing _rgToggleNewAirports() function is extended to mirror the active class onto the mobile button too, so the toggle state is visually consistent across both. .rg-hdr-left { display: none !important } hides the whole left band (which holds the Tools / Live / Alerts buttons + phase text — all FSUIPC-driven and irrelevant on mobile).
  • Category circles radically simplified. Per user request, the Map label, the All / None accelerator buttons, and the Invert button are all hidden on mobile. None were essential; all were consuming horizontal space that's better spent on larger, finger-friendlier circles. With those four items gone, the 7 category circles have the row to themselves.
  • Circles enlarged ~33%. Up from v3.19.16's 1.95 rem (~31 px) to 2.6 rem (~41.6 px) — now within striking distance of Apple’s 44 px HIG ideal. Gap between circles widened from 0.18 rem to 0.4 rem so adjacent circles can’t be confused by a fingertip near the boundary. The font size inside each circle scales up to 1.15 rem to match.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) sees no change: the Tools dropdown still works as before with all three menu items visible, the category row still shows Map + All + None + circles + Invert, and the new mobile-only ✦ button stays hidden.
v3.19.16 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Map header layout — matched the user-proposed compact arrangement. The screenshot-based suggestion fit two more circles on the category row and three more items on the right-band row than v3.19.14 did. Implemented by tightening gaps (0.3 rem → 0.18 rem) and trimming circle size from 2.1 rem (~33.6 px) to 1.95 rem (~31 px). Small reduction in tap target — the circles are still inside the usable range, just slightly below the “ideal” Apple-HIG 44 px target — in exchange for the cleaner layout.
  • Category row 1: now fits Map + All + None + circles 1-6. Only circle 7 + Invert wrap to row 2 (previously 5/6/7 + Invert).
  • Right band row 1: now fits Airport Owners + 🎨 + 💰 + ⭐ + the separator + ● + ⚙ (7 items). The ✕ close button wraps to row 2 alone — which doubles as a deliberate “destructive action gets isolation” cue. Owner-button font down from 0.95 rem to 0.85 rem; icon-button font from 1.05 rem to 0.95 rem; padding tightened a few pixels.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) sees no change. All sizing remains at v3.19.15 defaults on desktop.
v3.19.15 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Release notes modal — smooth scroll on mobile via content-visibility. User feedback after v3.19.14: the help modal feels smooth now, but the release notes modal is “painful” to scroll. Diagnosis: every delivered version since v2.x has a release entry, ~150 rich-text blocks with <code> spans, lists, <em>/<strong> markup — all rendered into the DOM when the modal opens. That’s a huge amount of compositor work for a phone to keep track of while scrolling.
  • The fix: apply content-visibility: auto + contain-intrinsic-size: 0 400px to each .release block on mobile. content-visibility: auto is a modern CSS property that tells the browser “skip painting and layout for this element when it’s off screen; render it just in time as it scrolls into view.” The intrinsic-size hint gives the browser a placeholder height so the scrollbar stays in the right position and entries don’t cause layout jumps when they render or de-render.
  • Browser support: iOS Safari 18+ (Sept 2024), Android Chrome since 2020, all modern desktop browsers. On older Safari / older Chrome the property is gracefully ignored — the modal renders as before, no regression. Users on iOS 18+ get a dramatic scroll-perf improvement; users on iOS 17 and earlier see the same behaviour they had before.
  • Same treatment for #helpModal .help-feature blocks. The help modal feels OK already after v3.19.14’s paint-containment work, but the same hint costs nothing and helps on older / slower phones.
  • Also added contain: layout paint on .modal-body on mobile so scrolling inside the modal doesn’t trigger layout / paint work outside it. Pairs naturally with the content-visibility change.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) renders both modals exactly as before. No content-visibility, no contain — the desktop CSSOM is byte-identical to v3.19.14.
v3.19.14 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Map pan smoothness on mobile. Phone users reported chunky / not-smooth map scrolling. Three CSS-only optimisations applied at the mobile breakpoint, all of which target the rendering pipeline without touching Leaflet config or any JavaScript handler — so desktop behaviour is byte-identical.
  • Compositor promotion. #rgMapContainer gets will-change: transform + transform: translateZ(0) on mobile. The first hint asks the browser to promote the element to its own compositor layer up front, so the first touch-pan isn’t penalised by GPU layer allocation. The second forces it onto a hardware-accelerated layer immediately rather than as a lazy promotion.
  • Paint containment on the region modal. #regionModal { contain: layout paint } tells the browser that paints inside the modal can’t leak out and layout changes inside can’t reflow siblings — so when the map repaints during pan, the rest of the page doesn’t get considered. Big win on devices with constrained painting budgets.
  • Decorative pulse animation suppressed on mobile. The .aa-new-ring CSS animation runs continuously on every “new airport” marker; on a dense map that can mean dozens of overlapping per-frame compositor-triggered repaints across the dot layer during pan. .aa-new-ring { animation: none !important } on mobile removes that work. New airports still read as new — the dot’s colour, the ★ glyph, and the breadcrumb chip all remain — just without the pulsing attention-getter ring.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) sees no change: no will-change, no contain, and the pulse animation continues on the “new airport” markers as before.
v3.19.13 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Help modal — PC-only feature sections hidden on mobile. v3.19.5 hid the AFK Alerts button and the Live menu from the UI on mobile (those features all require a connected PC sim) but their help docs were still rendered in the help modal, which was confusing — a mobile user could read about features they couldn’t actually use. Seven feature sections now carry a help-feature--pc class and are hidden on mobile: Plot SimBrief Flight Plan on the Map, Live Aircraft Tracking (FSUIPC), Flight Demo Mode, Alerts, Mobile Alerts, Aircraft Track / Trail, and Aircraft Data Panel. A short note at the top of the help body explains why those sections are absent (“Some PC-only features … are hidden from this help on mobile because they require a connected flight simulator”) and is itself hidden on desktop. All other feature sections (Point-to-Point Distance, Aircraft Panel, Owner Matrix, Map, Scenery Library, Pilot Payout, etc.) stay visible on both platforms because they work on touch.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) sees the full help modal exactly as before, including all seven PC-only sections.
v3.19.12 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Finger-friendly tap targets on the map header. The category-filter circles were 1.55 rem (~25 px) since v3.19.3 to fit all 11 items on one row at 390 px — well below Apple’s 44-px HIG minimum and Material’s 48-px guidance. Mobile testing confirmed users couldn’t reliably tap the right circle. Bumped to 2.1 rem (~33.6 px) for a much closer approximation of a real finger-target; if Invert wraps to a second row that’s cleaner than missing taps. The right-band buttons (Owner / Payout / Rating / Scenery / Close) were similarly shrunk in v3.19.4 to fit one row; they now sit at ~36-40 px high with larger fonts.
  • Main screen — Map / Help / Reset buttons enlarged. Were inline-styled at 0.78 rem font with 0.25 rem 0.7 rem padding (~20 px tall). On a phone that’s well below a reliable tap target. Mobile now scopes them to 1 rem font with 0.5 rem 0.85 rem padding (~36-38 px tall) so each button reads as a proper tappable action. The selector is scoped to .dist-sticky-hdr .btn-action so other action buttons on other screens aren’t affected.
  • Help and release-notes modals — no more horizontal stutter. Long unbreakable tokens in modal content (URLs, code spans like simfly_aa_afk_alarms, contiguous ICAOs in feature descriptions) were pushing past the modal body’s width and creating a horizontal scroll — what manifested as the “stutter” while reading. Mobile now applies overflow-x: hidden to .modal-body and overflow-wrap: anywhere; word-break: break-word to its descendants. The text content reflows cleanly within the viewport with no horizontal scroll. (Vertical scroll is preserved for long content.)
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) is byte-identical to v3.19.11. All overrides are scoped to mobile.
v3.19.11 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Owner-row layout on mobile — same width, swap button centered between. Mobile screenshots showed the Departure Airport Owner dropdown rendering narrow (sharing its row with the ‎⇄ swap button) while the Arrival Airport Owner dropdown wrapped to its own row at full width — visually unbalanced. The row is now a column stack on mobile: each dropdown gets the full viewport width and the ⇄ button sits centered as its own discrete row between them. Bonus: the swap button gets a slightly larger tap target (padding: 0.4rem 1.1rem) since it's now a primary affordance rather than a squeezed-in chip. Desktop layout (≥ 769 px) unchanged — horizontal row with the swap button between them as before.
v3.19.10 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Dep / Arr category circles regression — rows no longer collapse to height 0. Mobile testing of v3.19.8/9 showed the category filter circles rendering as a thin multicoloured smear above the Hide filtered button row instead of as 7 proper circles per side. Root cause: a desktop rule (.dist-cat-dual-row .dist-cat-pills-row { flex: 1 1 0; max-width: 34rem } in the base stylesheet) was written for the row-direction layout (each pills-row gets equal horizontal share). When v3.19.8 flipped the parent .dist-cat-dual-row to flex-direction: column to give each row its own line, that flex: 1 1 0 suddenly applied to the vertical axis — flex-basis: 0 + flex-grow: 1 inside a parent with no explicit height collapsed both children to height 0, and the 1.55-rem circles overflowed as a thin colored band. v3.19.10 adds .dist-cat-dual-row .dist-cat-pills-row { flex: 0 0 auto !important; max-width: none !important } on mobile so each row takes its natural content height and can span the full mobile viewport. Desktop layout (≥ 769 px) is unaffected — the override is nested inside the existing @media (max-width: 768px) block.
v3.19.9 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Boot loader hardened against hung fetches — no more splash freeze. A user reported the app freezing on the splash screen progress bar on an iPad in Safari and never loading. Root cause analysis pointed at the boot loader’s Promise.all of 8 startup fetches (airport database, region names, runways, airport categories, aircraft database, field overrides, pilot payouts, payout data): a single fetch that hangs without resolving or rejecting — which happens on captive-portal wifi, dropped cellular handoffs, and a few flaky CDN-edge conditions — blocks the whole Promise.all forever, freezing the loader. The 15 s splash safety-net timeout would eventually hide the splash but the underlying loader was still hung in the background.
  • What changed:
    • New _fetchWithTimeout(url, ms, opts) helper that wraps fetch() with an AbortController-based timeout (default 8 s). Feature-detects AbortController; falls back gracefully on browsers that don’t expose it. On the fast path (normal connection) there is zero behavioural change — the timeout only fires if the network actually hangs.
    • All 8 boot fetches in loadAirportDB() rewritten to use _fetchWithTimeout and each one now has a .catch() so a hung or failed fetch yields the fallback value (empty string, {}, or null) instead of rejecting the whole Promise.all. Previously the first three (airport DB / regions / runways) threw on !r.ok with no catch — that’s the freeze path the iPad user hit.
    • The category-CSV retry loop’s fetch() call also goes through _fetchWithTimeout so a 120-second TCP stall × 3 attempts can’t hold the loader hostage.
    • Splash safety-net timer dropped from 15 s to 10 s. With per-fetch timeouts in place, worst-case legitimate loading is ~8 s + a few hundred ms of processing.
    • The splash progress label now suffixes “(timed out)” when a fetch fails or times out, so the user can see which piece is missing instead of having to guess. Each failure also emits a console.warn so any future bug reports can include diagnostic info.
  • Zero desktop impact. The new timeout logic is invisible on a healthy connection — fetches resolve well within the 8 s budget and the timer is cleared on resolve. The splash safety-net change (15 s → 10 s) only matters if something is genuinely wrong with the network, in which case the user previously waited 15 s for the splash to dismiss and now waits 10. All other code paths are untouched.
v3.19.8 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Dep / Arr category filter rows now sit on their own lines. v3.19.7 shrunk the pills to 1.55 rem so a single row easily fits on a 390 px viewport, but the parent .dist-cat-dual-row was still flex-direction: row with flex-wrap: wrap — and because each pill row had overflow-x: auto the parent reported the children as "fitting" and never wrapped them. The two rows tried to share the viewport horizontally and each got ~half, cutting off circles 5-7 on both Dep and Arr. Forcing the wrapper to flex-direction: column on mobile gives each row the full width and the seven category circles + label + Inv button all sit comfortably on one line.
  • Satellite / Street Map toggle no longer disappears against satellite imagery. The button's background was rgba(10, 18, 36, 0.88) — fine over the clean uniform-toned street map, but against satellite tiles (where airport dots, terrain colours, and country borders all bleed through) it read as transparent and was hard to find. Mobile now uses 97% background opacity, swaps the white-tinted border for a cyan-tinted one (rgba(0, 200, 255, 0.55)), and adds a dual-layer drop shadow + 1-px dark halo so the button stands proud over any tile background. The font / padding also gets bumped up so the tap target matches the other map-corner controls. Light mode gets an equivalent treatment.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) is byte-identical to v3.19.7.
v3.19.7 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Main screen polish — less “PC app in portrait”, more native. Mobile testing of v3.19.6 surfaced a cluster of small issues that together made the main distance-planner screen feel like a desktop layout pushed into a phone window. v3.19.7 addresses them in one pass.
  • Floating chrome moved out of the way. The Dark Mode toggle was pinned at top: 1rem; right: 1rem and landed directly on top of panel content on a phone. Mobile now drops it to the bottom-right corner at 0.55 rem font with 65% opacity so it’s reachable but unobtrusive. The LCL/UTC clock overlay (moved to bottom-left in v3.19.3) was overlapping the Departure / Arrival airport-info cards — phones have their own OS clock, so the LCL/UTC display is now hidden on mobile entirely.
  • Section labels rescaled. The “Departure Airport Owner” / “Arrival Airport Owner” labels were 1.658 rem (sized for 1080p+) and dominated the screen. The “Departure” / “Arrival” ICAO-field labels were 1.463 rem. Both come down to ~1.05 rem on mobile so the controls below them — the dropdowns and ICAO inputs — become the visual focus, not the headings.
  • Dep / Arr category-filter strips now one row each. The 2.1 rem circles wrapped 7+Inv into a second row per side, so the filter section was four visual rows for what is logically two. Mobile drops circles to 1.55 rem with tighter gaps and switches the row from flex-wrap: wrap to flex-wrap: nowrap; overflow-x: auto with the scrollbar hidden — if a particularly narrow phone still can’t fit all 9 items, the user swipes the row rather than seeing it split into uneven lines.
  • “Hide filtered airports” promoted to a full-width primary action. Was a small pill button floating in its own row; now spans full width at 0.95 rem font with comfortable 0.55 rem padding so it reads as the section’s primary commit action rather than a tertiary toggle.
  • Distance matrix tighter. Cell font down to 0.82 rem so more columns fit on screen before horizontal scroll kicks in; ICAO headers preserved at 0.95 rem so they stay legible. The matrix is still scrollable when more columns exist than the viewport can show.
  • Departure / Arrival info cards stack vertically. The two airport-info cards (showing dep / arr name, country, runway, etc.) sat side-by-side via flex-direction: row; on a phone each card was barely 180 px wide and unreadable. They now stack with the dividing border becoming a top border on the second card.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px) is byte-identical to v3.19.6.
v3.19.6 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Android’s text-selection menu no longer hijacks the Tools dropdown. When a phone user tapped “Display New Airports” or “Measurement”, Android Chrome interpreted the brief contact as a text-selection gesture and popped its system Copy | Translate | Select all context menu over the dropdown — the dropdown item never received the tap. Fixed by adding user-select: none, -webkit-touch-callout: none, -webkit-tap-highlight-color: transparent, and touch-action: manipulation to every interactive chrome surface (buttons, pulldowns, chips, the SimBrief pill, modal close ✕, choice-screen cards, the choice-skip link). Form inputs are deliberately excluded so text editing still works. The touch-action bonus: removes Android Chrome’s 300 ms double-tap-to-zoom delay, so taps register immediately.
  • ✕ close buttons enlarged to finger-friendly tap targets. The chip ✕ (.rg-bc-x) was 0.81 rem font with 0.1 rem padding — about a 14 px hit area, well below Apple’s 44 px and Material’s 48 px minimums and basically impossible to hit cleanly with a thumb. The SimBrief pill ✕ was 0.95 rem with no padding (~16 px). The modal close ✕ was 28×28. Mobile now sizes them to 30×28, 40×36, and 38×38 respectively — small enough to keep visual proportion, large enough to reliably tap.
  • Measurement & Select Regions removed from the Tools menu on mobile. Both tools implement their draw-by-drag behaviour through mousedown → mousemove → mouseup with explicit e.originalEvent.button checks — pure mouse paths, not Leaflet’s touch paths. On a phone they were guaranteed to fail. Hidden via #rgRulerBtn, #rgSelectMenuBtn { display: none } in the mobile media query so the Tools menu shows only Display New Airports (a simple toggle that works fine on tap) on phones. Both tools still render normally on desktop.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) is byte-identical to v3.19.5.
v3.19.5 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Mobile — simulator-tied features hidden. Mobile devices can’t run MSFS or X-Plane, can’t expose FSUIPC, and so cannot supply the live telemetry that the AFK alarm engine and most of the Live menu’s items all depend on. Rather than make those features “look right but do nothing” on a phone, they’re now dropped from the mobile UI entirely so the experience reads as a focused subset of the desktop app rather than a broken superset.
  • What’s hidden on mobile:
    • The 🔔 Alerts button (and its badge) at the top of the map. AFK alarms fire on altitude / heading / distance / phase-transition events reported by FSUIPC. Without a feed there’s nothing to alarm on, so the entire alarm-config panel is gone on mobile.
    • The 🔌 Live pulldown menu, which on desktop holds FSUIPC Live, STKP Live, 📍 Follow A/C, the Aircraft Icon picker, 📱 Mobile Alerts setup, the 🎬 Flight Demo Mode trigger, and ✈ SimBrief Flight Plan fetch & plot. The first six are PC-sim-tied or are configured for a PC sim. SimBrief Flight Plan fetch could technically work on mobile, but losing it is minor — users on a phone can still file a route on SimBrief via the main screen’s orange File on SimBrief → button (which opens SimBrief’s dispatch page pre-filled, no Live menu needed).
  • What stays visible on mobile: the 🛠 Tools menu keeps its Measurement tool, Select Regions drawing, and Display New Airports toggle — all three work via touch. The category filter, the Owner / Payout / Rating / Scenery / Close cluster, the map search, the breadcrumb-filter strip, and tap-to-show airport tooltips (added in v3.19.4) all stay.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) is byte-identical to v3.19.4 — the bell and the Live menu still render exactly as before on a laptop or monitor.
v3.19.4 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • No more horizontal page scroll on the map view. Mobile screenshots showed the entire page sideways-scrolling on the interactive map screen: panning right revealed cut-off chip text on the left edge (“wners Displayed”), panning left revealed cut-off labels on the right (“Inve”rt). Something in the layout was producing a width greater than the visible viewport — the most likely culprit is the .world-map-bg background canvas sized to 100vw on a viewport that the browser reports as slightly narrower. Belt-and-braces fix: html, body { overflow-x: hidden !important; max-width: 100vw } plus the same on #regionModal and .rg-modal. Containment rule only — no layout impact when nothing overflows, so desktop is unaffected.
  • SimBrief route pill no longer hides behind Android’s nav bar. v3.19.1 used bottom: max(14px, env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 14px)) on the floating pill, expecting the safe-area inset to lift it above the browser’s bottom URL bar. iOS Safari respects that. Android Chrome returns 0 for the inset even with its ~90 px nav bar visible, so the pill ended up behind it. Mobile now uses a hard 80px floor (bottom: max(80px, env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 80px))) which clears the Android nav bar and is still well clear on iOS.
  • Airport tooltips now actually appear on touch. The map’s tooltip logic in _rgBindDotTip uses a 250 ms mouseover delay to avoid flashing as the cursor sweeps across the dot field. On touch that’s a death-trap: tapping a dot fires mouseover → touchend → mouseout in rapid sequence, mouseout clears the delay timer before the 250 ms elapses, and the tooltip never opens. v3.19.4 adds a touch-only click handler that opens the tooltip immediately on tap and pins it — a tracked global _aaPinnedTipMarker holds the currently-open tip so it stays visible until the user either taps another airport (the new tip replaces the old) or taps empty map (a new map-level click handler closes it). Touch detection uses matchMedia('(hover: none)') with an ontouchstart fallback — cached after first call.
  • Map header right band — one more notch tighter. Owner button font down to 0.75 rem, icon buttons to 0.85 rem, horizontal padding shaved a few pixels and the inter-button gap dropped from 0.25 rem to 0.18 rem. The 7-item row (Airport Owners · 🎨 · Payout · Rating · Scenery dot · Scenery gear · Close ✕) now actually fits one line on a 390 px viewport.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px) for all CSS. The JS additions auto-detect touch and only fire on touch devices — desktop hover behavior is byte-identical to v3.19.3.
v3.19.3 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Main screen sticky header — restructured for mobile. Screenshots from a phone showed the front-door distance-planner header was a train-wreck: the LCL/UTC clock pinned top-left was overlapping the “SIMFLY ACTIVE AIRPORTS” title, the cyan “Departure” and orange “Arrival” labels were colliding with the Õ Map / ? Help / × Reset button row, and the LCPH / HEBL ICAO inputs were sitting behind the orange File on SimBrief button. The header is built as a 3-column inline-style CSS grid (ICAO cluster | weather | actions) which collapses cleanly on desktop but has nowhere to put three full clusters at 390 px. On mobile the outer grid is overridden to grid-template-columns: 1fr so the three sections stack vertically: ICAO inputs on row 1, the weather button centered on row 2, the Map/Help/Reset + SimBrief actions on row 3. The nested inputs grid keeps its Departure | swap | Arrival shape but flexes to viewport width instead of clinging to its 110-px columns.
  • Clock moved out of the way. The #simfly-clock overlay was anchored top: 1rem; left: 1rem and on a phone landed directly on top of the page title. Mobile now pins it to bottom: 0.4rem; left: 0.4rem with smaller font (9 px) so it sits as a footer overlay rather than a headline-eater.
  • Map data panel hidden on mobile. The LAT / LON / HDG / ALT MSL / GS / IAS strip at the bottom of the interactive map relies on FSUIPC, which needs a PC sim — phones aren’t feeding it. The panel was therefore eating ~150 px of vertical map area to display nothing but em-dashes. #afkDataPanel { display: none } on mobile reclaims that real estate for the actual map. (If a mobile use case for the panel emerges later we’ll add a toggle.)
  • Map header tightened — rows fit on one line. v3.19.2’s 1.7 rem category circles still wrapped the seventh circle and Invert to a second row; bumping circles down to 1.55 rem (with 0.78 rem font + tighter padding) lets all 11 items (Map label + All + None + circles 1–7 + Invert) sit one row. The right band’s buttons (Owner / 🎨 / Payout / Rating / Scenery / Close) likewise shrink so the close ✕ no longer wraps to its own row.
  • Filter chips shrunk. The .rg-bc pills shown on the map (Category Colors, No Scenery Indicator, active filter breadcrumbs) were sized for 1080p (0.96 rem font, 0.69 rem horizontal padding). On mobile they were chunky enough that two adjacent chips would push the row over the viewport edge. Mobile cuts them to 0.72 rem font / 0.5 rem h-pad so several chips can sit one row.
  • Same gate: @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop (≥ 769 px viewport) is byte-identical to v3.19.2. All overrides target inline styles via structural selectors + !important so they only fire when the media query matches.
v3.19.2 June 6, 2026 Improved
  • Map header — three deliberate bands instead of one pile. v3.19.1 stacked the map header into three flex-wrapped rows but they all looked identical, reading as one cluttered cluster rather than three logical groups. On mobile the header is now a column stack with each row visually banded: the top row (Tools / Live / Alerts & phase status) and the bottom row (Owner / Payout / Rating / Scenery / Close) get a slight darker tint; the middle category-filter row gets a subtle cyan wash so the seven colored circles read as the "Map filter" group. A 1-px hairline between rows reinforces the grouping. Phase-status text now collapses (display:none via :empty) when there's no active phase, so the row sits tight against the Tools/Live buttons when idle.
  • Map search box — sized for a phone. The search overlay was a fixed 240 px wide box pinned to the top-left of the map — about 61 % of a 390 px phone viewport. On mobile it now sizes to min(180px, 50vw), freeing up real estate on the right for the breadcrumb filter strip.
  • Breadcrumb filter strip — drops to its own row on mobile. The active-filter breadcrumb pills (Owner / Region / Category etc.) used to pin at left: 256px next to the desktop search box. On mobile they would have either overlapped the shrunken search or pushed off-screen, so they now wrap to a second row at top: 46px, left-aligned with the search, and the strip allows wrapping so multiple active filters stack cleanly instead of clipping.
  • Same gate as before: @media (max-width: 768px) — desktop layout (≥ 769 px viewport) is byte-identical to v3.19.1. These polish rules don't exist in the CSSOM on a desktop browser.
  • Phase 2 still to come. Tap-to-pin tooltips on the map (so they stop dismissing on the next tap), tap-to-open owner legend (currently hover-only), and full touch-friendly versions of the AFK threshold scrub inputs are queued but not in this patch — those require JS, not just CSS.
v3.19.1 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Mobile Phase 1 follow-up — the screens users actually touch. v3.19.0 capped modal widths and outer page padding, but the two surfaces a mobile user sees first — the “What would you like to do” choice screen and the interactive map view — were still unusable on a phone. v3.19.1 fixes five concrete failures.
  • Choice screen — fits one phone height. The two big cards previously enforced a 220 px min-height floor plus generous padding, so the screen ran taller than the visible viewport and users had to scroll just to see the Explore map card. On mobile the floor is removed, card padding is halved, the icon shrinks 64 px → 38 px, and the title block’s margins tighten so the entire screen (eyebrow, version, title, sub, both cards, Skip link) fits a single phone screen.
  • Map header — no more overlapping buttons. The map’s top bar was a CSS grid (1fr auto 1fr) that crushed Tools / Live / Alerts on the left, the seven category circles in the middle, and Owner / Payout / Rating / Scenery / Close on the right into a single row — impossible at 390 px. On mobile the header switches to flex-wrap: each section gets its own row, the category circles shrink from 2.0 rem to 1.7 rem so all eleven items fit one line, and the phase-status text wraps to its own row centered below.
  • Airport tooltips — readable. The Leaflet tooltips were white-space: nowrap with 1.58 rem ICAO and 1.35 rem name/owner lines, so on a phone the box ballooned past the viewport. Mobile now allows wrap, caps width at 80 vw, and scales the line fonts down (ICAO 1.1 rem, body lines 0.85–0.92 rem). Note: tooltips still dismiss when you tap elsewhere — that’s Leaflet’s default touch behavior. A long-press-to-pin handler is planned for Phase 2.
  • SimBrief route pill — no longer chopped off. The floating pill at the bottom of the map was anchored at bottom: 14px — which on iOS Safari sits behind the bottom URL bar. Mobile now uses bottom: max(14px, env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 14px)) so it lifts above the safe-area inset; white-space changes from nowrap to normal so the route/meta/button cluster wraps gracefully instead of pushing off the right edge.
  • All rules gated by @media (max-width: 768px). Desktop layout (≥ 769 px viewport) is byte-identical to v3.19.0 — these rules don’t exist in the CSSOM on a desktop browser.
v3.19.0 June 6, 2026 Feature
  • Mobile responsive foundation — Phase 1. The app now renders cleanly on phone-sized viewports (iPhone 17, Galaxy S25-class and smaller) without forcing the user to pan horizontally just to read the page. A new pair of CSS breakpoints (@media (max-width: 768px) and @media (max-width: 480px)) tightens the page’s outer padding, shrinks the modal overlay gutter, caps every dialog (Help, Release Notes, Scenery Library, Developers, SimBrief Flight Plan Settings, Live Aircraft Icon, Demo Mode, New Pilot Codes, Manage Values, Mass Assign, AFK Config, AFK History, AFK Waypoint Alarm, AFK Geofence and its anchor picker) to the visible viewport width, and trims the global airport-info tooltip (which previously had a 450 px minimum width) so it fits a 390 px screen. All new rules are gated by the mobile breakpoints — a desktop viewport (≥ 769 px) sees zero CSS change and renders exactly as v3.18.2 did. This is the foundation pass: layout chrome no longer overflows on mobile. Subsequent phases will tackle touch-only interactions (long-press tooltips on the map, tap-to-open owner legend), tighter toolbar collapse in Region Mode, and per-modal interior layouts for the heavy power-user dialogs.
v3.18.2 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Descent Started — false alarms during turbulence suppressed. Extended in-cruise turbulence could previously trigger a false Descent Started alarm: a 15-second sustained altitude trend at < -200 fpm was enough to flip phase to DESCENT, and turbulence with autopilot ALT-hold engaged sometimes produced exactly that. The cruise-exit logic has been hardened on two axes: the trend window grows from 15 s to 30 s, and the cruise-exit threshold rises from ±200 fpm to ±400 fpm. Both changes apply symmetrically (CRUISE → CLIMB and direct CLIMB ↔ DESCENT crossovers use the same 30 s window and ±400 fpm threshold). The cruise-entry path is untouched — CLIMB → CRUISE still uses the 5 s fast window at ±200 fpm, so Cruise Reached fires within ~5 s of level-off exactly as before. Net effect on TOD detection: Descent Started fires roughly 15 s later at TOD than the v3.16.0–v3.18.1 behaviour (~30 s after sustained descent begins instead of ~15 s) — an extra ~375 ft of altitude loss at a 1500 fpm descent rate, still well before any operational change in the cockpit. Practical effect: turbulence essentially cannot fake a 30 s sustained 400 fpm trend with the autopilot engaged, because the autopilot correction window is much faster than 30 s.
v3.18.1 June 6, 2026 Fix
  • Telemetry panel buttons — larger. The three data panel control buttons (📍 Follow A/C, ⚙ options, ⏷ collapse) are now 36×36 px (up from 24×24 px) with proportionally scaled icons for easier tapping.
  • Scenery gear — larger. The ⚙ icon on the Scenery split button is now 1.17 rem (increased in two steps from 0.75 rem).
  • Map category filter — "Map" label nudged up. The Map label in the category filter row is shifted up 2 px to better align with the adjacent buttons.
  • Route tooltip — increased spacing. Gap between dep, arrow, arr, and nm elements increased; left/right padding widened. Arrow and nm separator no longer carry redundant inline spaces — spacing is now CSS-controlled throughout.
  • SimBrief chip — label and tooltip polish. Flight number label updated to Flt No:. The chip's "File on SimBrief" button now shows the same live tooltip as the main button (origin, destination, aircraft, flight number).
  • NEXT ALERT field — no more layout shifts. As different alarm labels cycle through the NEXT ALERT field on the data panel, the field now watermarks its minimum width to the widest label seen — adjacent buttons no longer shift. Width resets cleanly when the alarm list changes.
  • FSUIPC menu row — status dot mirrored. The green / yellow status dot that appears on the Live pulldown trigger is now also shown on the FSUIPC Tracking… menu row itself, driven by the same telemetry state.
v3.18.0 June 6, 2026 Feature
  • Timer alert — Repeat option. Countdown timer alarms now have a Repeat toggle. When enabled, the alarm fires its sound and alert as usual, then automatically dismisses and reschedules for the next interval — no manual reset required. Useful for periodic chimes (e.g. "remind me every 20 minutes").
  • Category filter — improved accessibility. The Map category filter (All / None / 1–7 circles / Invert) in the map header now uses the same font size as the other toolbar buttons, with larger numbered circles and larger All / None / Invert buttons.
  • Scenery button — gear icon & numeric dirty badge. The right-side settings icon on the Scenery split button has been changed from a pencil ✏ to a gear ⚙. When the library has unsaved changes, the old yellow pulsing glow is replaced by a numeric badge showing the exact count of unsaved changes. The tooltip on both halves of the button updates to show how many unsaved changes are pending.
  • Route tooltip — unified font, close button, swap on click. The on-map route label (dep → arr · nm) now uses Rajdhani throughout, matching the measurement tooltip. A close ✕ button in the label clears the route in one click without opening the matrix. Clicking the main label body swaps departure and arrival (same as clicking the SimBrief pill).
  • SimBrief "File on SimBrief" panel — dep/arr colours. The departure and arrival ICAOs in the floating route pill now use the standard cyan (departure) and orange (arrival) colour scheme used throughout the rest of the app.
  • Live button — dedicated FSUIPC telemetry indicator. The dot indicator on the Live button now exclusively reflects FSUIPC telemetry state: green = FSUIPC enabled and actively receiving data; yellow = FSUIPC enabled but not currently receiving (connecting or signal lost); no dot = FSUIPC not enabled. The dot no longer lights for a loaded SimBrief flight plan.
  • Mobile Alerts badge — right-aligned and vertically centred. The numeric badge on the Live → Mobile Alerts… menu row is now pushed to the far right of the row and centred vertically on the chip.
v3.17.0 June 5, 2026 Feature
  • Alert History — new "Save Log" button. A ⤓ Save Log button now appears in the Alert History modal footer (between the info line and the existing Clear History button). Clicking it downloads the full history log as a JSON file (simfly-alert-history-YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM-SS.json) regardless of which type or dismissal-reason filters are currently active. Each entry in the file includes the trip timestamp, alarm type, label, configured parameters, dismissal info, and the full aircraft telemetry snapshot captured when the alarm fired. Useful for post-flight review and for sharing log data when troubleshooting alert behaviour. The transient plotted flag (whether a map pin is currently active for that entry) is omitted from the export since map pins are not restored on re-import.
v3.16.1 June 5, 2026 Fix
  • Bug fix — Altitude alarm no longer fires on sim load when on the ground. The altitude-crossing alarm persists its prevAlt value to localStorage whenever any alarm triggers a save mid-flight. If the browser was closed between flights, that stale high-altitude prevAlt could survive to the next session; when the aircraft loaded at a lower airport elevation the first telemetry tick would see a crossing and fire the alarm instantly. On page load, prevAlt is now always cleared for Altitude and Cruise Reached alarms so the first tick re-seeds it from the actual position.
  • Bug fix — Cruise Reached alarm no longer fires immediately on takeoff. The v3.16.0 asymmetric phase detector uses a 5-second fast window for the climb→cruise transition. Immediately after liftoff that window still contains flat ground-altitude samples (vertical speed ≈ 0 fpm), causing a false climb→cruise transition in the first tick airborne and triggering the Cruise Reached alarm on the runway. The phase detector now requires the aircraft to have been in climb phase for at least 6 seconds before the fast window can authorise a cruise transition — ensuring the window is fully populated with post-liftoff altitude data before the check is applied.
v3.16.0 June 5, 2026 Feature
  • Cruise Reached — re-arm no longer fires instantly. Previously, re-enabling a Cruise Reached alarm (or adding a new one) while the aircraft was still in cruise phase would cause it to fire immediately on the next telemetry tick. The alarm now detects when it is armed mid-cruise and waits silently for the aircraft to leave cruise and re-enter before firing — so it always fires on a genuine new cruise-entry rather than the current one. Applies to both Adaptive and At specific altitude modes.
  • Cruise Reached — new "Only above" minimum altitude filter. An optional altitude field now appears on every Cruise Reached alarm card. When set, the alarm skips any cruise transition detected at or below that altitude, allowing it to ignore ATC-assigned climb-and-maintain altitudes (e.g. "climb and maintain FL240") and only fire when true cruise is reached at the expected higher altitude. Accepts feet (e.g. 18000) or flight levels (e.g. FL180). Supports mouse-wheel and click-and-drag scrubbing like other altitude inputs. Leave blank to fire at any altitude.
  • Descent Started — re-arm no longer fires instantly. Same pattern as the Cruise Reached fix above. Re-enabling a Descent Started alarm (or adding a new one) while the aircraft was already in descent phase used to fire it on the next telemetry tick. The alarm now detects that it was armed mid-descent and waits silently for the aircraft to leave descent before re-arming, so it always fires on a genuine new TOD rather than the current one.
  • Phase detection — asymmetric windowed VS, ±200 fpm threshold throughout. The phase detector has been rewritten around two rolling-window VS readings: a fast 5-second window and a trend 15-second window. The direction of transition picks which window is consulted: leaving CRUISE for climb or descent requires |trendVS| > 200 fpm over the trend window (sustained altitude trend — brief autopilot wobble that net-averages near zero cannot flip phase); entering CRUISE from climb or descent uses the fast window (|fastVS| ≤ 200) so the Cruise Reached alarm fires within ~5 s of level-off. Direct climb ↔ descent crossovers (rare) also use the trend window. Replaces the previous per-transition scheme (35 s for climb → cruise, 20 s for cruise → descent, 45 s for the rare climb → descent backwards-jump, plus a variable adaptive deadband). Trend window warm-up: until 14 s of altitude history have accumulated (page reload, MSFS crash recovery, post-pause), trendVSfpm falls back to the fast 5 s reading so transitions can still be evaluated promptly. The pre-existing v2.87.1 peak-climb-VS evidence gate was removed in this same pass — it had become redundant with the current GS-based ground rule and was actively trapping users who reloaded mid-flight (page reload zeroed peakClimbVS, and an aircraft already at cruise altitude could never grow it back, so phase stayed stuck on CLIMB forever). Side benefit: descent → cruise, descent → climb, and approach → climb (go-around) transitions all work now.
  • Map/matrix drag divider — hidden when the matrix is not displayed, cursor-capped when it is. The horizontal drag handle between the map and the route matrix is now hidden entirely whenever the route matrix is not currently displayed (full-screen map). The handle reappears automatically when a route is selected and the matrix becomes visible. While dragging, the cursor is capped at 40 px above the viewport bottom so the divider can never escape past the bottom edge of the browser window — moving the cursor further down simply holds the divider at the cap line, where it stays grabbable.
v3.15.4 June 5, 2026 Improved
  • New Airports preview — click to cycle background. The live preview box in the display options modal now supports three background modes — black, white, and 18% gray — cycling on each click. Three small swatches in the Preview row header show which background is active (highlighted cyan) and can be clicked directly to jump to any of the three. The background state persists for the session but is not saved to local storage.
v3.15.3 June 5, 2026 Improved
  • Version number on the startup choice screen. The app version now appears centered immediately below the "SIMFLY ACTIVE AIRPORTS" eyebrow on the "What would you like to do?" screen, matching the same muted Rajdhani treatment used on the splash screen. The eyebrow bottom margin is tightened so the version reads as a sub-label of the heading rather than a separate line.
v3.15.2 June 5, 2026 Improved
  • Version number shown on the loading splash screen. The app version now appears below the subtitle on the boot splash, in muted Rajdhani so it's visible but unobtrusive. Populated via the same DOMContentLoaded handler that already sets the header version label — the splash is always still visible at that point so it's guaranteed to show before the user can see it.
v3.15.1 June 5, 2026 Improved
  • New Airports options — pulse duration slider replaces Slow/Normal/Fast buttons. The three-button rate picker is replaced with a continuous range slider covering 1 s (extremely fast) through 30 s (extremely slow), with Fast/Slow end labels and a live numeric readout. The slider thumb adopts the chosen ring color via CSS custom property.
  • New Airports options — days field text centered. The "New Within" number input now uses centered text so the value sits cleanly in the field rather than being crowded against the spinner arrows.
  • New Airports options — live preview. A Preview panel at the bottom of the options modal shows a dot with two pulsing rings updating in real time as color and duration change — no need to Apply first to see the effect. The animation restarts cleanly on each change using a forced-reflow technique so the new duration takes effect immediately.
v3.15.0 June 5, 2026 Feature
  • New Airports — right-click display options. Right-clicking ✦ Display New Airports in the Tools menu opens an options panel with three settings: Color — choose from 8 presets (magenta, cyan, orange, green, red, yellow, purple, white) or a custom color picker; Pulse Rate — Slow (10 s), Normal (6 s), or Fast (3 s) cycle; New Within — how many days an airport counts as new (1–365, default 14), with drag up/down to scrub like the Alerts panel fields. A Reset Defaults button restores all three to the original configuration. All settings persist in browser local storage and apply immediately to the map. Left-click still toggles visibility as before.
  • FSUIPC — tracking toast no longer sticks when the browser isn't focused. The "Tracking via FSUIPC" reconnect toast is now suppressed when a background retry succeeds while the browser window doesn't have OS focus, preventing the 4 s auto-dismiss window from expiring before the user returns to the tab. Additionally, the initial "can't reach" failure toast is no longer re-fired after a successful connection has already been established, cutting off the toast refresh cycle that could keep it stuck on screen across repeated connect/disconnect cycles.
v3.14.1 June 4, 2026 Improved
  • 📍 Follow A/C pushpin added to the data panel. A new 📍 button sits on the bottom data panel immediately to the left of the ⚙ options menu, giving a third entry point for toggling Follow A/C without having to open the 🔌 Live pulldown. The button only appears while a live source is active (matching the Live-menu Follow button) and hides when the data panel is collapsed (matching the ⚙ menu). It lights cyan when Follow is on so the state is unmistakable at a glance, and the existing state machinery in _liveAcUpdateFollowBtn keeps all three buttons (Live menu, demo bar, data panel) in sync without any extra plumbing.
v3.14.0 June 4, 2026 Improved
  • Follow A/C now also cancels on right-mouse drag and on the Shift+drag zoom rectangle. Previously only Leaflet's left-drag pan cancelled Follow mode, which felt inconsistent with the other two map-navigation gestures available everywhere in the region map. Right-drag fires the cancel the moment the drag exceeds the 3-pixel threshold (a pure right-click — used to open the Quick-Rate popover or cancel a region draw — does not trip it). The Shift+drag zoom rectangle cancels on boxzoomstart, the moment the user begins the box. Both mirror the existing left-drag behaviour: panning or zooming to look around never fights the auto-centre.
  • Scenery Library — Save / Load renamed and grouped at the top of the ⋯ menu. "Export JSON" is now 💾 Save Scenery Library, "Import JSON" is now 📂 Load Scenery Library, and the two are adjacent at the top of the menu so the back-up workflow reads naturally. The remaining import-from-foreign-format options (📥 FS Addon Compare, Import scenery config) sit below them, with Clear all unchanged at the bottom. The 💾 icon matches the Save button in the bottom unsaved-changes banner.
  • Scenery Library — airports you touched this session float to the top of the list. When you open the Scenery Library modal with unsaved changes (the ⬤ Scenery button is glowing yellow), every airport you added, rated, or had its store / developer edited this session appears at the top of the list under the current sort. Common workflow: right-click a handful of map dots to set their star ratings, then open the modal to fill in Store / Developer info — those rows are now waiting at the top instead of being scattered alphabetically. A new _slDirtyIcaos set is populated at every mutation site and cleared on Save, Load, or Clear all so the float-to-top behaviour only applies to genuinely-unsaved edits.
v3.13.1 June 4, 2026 Fix
  • FSUIPC connection toast no longer blocks the data-panel ⚙ and ⏷ buttons. Two related bugs fixed together. (1) The toast at the bottom-right of the map paints directly over the data panel's settings (⚙) and collapse (⏷) buttons. After the toast was dismissed (or faded out) it left behind an invisible click-eater because pointer-events was never reset from auto back to none; the buttons stayed unresponsive even when the toast looked gone. The dismiss path now resets pointer-events alongside opacity. (2) The v3.12.0 auto-dismiss safety net used visibilitychange, which fires only when the browser TAB visibility changes — switching tabs in the browser. It does NOT fire when the browser WINDOW loses or regains OS focus while the AA tab stays the active tab. That's the common case while you're alt-tabbing to MSFS or FSUIPC during sim setup, so the auto-resume reconnect toast could sit there forever waiting for a tab-visibility event that never came. Added a parallel window.focus listener that runs the same past-deadline check, so a stuck toast clears the moment AA regains OS focus.
v3.13.0 June 4, 2026 Improved
  • Region map fills the entire viewport. The map modal now stretches edge-to-edge instead of leaving a 4% backdrop margin around it, reclaiming roughly 8% of horizontal and vertical pixels for the map itself. The rounded corners, glow shadow, and backdrop tint are removed since they have no surface to land on at full size. All header toolbars, the matrix results bar, the breadcrumb pill, and the floating legends (payout, scenery rating, owner panel) keep their existing positions and behaviours. ✕ Close and ESC still return to the main screen exactly as before.
v3.12.3 June 4, 2026 Fix
  • Region map matrix swaps dep/arr rows and columns when route is reversed. Clicking the route text on the breadcrumb pill or the floating SimBrief pill now swaps the entire region setup — the dep (blue) and arr (orange) circles exchange roles, their airport arrays swap, the matrix rebuilds with the new dep airports as rows and the new arr airports as columns, and the selected cell highlights correctly in the rebuilt matrix. Map dot colours and the circle handle markers update to match. Previously the breadcrumb and pill text swapped but the matrix structure was unchanged.
v3.12.2 June 4, 2026 Fix
  • Route matrix swaps dep/arr owners when ICAOs are swapped on the main screen. Clicking ⇄ between the ICAO fields (or the route text on the SimBrief pill when no dot-selected route is active) now also swaps the owner dropdowns and matrix row/column structure — so the matrix immediately shows the new departure owner as rows and the new arrival owner as columns, with highlights crossed to the correct cell. Previously the ICAO inputs swapped but the matrix retained the old owner layout.
v3.12.1 June 4, 2026 Improved
  • WCAG 2.2 accessibility pass — low-risk fixes. A targeted round of accessibility improvements with no functional or visual changes for sighted users: skip-to-content link added for keyboard navigation; app body wrapped in a semantic <main> landmark; aria-label added to the ⛅ weather, ⇄ ICAO swap, and ⇄ owner swap buttons (previously labelled only via title, which screen readers do not reliably expose); :focus-visible styles added to category filter, Invert, Hide filtered, and aircraft-filter buttons; dark-mode muted text lightened from #6a8ab8 to #8facd4 for improved contrast; dimmed matrix cells in light mode bumped from 18% to 38% opacity so filtered-out values remain legible; autocomplete="off" removed from ICAO inputs (the attribute was redundant alongside spellcheck="false" and unreliably honoured).
v3.12.0 June 3, 2026 Improved
  • Closing the Payout / Rating panels keeps the filter on. Clicking the ✕ on the Pilot Payout or Scenery Rating legend now just hides that panel — the underlying color mode / filter stays applied to the map and matrix. Use the matching breadcrumb chip to actually turn it off. Clicking the 💰 Payout or ⭐ Rating button again re-opens the panel without flipping the mode. Removes a common stumble where peeking at the panel and then closing it would unexpectedly clear the filter.
  • FSUIPC toast no longer sticks when tab regains focus. If the page is backgrounded (e.g. switching to MSFS) while the green 🔌 Tracking via FSUIPC… toast is on screen, the browser pauses CSS transitions and throttles timers, so the toast could remain visible after returning. Added a visibility-aware dismissal: as soon as the page becomes visible again, any toast past its 4 s lifetime is force-removed. Clicking a toast also dismisses it immediately.
  • Tools menu — Measurement moved above Select Regions. The 🛠 Tools pulldown now lists 📐 Measurement first, then ⊙ Select Regions, then ✦ Display New Airports. Matches the order most use the tools in.
  • KFLL flagged as a new airport. KFLL (Fort Lauderdale-Hollywood Intl) marked with first_seen: 2026-06-01 in the public payout-data feed, so the magenta new-airport pulse and tooltip appear on the region map.
v3.11.0 June 3, 2026 Improved
  • SimBrief pill — click route to swap dep/arr. Clicking the DEP → ARR text on the floating SimBrief pill now swaps departure and arrival in one click. When a dot-selected route is active the map breadcrumb and highlight update too; otherwise the main-screen fields swap. The route text shows a pointer cursor and dims on hover as a visual affordance.
  • SimBrief Flight Plan settings — right-click instead of Shift-click. Right-clicking the ✈ SimBrief Flight Plan item in the Live menu now opens Flight Plan settings (Pilot ID, waypoint labels, route color). All tooltips, prompts, and help text updated accordingly.
  • SimBrief pill — improved readability. The flight number label now reads Flt: XXXXXX, the meta line font is larger (1.14 rem), the text is full white, and the File on SimBrief button uses the same orange accent color as the main-screen button. Tooltips added to all pill elements.
  • New-airport indicator — Tools menu toggle. A ✦ Display New Airports item in the Tools menu lets you show or hide the new-airport pulse rings. The item only appears when there are airports with a first_seen within the last 30 days. Active state is highlighted magenta.
  • New-airport indicator — magenta color. The pulse ring, star badge, and tooltip banner are now magenta (#d400aa), with a white ✦ New Airport label on the tooltip. Ring cycle slowed to 6 s for a subtler, less distracting animation.
  • Security — XSS fix in map dot tooltips. Airport names, owner names, and ICAO codes are now HTML-escaped before being interpolated into the region-map tooltip HTML string, closing a potential XSS path via the upstream OurAirports data source.
v3.10.0 June 3, 2026 Feature
  • New-airport pulse rings on the map. Airports added to SimFly within the last 30 days now display an animated amber/gold pulsing ring on the map so they are easy to spot at a glance. The ring is a double radar-sweep effect that radiates outward from the airport dot and fades; it disappears automatically once the airport is more than 30 days old. Date-added data comes from payout-data.json, which is stamped with a first_seen date by the hourly GitHub Action the first time each airport appears.
v3.9.3 June 3, 2026 Fix
  • New-pilot confirm modal no longer hidden by the map. Clicking File on SimBrief from the route pill while the route involved pilots without registered codes opened the “New Pilot Codes” confirmation modal underneath the region map overlay. The map (#regionModal) sits at z-index:9000 and the generic .modal-overlay class only reaches z-index:1000, so the confirm was painted but invisible. Added the same z-index:9500 override #fpSettingsModal and #demoModal already use to #npcModal.
v3.9.2 June 3, 2026 Fix
  • Route pill “File on SimBrief” button now works. Clicking the button inside the map had no effect because Leaflet’s drag handler was intercepting the mousedown before the button’s click event could form a trusted user gesture (same root cause as the demo bar slider fix in v2.97.7). Applied L.DomEvent.disableClickPropagation + disableScrollPropagation to the pill element the first time a route is set, identical to the protection on #demoFloating.
v3.9.1 June 3, 2026 Fix
  • Scroll-wheel zoom step size restored. v3.9.0 set zoomSnap:0 globally so the dual-mouse zoom rAF loop could use fractional zoom levels — but Leaflet’s internal sigmoid formula halves the per-notch step size when zoomSnap is 0, making scroll-wheel zoom require ~2× as many notches to cover the same distance. Fixed by reverting zoomSnap to its default (1) at map init; the dual-mouse zoom gesture now temporarily sets it to 0 on begin and restores it to 1 on release, so fractional zoom is available only during the gesture.
v3.9.0 June 3, 2026 Feature
  • Dual-mouse zoom. Hold both left and right mouse buttons simultaneously and drag vertically to zoom: up zooms in, down zooms out. Speed is proportional to how far you drag from the grab point (joystick style — return to the start position to stop). The zoom is centred on the cursor location where both buttons were first pressed. Cursor changes to a vertical-resize arrow while active. Scroll-wheel zoom is completely untouched.
v3.8.3 June 3, 2026 Fix
  • Reverted smooth wheel zoom (v3.8.2). The custom scroll handler reduced sensitivity and introduced per-frame lag that made zooming feel slower and choppier than Leaflet’s default. zoomSnap:0 and the custom wheel listener removed; Leaflet’s built-in scroll wheel zoom restored.
v3.8.2 June 3, 2026 Improved
  • Smooth wheel zoom. Reverted in v3.8.3 — sensitivity and frame-rate felt worse than the Leaflet default.
v3.8.1 June 3, 2026 Fix
  • Route pill re-appears after navigating away and back. Dismissing the SimBrief pill for a given route now only suppresses it until the route changes — even changing back to the same dep/arr pair re-shows the pill. Previously the dismiss was sticky for the lifetime of the session regardless of subsequent route changes.
v3.8.0 June 3, 2026 Feature
  • SimBrief route pill on the map. Whenever both a departure and arrival are set, a pill slides up from the bottom centre of the interactive map showing the route (DEP → ARR), selected aircraft, and synthesized flight number — the same information shown when hovering the File on SimBrief → button in the main planner. The pill has its own ✈ File on SimBrief → button so you can dispatch directly from the map without switching back to the planner. Dismiss with ✕; the pill stays hidden for that specific route and re-appears automatically if you change either endpoint.
v3.7.5 June 3, 2026 Improved
  • Telemetry panel starts collapsed. The data panel now defaults to minimised on every page load and auto-expands only when live data is flowing (FSUIPC connected or demo running). It auto-collapses again when the source disconnects or demo stops. The manual toggle still works as before.
  • Zero vertical speed no longer shows a sign prefix. When VS rounds to 0 fpm the level-flight dash was displayed as a prefix (– 0 fpm). The dash is now suppressed at exactly zero so it reads 0 fpm.
  • Payout palette bar live-refreshes while the colour picker is open. Changing a stop’s colour via the native picker now updates the tick handle swatch in-place on every input event, and triggers a full re-render when the picker closes (“change” event). Previously the bar only updated after separately clicking a tick handle.
v3.7.4 June 1, 2026 Fix
  • Clear-All circle glyph properly centered. v3.7.3 nudged the ⊗ glyph 1px left and 2px down to try to optically correct font metrics — that overshot and pushed it off-center the other way. Removed the offset; the glyph is now centered geometrically inside the circle by simple flex + line-height:1.
v3.7.2 June 1, 2026 Improved
  • Map breadcrumb “Clear All” button is now a round circle that matches the height of the other filter pills. The ⊗ chip used to render as a wider rounded capsule that was visibly taller and a different shape from the sibling pills (Color Mode, Scenery Indicator, owner / category filters), making the breadcrumb strip look uneven. It’s now a perfect circle the same height as those pills, so the strip reads as a tidy row of equal-height controls with the circle clearly distinct as the “remove everything” action.
v3.7.1 June 1, 2026 Fix
  • Main app no longer flashes briefly before the startup choice appears. In v3.7.0 the choice overlay was revealed about 600 ms after the splash started fading, so during the splash’s ~550 ms fade-out the matrix showed through underneath. The choice now begins its fade-in at the moment the splash starts dismissing, so the choice is already fully painted (under the still-opaque splash) by the time the splash reaches transparency — the user sees the splash dissolve directly into the choice screen.
v3.7.0 June 1, 2026 Feature
  • Startup choice screen — pick owner mode or map mode before diving in. Once the loading splash fades, you now see a friendly dual-choice screen asking what you’d like to do: Show airports by Owner (jump to the classic matrix when you already know the dep/arr owners you want) or Explore map (open the interactive map to browse by location, payout, region, and to track flights or set up in-flight alerts). The choice is bypassed automatically when Recip Tracker or RM Lite hands you off with a specific intent — if a dep/arr/depOwner/arrOwner param is present you go straight to the matrix, and a pilots= param goes straight to the map — so the launch shortcuts from those apps still work without an extra click. A Skip link and the Esc key both dismiss the choice and leave you on the matrix.
v3.6.21 May 30, 2026 Improved
  • Selected route now shows as a breadcrumb pill instead of crowding the header. When you pick a departure and arrival by clicking map dots, the route used to display as large ICAO text in the header and add a separate ✕ Clear route button — on smaller screens that text collided with the category filter in the middle of the header. The route now appears as a compact ✈ DEP → ARR pill alongside the Flight Plan and Aircraft Track pills: click its ✕ to clear the route, or click the pill to swap departure and arrival. The old header button is gone.
v3.6.20 May 30, 2026 Fix
  • Alerts panel buttons no longer spill outside the panel on shorter screens. The row of footer buttons (Test Sound, Alert History, Save / Load, Enable / Disable / Delete All) wraps to two rows on narrower windows, and on shorter screens that second row was overflowing off the bottom of the Alerts panel. The panel now lets its alarm list shrink and scroll to make room, so the footer buttons always stay inside the panel — the list area keeps the same comfortable size whenever there's room for it.
v3.6.19 May 30, 2026 Improved
  • Consistent button heights across the map header. The header buttons had drifted to a few different heights — the Airport Owners button and the 🎨 palette button sat noticeably shorter than the rest. Every action button (Tools, Live, Alerts, Airport Owners, palette, Payout, Rating, Scenery, Close) is now a single uniform height, and the category filter chips (All / None / 1–7 / Invert) are all sized to match each other too, so the whole header reads as one coherent toolbar. The sizes track automatically as the window narrows, so they stay aligned at every width.
v3.6.18 May 30, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery / Highlight button matches the other header buttons' height. The scenery split button was rendering taller than its neighbours on narrower screens. Its extra height came from the Airport Owners area growing to two lines (see below); with that fixed, the button now sits flush with Payout, Rating, and Close.
  • 🎨 palette button stays beside the Airport Owners menu instead of dropping below it. When the header got tight, the colour-palette button wrapped onto a second line under the Airport Owners button. It now stays inline, and the Airport Owners button drops its "active / total" count in compact mode so there's room — showing just "Airport Owners" until the window is wide again.
  • Scenery button label collapses with the rest of the bar. At the width where every other header button drops its text label, the scenery button now collapses its mode word (Scenery / Highlight / Only) to just its dot icon too, instead of staying conspicuously wide. The ✏ edit affordance stays put.
v3.6.17 May 30, 2026 Fix
  • Map header no longer collides at narrower screen widths. On screens around 1500–1650 px wide (notably the 1512 px MacBook width), the 🔔 Alerts button could overlap the "All" button of the category filter because the left-hand tools were getting crushed while the right-hand controls held their width. The header now collapses the Tools / Live / Alerts buttons to their icons a little earlier, before anything can overlap, so it stays clean and readable as the window narrows. The dropdown menus keep their full text labels.
v3.6.16 May 30, 2026 Fix
  • Flight Demo Mode now always leaves room to descend on short flights. On short routes the demo aircraft could still be climbing toward a high cruise altitude when it was already time to come down, then arrive at the destination far too high. The demo now caps the climb to a Top-of-Descent envelope: it never climbs higher than the remaining track distance can bring it back down from, so it tops out at a sensible altitude and flies a realistic descent to landing every time. Normal and long flights — which already have room to climb and descend — are unchanged.
v3.6.15 May 30, 2026 Feature
  • Collapse / expand button on the Flight Demo control bar. The demo control bar now has the same show/hide toggle as the telemetry panel — a small ⏷ / ⏶ button in the bottom-right corner of the bar. Collapsing hides all the playback controls and shrinks the bar to a compact pill so it stops covering the map; clicking again brings the controls back. The collapsed state is remembered across sessions.
v3.6.14 May 29, 2026 Improved
  • Double-click aircraft now zooms to 3 nm width. The aircraft double-click zoom (added in v3.6.12) now zooms to a 3 nm wide view instead of 2 nm, matching the airport double-click zoom for a consistent feel.
v3.6.13 May 29, 2026 Fix
  • Telemetry panel collapse/expand button no longer blocked by toast notifications. After a toast appeared (e.g. starting Flight Demo Mode), the faded-but-still-present notification in the bottom-right corner kept intercepting clicks — so the collapse/expand arrow on the telemetry data panel stopped responding (collapsing worked, but re-expanding didn't, because the collapsed arrow sat a few pixels lower, right under the toast). Toasts are now click-through, so they never swallow clicks on the controls beneath them.
v3.6.12 May 29, 2026 Improved
  • Double-click aircraft to zoom in to 2 nm width. When a live aircraft is displayed (FSUIPC or demo), double-clicking the aircraft icon centers the map on the aircraft and zooms to a 2 nm wide view. Follow mode is turned off so the map stays put after zooming.
v3.6.11 May 29, 2026 Fix
  • Airport count on main screen now matches the map pill. The stats line (e.g. "3 owners · 999 airports") was counting every raw ICAO token in the input, including ICAOs that don't exist in the airport database and duplicates across owners. The map's Category Colors pill only counts airports it actually renders — unique ICAOs that resolve in the database. The stats count now deduplicates by ICAO and excludes unresolvable entries, so both numbers always agree.
v3.6.9 May 29, 2026 Improved
  • Finer Track Deviation readout under 1 nm. The TRK DEV data-panel field now shows 2 decimal places when the deviation is below 1 nm (e.g. 0.42 nm), so small drifts off the planned track read clearly; 1 nm and above still show 1 decimal.
v3.6.8 May 29, 2026 Improved
  • Demo holds runway heading 45 seconds. With the realistic takeoff roll now in place (v3.6.7), the runway-heading hold after takeoff is shortened from 90 to 45 seconds before the aircraft turns to follow the plan.
v3.6.7 May 29, 2026 Fix
  • Demo aircraft no longer climbs like a helicopter on takeoff. The synthetic aircraft was gaining altitude before it built up speed, so it appeared to ascend vertically before moving. It now performs a proper takeoff roll — accelerating down the runway at field elevation until rotation speed, then climbing — so it tracks laterally as it climbs out.
v3.6.6 May 29, 2026 Improved
  • Demo holds runway heading longer after takeoff. The synthetic aircraft now flies the departure runway heading for 90 seconds after rotation (was 30 s) before turning to follow the plan — it was still turning onto course too soon.
v3.6.5 May 29, 2026 Improved
  • Demo ELAPSED shows simulated flight time. In Flight Demo Mode the ELAPSED data-panel field now tracks simulated time since takeoff — matching the scrub bar — instead of real wall-clock time, so a sped-up demo reports the flight time it represents. Live / FSUIPC flights are unchanged.
  • Demo turns onto course 30 seconds after takeoff. The synthetic aircraft was beginning its turn-to-course too early; it now holds runway heading for a fixed 30 seconds after rotation before following the plan (replacing the altitude-based trigger).
  • Demo control bar — second row. Stale, Disconnect, and Exit now sit on their own row, centered, beneath the playback controls.
v3.6.4 May 29, 2026 Improved
  • Airport tooltips wait briefly before showing. Map airport tooltips now appear after a 250 ms hover, so sweeping the cursor across a dense field of airport dots no longer flashes tooltips.
  • Matrix close button is red. The ✕ that dismisses the route matrix is now red instead of blue, so it reads clearly as a close action.
v3.6.3 May 29, 2026 Improved
