  • Radius shown while resizing a region ring. Dragging the edge handle of a drawn departure or arrival region now floats the live radius (“N nm”) next to the cursor, just like the initial region draw does.
v3.6.2 May 29, 2026 Feature
  • New Track Deviation telemetry + alert. A new TRK DEV data-panel field shows how far you are abeam the SimBrief route (cross-track distance in nm). It's hidden by default — add it from the ⚙ panel-customise menu (under Navigation). Track deviation is also a new Conditional alert parameter (default threshold 10 nm), so you can get alerted when you drift off the planned track.
  • Demo flight-time estimate now actually refines. The scrub bar's total time was reproducing the original build-time estimate; it now measures the pace you're actually flying (including turn overhead) and converges the total on the real arrival time as the flight progresses.
  • Demo scrub time stops at touchdown. The scrub bar no longer keeps counting up after the flight has landed.
v3.6.1 May 29, 2026 Improved
  • Click a layer pill to zoom to it. Clicking the ✈ Flight Plan or Aircraft Track breadcrumb pill (the label, not the ✕) now zooms the map so that route or track fills about 90% of the viewport. The ✕ still clears the layer as before.
v3.6.0 May 29, 2026 Feature
  • New NEXT data-panel field. The aircraft data panel now has a NEXT field showing the ident of the next un-passed waypoint on the loaded SimBrief route, next to the existing position/altitude/speed fields.
  • Demo Mode follows the flight plan once it reaches 2,000 ft. The synthetic aircraft now holds runway heading on the initial climb and turns to follow the plan as soon as it has climbed 2,000 ft above field elevation — instead of waiting until it was 5 nm from the departure airport. Turns onto course happen at a realistic point regardless of how fast the aircraft is moving.
  • Demo speed buttons are now 1×, 3×, 10×. Replaces the old 0.25× / 0.5× / 1× / 3× / 5× set with the three most useful playback speeds (the fine-tune slider still covers everything in between).
  • Demo flight time estimate refines as you fly. The scrub bar's total flight time now subtly adjusts during cruise to converge on a more accurate figure as the flight progresses, rather than staying pinned to the initial build-time estimate.
  • Demo elapsed timer now starts and stops with the flight. Starting a demo resets the ELAPSED telemetry timer, and it now freezes at touchdown (and clears when you stop the demo) instead of ticking on after landing. Live / FSUIPC flights are unaffected.
  • “Clear Aircraft Track” removed from the Live menu. The green Aircraft Track pill in the map filter strip (added in v3.5.0) already clears the trail in one click, so the duplicate menu row is gone.
v3.5.1 May 29, 2026 Improved
  • Flight Plan and Aircraft Track chips now match the other filter chips. The two breadcrumb chips added in v3.5.0 are sized the same as the rest of the filter strip instead of using a smaller style. The Aircraft Track chip keeps its green label as a cue.
v3.5.0 May 29, 2026 Feature
  • Clear the SimBrief flight plan and aircraft track from the map breadcrumb strip. When a SimBrief plan is plotted, a small ✈ Flight Plan chip appears in the filter strip; when an aircraft breadcrumb track is on the map, a small green Aircraft Track chip appears. Click the ✕ on either to remove that layer in one step, without opening the Live menu. The chips are intentionally small and unobtrusive since they're shown for most of a flight, and they appear/disappear automatically as the plan and track come and go.
v3.4.6 May 29, 2026 Fix
  • Bulk sound menu says "both alarms" for two. Continuing the v3.4.5 grammar fix, the 🔊→All headers now read "both alarms" when exactly two alarms exist (rather than "all 2 alarms") — one alarm reads "the alarm", three or more "all N alarms".
v3.4.5 May 29, 2026 Fix
  • Bulk sound menu reads correctly with one alarm. The 🔊→All picker headers said "Set sound on all 1 alarm" when only one alarm existed. They now read "Set sound on the alarm" / "Set "Sound for" on the alarm" in the singular, and "all N alarms" for two or more.
v3.4.4 May 29, 2026 Fix
  • Altitude fields reject typed negatives too. Following the v3.4.3 conditional-alarm clamp, an audit found the Cruise Reached and Altitude (crossing) fields would still accept a negative value if you typed it (dragging was already blocked). A negative cruise target could fire the alarm instantly. Typing a negative altitude now snaps to 0, matching the drag behavior. Other Alerts fields (timer, geofence radius, auto-dismiss, telemetry-loss) and the Flight Demo autopilot (heading, altitude, speed) were checked and already clamp correctly.
v3.4.3 May 29, 2026 Fix
  • Conditional alarms no longer accept impossible negative thresholds. Distance, time, and airspeed conditions now clamp to a minimum of 0 (a threshold like "distance > −5" could never mean anything). Vertical speed and Altitude are intentionally left signed: vertical speed uses negatives for descent (e.g. "VS < −1500 fpm"), and altitude can be negative at below-sea-level airports. Both typing a negative and dragging below zero are now prevented on the clamped fields.
v3.4.2 May 29, 2026 Fix
  • Dragging a number field no longer closes the dialog. When you dragged to adjust a value and released the mouse outside the dialog, the dialog would close (the browser treats a drag that ends on the backdrop as a click on the backdrop). The drag-release is now ignored for close-on-backdrop, so the dialog stays open; clicking the backdrop normally still closes it.
v3.4.1 May 29, 2026 Fix
  • Up/down arrows work again on alarm number fields. The new click-and-drag scrubbing introduced in v3.3.0 was swallowing clicks on the little up/down spinner arrows of number inputs (timer h/m/s, geofence radius, auto-dismiss seconds, telemetry-loss threshold). The arrows now work normally again — click them to step the value — while click-and-drag still scrubs from anywhere else in the field.
v3.4.0 May 29, 2026 Feature
  • Loading splash screen with progress bar. When the app starts it now shows a splash screen with a live progress bar and the name of each component as it loads — airport database, region names, runways, airport categories, aircraft database, field overrides, pilot payouts, then route-distance computation and the owner airport list. The component names flick by quickly on a fast connection; the screen fades away on its own once everything is ready (and never hangs — it dismisses after a safety timeout even if a data source is slow or offline).
v3.3.0 May 29, 2026 Feature
  • Click-and-drag to scrub any alarm number. The drag-up/down gesture that already adjusted altitude fields now works on every numeric field in the Alerts panel — timer hours/minutes/seconds, geofence radius, auto-dismiss seconds, and the telemetry-loss threshold — plus the radius and auto-dismiss fields in the Geofence and Add Waypoint Alarm pop-ups. Press on the field and drag up to increase or down to decrease; a quick click still focuses it for typing.
  • “Send to phone” checkbox in the Geofence and Add Waypoint Alarm pop-ups. You can now forward these alarms to your phone right when you create them, instead of opening the alarm card afterward to flip the toggle. The checkbox only appears once a Mobile Alerts provider is configured under Live → Mobile Alerts.
  • New alarms auto-dismiss after 10 seconds by default. “Sound for” now defaults to Auto-dismiss after… (10 seconds, or your saved preference) rather than sounding indefinitely, so an alarm you miss stops on its own. Switch any alarm back to Indefinite if you want it to keep sounding until dismissed.
  • Set “Sound for” on every alarm at once. The bulk button at the bottom of the Alerts panel (formerly sound-only) now also lets you apply an auto-dismiss duration — indefinite, 5, 10, 30, or 60 seconds — to all alarms in one click.
  • Badge on the Mobile Alerts menu. Live → Mobile Alerts now shows a count of how many active alarms are set to forward to your phone, so you can tell at a glance whether anything will reach you while you’re away.
  • Faster Cruise Reached and Descent Started alerts. The phase detector now confirms a cruise transition after 35 seconds of level flight (was 60) and a descent after 20 seconds (was 30), so these alerts fire noticeably sooner after the real event. The climb-evidence guard and 30-second smoothing that prevent false alarms on the ground or during brief altitude wobbles are unchanged.
v3.2.0 May 28, 2026 Improved
  • Aircraft weight data now comes from the shared aircraft database. Maximum takeoff weight, usable fuel, and empty weight (the figures behind required-runway, landing, and range calculations) used to be baked into the app. They now load from the same community aircraft database that already supplies the model list, categories, and distances — three new columns (MTOW_lb, Fuel_lb, OEW_lb). Adding or correcting an aircraft is now a single edit to that database, and Active Airports and Recip Tracker stay perfectly in sync automatically. There is no built-in fallback, so weight-based figures appear only once the database has loaded.
v3.1.1 May 28, 2026 Fix
  • Low fuel loads from Recip Tracker now carry over correctly. When launching Active Airports from Recip Tracker's Plan Flight, a fuel load below 40% was silently snapping back to 100% on arrival — an old lower limit on the incoming fuel value. The limit now allows the full 0–100% range (matching the fuel slider and saved preferences), so whatever fuel and payload you set in Recip Tracker is exactly what Active Airports opens with.
v3.1.0 May 27, 2026 Feature
  • FSUIPC live tracking now auto-resumes after a page reload. If you were connected to FSUIPC when the tab refreshed (intentionally, or via a browser crash / restore), AA now silently re-opens the WebSocket on the next page load and brings back the aircraft marker, breadcrumb trail, and Follow A/C state exactly as they were — no more re-clicking FSUIPC Live and re-enabling follow every time the page reloads mid-flight. Connect-intent is persisted to localStorage under simfly_aa_fsuipc_wanted, written when you start tracking and cleared the moment you click ⏹ FSUIPC Tracking… to stop — so a deliberate disconnect stays disconnected across reloads.
  • Auto-resume is silent and self-limiting. The reconnect on reload suppresses the “connecting…”, retry, and failure toasts (a single confirmation toast fires only on a successful reconnect), and it gives up after 2 minutes if the sim can’t be reached. That cap means opening AA on a machine with no FSUIPC running — a laptop away from the sim PC, for instance — won’t hammer localhost:2048 forever; it tries quietly for two minutes, then stops and clears the intent so future reloads don’t retry either. A manual click, or any connection that succeeds at least once, is never capped — mid-flight socket drops still retry indefinitely as before.
  • Help text corrected. The Live Aircraft Tracking help section said the background reconnect fires every 10 seconds; the actual interval has been 15 seconds since v2.88.0. Updated to match, and documented the new auto-resume behaviour.
v3.0.13 May 27, 2026 Improved
  • Multi-tab sync now happens silently. Removed the Sync toast checkbox from the Alerts modal footer and dropped the “🔁 Synced changes from another AA tab” toast that fired on every cross-tab reload. The sync itself is unchanged — alarm, group, and history changes still propagate between open AA tabs the moment one tab writes to localStorage, with the same 250 ms debounce and the same in-memory reload + UI re-render — you just don’t get a visual notification anymore. Removed: the _AFK_LS_MULTITAB localStorage key, the get/set helpers, the checkbox markup, and the modal-open handler that reflected the persisted state. Existing simfly_aa_afk_multitab_toast values left behind in browser storage are orphaned but harmless.
v3.0.12 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo aircraft now captures and tracks the extended runway centerline on final. Pre-3.0.12, the runway-heading lock at the destination was a flat “hold destRwyHdg within 5 nm of the airport” with no lateral-offset correction — so if the FAF turn was even slightly wide, the aircraft would parallel the centerline, fly past the airport, U-turn back when it left the 5 nm bubble, then re-grab runway heading and fly past again, circling endlessly without ever crossing the threshold. Replaced with pure cross-track-error proportional control: the heading target is now destRwyHdg ± min(45°, xtErr × 25°/nm), so even a wide FAF turn is captured at a localizer-style intercept angle and the aircraft tracks the extended centerline all the way to the threshold.
  • Demo aircraft now actually stops at the destination. Added a hard-landing detector that fires when the aircraft has crossed the runway threshold along the approach direction at below 500 ft AGL. On trip, position is snapped to the threshold, groundspeed is zeroed, altitude is set to field elevation, and a latch is set that short-circuits all further integration ticks. Belt-and-suspenders: even if centerline capture is imperfect, the demo can no longer enter the “circle past the airport forever” state.
  • Approach-speed deceleration now begins early enough for the FAF turn. The v3.0.10 deceleration trigger only fired when the FAF was the immediate next waypoint — one leg of decel, and at jet descent speeds (250–330 kt) the integrator's 2 kt/sec slow-down needs 3–6 nm just to reach approachKts. On routes where the leg leading into the FAF was short, the aircraft was still mostly at descent speed when it hit the turn, blowing the turn radius out to 2–3 nm and producing the wide overshoot. Fixed: speed target is now capped to approachKts whenever the FAF is in the remaining route and within 7 nm of the aircraft — enough lead for the slowest decel profile (widebody 330 → 150 kt over ~6 nm).
  • Threshold-crossing and landing-roll speed now scale with aircraft category. The arrival guard and end-of-route speed profile were hardcoded to 80 kt — a reasonable Vref for a Cessna 172 but ~40 kt too slow for a Citation X (Vref ~120 kt) and ~60 kt too slow for a 777 (Vref ~140 kt). Replaced with a per-category derived landingKts = max(60, approachKts − 20), applied in both the arrival guard and the speed-profile builder. Light GA still lands ~60 kt; bizjet ~110 kt; widebody ~130 kt.
v3.0.11 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Time budget and slider bar now track actual flight progress. The demo engine computed climb and descent distance using full cruise speed, but the aircraft actually averages 77.5% of cruise during climb (speed ramp 55% → 100%) and 85% during descent (ramp 100% → 70%). This 15–22% overestimate caused the budget to exhaust 15–20 minutes before the aircraft physically arrived — leaving the scrubber frozen with “19:42 remaining” while the plane was already on short final. Fix: climbNm and descentNm are now derived from the correct integral-average groundspeed (× 0.775 and × 0.850). The cruise phase absorbs the reclaimed distance, keeping total route nm accurate while aligning the time machine with the integrator.
  • Approach phase now covers the actual FAF → threshold segment. The legacy approach phase was a fixed 45 s “virtual” final that was glued to the end of the timeline after the aircraft reached the destination — adequate when the route ended at the airport, but far too short now that the 8 nm FAF leg is a real route segment. The approach phase is now computed as fafNm / approachKts × 3600 seconds (≈ 206 s for a narrowbody at 140 kt), covers only the FAF → threshold distance, and the slider advances through it rather than freezing at 100%.
  • Approach phase correctly models the 3° glideslope. _demoStateAt now walks altitude from the FAF intercept height (fafNm × tan(3°) × 6076 ≈ 2548 ft AGL) down to threshold elevation during the approach phase, and uses the category approachKts for speed instead of the hardcoded 140 kt. The descent phase likewise targets the FAF altitude (not a fixed 1500 ft AGL) so the vertical profile is consistent end-to-end.
  • Approach phase budget is excluded from the climb/descent scaling check. The short-route V-profile scaling now fits climb+descent into 90% of the pre-FAF route distance (not total route), so the 8 nm approach leg is never cannibalised by the scale factor on very short routes.
v3.0.10 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo aircraft no longer overshoots the FAF turn. The aircraft was arriving at the FAF at descent speed (~294 kt for a narrowbody), giving a turn radius of roughly 2.7 nm and requiring 52 seconds to complete a 90° turn — producing an obvious bulge well past the runway centerline. Root cause: the approach speed phase only activated after all route distance was consumed, so the FAF leg was flown at full descent speed. Fix: the engine now detects when the aircraft is on the leg leading into the FAF (pointer targets the FAF) and immediately caps the speed target to the category approach speed (65 kt Light GA up to 150 kt Widebody). The aircraft decelerates over the full approach leg distance, arriving at the FAF near Vref and executing a compact standard-rate turn with a radius under 0.7 nm.
  • Demo VNAV now tracks a 3° glideslope on final approach. Previously the FAF→threshold leg used the time-based descent altitude profile, which targeted ~1 500 ft AGL at end-of-descent. This left the aircraft hundreds to thousands of feet above a correct ILS glideslope when it intercepted the FAF. Fix: the FAF point now carries an explicit altitude constraint equal to the 3° glideslope intercept (destElev + 8 nm × tan(3°) × 6076 ≈ destElev + 2548 ft). On SimBrief plans (hasVnav = true) the existing _demoVnavManagedAlt interpolator picks this up and tracks the glideslope by position rather than by time. On non-VNAV (hand-constructed) plans a direct position-based fallback injects the same glideslope target into the altitude command.
  • Category approach speeds added to performance table. The _DEMO_PERF table now carries an approachKts field for each SimFly category (65–150 kt). The approach speed is used for the new final-leg speed cap and ensures the arrival deceleration profile is realistic across all aircraft types, not just hardcoded for narrowbodies.
  • VNAV annotation now runs before FAF insertion. _demoAnnotateVnav previously ran after the synthetic FAF was spliced in. Had the FAF carried an altFt at that point, it would have been counted as a raw waypoint constraint — turning on VNAV mode for hand-constructed plans that have no real altitude data. The order in _demoBuildPlan is now: compute field elevations → annotate raw waypoints → insert FAF (with synthetic altitude for VNAV plans only). This ensures hasVnav is set only by actual SimBrief altitude constraints.
v3.0.9 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo FAF now inserts correctly before the approach segment, not inside it. SimBrief navlogs frequently include the destination airport as the final navlog fix (at the airport reference point), which means raw.waypoints already contains a KPDX fix immediately before raw.dest. The v3.0.0 splice inserted the FAF only between raw.dest and the point just before it — leaving the KPDX navlog fix stranded between RALEE and the FAF, producing the route RALEE → KPDX → FAF → Threshold. The aircraft would route to the airport first, then fly southeast to intercept the FAF, then turn northwest to land — visibly backward. Fix: before inserting the FAF, all interior waypoints within _DEMO_FAF_NM (8 nm) of the runway threshold are removed. Terminal STAR fixes that coincide with or near the airport reference are stripped out; the route then correctly reads last-enroute-fix → FAF → Threshold for a clean extended-centerline approach.
v3.0.8 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo runway selector now scores by length, ending decommissioned-runway wins. Diagnostic data confirmed that KPDX’s decommissioned runway 3/21 (6,000 ft, heading 225°) was consistently beating the active runways 28R/28L (9,825–11,000 ft, heading 299°) because the inbound bearing from RALEE (~261°) sits almost exactly between the two headings. With purely angular scoring, a fraction-of-a-degree difference decided the winner — and 225° happened to be marginally closer than 299° to 261°. Fix: runway length is now factored into endpointScore. Without wind data the formula is lengthFt × 1000 − approachDiff, making a 5,000 ft length difference worth thousands of points versus a fraction of a degree — the decommissioned runway cannot win unless no longer runway is available. With live wind data the formula is headwindKts × 200 + lengthFt; a 5,000 ft length advantage requires approximately 25 kt of headwind deficit to be overridden, consistent with real crosswind limits. At KPDX with no wind, the 11,000 ft runway 28L is now selected and the FAF is placed on the extended runway 28 centerline to the east of the airport.
v3.0.7 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo runway selector — approach alignment gate corrected to 40°. The v3.0.5 gate of 60° was computed incorrectly: RALEE sits roughly due east of KPDX, giving an inbound bearing of ~269°. The decommissioned runway 21 (heading 210°) is 59° off that bearing — which passes a > 60 check since 59 is not greater than 60. Reducing the gate to 40° correctly excludes runway 21 (59° > 40 = true) while runway 28 (12° off) passes cleanly. Airports with no runway within 40° of the inbound bearing fall through to the unconstrained pass 2 (wind-only selection) as before.
v3.0.6 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Vertical speed telemetry now shows instantaneous rate, not a 30-second average. The VS value displayed in the telemetry panel was computed by dividing the total altitude change over the last 30 seconds by 30 — a heavily smoothed figure that lagged reality significantly during climb and descent transitions. The phase tracker still uses the 30-second average internally (it needs stability to avoid false climb/cruise/descent transitions), but the publicly exposed vsFpm() now returns an instantaneous reading derived from the last two altitude samples. The displayed value reflects the actual rate of change over the most recent polling interval.
v3.0.5 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo runway selector — approach alignment gate tightened from 120° to 60°. With a 120° gate, a decommissioned KPDX runway (heading ~210°) was passing the course-change check (70° off the ~280° KSEA→KPDX inbound bearing) and beating runway 28 on headwind score whenever northerly winds were present. The FAF was then placed on the backcourse of that runway — 8 nm to the north of the airport — instead of to the east near the real approach corridor. Narrowing the gate to 60° excludes any runway whose approach heading differs from the inbound route bearing by more than 60°; runway 28 (1° off) passes cleanly while the aberrant entry (70° off) is rejected. If no runway qualifies in pass 1, the fallback pass uses wind-only scoring with no alignment constraint, so airports with oblique approach geometry are unaffected.
  • Reverted nearest-forward waypoint advance (v3.0.4 regression). The scan searched all remaining waypoints for one closer than the current target and jumped ahead to it. On routes where the destination is already geometrically closer than intermediate enroute fixes (common when approaching from the far side of a route arc), the aircraft skipped directly to the runway threshold before reaching the FAF, activating the runway lock from the wrong approach angle and causing large orbits over the destination. Removed entirely; normal turn-anticipation advance is sufficient.
v3.0.4 May 27, 2026 Improved
  • Demo autopilot routes directly to the FAF when it is the nearest remaining waypoint. Previously the aircraft followed route waypoints strictly in order — if the FAF was already the geographically closest remaining point, the aircraft would still fly to the next intermediate STAR fix first, then turn back toward the FAF. The advance logic in _demoApMaybeAdvanceWpt now scans forward through remaining waypoints at each tick: if any later waypoint is more than 1 nm closer than the current target, the pointer advances to it. The 1 nm guard prevents spurious skips on near-equidistant fixes; scanning in route order means the aircraft takes the first closer fix encountered rather than leapfrogging past genuinely necessary intermediate turns.
v3.0.3 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo runway selector — short and historical runways excluded from ILS approach planning. The OurAirports runway database includes historical, decommissioned, and secondary strips at many airports alongside their primary commercial runways. With no length filter, the selector could choose a short taxiway-width strip or a closed runway whose extended centerline places the virtual FAF in the wrong direction — for example, at KPDX an old runway 3/21 entry (heading ~210°) would beat runway 28 on course-change angle and place the FAF north of the airport rather than to the east. Fix: runways shorter than 5 000 ft are now skipped before scoring. This threshold comfortably admits all primary jet runways while excluding helipads, ultralight strips, and most decommissioned secondary pavements.
v3.0.2 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo route line now shows the FAF waypoint and runway threshold. The pink route line was rendered from the original raw flight plan (unchanged from the SimBrief import), so it drew directly from the last STAR fix to the airport reference point with no FAF detour visible. The aircraft itself was navigating via the FAF correctly, but the rendered route didn’t reflect it. _demoLaunch now builds the SimBrief shape from plan.points instead of raw.waypoints, so the displayed route includes the FAF waypoint and the final segment ends at the selected runway threshold. On routes where the STAR approach direction is already roughly aligned with the runway, the FAF is nearly colinear with the direct path and the deviation is subtle; on routes with a more oblique approach the FAF intercept and final centerline segment are clearly visible as a dog-leg onto the runway heading.
v3.0.1 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo runway selector — no longer picks a runway whose FAF requires a course reversal. v3.0.0 selected the runway with the highest headwind component without considering whether the aircraft could reasonably reach that runway’s Final Approach Fix from its current direction. When Portland’s prevailing westerly winds were active, the selector chose runway 10 (eastbound) over runway 28 (westbound), placing the FAF 8 nm west of the airport — which required the aircraft approaching from the northeast (via RALEE) to fly a large looping orbit around Portland before lining up. Fix: a two-pass selector now first screens candidates to those within 120° of the inbound route bearing; headwind scoring applies only within that eligible set. If no runway passes the gate (unusual approach geometry), a second pass relaxes to all candidates and picks by alignment. For the KSEA→KPDX route approaching via RALEE, runway 10 is 154° off the inbound bearing and is rejected; runway 28 (26° off) is selected regardless of wind direction. New constant _DEMO_FAF_MAX_COURSE_CHANGE_DEG = 120.
v3.0.0 May 27, 2026 Feature
  • Demo autopilot — weather-based runway selection with extended centerline intercept. The demo engine now queries CheckWX for the destination airport’s current METAR before building the flight plan. The runway end with the highest headwind component is selected; when no METAR is available the selection falls back to the runway whose heading best matches the route’s inbound bearing. A virtual “FAF” waypoint is inserted into the route plan 8 nm outbound from the landing threshold on the extended runway centerline, so the aircraft intercepts the final approach course and flies the last segment aligned with the runway rather than angling in from whatever direction the last STAR fix happened to be. The dest point is also redirected to the actual runway threshold coordinates so the final segment precisely tracks the centerline. The runway heading is taken directly from the selected threshold rather than being estimated from the route bearing — no rounding through the 10° runway-ident quantisation. The weather fetch runs with a 4-second timeout and a _demoPendingStart guard prevents the Start button from firing twice while the fetch is in-flight; the demo starts immediately using route-based fallback if weather data is unavailable.
v2.99.8 May 27, 2026 Improved
  • Demo arrival guard — uses remaining track miles instead of straight-line distance. The v2.99.7 arrival guard (which prevents the aircraft stopping short when the time-phase machine fires “ground” too early) was checking straight-line distance from the aircraft to the destination. Replaced with _demoRemainingRouteNm(), a new helper that sums the distance to the current target waypoint plus every remaining planned leg to the destination. On routes where the STAR arcs away from the airport, track miles remaining is longer than the crow-flies measure — the straight-line check would have allowed the guard to drop while the aircraft still had one or more waypoints left to thread. The helper degrades to straight-line distance once the aircraft is on the final direct leg to the destination, so there is no change in behaviour for that case.
v2.99.7 May 27, 2026 Fix
  • Demo autopilot — aircraft no longer stops short of the destination. On longer routes with STAR approaches (e.g. KSEA→KPDX), the simulated aircraft was stopping ~15 km before the destination and showing the ground phase while still airborne. Root cause: the time-based phase machine fires the arrival-ground transition at a fixed elapsed-second deadline, but the integrator’s GS profile averages ~78% of cruise speed during climb and ~85% during descent, so the aircraft physically lags behind the time budget. When ground fired, the speed target dropped to zero and the aircraft stopped wherever the integrator happened to be. Fix: an arrival guard in _demoTick checks whether the integrator is still more than 0.3 nm from the destination when the time-phase fires ground. If so, it holds approach phase active (GS target 80 kt) so the aircraft keeps decelerating toward landing speed and continues navigating to the runway. The ground phase is then allowed to engage only once the aircraft is within the 0.3 nm arrival threshold.
v2.99.6 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Demo autopilot landing — aircraft no longer “lands” at an intermediate STAR fix. Symptom: a KSEA→KPDX flight teardropped at BOOSR (a STAR fix ~8 nm NE of KPDX) and stopped there instead of continuing through RALEE to the KPDX runway. Two bugs combined:
    • Skip-bubble was eating STAR fixes. _demoApSkipBubbleFwd skipped any waypoint within 5 nm of dep or dest. The dep-side skip is correct (keeps the aircraft on the extended-runway centerline during initial climb, ignoring SID fixes inside that 5 nm cone), but the dest-side skip caused real STAR transition fixes — RALEE for KPDX, similar approach fixes elsewhere — to be silently jumped over. Aircraft then headed direct from BOOSR to the airport at a strange angle. Dest-side skip removed; arrivals are flown waypoint-by-waypoint as published.
    • Runway lock fired on lateral distance alone, ignoring leg position. The arrival runway-heading capture in _demoApAutoTargetHdg fired the moment the aircraft was within 5 nm of the destination from any angle, including while still on intermediate STAR fixes that happened to be geographically near the airport. Result: aircraft grabbed the runway heading from the wrong angle and teardropped trying to capture it. Now gated on _demoNextWptIdx >= pts.length - 1 — the lock only fires once the aircraft has progressed through every intermediate waypoint and is on the final leg to the airport.
    Combined effect: arrivals now follow the published STAR through every fix, and the runway capture only engages on the actual final approach segment. Verified against a synthetic plan where RALEE sits 4.5 nm from KPDX (well inside the old skip bubble) — aircraft now flies KSEA → ANGOO → BOOSR → RALEE → KPDX, with the runway lock firing only at RALEE→KPDX transition. Note: if the simulation’s elapsed-time phase machine still occasionally fires “landed” while the integrator is a fraction of a nm short of the airport, that’s a separate concern; please report any “lands a mile from the runway” symptoms after this fix and we can extend the phase machine to wait on physical arrival.
v2.99.5 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • Demo autopilot turn anticipation — fixed the “turns too soon” symptom. v2.98.9 added a 2.0 nm floor on the turn-anticipation distance, but that floor ended up dominating the tan-based formula for nearly every realistic turn. At 250 kt with 25° bank, R ≈ 1.95 nm, so a 30° turn’s tan-formula value of 0.52 nm got pushed to 2.0, a 60° turn’s 1.13 nm went to 2.0, even a 90° turn’s 1.95 nm went to 2.0. Result: the aircraft anticipated ~2 nm out for every waypoint regardless of how shallow, cut every corner aggressively, never crossed the waypoint, and visually appeared to be flying direct to the next waypoint instead of tracking the planned line. Floor lowered to 0.1 nm — the tan-formula now governs as designed: shallow turns get tiny lead (~0.1 nm for ≤10°), 30° gets 0.52, 60° gets 1.13, 90° gets 1.95, and sharper turns are still capped at R for stability.
  • Payout palette — the % input on the dragged stop’s row now updates live during the drag. Previously the number input only refreshed on mouseup (when the full editor re-rendered), so you couldn’t see the % change while sliding the tick. Now the input value follows the drag in real time.
  • Payout palette — per-stop label clarified. The “%” label after each row’s percentage input now reads “% pilot payout”, so a row with 60 in the input clearly reads as “60 % pilot payout.”
  • Payout palette — persistence footer rewritten. “Persisted in this browser” replaced with the friendlier “Your settings will be saved automatically”.
  • Airport info cards (below the matrix) — DEP / ARR headers restyled. The “DEPARTURE ▶” and “▶ ARRIVAL” card headers were rendered in Orbitron uppercase at 1.44 rem. They now match the style of the main-screen “Departure Airport Owner” / “Arrival Airport Owner” labels: Apple system font, 1.658 rem, regular weight, mixed case — so the headers read as “Departure ▶” / “▶ Arrival” in the same visual language as the owner labels above. Per-card cyan / orange colours preserved (they already matched the owner-label colours).
v2.99.4 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Compound search now composes with single-axis +. v2.99.2 / v2.99.3 added compound stacking and removal, but mixing a single-axis additive query (e.g. +United States) on top of an active compound filter (e.g. Kyndra & Europe) silently failed — the new owner / country / region / continent showed up as a breadcrumb chip but contributed zero airports because the compound gate replaced the per-axis OR gate and only honoured tuples that referenced both an owner AND a location. Fix: compound tuples now support wildcard sides — ownerName: null means "any owner," locType: null means "any location." When a single-axis + fires while compound is active, the activator appends a wildcard-other-side tuple (e.g. {ownerName: null, locType: 'country', locKey: 'US'}) so the new search contributes to the visible union as the user intended. Example: Kyndra & Europe then +United States now correctly shows Kyndra’s 3 European airports PLUS every SimFly airport in the US. - single-axis removal continues to work via the existing auto-clear path: removing US from the country chip drops the matching wildcard tuple from compound, leaving the original Kyndra & Europe intersection.
v2.99.3 May 26, 2026 Feature
  • Compound search supports - to remove from the stack. Exact inverse of v2.99.2’s + stacking. Prefix a compound query with - to drop the matching tuple from the active compound union. Example: with Kyndra @ Europe + +jmribe @ Europe + +CapitanCabal @ Europe active (3 stacked tuples, ~22 airports visible), typing -jmribe @ Europe drops jmribe’s European intersection and the map snaps to the remaining ~11 airports. Removing the last surviving tuple clears the compound filter entirely.
  • Smart negative dropdown. When - is the prefix, the dropdown filters to show only compound tuples currently in the active stack — typing -CapitanCabal @ USA when no such tuple is loaded shows a “Nothing to remove” hint instead of a clickable row that would be a confusing no-op. Action text on negative-mode rows reads “Remove from union →” so the consequence of clicking is unambiguous.
v2.99.2 May 26, 2026 Feature
  • Compound search stacks with +. Prefixing a compound query with + now appends it to the active compound filter instead of replacing it. Build a multi-pilot/multi-region union one query at a time: Kyndra @ Europe shows Kyndra’s 3 European airports; add +jmribe @ Europe to also include jmribe’s European airports; add +CapitanCabal @ Europe; and so on. Each query stays as its own (owner AND location) intersection; the visible map is the union of those intersections. Mixing destinations works the same way — Kyndra @ Europe then +Kyndra @ USA shows Kyndra’s European and US airports together. A compound query without the + prefix still wipes prior filters and starts fresh, as before. Re-typing an already-active compound query is a no-op (deduped on owner+location key).
  • X’ing a chip drops only the compound rows that referenced it. When the active compound array has multiple tuples, X’ing out one of the owner or location chips removes only the compound entries that depended on that chip — surviving tuples continue to filter. Any orphan chips (owners or locations no longer referenced by any compound tuple after the drop) disappear automatically so the breadcrumb strip stays in sync with what’s actually visible.
v2.99.1 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Compound `owner @ place` search now actually intersects. v2.99.0 set the owner filter and the matching country / region / continent filter side-by-side, but the existing dot-rendering inclusion gate OR-combines those axes (intentionally — that’s how the additive + prefix accumulates filters). Result: the dropdown row read “Kyndra in Europe · 3 airports” but clicking it showed every airport in Europe (the union, not the intersection). Fix: new dedicated _rgMapCompoundFilter slot replaces the OR inclusion gate with an AND check at all three gate sites (_rgDotPassesMapFilters + both dep/arr highlight loops). Per-axis filter Sets are still populated so the breadcrumb chips render unchanged — and the slot auto-clears in _rgApplyMapFilter the moment the user X’s either chip, reverting the surviving filter to standard OR-gate behavior.
v2.99.0 May 26, 2026 Feature
  • Map search: find all of an owner’s airports inside a place. Type any owner and any place into the map search box joined by @ or & — e.g. Kyndra @ Texas, CapitanCabal & Europe, Kyndra @ USA. The two delimiters are interchangeable. Order doesn’t matter — Texas @ Kyndra works the same. The dropdown shows one row per (owner, place) match with the airport count; clicking activates both the owner filter and the corresponding country / region / continent filter atomically.
  • Place side accepts every geographic level. Continents (Europe / EU / North America / NA), countries (United States / US / USA, United Kingdom / UK, Germany / DE, Japan / JP), and sub-national admin units of every flavour — states, provinces, oblasts, prefectures, regions — by full name (Texas, Bavaria, Ontario, Tōkyō Prefecture), 2-letter suffix (TX), or full ISO code (US-TX, JP-13). Small alias list handles USA, UK, UAE.
  • Ambiguous places get disambiguated by qualifier chips. Kyndra @ Georgia can mean the US state or the country — the dropdown lists every interpretation that has matching airports with a parenthetical: “Georgia (United States)” vs “Georgia (country)”. Same for Washington (state vs D.C.), Punjab (India vs Pakistan), and any other cross-level or cross-country collision.
  • Email-safe delimiter handling. A bare @ with no surrounding whitespace is only treated as a compound split if the right side doesn’t look like an email domain (no dot). So user123@hotmail.com still searches as a single owner name, but user123@hotmail.com @ Texas works as a compound query. The & delimiter follows the same rules for consistency.
v2.98.11 May 26, 2026 Feature
  • Three new telemetry fields (hidden by default). All toggleable from the data panel's ⚙ menu:
    • DIST FROM DEP — great-circle nautical miles from the departure airport to your current position. Pairs nicely with the existing DIST TO DEST for a full picture of how far along the planned trip you are.
    • DIST TO NEXT — distance to the next un-passed waypoint on the SimBrief route. Uses the same leg-detection logic as TRK MILES REM (pick the leg whose endpoints best bracket the aircraft, target the end of that leg).
    • ETA NEXT — time-to-next-waypoint, computed from DIST TO NEXT and current ground speed. Hides when GS ≤ 5 kt (taxi / stopped).
    All three fields hover-tooltip with km equivalents and the next-waypoint ident where applicable.
  • Three matching conditional-alarm parameters. The 🧩 Conditional alarm type now accepts Distance from departure, Distance to next waypoint, and ETA to next waypoint as comparison parameters. ETA is treated as a time-unit param with the same seconds/minutes/hours picker the other time params use. Build alerts like "ETA to next waypoint < 2 minutes" for an inbound-fix heads-up or "Distance from departure > 100 nm" for a milestone notification.
  • Multi-tab sync toast is now opt-out. The 🔁 Synced changes from another AA tab toast that fires when another open AA tab persists an alarm-related change can be suppressed via the Sync toast checkbox in the Alerts modal footer. Default is on (matches pre-2.98.11 behaviour); uncheck to silence. Persisted in localStorage under key simfly_aa_afk_multitab_toast.
v2.98.10 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Demo aircraft now tracks the displayed magenta route polyline accurately. Symptom: on legs with any east/west bias the aircraft track drifted off the polyline by 1–3 nm at mid-leg, ending up "well east of" (or west of) the next waypoint instead of crossing through it. Root cause: the SimBrief route polyline is rendered with L.polyline, which draws each segment as a straight line in screen space — that's a rhumb line (constant compass heading) on the earth under Web Mercator. But the demo aircraft was navigating by great-circle — _demoBearing returns the initial great-circle bearing, recomputed every tick. On any leg that isn't exactly N–S or E–W, the great-circle bows toward the nearer pole, so the aircraft's track sat NORTH+EAST of the displayed polyline on a SW-bound leg in the northern hemisphere (and similarly elsewhere). Magnitude: ~1–3 nm at mid-leg for a 50 nm leg at mid-latitudes — small per leg, but very visible on a zoomed-in scenery view and large enough that the turn-anticipation advance could fire while the aircraft was still off the line, leaving it permanently offset. Fix: new _demoRhumbBearing helper (Mercator-projected atan2). The wpt-to-wpt heading function and the turn-anticipation in _demoApMaybeAdvanceWpt now both use rhumb bearings, so the aircraft tracks the same straight-segment line the user sees. Runway alignment continues to use true headings from ourairports; the integrator's per-tick position walk is still great-circle (the difference over a 1 sec integration step is well below 0.001 nm).
v2.98.9 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Demo aircraft no longer initiates turns far too soon — two related math bugs in the wpt-advance logic.
    • Turn-anticipation distance was unbounded for sharp turns. The geometric formula antDist = R · tan(δ/2) is correct for an arc that exactly tangents both legs, but tan grows superlinearly past 45°. At cruise (R ≈ 3.9 nm at 353 kt), a 90° turn gives 3.9 nm of anticipation, a 120° turn gives 6.75 nm, a 150° turn gives 14.6 nm, and δ→180° blows up to infinity. Capped at R (one turn radius). Up-to-90° turns are unchanged (the formula already produces ≤R there); sharper turns now use the tightest geometric anticipation and the aircraft accepts a mild overshoot rather than starting the turn an absurd distance in advance.
    • outBear was computed against in-bubble wpts the heading function would skip. _demoApMaybeAdvanceWpt used pts[idx+1] directly for the outgoing bearing, but _demoApAutoTargetHdg skips over any wpt inside the 5 nm dep/dest bubble. When the raw next wpt was an in-bubble STAR fix, the advance computed the turn against a wpt the aircraft would never actually fly toward — giving a wildly wrong δ and a wildly wrong antDist. Both functions now use a shared _demoApSkipBubbleFwd helper, so they can't drift apart and turn anticipation always reflects the wpt that's actually next on the aircraft's flight path.
v2.98.8 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Save Demo Flight Plan — suggested filename now reflects the current dep/dest. Pre-2.98.8 the save logic preferred any previously-loaded demo file over the active SimBrief plan, which meant after you'd loaded a demo file then later loaded a different SimBrief plan, both the suggested filename AND the saved content kept reflecting the stale demo file's airports. Priority flipped: the current SimBrief plan now wins, so saves track whichever airports are currently in play. The "re-save a loaded demo file under a new filename" workflow from v2.97.23 still works whenever no SimBrief plan is loaded — that's the only case where the loaded demo file is unambiguously the current intent.
v2.98.7 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • Demo runway alignment now uses ourairports' published true headings. Pre-2.98.7 the picker derived each runway end's heading from the runway identifier × 10° (mag, quantized to 10°) and then pushed it through a centred-dipole magnetic-variation model. Two compounding error sources — in regions with significant declination, the combined error could push the "closest end" calculation past the midpoint between LE and HE and have it choose the wrong end, sending the demo aircraft off in roughly the opposite direction from where the route wants to go. v2.98.7 captures le_heading_degT / he_heading_degT (and the threshold lat/lon for a future "start at threshold" follow-up) from the ourairports runways CSV at load time, and the picker now compares against those true headings directly. Sub-degree precision, no quantization, no magnetic-variation guesswork. The picker reliably chooses the right runway end, and the 5 nm boundary transition from runway heading to route heading becomes a small course correction instead of a wide recovery turn.
  • Internal simplification. Drops three mag⇔true round-trips that previously sat between the picker, the integrator seed, and the runway override path — everything now stays in TRUE from the picker through the integrator. The AP HDG display still converts true→mag at the user-facing boundary, so the cockpit readout convention is unchanged. Graceful fallback: if a CSV row lacks the heading column (uncommon, mostly helipads / waterways), we reconstruct from ident × 10 mag and dipole-convert just like the old path.
v2.98.6 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Demo aircraft no longer wanders off the flight plan when wpts sit inside the 5-nm dep/dest bubble. The v2.98.0 runway-alignment feature added wpt-skipping in _demoApAutoTargetHdg (don’t target a SID/STAR fix that’s within 5 nm of the airport — that would pull the aircraft off the runway-extended centerline). But _demoApMaybeAdvanceWpt was still tracking the un-skipped _demoNextWptIdx, so when the aircraft was flying direct to the first wpt OUTSIDE the bubble, the advance check kept measuring distance to a wpt the aircraft wasn’t even targeting. The aircraft would overshoot its real target, then the heading function would swing it back to chase the now-behind wpt — classic wander. Fix: _demoApMaybeAdvanceWpt now applies the same in-bubble skip to _demoNextWptIdx before its turn-anticipation check, keeping the advance pointer in lockstep with the heading target. Unrelated note: the magnetic-conversion (v2.98.1) and whole-degree rounding (v2.98.5) don’t affect tracking — the integrator math works in true bearing internally and the round-trip is lossless; those are purely display/input concerns.
v2.98.5 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Autopilot HDG now reads in whole degrees. The managed-mode auto-target (continuous great-circle bearing converted to magnetic) and the selected-mode setpoint (drag / scroll / programmatic seed from live state) both round to the nearest integer degree before display. Matches the real-world MCP convention — no more 91.5° on the readout. Rounding happens at storage time for setpoints and inside the display formatter for the auto-target, so the readout is always integer regardless of source.
v2.98.4 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • Right-click a tick bar to delete its stop. Same semantics as clicking the row's ✕ — the 2-stop floor is preserved (right-click is silently a no-op when only two stops remain, so you can't end up with an invalid palette). The native context menu is suppressed on tick bars only; right-click elsewhere in the editor still does whatever the browser does by default.
v2.98.3 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • Payout palette tick bars are now directly draggable. Each tick on the preview gradient is now a grab-target in its own right — mousedown and drag horizontally to reposition that stop without going back to the swatch row. The tick handle is rendered in the stop’s own color (white-outlined chevron above the gradient + white bar) so overlapping ticks remain distinguishable side-by-side rather than collapsing into one indistinct line. While dragging, the active tick lifts above any overlapping siblings via z-index and gains a gold outline + glow so you always know which one you’re moving. Sorting and persistence run only on mouseup — the dragged stop is tracked by reference (not array index) so passing through another stop’s % mid-drag doesn’t lose the handle. After release, rows re-sort and the legend / map / matrix repaint as usual.
  • Maximum stops raised from 5 to 10. The palette editor accepts up to 10 color stops now, persisted under the same simfly_aa_payout_palette localStorage key. Add stop remains the same; the cap just doubles.
v2.98.2 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • Pilot Payout palette — drag swatches onto the gradient bar to set their position. Each row's color swatch is now a draggable handle. Drop it anywhere on the preview gradient and the stop's % is set to that x-position (mapped 1:1 over the 0–100 % bar). After the drop, the rows in the editor auto-rearrange so they're always listed low → high by %. The preview gradient now uses an absolute-pct layout (each stop sits at its own % position on a 0–100 bar) and shows small white tick markers above the bar at each stop's location, so you can see where every stop currently lives. The native color picker still opens on click of a swatch — drag only fires when the mouse actually moves before release. The bar height was bumped slightly (26 px) for an easier drop target. Typed pct changes also re-render the row order so the list stays sorted.
v2.98.1 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • Autopilot panel now shows the target, not live telemetry. HDG / ALT / SPD on the AP strip are the values the autopilot is currently chasing — in Managed mode the auto-target (next-wpt bearing / VNAV alt / plan-baseline gs), in Selected mode the user setpoint. Live telemetry continues to show in the floating data panel where it always did. Makes the AP strip a true setpoint display rather than a slow-tracking mirror of the live state.
  • Alert modal — click-and-drag now scrubs alt and speed fields instead of dragging the card. All altitude inputs (Cruise Reached, Altitude crossing, conditional altitude conditions) and every conditional value field (IAS, GS, VS, distances, time thresholds) now use the AP-style drag gesture: mousedown and drag vertically to bump the value (up = increase). Linear 1 step per 4 px for the first 20 px, then 3× acceleration above. Steps are 100 ft on altitude fields (snapped to whole 100s, with FL/feet display preserved) and 1 unit on every other field. Mouseup without movement still focuses the input for typing. Card-drag reorder no longer hijacks the input — onmousedown and ondragstart are both stopped at the input boundary. Cursor switches to ns-resize over each wired input.
  • Demo headings are now MAGNETIC. Every user-facing heading in Flight Demo Mode — the AP panel HDG display and input, the runway-alignment picker (TO/LDG), and the runway-aligned departure seed — is now in magnetic reference, matching published runway numbers and chart headings. The integrator math keeps working in true bearing (because great-circle _demoBearing returns true) and the conversion happens at every I/O boundary via new _demoMagVar(lat,lon) / _demoMagToTrue() / _demoTrueToMag() helpers. The magnetic-variation model is a centred-dipole approximation of Earth's field (geomagnetic north pole 80.7°N / 72.7°W) — accurate to roughly ±5–10° at populated latitudes, which is enough for the demo's visual alignment with satellite scenery. Setpoints stored in mag frame; conversion to true happens once per tick when feeding the integrator.
v2.98.0 May 26, 2026 Feature
  • Demo autopilot — align with the actual runway on takeoff and landing. The AUTO heading now holds the dep-runway heading for the first 5 nm after takeoff and the arr-runway heading for the last 5 nm before landing, so the simulated track lines up with the satellite-scenery runway centerline instead of cornering directly toward the first/last waypoint. The runway end is picked by matching the airport's published runway headings against the actual bearing the route takes leaving / approaching the airport (the closest match wins). Waypoints that fall inside the 5-nm exclusion bubble are skipped so a SID or STAR fix near the airport doesn't pull the aircraft off the runway-extended centerline. Initial seed heading also comes from the dep runway, so the aircraft starts pointed correctly down the strip.
  • Map breadcrumb strip — one-click clear-all. When any filter is applied, a ⊗ chip appears at the front of the breadcrumb strip. Clicking it removes every applied filter (payout threshold, scenery rating, category filter, owner / country / region / continent / developer includes, all exclusion sets, pinned ICAOs) in one shot and returns the strip to its two-default state: color mode + scenery indicator. Toast confirms.
  • Alert altitude inputs — mouse-wheel scrub. Every altitude input in the Alerts modal (Cruise Reached, Altitude crossing, and altitude-type conditions inside Conditional alarms) now accepts mouse-wheel input. Each notch = ±100 ft; hold Shift for ±1000 ft. Snaps to the nearest whole 100 ft and preserves FL/feet display form (a value originally typed as FL350 stays in flight-level notation when scrolled).
  • Alert groups — checkbox + delete X moved to the right, matching standalone alerts. The group master toggle (enable/disable every member at once) and a new group-delete ✕ now sit on the right side of the group header in the same cluster pattern standalone alert cards use. The delete X removes the entire group AND every alarm in it in one click; a toast confirms how many alarms were removed. The Ungroup button still does what it always did (members become standalone alarms again).
  • Demo speed presets and slider range — bigger acceleration headroom. The three "main" speed buttons are now 1× / 3× / 5× (was 1×/2×/3×); the fine-tune slider now goes up to 25× (was 10×). The 0.25× and 0.5× slow-mo buttons are unchanged.
  • Pilot Payout — custom palette editor. Right-click on the payout gradient bar in the legend to open a palette editor. Place 2–5 color stops along the gradient at any percentages you want; each stop has a native color picker and a % position field. The change applies instantly to map dots, the matrix, the legend bar, and the gradient preview, and the palette persists to localStorage (key simfly_aa_payout_palette) so it survives across sessions. A Reset button restores the original five-stop default. The 0% no-data grey is fixed (not a user stop).
v2.97.26 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • ✈ Autopilot panel — drag the LCDs to scrub HDG / ALT / SPD. Click anywhere on a value display and drag vertically to bump the setpoint. Up = increase, down = decrease. Small moves make small changes (linear 1 step per 2–4 px); larger drags accelerate (each pixel beyond the first 20 counts 3×) so you can sweep across the full range without an endless drag. Releasing without movement still focuses the input for typing — the existing <Enter> commit path is unchanged. Cursor switches to ns-resize over each LCD so the affordance is visible.
  • HDG now uses the aviation 1–360 convention. 360 means north (not 0); 361 wraps to 1; −90 wraps to 270; 720 wraps to 360. The wrap formula is ((v − 1) mod 360 + 360) mod 360 + 1, exposed via _demoNormHdg360(). The integrator continues to use its 0–359 internal form; only the display + setpoint storage adopts 1–360. Input bounds updated to min=1 max=360.
  • ALT snaps to whole 100-ft increments. Dragging or typing 8016 settles at 8000; 8050 rounds up to 8100; 8049 rounds down to 8000. Snap happens inside _demoApSetAlt via Math.round(v / 100) * 100, then the cat-derived service ceiling clamps the upper bound. Input step changed from 500 to 100 so the browser's native ▲/▼ arrows also follow the convention.
  • SPD continues to clamp to the aircraft's category min/max — Cat 1 caps at 60–180 kt, Cat 6 at 180–520 kt, Cat 7 at 200–560 kt — whether the value comes from the drag, the input, the ▼/▲ steppers, or programmatic _demoApSetGs calls. Drag below or above the category band silently clamps to the band edge.
  • Verified: 7-case HDG wrap probe (1 / 360 / 0 / 361 / −90 / 720 / 90.5), 6-case ALT snap+clamp probe, 3-case SPD clamp probe (Cat 6 = 180–520), 9-case drag-math probe (HDG/ALT/SPD × small/medium/large) — every assertion passes. Cursor:ns-resize confirmed on the LCDs.
v2.97.25 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • 🎬 Demo modal — Load and Save button order swapped. 📂 Load Demo Flight Plan now comes before 💾 Save Demo Flight Plan, matching the natural workflow (you typically load an existing plan before saving a modified copy, and on first run you can only Load since Save needs something to save).
v2.97.24 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • Disabled state on modal action buttons is now unmistakable. Pre-v2.97.24 the .npc-btn class had no disabled styling at all — only the browser's default ~50% opacity, which on a filled accent-coloured button still read as "active". The Demo modal's Save / Start buttons were the visible offender. New rule covers every variant (.npc-btn-save, .npc-btn-cancel, .npc-btn-file): when disabled, the button drops to 40% opacity (50% in light mode), strips its filled background, replaces accent border/text with the muted gray-blue, and switches the cursor to not-allowed. Hover is also disabled so a stuck cursor on the button doesn't suggest interactivity. Both :disabled attribute and .disabled class selectors match, so HTML-attribute and JS-class gates both render correctly.
v2.97.23 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • 🎬 Save Demo Flight Plan now accepts a loaded demo file as source. Pre-v2.97.23 Save required a SimBrief plan since that was the only path that distilled into the demo file shape. But if the user had loaded a demo file from disk (no SimBrief), they couldn't re-save it under a new filename — even though the data was in memory and ready to write. _demoSavePlan now prefers _demoLoadedPlanRaw when present, falling back to _demoRawFromSbOfp(_sbOfpLastData) — same source-priority pattern _demoStart uses.
  • Save + Start buttons now share the same gate. Both enable whenever any plan is loaded (SimBrief OR demo file); both disable in the no-plan state with the same "Load a flight plan first" wording. When only a demo file is loaded the Save tooltip adds a note clarifying that the action re-saves that file (with an editable filename via the Save As dialog).
v2.97.22 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode modal — Start button gates on plan presence. Pre-v2.97.22 the ▶ Start Demo button was always enabled; clicking it with no flight plan loaded fired an error toast. Now the button is properly disabled (with adaptive tooltip) when there's no plan, matching the existing behaviour of the Save Demo Flight Plan button.
  • Distinct gating per axis. Save still requires a SimBrief plan (since that's what gets distilled into the file). Start accepts either a SimBrief plan OR a previously-loaded demo file from disk — same gate the underlying _demoStart function uses. Tooltips adapt: Start reads "Load a flight plan first to enable Start" when disabled, "Start the demo with the loaded flight plan" when enabled.
  • Toast message corrected. The fallback error toast inside _demoStart previously read "load or save a flight plan first" — misleading, since save doesn't help (save writes a plan to a file, it doesn't load one into the engine). Now reads "load a flight plan first" to match the tooltip wording.
v2.97.21 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • ⏱ ELAPSED menu — "Restart" reads as "Start" when the timer isn't running. The third button on the right-click menu always showed "↻ Restart", which implies an in-progress timer that you're resetting. From idle / armed / paused / stopped there's nothing to restart — you're starting the timer fresh. Label adapts to state: ↻ Restart only when state is running; ▶ Start otherwise. Tooltip adapts too: "Reset value to 0 and start counting from now" (running) vs. "Start the timer at 0 — counts up from now until landed or manually stopped" (everything else). Underlying action unchanged — always resets to 0 and runs from now.
v2.97.20 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • ⏱ ELAPSED no longer lost when loading the next leg's flight plan after landing. _liveAcMaybeClearForNewFlight (called when a new SimBrief plan loads and the aircraft is on the ground) was calling the full _afkResetTakeoff() — left over from pre-v2.97.4 when landing zeroed wheels-up to start fresh. With v2.97.4's armed-after-landing behaviour, the new-plan-load path then wiped the just-frozen flight time off the ELAPSED display. The user's expected flow — land → see flight time stay visible → load next leg's plan → flight time still visible → take off → counter reset to 0:00:00 — was broken by the second arrow.
  • New _afkResetForNewFlight() helper. Soft reset that clears takeoff-tracking state (_afkTakeoffAt, sustained-above-40 timer, landing candidate timer) but preserves the ELAPSED display when the field is in a state the user can reasonably expect to keep seeing — armed (just landed), paused or stopped (manual control). From idle or stale running, falls through to the full _afkResetTakeoff() so a leftover 1-hour-old takeoff timestamp doesn't keep ticking on the new flight's display.
  • Verified four scenarios: armed (1800 s frozen) survives plan load with frozen value intact → preserved; idle stays idle → unchanged; paused (300 s frozen) survives → preserved; stale running (1 hr old timestamp) gets the full reset → cleared to idle. Auto-takeoff from armed still drops the frozen value naturally on the next wheels-up event — same as before, just no longer pre-empted by the plan-load reset.
v2.97.19 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • ⏱ Telemetry fields no longer flash when clicking the ELAPSED Controls menu. Pre-v2.97.19 _afkElapsedMenuClick called _afkUpdateDataPanel(_liveAcLastData || {lat:0,lng:0,alt:0,gs:0}) to force an immediate panel refresh after the menu action. The fallback zeroed dummy meant that — with no live telemetry — every field on the data panel rendered using lat:0, lon:0, alt:0, gs:0 as if the aircraft were briefly parked at Null Island: LAT became 0.0000, ALT became 0 ft, PHASE became ground, etc. The next idle render-cycle reverted everything to —, producing a one-frame flash of all telemetry fields.
  • Extracted _afkRenderElapsedField() helper. The ELAPSED-rendering block moved out of _afkUpdateDataPanel into a dedicated function that updates only the #afkDpElapsed field's value + tooltip based on the current state machine. _afkUpdateDataPanel now calls the helper at the end (single source of truth, behaviour preserved for live telemetry). _afkElapsedMenuClick calls only the helper, so every other field stays untouched.
  • Verified: with the panel at em-dash for every field (no live telemetry) and a synthetic ELAPSED state transition (idle → running with a 30 s backdated takeoff → Pause), only afkDpElapsed changes (from — to 0:30); LAT / LON / ALT / GS / IAS / VS / PHASE / DIST TO DEST / TRK MILES REM / REMAINING / ETA fields all stay at — with no flash.
v2.97.18 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • ⏱ ELAPSED Controls popup now opens upward and stays on-screen. Two related issues fixed: (1) the popup used hardcoded approximate dimensions (w=200, h=180) for its viewport-clamp math, but the actual rendered menu is 190×199 px — so at the bottom of the screen the bottom edge spilled 15 px past the viewport. (2) The popup always placed at the cursor position and extended downward. Since the ELAPSED field is at the bottom of the data panel (at the bottom of the screen), the menu typically launched off the bottom of the viewport.
  • Now: render-hidden first, measure real offsetWidth/offsetHeight, then position with an upward preference. The menu tries to place itself above the cursor with an 8 px gap (so it appears over the data panel, not under it). Only when that would clip the top of the viewport does it fall back to placing below the cursor — preserving useful UX for any future caller that opens the menu from a top-of-screen anchor. Horizontal placement keeps the simple clamp into the viewport with 8 px padding.
  • Verified against five cursor positions (bottom centre / middle / top / bottom-right corner / bottom-left corner): every case stays fully on-screen, and four of the five place the menu above the cursor as preferred. The exception is "cursor near top" where there's no room above — correctly falls back to opening downward.
v2.97.17 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Region-draw ✕ Cancel button now actually cancels. The overlay button on STEP 1 / STEP 2 fought with the Leaflet map's mousedown handler: clicking Cancel let mousedown bubble through the overlay (which has pointer-events:auto) down to the map, which saw _rgPhase === 'idle' and immediately transitioned to dep_drag — creating a dep pin AT the click point on the overlay. The button's onclick then fired and called rgExploreReturn() which tore everything down... but the fight between the two left the user effectively stuck on STEP 1 with stray state. Same class of bug as the demo-bar slider issue from v2.97.7. Fix: onmousedown="event.stopPropagation()" + onclick="event.stopPropagation();_rgCancelDraw()" on the button itself, so the map handler never sees the event. Verified end-to-end: a synthetic mousedown on the Cancel button no longer reaches the map, and clicking transitions phase from idle → explore cleanly.
v2.97.16 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • Light-mode breadcrumb pills now readable on satellite imagery. Pre-v2.97.16 the map breadcrumb chips (Category Colors (N), Owners filter, etc.) in light mode used background:rgba(0,102,153,0.08) — 8% transparent teal that worked fine against the cream street-map tiles but vanished into dark satellite imagery. Brought into line with the other map overlay patterns (.rg-phase-overlay, .demo-floating, .rg-meas-floating) which all use ≥ 94% opacity cream with a drop shadow. The breadcrumb is now rgba(250,248,243,0.94) + border:rgba(0,102,153,0.50) + box-shadow:0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.22), so the pill reads as a lifted cream chip on any underlying tile.
v2.97.15 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • ⏱ ELAPSED right-click menu now sits above the map. Pre-v2.97.15 the menu (v2.97.4) used position:fixed with z-index:1100 — below the #regionModal map overlay at 9000, so right-clicking the ELAPSED field while the map was open opened the menu behind the map. Bumped to 9700 to match the sibling .afk-ctx-menu (alarm-card right-click) which already used the same value. Swept for the same class of bug across every position:fixed overlay: .afk-ctx-menu (9700), .afk-test-sound-menu (9800), #afkTripOverlay (9700), .sl-autocomplete (11000), .rg-owner-legend/panel (9500–9600) all correctly already above the map. Convention now documented in the CSS comment: position:fixed overlays that should appear over the map must be ≥ 9500.
v2.97.14 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • App-wide light-mode contrast pass — second sweep. v2.97.13 fixed the demo / autopilot / AFK group classes but the wider audit had been too narrow. A comprehensive sweep across every UI surface (map measurement controls, AFK Alert History panel, Test Sound popup, ELAPSED menu, quick-rate hint) surfaced 11 additional selectors that lacked a body.light-mode override and were near-invisible on the cream surface. Dark mode untouched.
  • Map measurement: ✕ close badge + route sub-label. The corner-of-label close badge on each saved measurement ring used color:#fff on a dark pill background with no light-mode rule, vanishing on cream. New override gives it dark text on a cream pill. Route-total sub-label (cumulative distance / leg count) similarly had rgba(255,255,255,0.78) with no override; now uses dark text in light mode.
  • AFK Alert History panel: full readability pass. Eight selectors (.afk-hist-row-title, .afk-hist-time, .afk-hist-pill + 5 colour variants, .afk-hist-section-body, plus the per-row 📌 Pin / ✕ Delete action buttons including their hover + active states) had pale-on-pale colour pairs that ranged from "uncomfortable to read" to "completely invisible" against cream. Each now has a darker variant in light mode — pills get distinct dark hues (slate, amber, teal, violet, navy) so the at-a-glance category is preserved.
  • AFK Test Sound popup. The chooser popup that appears when clicking 🔊 Test Sound used a dark-navy gradient background with no light-mode counterpart, so the entire popup floated as a dark slab on the cream surface. New override gives it a cream gradient with a teal border, and the row-hover background switches from cyan-on-dark to dark-cyan-on-cream.
  • ELAPSED right-click menu — button opacity bumped. v2.97.4 introduced a light-mode override at background:rgba(255,255,255,0.6) for the menu buttons. The 0.6 opacity blended into the cream surface; bumped to 0.85 with darker border (0.18 alpha vs 0.12) so the four control buttons (Pause / Stop / Restart / Reset) have proper structural visibility.
  • Quick-Rate hint text. The "right-click any airport dot to set a rating" instruction in the Quick-Rate popover was #a87800 light-mode — readable but uncomfortable. Darkened to #6a5200 and weight bumped to 600.
v2.97.13 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • Light-mode contrast pass — dark mode untouched. Audited every CSS rule with weak light-mode color / font-weight choices that would render too pale against the cream surface. 10 candidates surfaced; verified at source and visually via screenshot.
  • Demo Autopilot panel: ⌖ GO button now legible in light mode. Pre-v2.97.13 inherited dark-mode pale-pink text on pale-magenta tint, effectively invisible on the cream AP-column surface. New body.light-mode override uses dark purple text (#5a1e7a) on a darker magenta tint, matching the Selected mode button styling for visual consistency. Hover state also overridden so the contrast holds through interaction.
  • Demo floating bar & AP panel headers strengthened. .demo-floating-label (🎬 Demo) and .demo-ap-hdr-title (✈ AUTOPILOT) bumped from 85% opacity translucent magenta / navy to solid dark colors (#7a1a8a / #1a3050) with explicit font-weight:700. The labels are small (0.7–0.78 rem) and previously washed out against the cream surface.
  • .demo-ap-axis-label opacity bumped from 0.65 to 0.9. The HDG / ALT / SPD / DIRECT column captions are 0.62 rem with 0.18 em letter-spacing — that small letterspaced text needs more contrast in light mode than dark to read cleanly.
  • AFK Alerts: group-mode pills + Ungroup button now have light-mode overrides. Pre-v2.97.13 the CASCADE / CONCURRENT / SEQUENTIAL pill (data-mode variants) and the «Ungroup» button kept their dark-mode pale gold (#ffc870), pale violet (#d0a0ff), and pale orange (#ffaa55) text colors on cream backgrounds — near-invisible. New light-mode rules use dark amber (#6a3a00) for CONCURRENT, dark violet (#3a1a6a) for SEQUENTIAL, and dark orange (#8a3e00) for Ungroup on darker tinted backgrounds.
  • Verified no dark-mode regressions. All affected rules are scoped under body.light-mode; computed-style probe confirms dark-mode rendering is byte-for-byte identical to v2.97.12.
v2.97.12 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • App-wide validation hardening pass. Audited every JSON.parse, localStorage.getItem, fetch, parseInt/parseFloat, getElementById, prompt(), and .value call site outside the demo namespace. 14 candidates surfaced; 9 were false positives (already defended by existing try/catch + shape checks). 5 needed defensive work, all surgical no-disruption fixes.
  • SimBrief Pilot ID + SimToolkitPro token prompts now cap input length. Both prompts previously accepted arbitrary text — a careless paste of clipboard contents could persist a multi-kilobyte string into localStorage. Real PIDs are numeric and well under 32 chars; real STKP tokens are ~32-char hex. Caps set to 64 chars and 256 chars respectively (substantial headroom for any future format change) plus a trim() to strip stray whitespace.
  • STKP alt / gs NaN guard before marker render. parseInt('garbage', 10) yields NaN; without a guard the live-aircraft marker would store NaN altitude / ground speed in _liveAcLastData, poisoning the _afkPhase altitude buffer and VS computation downstream. lat / lng already bail in _liveAcRenderMarker's !isFinite guard, so only alt / gs needed the extra check.
  • _afkReadModalDuration null-guards DOM lookups. The function previously did document.getElementById(...).value with no null check. In practice the modal is always rendered when the function is called, but a programmatic caller (e.g. a future import path) could trip the lookup. Now uses optional access with sensible defaults (indefinite mode, user's saved seconds default) when either element is missing.
  • Nearest-METAR GeoJSON coordinates validated before destructuring. The aviation weather API returns geometry.coordinates as a [lon, lat] pair; a malformed response with a 0- or 1-element array would have destructured undefined values, propagating NaN through _haversineNm and producing an incorrect distance-based cache decision. Now validates array length and that both elements are finite numbers before reading.
v2.97.11 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — bug + validation pass (no FSUIPC regressions). Audit of the demo engine surface flagged five issues:
  • (1) Broken file-validation condition in _demoLoadPlanFile. Pre-v2.97.11 the check !Array.isArray(...) === false && !Array.isArray(...) evaluated to false for any input — the error toast for malformed waypoints was dead code. Replaced with clean two-stage validation: explicit dep / dest presence check, then waypoints-must-be-an-array check.
  • (2) Lat / lon range validation on demo file load. Pre-v2.97.11 any numeric value was accepted (so lat: 999 in a hand-edited file would load and produce nonsense great-circle math). Now rejects with a toast when dep/dest coords are out of |lat| ≤ 90 / |lon| ≤ 180 ranges. Per-waypoint filter also tightened from isFinite to range-checked.
  • (3) Telemetry-loss buttons retained .active state across demo runs. Clicking 📡✖ Stale or ⛓✖ Disconnect, then ✕ Exit, then starting a fresh demo: the buttons still appeared engaged even though _demoSuspendEmit was reset to false. _demoStop now also clears the .active class on both buttons.
  • (4) Scrub-while-suspended hid the marker. Scrubbing the slider while in Stale / Disconnect mode jumped the aircraft to the new position but kept the marker hidden, so the user couldn't see what they were scrubbing to. _demoScrubTo now clears _demoSuspendEmit + both button states, on the rationale that scrubbing is fundamentally a "show me the plan at this point" action that should resume visibility.
  • (5) DIRECT TO accepted index 0 (dep airport). The UI already excludes index 0 from the pulldown, but _demoApDirectTo only checked i ≥ 0 — a programmatic caller could send the aircraft flying back to the dep airport. Tightened to i ≥ 1 as defense in depth.
  • Regression check on real FSUIPC tracking: clean. Six sites outside the demo namespace were touched across v2.97.0–v2.97.10 (_liveAcStopOthers branch addition, _liveSrcIsFsuipcLike helper, _liveAcUpdateFollowBtn demo-button mirror, _liveAcTooltipHtml demo source, _afkResetTakeoff elapsed-state clear, _afkTakeoffTrack state-respect + arm-on-landing). Each change is either additive (new branch / new helper / new field) or a strict superset of the original behaviour. STKP correctly remains rejected for telemetry-loss alarms (regression test confirmed). FSUIPC takeoff / landing / staleness detection unaltered. The ELAPSED arm-on-landing is the explicitly requested behaviour change — previous flights' time now stays visible during taxi-in instead of blanking to —.
v2.97.10 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — Save Demo Flight Plan now uses a real Save As dialog. Pre-v2.97.10 the Save button fired a fixed-name download to the browser's default Downloads folder with no user input. Now uses the File System Access API's showSaveFilePicker on Chrome / Edge so the user gets a real OS Save As dialog — edit the filename, pick any folder, OS-level overwrite confirmation. The auto-generated name (SimFly_Demo_FlightPlan_<DEP>_<DEST>.json) is suggested but the user can change it to anything.
  • Graceful fallback for Firefox / Safari. Browsers without showSaveFilePicker get a prompt() with the suggested filename pre-filled — user can edit the name (location is still the default Downloads folder due to browser sandboxing). Filenames without a .json extension get one appended automatically. Both paths show a confirmation toast naming the final filename.
  • Cancel is silent. Dismissing either dialog (modern picker's Cancel button, or empty prompt) returns silently without falling through to the other path or showing an error toast.
v2.97.9 May 26, 2026 Feature
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — real per-waypoint VNAV from SimBrief altitudes. Pre-v2.97.9 the demo's Managed ALT mode used a single-triangle phase profile (one climb segment to cruise FL, one cruise level-off, one descent to dest) regardless of how the route was structured. The SimBrief OFP actually carries an altitude_feet field on every navlog fix — the planned cruise altitude at that point — that we were silently discarding. Now captured at OFP-import time and used as a real VNAV altitude target per leg.
  • VNAV path: linear interpolation by distance fraction between adjacent constrained fixes. The engine knows which leg the aircraft is on (via the same _demoNextWptIdx pointer LNAV uses), computes the fraction of the leg already traversed, and lerps between fromPt.altFt and toPt.altFt. So a SimBrief plan with TOC at FL120 over OLM, step-climb to FL360 by BTG, cruise across CONUS, TOD at MEMPHIS, descent into KJFK now produces a realistic step-climb + cruise + step-descent VNAV profile instead of a generic triangle.
