Aircraft still integrates toward those targets at category climb/descent FPM, so a Cat 1 Cub asked to climb to FL120 still takes the time it should.
  • Waypoint constraint backfill. Not every navlog fix gets an altitude from SimBrief — SID/STAR transitions sometimes leave it blank. _demoAnnotateVnav fills in any missing intermediate altFt by linear-interpolating along cumulative great-circle distance between the nearest specified neighbours, anchored on dep/dest field elevation. So the engine always has a target at every point on the route.
  • Dep / dest elevation now sourced from the OFP. Pre-v2.97.9 these came only from airportDB; the OFP's origin.elevation and destination.elevation are now preferred (more authoritative for some non-mainstream fields). Falls back to airportDB then to 0.
  • Save / Load Demo Flight Plan preserves VNAV. The JSON file format now stores altFt on each waypoint and elev on dep/dest. Older v1 demo plans (no altFt) load fine — the engine sees no VNAV constraints and falls back to the phase model, exactly as v2.97.5–v2.97.8 did.
  • Fallback to phase model when no VNAV data. If the loaded plan has no waypoint altitudes (e.g. a v1 demo file, or a hand-constructed plan), Managed ALT keeps using the existing single-triangle profile so the demo still flies a sensible vertical path. The transition is automatic and invisible — plan.hasVnav is set true only when at least one raw intermediate waypoint had a real altitude.
  • Speed VNAV not addressed. SimBrief doesn't expose a useful per-fix speed target, so Managed SPD continues to use the phase model (cruise speed during cruise, decel through descent and approach). The MCP knob is still there for hands-on speed control.
v2.97.8 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — AP mode toggle labels expanded. The MCP-abbreviated MNG / SEL button labels are now spelled out Managed / Selected — clearer for users not steeped in Airbus FCU jargon. text-transform:uppercase dropped (mixed-case reads better at small font sizes), letter-spacing relaxed from 0.16 em to 0.04 em so the longer words don't crowd the column.
v2.97.7 May 26, 2026 Fix
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — sliders no longer pan the map. Dragging the speed slider on the floating bar (and, less visibly, the scrub slider and any future input drag) panned the underlying Leaflet map instead of moving the thumb. Root cause: mousedown on the slider thumb bubbled down through the absolutely-positioned floating bar into the map container, where Leaflet's drag handler interpreted it as a pan-start. Fix: applied L.DomEvent.disableClickPropagation + disableScrollPropagation to #demoFloating and #demoApPanel on first demo start. Leaflet's helpers stopPropagation on the relevant pointer + wheel events at the container boundary, so every nested control (sliders, steppers, inputs, dropdowns, buttons) behaves correctly without per-control wiring. One-shot guarded; re-applying on a second demo run is a no-op.
v2.97.6 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — ✈ AUTOPILOT panel redesigned (compact + true MCP). v2.97.5's panel had 4 stacked rows per axis (label, value+stepper, SET+AUTO, state-line) and was anchored at a fixed bottom: 84px, which caused visible overlap with the floating control bar whenever the bar wrapped to a second row. Redesigned to mimic a real Airbus FCU / Boeing MCP: single LCD-style numeric window per axis with inline ◀/▶ steppers, one mode toggle below (MNG = managed/follow-plan ↔ SEL = selected/manual). LCD glows green for MNG, magenta for SEL — matching real FCU semantics. Editable display: click the number, type a new value, Enter to commit. Min column width dropped from 9 rem to 6.5 rem, padding tightened throughout. Panel total height roughly halved.
  • Dynamic positioning above the floating bar. New _demoApRepositionPanel() measures the floating bar's current offsetHeight and sets the AP panel's bottom = 20 + barHeight + 10 px. Called on toggle, on every 4 Hz bar update, and on viewport resize via the existing bar refresh. So however the floating bar wraps (one row on wide displays, two rows on mid, three rows on narrow), the AP panel always sits cleanly above it with a 10 px gap and never overlaps. No more "AP panel hides the Pause button" issue.
  • SET buttons removed — Enter commits. v2.97.5 had separate SET and AUTO buttons for each axis. Real MCPs don't have SET buttons; you turn a knob to dial in the value and push it to engage. We approximate: ◀/▶ steppers and direct typing both commit instantly (the steppers seed manual mode with the current live value first, so a single click does the intuitive thing). The single MNG/SEL toggle below each axis serves as both the engage and release control. Net result: each axis goes from 4 vertical elements down to 3.
  • State-line removed — LCD colour is the state. v2.97.5 had a separate sub-line per axis (AUTO — 142°, CAPTURE FL350) explaining the current state. Redundant once the LCD itself colour-codes the mode. The line is gone; column total height drops another ~14 px.
v2.97.5 May 26, 2026 Feature
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — ✈ AUTOPILOT panel for hands-on demo flying. New ✈ AP button on the floating control bar opens a compact MCP-style panel above the bar with four columns: HDG, ALT, SPD, and ⌖ DIRECT. Each axis carries a setpoint display, ▼/▲ steppers (10° / 1000 ft / 10 kt), a SET button that commits the entered value, and an AUTO button that releases the axis back to the plan baseline. DIRECT TO has a waypoint pulldown of every plan waypoint (excluding the dep airport) with a snazzy ⌖ GO button using the aviation direct-to glyph. Engaged setpoints glow magenta; AUTO axes show the current live value with a cyan tint.
  • Engine restructure: integrating model. Pre-v2.97.5 the engine was a pure function of simSec — _demoStateAt(t) looked up position/heading/alt/gs from the precomputed plan, which made hands-on control impossible (the next tick would always snap back to the plan). New per-tick integrator carries live aircraft state (lat, lon, hdg, alt, gs) and advances each tick by simulated dt seconds toward the target HDG / ALT / SPD. Plan-mode (AUTO) targets still come from _demoStateAt for alt/gs and from the next-wpt bearing for heading, so a demo with no autopilot input behaves identically to v2.97.4 — arcs, phase transitions, climb/cruise/descent profiles all unchanged. Once any axis is engaged, its target is the user setpoint until the user clicks AUTO or Restart.
  • Coordinated-turn heading integration. Heading advances toward target at the same standard-rate cap (3°/sec) and 25° bank as the precomputed arc engine, so the visual feel matches whether the aircraft is auto-tracking the plan or hand-flown via the HDG knob. Altitude and speed integrate at the aircraft's category-derived climb / descent / accel rates — a Cat 1 Cub asked to climb to FL180 won't get there in 30 seconds; an A320 asked for FL360 climbs at 2200 fpm just like the plan would have.
  • Per-category sanity limits on user setpoints. SPD and ALT inputs clamp to the selected aircraft's category: Cat 1 caps at 180 kt / 18 000 ft, Cat 7 at 560 kt / FL430. Heading wraps 0–359. So a "set speed to 500" on a Cub silently becomes "set to 180" rather than producing impossible physics.
  • Direct-to as a next-wpt index shift. DIRECT TO <wpt> sets the engine's next-wpt pointer to the chosen waypoint and clears any manual HDG; the heading AUTO logic then targets the bearing from current position to that waypoint. After reaching it, the plan resumes from that point automatically. No extra state to manage, no second LNAV mode to maintain.
  • Turn anticipation. The integrator advances the next-wpt pointer at the tangent-distance for the upcoming course change (R · tan(δ/2)) rather than waiting until the aircraft is on top of the waypoint. The arc onset matches what the precomputed track produced — the aircraft starts banking before the waypoint instead of overshooting it.
  • Scrub slider snaps back to plan baseline. Moving the scrub slider now resets all three AP axes to AUTO and snaps the live aircraft state to whatever the plan says it should be at that simSec. "Scrub to here" remains a pure plan-view action; any manual setpoints drop along with the position change.
v2.97.4 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • ⏱ ELAPSED — state machine + right-click controls + auto-arm on landing. Pre-v2.97.4 the data-panel ELAPSED field had only two states: not set (shown as —) and ticking from takeoff. Landing detection zeroed it to —, telemetry drops and demo-mode runs left it stuck ticking forever, and there was no manual way to pause, stop, restart, or reset. New five-state machine: idle / running / paused / stopped / armed, persisted to simfly_aa_afk_elapsed_state + simfly_aa_afk_elapsed_frozen so it survives reloads.
  • Auto-arm instead of auto-blank on landing. The existing 60-second-sustained-gs < 30 landing detector now calls _afkElapsedArmLanded() instead of _afkResetTakeoff(). The field freezes at the actual flight time (e.g. 1:32:47) and shows it in muted style with a "Last flight — armed, next takeoff resets to 0:00:00" tooltip. As soon as the next telemetry stream re-triggers wheels-up detection, the frozen value drops, ELAPSED restarts from zero, and ticks normally. So you can see how long your last leg took while waiting on the tarmac, AND the timer is ready for the next leg without any manual intervention.
  • Right-click the ELAPSED field for manual controls. Popover anchored at the cursor with four actions: ⏸ Pause (toggles to ▶ Resume when paused — value frozen visible, will resume from same value on next toggle); ⏹ Stop (value frozen visible, auto-start blocked until Restart or Reset); ↻ Restart (reset value to 0 and start counting from now, regardless of telemetry); ✕ Reset (clear to — and arm for the next detected takeoff). Disabled states adapt to current state — Pause is disabled when the field is idle/stopped/armed, Reset is disabled when already idle. Click outside or press Esc to close.
  • Takeoff detection now respects state. Auto-takeoff (the 58 kt single-tick path AND the 10-second-above-40 kt sustained path) only fires from idle or armed. From paused or stopped, the user controls the lifecycle — no surprise auto-resume on a stray telemetry blip. The armed→running transition resets the frozen value to zero as part of becoming live again.
  • Backward-compat migration on load. If _afkTakeoffAt was previously persisted but the new state key is missing (pre-v2.97.4 mid-flight reload), the load path promotes to running so the field continues ticking exactly as before. Existing flights aren't disturbed.
v2.97.3 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — 📍 Follow button on the floating control bar. Mirrors the 📍 Follow A/C behaviour available in the Live menu for real FSUIPC tracking: clicking the button toggles map-follows-aircraft mode for the synthetic demo flight. State is shared with the existing Live-menu button (both use the same _liveAcFollow global and the same _liveAcToggleFollow handler), so persistence across sessions, auto-disable on manual map drag, and the icon-picker shift-click are all inherited for free. _liveAcUpdateFollowBtn extended to mirror the active state onto both buttons whenever it fires — no separate sync plumbing in the demo engine.
v2.97.2 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — visibly smooth turns. v2.97.1's arc engine was correct in principle but produced visible "snaps" at waypoints in practice for two reasons. (1) The 45 % leg-fit rule was rejecting most arcs on dense SimBrief routes — SID / STAR transitions with 5–15 nm leg lengths can't fit a 5.5 nm cruise turn radius, so the engine fell back to a hard corner exactly where the user expects to see a curve. New shrink-to-fit rule: always emit an arc unless the absolute minimum radius (0.15 nm) can't fit either — the bank angle implicitly steepens for tighter turns, mirroring what a real FMS does when ATC instructs a tight intercept. (2) Heading was held constant per track sub-segment, so the icon stepped in 5° increments at each arc node boundary. _demoStateAt now linearly interpolates heading between adjacent track nodes (wrap-aware across the 0°/360° seam), so the icon rotation is continuous through the entire arc. Arc step density bumped from 5° to 2° so even the discrete heading samples are buttery smooth. Maximum per-tick heading delta dropped from ~5° to under 2.5° at the steepest spot.
  • Minimum-turn threshold lowered from 5° to 3°. A 4° course change with no arc still produces a visible kink at cruise scale; the lower threshold means a gentle airway-to-airway transition now also renders as a smooth curve.
  • New constants: _DEMO_MIN_RADIUS_NM = 0.15 and _DEMO_LEG_FIT_FRAC = 0.48. Pulled out of the build code so the limits can be tuned in one place. The 0.15 nm floor stops degenerate situations (180° back-track on a 0.5 nm leg) from emitting a pirouette that overshoots the route entirely.
v2.97.1 May 26, 2026 Improved
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — speed-based turn radii at waypoints. v2.97.0's engine snapped the marker bearing instantly at each waypoint, producing visibly fake corners. The route walker now expands every interior waypoint into a coordinated-turn arc using the standard R = V² / (g · tan(bank)) formula at 25° bank, with per-waypoint radius derived from the expected groundspeed at that point on the route — ~0.4 nm for a Light GA cruise pass, ~5.5 nm for jet cruise. The marker actually flies the arc (heading changes continuously, position follows the circle), not just rotates. Below 5° of deflection the arc is skipped (no visible turn anyway); turns too tight to fit on either adjacent leg fall back to a hard corner rather than overshooting the route.
  • Internal engine restructure: segments → track. Plan-build now produces a denser polyline that bakes the turn arcs into the geometry (arc step spacing 5°, minimum 4 nodes per arc), with cumulative distance and heading pre-computed per node. _demoStateAt walks the track in a single pass with no trig in the hot path beyond the inter-node great-circle interpolation it already did. Straight-line segments are still computed and stored as the fallback for degenerate two-point plans.
  • Demo modal z-index fix. v2.97.0's #demoModal inherited the default .modal-overlay z-index (1000), which sat below the region map's 9000 — opening the modal from the Live menu while the map was visible put it behind the map. Now explicitly set to 9500, matching fpSettingsModal and liveAcIconModal.
v2.97.0 May 26, 2026 Feature
  • 🎬 Flight Demo Mode — drive AA's telemetry & alerts from a synthetic flight. New 🔌 Live → 🎬 Flight Demo Mode menu item opens a configuration modal for running AA at sites where MSFS / FSUIPC is unavailable (corporate networks, kiosks, presentations). Pick an aircraft, supply a flight plan (Save the currently-loaded SimBrief plan to a JSON file, or Load one previously saved), and click Start.
  • Design principle: downstream systems can't tell it apart from FSUIPC. The synthetic engine emits packets through the same _liveAcRenderMarker chokepoint FSUIPC uses, at the same 1 Hz cadence, with the same field shape. AFK alerts, data panel, breadcrumb trail, follow-aircraft mode, stale-check, NEXT ALERT / ALERT ETA fields, mobile-alerts dispatch, and the Loss-of-Telemetry alarm all behave identically to a real flight. Two gating sites that previously hard-coded _liveAcSource === 'fsuipc' (the telemetry-loss eval and its _afkETA.compute branch) now run through a new _liveSrcIsFsuipcLike() helper that accepts both fsuipc and demo — so the floating bar's 📡✖ Stale button actually trips the CTD alarm in the demo, not just greys the icon. The tooltip source row reads DEMO when in demo mode (was previously ?).
  • Per-SimFly-category performance profiles. The engine synthesizes climb / cruise / descent from the selected aircraft's SimFly category (1 → 7), not per-airframe data. Cat 1 (Light GA) sits at 4,000 ft / 110 kt, Cat 5 (Bizjet) at FL350 / 380 kt, Cat 7 (Widebody) at FL380 / 470 kt. Routes shorter than the full climb + descent profile auto-scale the V to fit so the aircraft still reaches a sensible step-cruise altitude rather than spiking through it. Aircraft pulldown is sorted by category then alphabetically, displayed as Cat N — ICAO — Name.
  • Save / Load Demo Flight Plan to disk. Save distills the currently-loaded SimBrief flight plan to a compact JSON file containing departure, destination, and every waypoint (name + lat/lon). Load reads it back and renders the route immediately. Save is disabled until a SimBrief plan is loaded. File names follow SimFly_Demo_FlightPlan_<DEP>_<DEST>.json.
  • Floating playback control bar. While the demo is running, a control bar appears at the bottom of the map (modelled on the Measurement bar but anchored low instead of high). Carries ⏸ Pause / ▶ Play, ⏮ Restart, a scrub slider for jumping anywhere along the flight, current phase label, speed presets (0.25× / 0.5× / 1× / 2× / 3×) plus a free-slider for arbitrary 0.1× – 10× playback, and an elapsed / total time readout.
  • Two telemetry-loss simulation buttons. 📡✖ Stale stops emitting packets but keeps the marker on the map — after 30 s the icon greys out and any configured Loss-of-Telemetry alarms trip per their threshold, exactly as they would during a real FSUIPC stall. ⛓✖ Disconnect clears the live-aircraft marker entirely (mirrors a full FSUIPC drop). Both toggle on/off, so you can demonstrate the alarm tripping AND the recovery in one session.
  • Minimal kiosk lockdown. While the demo runs, the 🔌 FSUIPC Live, 🛩 STKP Live, and ✈ SimBrief Flight Plan buttons are disabled to prevent the marker getting yanked out from under the synthetic packets. Everything else — panning, airport tooltips, alerts panel, data panel, scenery library — stays fully interactive so you can walk an audience through any feature during playback.
v2.96.2 May 25, 2026 Improved
  • 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD) — pre-trip display gate lowered to a flat 10 s. v2.96.1 gated the countdown at min(30 s, thresholdSec * 0.5), which meant a 5-minute threshold sat blind for the first 30 s of any telemetry interruption before the panel and card lit up. New gate is a flat 10 s: FSUIPC pushes faster than 10 s during normal flight, so anything above 10 s is "telemetry has actually stopped" rather than a blip, and the readout now engages noticeably sooner. Sub-10 s pause-menu micro-trips still self-suppress so the panel doesn’t flash a value.
  • 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD) — Trip after minimum raised from 10 s to 30 s. With the 10 s display gate above, any threshold below 30 s would fire with little or no visible countdown — the alarm would trip before "trips in H:MM:SS" had a chance to mean anything. Enforced in five places: _afkETA.compute guard, _afkEvalTelemetryLoss guard, _afkSetTelemetryLossThreshold clamp, _afkSetTelemetryLossUnit seconds-mode clamp, and the card editor’s seconds-mode input min. Tooltip rewritten to explain why the floor is 30 s. Minutes mode unchanged (still ≥ 1 min).
  • Silent load-time migration for stored sub-30 s thresholds. _afkAlarms._load() now upgrades any persisted telemetry-loss alarm with thresholdSec < 30 to 30 on read. Pre-v2.96.2 alarms saved at the old 10 s minimum would otherwise be silently rejected by both eval and compute under the new floor (they’d never fire and never show a countdown). No toast or modal — the upgrade is invisible to the user and the alarm just keeps working at the new floor on first reload.
v2.96.1 May 25, 2026 Fix
  • 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD) — countdown now appears on the alarm card. v2.96.0’s card-side countdown silently never appeared. Root cause: _afkETA.compute had a !ac early-bail above the new telemetry-loss branch, and the card live-update path calls compute(a, null) — so the function returned NaN before it ever evaluated the staleness window. Moved the telemetry-loss branch up next to the timer branch (both are aircraft-state-independent — their inputs are wall-clock and FSUIPC fix staleness, not aircraft position), so the card subtitle now picks up the countdown as expected. Subtitle is rendered inside the always-visible card header row, so the countdown shows whether the card is collapsed or expanded.
  • 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD) — NEXT ALERT / ALERT ETA clear when telemetry is restored. v2.96.0 parked the threshold value (e.g. 5:00) on the telemetry panel any time conditions were met, so a healthy stream made the panel claim a CTD trip was always 5 minutes away. Added a staleness gate: _afkETA.compute only returns a finite ETA once staleSec exceeds min(30 s, thresholdSec * 0.5). Below the gate (normal telemetry or restored telemetry after a brief blip) the function returns NaN and both fields revert to — (or to the next-soonest alarm). Above the gate (telemetry has been quiet long enough to be a real interruption) the countdown ticks down to the trip moment. Restoration before the alarm fires — a fresh fix lands — instantly drops staleSec back near zero and the fields clear.
v2.96.0 May 25, 2026 Improved
  • 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD) — live grace countdown everywhere. The CTD canary now surfaces a live "trips in H:MM:SS" readout in three places: appended to its alarm card subtitle, in the telemetry panel’s NEXT ALERT field (when it’s the soonest-tripping armed alarm), and in ALERT ETA. With FSUIPC pushing fixes normally the value parks near the configured threshold (each fresh fix resets the staleness clock); during a real CTD it ticks down in real time, so you can see the exact moment the alarm is about to fire instead of just waiting and hoping. Same H:MM:SS format every other ETA-predictable alarm uses, so the panel layout stays steady.
  • _afkETA.compute now handles telemetry-loss. Returns thresholdSec − staleSec, gated by the same conditions as _afkEvalTelemetryLoss: FSUIPC must be the active source, the aircraft must be airborne (any phase except ground), and at least one fresh fix must have arrived under the current source session. On the ground, pre-flight, or with no live source, the function returns NaN and the countdown self-suppresses from every surface — no misleading "5:00 until trip" while parked at the gate.
  • 1 Hz card heartbeat extended. The existing 1 Hz setInterval that re-ticks visible Timer card subtitles now also ticks Loss-of-Telemetry cards (new _afkUpdateTelemetryLossCardLive helper), so the in-card countdown advances smoothly even when no FSUIPC fix is being processed and the user isn’t touching any input.
v2.95.0 May 25, 2026 Feature
  • ⛓ CONCURRENT & SEQUENTIAL groups — mode-aware dismissal. Pre-v2.95, dismissing a fired non-cascade group disabled every member — same as CASCADE. That defeated the intent: a group like [🧩 GS > 400] + [🧩 alt > FL350] would fire its composite alert once, then the individual sentries would all be silently disabled for the rest of the flight. Per user feedback, CONCURRENT and SEQUENTIAL group dismissals now re-arm every member instead of disabling them. Members keep their enabled toggle on, their transient state is reset (cruise state.fired clears, geofence wasInside re-seeds, etc.), and they can independently trip again later in the flight. The per-group _fired flag is what blocks the composite alert from re-firing — it stays set until the next ground transition, so you get one composite per group per mission, but the underlying alarms remain useful individually. CASCADE mode behaviour is unchanged (its whole point is "one fires, rest go silent").
  • Per-alarm state.everTripped flag. Set inside _afkAlarms.trip() on every trip event; cleared on ground transition. SEQUENTIAL group completion now reads everTripped OR the legacy sequentialMet OR current tripped status, so the persistent "this member tripped at some point" check is robust across the new dismiss-and-rearm flow. CONCURRENT completion still requires same-tick coincidence (every enabled member currently in tripped status); that's the defining distinction from SEQUENTIAL.
  • Two distinct dismiss toasts now. CASCADE shows the existing "⛓ "Approach Reminders" cascade: dismissed 3 sibling alerts (1 also-tripped + 2 still-armed). See Alert History." Non-cascade shows the new "⛓ "Approach Reminders" (concurrent) dismissed. 3 members re-armed — group will not re-fire this mission." So at a glance you know whether the cascade silenced alarms (cascade) or kept them productive (concurrent / sequential).
  • Help-modal CONCURRENT / SEQUENTIAL blocks rewritten to describe the new mode-aware dismissal behaviour and the re-trip allowance. Save / Load JSON files preserve mode unchanged (legacy and still reads as concurrent).
  • Resolves audit finding #3 (concurrent deadlock). The pre-v2.95 dismiss-disables-all behaviour combined with the eval-time sibling-tripped gate could leave a CONCURRENT group in a state where some members were tripped, some were disabled, and the completion check could never satisfy. The new re-arm-instead-of-disable path makes the deadlock impossible by construction.
v2.94.3 May 25, 2026 Improved
  • 🧩 Conditional — time hint labels h / m / s explicitly. v2.94.2 added the unit pulldown but the hint span next to the input still rendered via _afkFmtHMS — the colon format (1:30:22) — which leaves you guessing whether the leading number is hours or days. Switched to _afkFmtHMSLong which labels each component (1h 30m 22s) and skips leading zeros (90 seconds reads as 1m 30s, not 0h 1m 30s). The same format flows through the collapsed-card subtitle and the trip-overlay row via the shared _afkCondFmtValue helper, so the conditional alarm’s presentation is consistent everywhere a threshold is shown.
v2.94.2 May 25, 2026 Improved
  • 🧩 Conditional — time params get an explicit unit pulldown. Per user feedback that the Alert ETA textbox was confusing. Pre-v2.94.2 a time-based row (Alert ETA, Remaining time, Elapsed time) accepted four input formats: plain seconds (3600), M:SS, H:MM:SS, and Nh / Nm / Ns tokens (1h 30m 22s) — clever for power users, opaque for everyone else. The unit was always seconds with no visual cue. Now: a small seconds / minutes / hours pulldown sits between the value input and the hint span. Plain numbers are multiplied by the chosen unit’s seconds count, so typing “1.5” with unit=hours gives a 5,400-second threshold and the hint to the right reads "= 1h 30m 0s". The pulldown re-interprets the existing typed value on change — switching units updates the canonical threshold live. Sensible per-param defaults: Alert ETA defaults to seconds (typical 30–300 s), Remaining and Elapsed default to minutes (typical 30–300 min). The legacy H:MM:SS / 1h 30m composite formats still parse correctly (they bypass the unit), so pre-v2.94.2 saved files keep working without migration; old conditions without a valueUnit field default to seconds on render.
v2.94.1 May 25, 2026 Improved
  • Group-batched mobile push — group name only. v2.94.0 sent one composite push per non-cascade group completion (replacing N separate buzzes), but included a bullet-list roster of every member alarm. Per user feedback that’s noisy — the point of a group is that it’s ONE logical event. The push now reads "🔔 ⛓ Approach Reminders (concurrent) — 5 alerts tripped." with no member roster. The provider title still uses the group name so the phone surfaces it cleanly.
v2.94.0 May 25, 2026 Improved
  • Trip overlay — Escape now dismisses too. v2.88.0 added Enter as a dismiss shortcut. Escape is the universal “close modal” key — pilots juggling sim controls reach for it instinctively. Both keys now hit _afkDismissAll on the open trip overlay; cursor focus doesn’t matter (the handler is installed at capture phase on document).
  • Mobile push consolidated for group completions. Pre-v2.94 a CONCURRENT or SEQUENTIAL group with N members produced N separate phone notifications when it completed — one logical event, N buzzes. _afkOnTripped now splits newTrips into per-group buckets (for non-cascade groups) and a loose-alarms bucket. Loose alarms still push individually (existing behaviour); each group sends ONE composite push titled with the group name plus a bullet-list of every member that tripped together. CASCADE groups are unaffected (their members trip individually by design).
  • Cascade dismiss now surfaces a toast naming what it affected. Pre-v2.94, dismissing one member of a CASCADE group silently disabled the other N members — the Alert History captured it but you had to open the modal to find out. A one-shot toast (“⛓ "Approach Reminders" cascade: dismissed 3 sibling alerts (1 also-tripped + 2 still-armed). See Alert History.”) now fires when sibling alerts are affected, telling you exactly how many alarms were tripped vs. still-armed at the moment of the cascade. The toast only emits when siblings were actually affected; a one-member group or a dismiss-from-history of an already-cancelled alert doesn’t fire it.
  • ⏰ Timer pause/resume preserves the elapsed countdown. Toggling a countdown Timer off then back on used to restart the full duration from scratch (a 10-min countdown disabled at the 9-min mark would re-arm with 10 min remaining). Now: toggle-off captures state.pausedRemainingMs = targetMs − now; toggle-on consumes that value to compute the new targetMs. So a 10-min countdown disabled at 1 min remaining resumes with 1 min remaining. Clock-mode timers are unaffected (their target is absolute wall-clock time). Editing any timer param (mode, countdown digits, clock time) clears the paused-remaining-ms so the new params take effect on the next arm.
  • "Set duration" renamed to "Auto-dismiss after…". The Sound for dropdown’s second option was misleadingly labelled — it sounded like “how long the sound plays before stopping” but in fact the entire alarm is also dismissed and disabled at the same instant (same as a manual ✕ click). New label states what actually happens; the dropdown and input tooltips spell out the full behaviour. Three places renamed: per-alarm card editor, Geofence quick-add modal, Waypoint quick-add modal.
  • 🔊→All bulk-sound button. New Alerts-modal-footer button next to 🔊 Test Sound. Click opens a sound picker (same popup style as the test-sound chooser); the chosen sound applies to every alarm in the queue in one shot. Confirmation toast names the sound and how many alarms were actually updated (skipped if every alarm already used that sound). Saves the “edit 7 alarm cards one by one to set the same sound” clickfest.
  • 📜 Alert History — type + dismissal-reason filters. Two dropdowns added to the History modal toolbar: type (all 8 alert types) and reason (user / timer / group cascade / awaiting dismissal). Live-filter on change. Meta line updates to “showing N of M (filtered)” so the user knows the total count is still 100. Empty-filter-result state shows a hint pointing back to the unfiltered count.
  • Sound priority documentation expanded. The audit flagged that list-order-equals-priority wasn’t obvious. The help-modal Alerts block’s “Reorder & prioritize” paragraph now spells out the four implications: drag top-priority alarms to top, only one sound plays at a time, dismissing the leader hands audio to the next-highest, and list order also drives Save/Load file ordering and trip-overlay row order. The drag-handle tooltip already named this behaviour in v2.82.0; this update extends the long-form help.
v2.93.1 May 25, 2026 Improved
  • Post-reload airborne detection — threshold tuned. v2.93.0 required gs ≥ 80 kt AND alt ≥ 5,000 ft MSL to recognise “already airborne” on the first telemetry sample after a mid-flight browser reload. The altitude gate excluded low-altitude GA cruise (a Cub at 2,500 ft, helicopter sightseeing, bush flying) — those pilots would have to wait 10 s for the sustained-gs > 40 kt fallback. Altitude gate dropped; GS floor lowered to 58 kt. 58 kt clears the fastest realistic taxi (~30 kt; even an aggressive 50 kt high-speed taxi qualifies as “not airborne”) but lands well below the cruise speed of the slowest piston single in common use (Cub / Cessna 152 at 75–95 kt). Cruise & Descent alarms now arm immediately on the first valid sample after reload for the full speed range, jet cruise through Cub cruise.
v2.93.0 May 25, 2026 Fix
  • Cruise & Descent alarms now work after a mid-flight browser reload. Both alarms guard against high-elevation-airport false trips by requiring this session’s wheels-up event (10 s of sustained gs > 40 kt). Reloading the AA tab mid-flight cleared that flag, so a pilot who added a cruise alarm post-reload silently never got it — the guard saw _afkTakeoffAt = 0 and returned false on every tick. _afkTakeoffTrack now also recognises “already airborne” from the first valid telemetry sample (gs ≥ 80 kt AND alt ≥ 5,000 ft MSL) and back-dates the takeoff timestamp so the alarms arm immediately. Thresholds chosen to keep a fast taxi or high-elevation parking spot from triggering it.
  • Cruise@FL alarm fires immediately if armed at or above the target altitude. The threshold path required a crossing from below (prev < thresh && ac.alt ≥ thresh), which can never happen if you add the alarm while already cruising at-or-above the threshold — prevAlt seeds at current altitude and the crossing condition is never satisfied. New seed-tick check: if the first valid sample is at-or-above the threshold AND the phase detector says we’re in cruise, fire immediately instead of sitting silently armed forever.
  • Multi-waypoint alarms auto-rearm after dismiss instead of disabling. A Waypoint alarm with scope=all (every fix in the route) or scope=specific with > 1 fix picked used to fire on the first qualifying overflight, then auto-disable on dismiss — meaning “alert me at every waypoint” effectively meant “alert me at the first one then go silent.” The per-fix trippedFixes map was already preserved across re-arming; the dismiss path now skips the enabled:false step for standalone (non-group) waypoint alarms that still have un-tripped candidates ahead. Group-cascade dismiss still disables every member — the auto-rearm only applies to ungrouped waypoint alarms.
  • Geofence CPA fly-by fallback for tiny fences at speed. At 10 s STKP polling × 500 kt ground speed the aircraft moves ~1.4 nm between samples. A 0.5 nm fence flown at speed could be entirely overflown between two samples — the aircraft is OUTSIDE in both, wasInside never flips, and neither enter nor exit fires. New heuristic: if the previous-sample distance was within 2× the radius (safety band), the current distance is now further (recession), and the minimum-of-the-two was inside that safety band, treat as a virtual fly-by and trip the alarm. Catches the bullet-past case without false-tripping on aircraft that approach-but-don’t-enter.
  • 🧩 Conditional alarm — “awaiting data” diagnostic. Conditional alerts that reference parameters which currently have no value (e.g. Distance to destination with no SimBrief plan loaded, Distance to waypoint with no fix picked, Elapsed time before takeoff, Alert ETA with no upcoming alarms) used to silently never fire and the user had no way to know why. The alarm card now shows an inline yellow-tinted warning panel listing each blocking condition and the reason it can’t be evaluated (“no live telemetry”, “no SimBrief plan”, “no waypoint picked”, “on ground (no takeoff)”, “awaiting data”). Re-evaluates every time the alarm list re-renders, so the warning disappears once the missing data shows up.
  • Multi-tab localStorage sync. Opening AA in two browser tabs (intentionally for monitoring, or accidentally) silently corrupted state: tab B’s changes persisted, tab A kept its stale snapshot, then tab A’s next save() overwrote everything B had done. A new window.addEventListener('storage', …) handler listens for changes to the three alarm-related localStorage keys (alarms, groups, history), reloads in-memory state from disk, and re-renders the alarm list / map overlays / badges / trip overlay. Coalesced with a 250 ms debounce so rapid bursts (drag-reorder, batch updates) don’t cause render storms. Brief toast (“🔁 Synced changes from another AA tab”) on each sync so the user knows what happened.
v2.92.4 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • Group MODE preserved across browser reload. Same shape as the v2.92.2 file-import bug, on a different persistence path. _afkGroups._load() only cherry-picked {id, name, collapsed} from the saved registry even though _save() serialised the full group object (including mode and _fired). Net effect pre-v2.92.4: refreshing the browser silently dropped SEQUENTIAL / CONCURRENT mode back to CASCADE. _load() now also reads mode (normalising legacy and → concurrent) and the per-group _fired flag — so reloading mid-flight preserves both the user’s chosen mode and the “this group has already fired this mission” gate. Without the _fired half, a fired sequential group could re-fire on the first tick after reload (member-side state.sequentialMet IS preserved on reload, so the completion check would re-pass).
v2.92.3 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • 🧩 Conditional alert — new condition rows default to DISABLED. Companion to v2.92.1’s empty-conditions default. When the user clicks + Add condition, the new row lands with its checkbox off. Same defensive rationale: a user building a conditional alert mid-flight could otherwise add a row, find the default threshold (GS > 200 kt) already satisfied by current telemetry, and watch the alarm fire before they could tune the value. Now the workflow is: add row → set parameter / comparator / threshold → toggle the checkbox on when ready. The empty-state hint and the per-row checkbox tooltip both call out the new behaviour so it doesn’t come across as broken.
v2.92.2 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • ⛓ Group MODE is now preserved across Save / Load. Critical regression found during an audit pass: _afkGroups._injectFromImport() only copied name and collapsed from the saved group record — the mode field (cascade / concurrent / sequential) was silently dropped on import. Any saved CONCURRENT or SEQUENTIAL group reloaded as CASCADE, completely changing the alert behaviour. Bug introduced in v2.91.0 (when group modes were added) and made more visible in v2.92.0 (when SEQUENTIAL was added). Both the export side (groups now serialise mode as a first-class field) and import side (passes mode through to _injectFromImport, which now applies it via setMode) are fixed.
  • Exported JSON no longer contains the transient _fired group flag. v2.92.0’s per-group fired-this-flight flag was being JSON-serialised because the export grabbed the live group object by reference. It was harmless (import didn’t read it) but polluted the file and could confuse a human reading the JSON. Export now constructs a clean group record with just { id, name, collapsed, mode }.
  • Post-import GC. Defensive: if any imported alarm gets filtered out (e.g. unrecognised type from a future file format) and its group ends up with fewer than 2 members, the group record now gets cleaned up rather than persisting as a ghost. Pre-import _gc only cleaned up the wiped current state; the new post-import call covers the imported state too.
  • Audit pass. Verified that all 8 alert types (Timer, Geofence, Waypoint, Cruise Reached, Descent Started, Altitude, Loss of Telemetry, Conditional) preserve their full params on save/load, and that transient state (state.fired, state.prevAlt, state.wasInside, state.trippedFixes, state.sourceArmedAt, state.sequentialMet, status, trippedAt) all reset to fresh per-type defaults via _afkResetTransientState. Timer’s absolute targetMs recomputes from params on load so a saved “30-min countdown” reloads as “30 min from now”. Conditional with empty conditions[] round-trips correctly and the eval bails. Saved-while-tripped never re-fires on load (status forced to 'armed'). Backwards-compat: missing cruiseMode / thresholdUnit / pre-v2.92 group mode all fall through to safe defaults.
v2.92.1 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • 🧩 Conditional alarms now start with an empty conditions list. The v2.91.0 default seeded "Altitude > 10,000 ft" as an example row — convenient, but if you happened to already be above 10,000 ft when adding the alarm, the first eval tick after enabling tripped it immediately. Empty is the safe default; the eval bails when there are no enabled conditions, and the editor now shows a yellow-tinted hint row reading "No conditions yet — click + Add condition below to set up a trigger. The alarm will not fire until at least one enabled condition is in place." Short-description on the card also distinguishes "no conditions configured" from "configured but none enabled" so the state is clear from a collapsed card too.
v2.92.0 May 24, 2026 Feature
  • ⛓ Alert Groups — three explicit modes. The v2.91.0 binary CASCADE / AND replaced with a three-way cycle on the group header pill (click to advance):
    • CASCADE — default. First member to trip fires; rest auto-disable. (Unchanged.)
    • CONCURRENT — renamed from AND for clarity. Every member must be in tripped status at the same moment for the group to fire. A drop-out disarms the group.
    • SEQUENTIAL — new. Each member's first trip is silently noted and persists for the rest of the flight (even if the member is later dismissed individually). Group fires when every enabled member has tripped at any point this mission — no simultaneity required.
  • ✓ pip on tripped-but-suppressed cards. When a member of a CONCURRENT or SEQUENTIAL group has tripped but the group hasn't completed yet, the member's card shows a small green ✓ next to the alert name — so you can see progress toward group completion without opening any modal. Pip clears when the group fires (and a normal trip overlay takes over) or on the next ground transition.
  • Backwards-compat. v2.91.0 saved files with mode: "and" read back as "concurrent"; persistence updates the field to the new name on the next save. CASCADE / non-grouped alarms behave identically to v2.91.0.
  • Persistence semantics. Sequential's per-member state.sequentialMet flag and the per-group _fired flag are both cleared on the next ground transition, matching the existing one-shot rearm pattern used by Cruise Reached / Descent Started / Altitude crossing / CTD / Conditional.
v2.91.0 May 24, 2026 Feature
  • New alert type: 🧩 Conditional. Fire when a custom combination of live telemetry parameters is met. Each alarm carries one or more condition rows; each row picks a parameter + comparator (> or <) + threshold. The combinator chooses AND (every enabled row must hold) or OR (any row fires). Ten parameters: Altitude (MSL), IAS, GS, VS (signed fpm), Distance to destination, Distance to waypoint (with waypoint picker), Track miles remaining, Remaining time, Elapsed time, Alert ETA. Time fields accept seconds, H:MM:SS, or H/M/S tokens. Per-row checkboxes disable conditions without removing them. One-shot per flight; auto-rearms on next ground transition. Example use: "alert me when GS > 400 kt AND altitude > FL350 AND elapsed > 1h 15m".
  • ⛓ Alert Groups — new AND mode. A pill on the group header toggles between CASCADE (default, pre-v2.91.0 behaviour — first member fires and disables the rest) and AND (every member must reach a tripped state simultaneously before the group fires its single composite alert; individual member trips are silent while the group is incomplete; one drop-out disarms). Best for compound milestones — e.g. [Cruise Reached] + [Conditional: GS > 400 AND alt > FL350] fires only when both hold at once. AND mode skips the cascade sibling-disable so every member can keep evaluating.
  • 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD) — seconds unit support. The threshold now has a minutes / seconds unit picker. Use seconds (down to 10 s) when you want fast detection of a stuck telemetry stream — e.g. a 15 s threshold for a CTD canary tuned tighter than the default 5 min pause-menu tolerance. Note the 15 s watchdog interval still sets a floor on actual detection latency (a 15 s threshold fires somewhere between 15 s and 30 s after stream silence). Eval floor lowered from 30 s to 10 s.
  • Region matrix — diagonal line now extends corner-to-corner. The CSS-gradient stripe that the corner cell used had its ends fall on the cell EDGES (not corners) for non-square cells. Switched to an inline SVG line that goes exactly from top-left corner to bottom-right corner regardless of aspect ratio. Shared between the main matrix and Region Mode matrix.
  • Region matrix corner cell — ARR text moved up 15 px. Combined with the new corner-to-corner diagonal, the bigger Region Mode cell layout was landing ARR uncomfortably close to the line. Region Mode only; main matrix cell unchanged.
v2.90.11 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Alerts modal — "Sound for" dropdown wording. The second option in the Sound for picker (per-alarm card + Geofence quick-add + Waypoint quick-add) reads Set duration instead of the previous Specific time…. The new label more accurately describes what picking the option lets you do (choose how long the sound runs before auto-dismiss).
  • Region matrix — corner cell 1:1 ARR / DEP labels less crowded. The diagonal-split corner cell shared between the main distance matrix and the Region Mode matrix looked tight in Region Mode because the default .rg-matrix th padding (0.42rem 0.75rem) squeezed the inner .matrix-corner-diag div, pulling the ARR / DEP labels visually closer to the diagonal line. Region Mode’s corner cell now overrides padding to 0.15rem 0.2rem — matching the main matrix’s .dist-corner-cell — so the labels get the same breathing room around the diagonal. No change to the shared diagonal split layout itself.
v2.90.10 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Mobile Alerts — non-Latin-1 input is now prevented at the keystroke, not just sanitized at send. The v2.90.9 fix sanitized headers right before fetch(), which prevented the crash but allowed problematic characters to be stored silently and quietly dropped from outgoing pushes. v2.90.10 catches them at input time on the three surfaces that feed HTTP headers (or could in future): the per-alert name field on every alarm card, the group name field on every group header, and the ntfy Title field in Mobile Alerts settings. As you type, the common typographic Unicode (em/en dashes, curly quotes, ellipsis) is transliterated to ASCII equivalents on the fly; anything outside Latin-1 (CJK, emoji, RTL scripts) is dropped before it ever lands in localStorage. Caret position is preserved as best as possible. Tooltips on the affected inputs and the in-app Help → Mobile Alerts → ntfy entry now explain the restriction so the silent character disappearance isn’t surprising. Blur-time backstop also runs the same cleaner so any programmatic textContent edit gets normalized too.
v2.90.9 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • Mobile Alerts fix — "String contains non ISO-8859-1 code point" error on outgoing pushes. The v2.88.0 alert-type-to-title feature appended " — " (em dash, U+2014) between the configured title and the alarm type. HTTP/1.1 header values must be ISO-8859-1 (code points ≤ 0xFF), and the em dash is outside that range, so fetch() threw a TypeError before the request ever left the page — visible to the user as a soft toast "Mobile alert failed (Geofence): ... String contains non ISO-8859-1 code point". The in-tab sound + overlay still worked; only the phone push silently failed. Two-layer fix: (1) separator changed to ASCII " - " so the typical case never goes through the encoder, and (2) new _maToLatin1Safe() + _maSanitizeHeaders() defensively transliterate the common typographic Unicode (em/en dashes, curly quotes, ellipsis) back to ASCII and drop anything still > 0xFF from every header value before the fetch — so user-typed titles or per-alert names containing accented letters / non-Latin scripts no longer break the push either.
v2.90.8 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • Console errors gone when moving the mouse across the main page. Two document-level tooltip-delegation handlers — the weather-dot tooltip in _wxInitTooltip and the main matrix-tooltip delegation in _calcDistance — called e.target.closest() without checking that e.target is an Element. When the cursor moves directly over text content, browsers fire mouseenter/mouseleave/mouseover/mouseout events with the Text node as the target — and Text nodes don’t have a closest() method. Result: TypeError: e.target.closest is not a function emitted on every mouse move until the cursor hit an actual Element. Behaviour was visually unaffected (the throw caused the handler to bail, which is what an early return would have done anyway), but the console noise was distracting and made real errors harder to spot. New _wxClosest() helper guards the weather handlers; inline typeof t.closest === 'function' guards the matrix-tooltip ones. Three other document/scoped tooltip handlers (scenery star clicks, region-matrix tooltip delegation inside rgMatrixWrap) use the same pattern but are bounded to areas where Text-node targets aren’t observed in practice; left untouched to avoid speculative changes.
v2.90.7 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Help text — step-climb handling for Cruise Reached. The Cruise Reached help-feature block now (a) reflects the v2.90.5 Mode dropdown (Adaptive vs Specific altitude) instead of the obsolete "leave the field blank" wording, and (b) describes the canonical step-climb workflow: add one Cruise Reached alarm per level-off altitude in At specific altitude mode (e.g. FL210, FL280, FL380), each fires independently as the aircraft climbs through its threshold, dismissing one auto-disables only that one so the rest stay armed for subsequent steps. Suggests combining with per-alert names ("Initial cruise — FL210", "Step climb 1 — FL280", etc.) so they're distinguishable in the trip overlay, the Alert History log, and on map pins. Also notes the related quirk: Adaptive mode can’t self-trigger if you arm the alarm while already cruising, since the phase detector requires evidence of a climb in the current session before promoting to cruise.
v2.90.6 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • ⏰ Timer alarms now always default to a 10-minute countdown. Pre-v2.90.6 (v2.74.0+) new Timer alarms pre-filled with whatever values the user had last typed — saved to localStorage under simfly_aa_afk_timer_last. That surprise-behaviour is gone: every newly-added Timer alarm starts at Countdown · 0 h 10 m 0 s regardless of history. The localStorage key is proactively cleared at module load so any stale value from a prior version is wiped. Load from File is unaffected — alarms imported from a saved JSON file still honor whatever timer values were saved in the file, since that path bypasses the “new alarm” defaults entirely.
v2.90.5 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • ─ Cruise Reached: explicit Adaptive vs Specific altitude mode selector. Pre-v2.90.5 the Cruise alarm had a single text input with a grey "10000 or FL350" placeholder. Leaving it blank ran the adaptive phase-detector path; typing a value switched to a climbing-through-altitude trigger. The placeholder looked like real data, so users often set the alarm expecting target-altitude behaviour without realising they were in adaptive mode. Now: a Mode dropdown picks between Adaptive — fire on detected cruise transition (the default for new alarms) and At specific altitude — fire when climbing through. The altitude input row is only rendered in Specific mode, so the form never shows an ambiguous empty input. Adaptive mode also gets a one-line caption explaining what the phase detector does. Backwards-compat: existing alarms get the right mode inferred from their saved altitude (> 0 = Specific, else Adaptive). New persistent param `cruiseMode`; both _afkSetCruiseAlt and the new _afkSetCruiseMode setter keep it in sync.
v2.90.4 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • 🛠 Tools menu green-dot indicator now reflects only what's actually in the Tools menu. Pre-v2.90.4 the dot lit when a SimBrief flight plan was loaded — but the SimBrief Flight Plan entry moved out of Tools and into the 🔌 Live menu in v2.89.0, so the dot was lighting up for a menu item that no longer lived there. Tools dot now lights only when Measurement mode is active OR the region matrix is visible (the two items actually in the Tools menu). The plan-loaded indicator moved to the Live menu's dot where SimBrief Flight Plan now lives, so the visual still tells you something useful — just on the right menu. Wired into `_rgUpdatePhaseText` so the Tools dot tracks every region-mode phase transition without those callers needing to know about the pulldown.
v2.90.3 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD) — STKP references stripped from help, tooltips, and editor copy. The v2.90.0 surfaces around the new CTD alert all called out STKP as a "not eligible" source (alongside “no live source”). Since STKP isn’t being further developed, those mentions are gone — the alert’s app-help feature block, the alert-icon tooltip, the editor footer hint, and the eval’s code comments now reference only “FSUIPC” and “no live source”. Pre-existing STKP integration code elsewhere in the app (the hidden STKP Live button, the SimToolkitPro fetch module, the source-label code) is unchanged.
v2.90.2 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD) — fixed two false-fire paths found during post-ship audit.
  • Toggle-cycle false fire (FSUIPC off → on mid-flight). When the user turned FSUIPC off and then back on, the v2.90.0 eval's fallback clock used alarm.state.sourceArmedAt (the moment the alarm armed — potentially hours ago), so the very first watchdog tick after re-enabling FSUIPC saw a huge staleness window and tripped immediately. The eval now requires a fresh fix to have arrived under the current FSUIPC session before staleness is meaningful (_liveAcLastUpdate > 0). No fix yet → no fire. The alarm can only credibly "lose" telemetry after it has had it.
  • Sleep/wake false fire (laptop lid close, OS suspend, hibernate). setInterval silently pauses while the OS is suspended, but performance.now() does not — so on resume the next watchdog tick saw a multi-minute gap since the last fix (the entire suspend duration) and tripped before FSUIPC could reconnect. The watchdog now compares this tick's performance.now() to the previous tick's. A gap > 60 s = the system was suspended; install a 60-second post-wake grace window during which the eval skips entirely. FSUIPC's 15-second silent retry + ~1-second fix-arrival delay fits comfortably inside that window, so a real fresh fix lands well before the watchdog is allowed to fire again.
v2.90.1 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD) now defaults to Klaxon. CTD is a genuine emergency cue — the loudest, most attention-grabbing sound in the library is the right default, not the gentle Digital Clock. Existing telemetry-loss alarms keep whatever sound they were already set to; only newly-added ones get Klaxon. Switch sound per-alarm via the dropdown on the card as usual.
v2.90.0 May 24, 2026 Feature
  • New alert type: 📡 Loss of Telemetry (CTD). A "did MSFS just crash?" canary. Fires when all three conditions hold: FSUIPC Live tracking is ON, the aircraft is airborne (any phase except ground), AND no telemetry update has arrived for the configured timeout. Default 5 minutes — sized generously to tolerate normal MSFS pause-menu trips (opening Map / ATC / Settings, which freezes FSUIPC pushes, will not false-alarm). Single setting: Trip after N minutes (1–60). Catches MSFS crash-to-desktop, sim freezes / hangs, FSUIPC server crashes, accidental FSUIPC closures mid-flight — situations where the green aircraft icon would otherwise just go silently stale. Re-arms automatically on the next ground transition for the next flight.
  • Independent watchdog — the regular alarm tick runs inside the live-aircraft fix-arrival path, so a real CTD freezes the very loop that could detect it. This alert is driven by a separate 15-second interval timer that keeps running independent of fix arrivals, so it can still fire when the telemetry stream itself is the thing that went silent. The watchdog evaluates only telemetry-loss alarms (no-op when none exist) so the overhead is one negligible Array.filter every 15 s.
  • Alphabetical palette position. Slots into the Add Alert sidebar between Geofence and Timer (alphabetical order maintained).
v2.89.2 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • Live menu — removed the duplicate "Clear Flight Plan" row. The v2.89.0 reorg added a dedicated ✕ Clear Flight Plan item below the separator without realizing the ✈ SimBrief Flight Plan item above already toggles between Load and Clear (via _sbOfpButtonClick): with no plan loaded it fetches, with a plan loaded it clears. The duplicate is gone; the SimBrief Flight Plan row remains the single load/clear control. ✕ Clear Aircraft Track stays — there's no load counterpart for the breadcrumb trail.
v2.89.1 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • Telemetry-panel hover tooltips no longer flash at 1 s intervals. The v2.88.0 hover tooltips on Dist to Dest, Trk Miles Rem, Alert ETA, Elapsed, and Next Alert were native browser title-attribute tooltips. The data panel re-renders every tick (~1 Hz), and writing the `title` attribute — even with the same value — dismisses the active native tooltip, producing a visible flash on every tick. The new _afkSetTitleSafe() setter (used by every tick-frequency title update in the data panel) (a) no-ops when the desired title is byte-identical to the current one and (b) skips the write while the cursor is hovering the target. The tooltip stays stable for the duration of the hover; the next mouseleave/mouseenter cycle picks up whatever the most-recent tick produced.
v2.89.0 May 24, 2026 Feature
  • Per-alert names. Every alert can now be named (up to 30 characters) just like groups can — click the editable name slot at the top of any alert card and type. The name flows through to the Alert History log, the Next Alert telemetry panel, plotted alert pins on the map, and Save/Load files. Geofence labels share the same field so renaming one place updates everywhere. Group names also gained the 30-char cap with the same live-typing enforcement.
  • Telemetry labels — Alarm → Alert. Renamed NEXT ALARM to NEXT ALERT and IN to ALERT ETA to match the rest of the UI's "Alert" terminology pass.
  • Add Alert palette — alphabetized, renamed. Sidebar title changed from "Add alarm" to "Add Alert" and the type buttons are now in alphabetical order: Altitude, Cruise Reached, Descent Started, Geofence, Timer, Waypoint.
  • Armed counter moved to the Alerts modal title bar. Was at the bottom-left of the footer; now sits next to the modal title. Frees up footer space so the button row never wraps. Pluralization is now proper ("1 alert armed" / "All 3 alerts armed" / "2 of 5 alerts armed") — no more lazy "alarm(s)".
  • Alerts modal footer reordered. Now: [Test Sound] [Alert History] [Group X (when active)] | [Save] [Load] | [Enable All] [Disable All] [Delete All]. Enable All moves next to its destructive siblings because they share the "bulk state flip" semantics; Save/Load cluster reads as a distinct group between them.
  • Drag-to-add — bigger, clearer drop zones. The drop targets at the top and bottom of the alerts list now appear during an Add-from-menu drag (was reorder-only), expand to 44 px tall, and self-label "Drop at top" / "Drop at bottom" so it's obvious where the new alert will land. The existing top drop spot is now a first-class, visible insertion target.
  • Drag-to-add — fixed name-field paste bug. Dropping a draggable Add Alert button onto an existing alert/group name field used to insert the literal "add:alarmtype" text. The drag now uses a custom MIME type instead of text/plain, and editable name spans block drop entirely, so the bug is gone.
  • SimBrief Flight Plan moved to the Live menu. Now the first item under 🔌 Live (above FSUIPC Live). The Tools menu keeps Select Regions + Measurement only. Reflects how SimBrief data informs the live-flight surface (route line, end rings, waypoint markers, Trk Miles Rem, ETA, waypoint alarms).
  • Live menu — Clear items disabled when nothing to clear. Clear Flight Plan greys out when no SimBrief plan is loaded; Clear Aircraft Track greys out when no breadcrumb points exist. Hovering shows a short "No … currently loaded" tooltip. Avoids the dead-click surprise of an enabled button that does nothing.
v2.88.0 May 24, 2026 Feature
  • Alarm modal — Enter dismisses. Pressing Enter while the full-screen ALARM overlay is on screen now triggers DISMISS ALL, matching the visual "single primary action" of the modal. A small hint sits below the button so the shortcut is discoverable.
  • Alerts modal — Enable All button. A new Enable All button (paired with the existing Disable All) re-arms every configured alarm in one click, including rescheduling timer countdowns. Useful for re-arming after a Disable All or after importing a saved alarm set.
  • Alerts modal — armed-count text 40% larger. The footer status ("n of m alarm(s) armed") is now visible across the room and wraps gracefully so the new Enable All button doesn't crowd it.
  • Test Sound — sound picker. Shift-click (or right-click) the 🔊 Test Sound button to open a popup menu listing every built-in sound — pick one directly instead of cycling through them. Plain click still cycles as before.
  • Alerts list — drag-from-menu to insert. Every type in the left-hand Add alarm column is now draggable. Drop one onto the alarms list to add it at a specific position (between two existing alarms, at the very top, or at the very bottom) instead of always appending at the end. Click still works for append-and-done.
  • Alert History — flight-progress telemetry. Each logged alert now captures Elapsed (time since takeoff), Remaining (time to destination), Dist to Dest, and Trk Miles Rem alongside the existing position / speed / altitude. For indefinite-duration alarms, the dismissal pill now shows how long the alarm sat unacknowledged (e.g. "user dismissed · 14:32:11 · ack after 2m 17s").
  • Telemetry panel — hover tooltips. Hover any of the following fields for extra context: NEXT ALARM (full alarm configuration), DIST TO DEST / TRK MILES REM (km equivalent), IN (predicted trip time in local + Zulu), ELAPSED (takeoff time in local + Zulu).
  • FSUIPC — adaptive push cadence. Push rate is now 500 ms on the ground (taxi detail) and 1000 ms in flight (eases render load). The cadence switches automatically on phase change and the toast no longer advertises a fixed number.
  • FSUIPC — silent 15 s retry. Lost connections retry in the background every 15 s without spamming a toast on each attempt. The very first failure still surfaces a clear toast so the user knows tracking didn't start.
  • Aircraft track — collapse stationary points. While GS=0, the breadcrumb trail now captures a single "I stopped here" point instead of accumulating thousands of identical samples. Resumes high-detail capture the moment motion is detected.
  • Mobile Alerts — provider clarity. Every provider except ntfy.sh now displays as "(beta)" in the dropdown to signal which one is production-ready. ntfy.sh remains the recommended default.
  • Mobile Alerts — ntfy defaults & topic cap. ntfy.sh topic is now hard-capped at 8 characters (maxlength on input + validate-on-save) with a matching note in the provider description. Priority defaults to Max (sound + bypass Do Not Disturb).
  • Mobile Alerts — alert type in title. Outgoing notifications append the triggering alarm type to the configured title for providers that support a title field (ntfy.sh, Pushover) — e.g. "SimFly Active Airports — Top of Descent" — so the phone surface shows what fired without reading the body.
  • Alert pins — ✕ to dismiss + hover details. Plotted alert pins on the map now carry their own ✕ close badge (matching the distance-measurement style) and surface the full alert details on hover (label, params, trip time, acknowledgement delay, telemetry snapshot). Removes the need to open the History modal just to dismiss a single pin.
  • Region Selection — relocated. The ⊙ Select Regions button moves out of the top header bar and into the first slot of the 🛠 Tools menu, where it self-relabels to Dismiss Regions while a matrix is displayed. The old ⊙ DONE button is gone — dismiss the matrix via the new ✕ button at the far right of the matrix results bar. The "Cancel" button during region drawing now lives inside the floating STEP 1 / STEP 2 overlay so the header stays clean.
v2.87.18 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • "Save to File" filename now uses "alerts" for UI consistency. Downloaded file naming changed from simfly-alarms-YYYY-MM-DD-...json to simfly-alerts-YYYY-MM-DD-...json. The user-facing modal title, button labels, help-modal section, and release notes have all called this system "Alerts" for some time; only the export filename still used the legacy "alarms" wording. Internal identifiers (localStorage keys like simfly_aa_afk_alarms, internal variable names) are unchanged to avoid breaking saved data — this is purely the user-visible download filename.
v2.87.17 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • METAR string font +50%. .tip-wx-raw font-size 0.71rem → 1.065rem. The microscopic size was inherited from the compact matrix-tooltip pop-up where vertical space is tight; in the dep/arr info cards the METAR has its own row and benefits from being properly readable. Other weather metadata (flight-category pill, nearest-station note) unchanged.
v2.87.16 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Info-card Courier text +35% for readability. Two CSS rules touched: .dist-map-info-card (the body lines — ICAO codes, owner, payout, category, elevation) 0.9rem → 1.215rem, and .dist-map-info-card strong (the airport name at the top of each card) 1rem → 1.35rem. The Orbitron section labels (DEPARTURE / ARRIVAL, and the aircraft-suitability sub-section label) were unchanged — only the Courier-family text scaled.
v2.87.15 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Info-card title arrows swapped to point semantically. Was "▶ Departure" / "Arrival ▶" — the arrows pointed inward toward the card body. Now "DEPARTURE ▶" / "▶ ARRIVAL" — the arrows point in the direction implied by the word itself: departing from the card (out the right side), arriving at the card (in from the left). Tiny tweak, just the two literal triangle characters reordered in the HTML.
v2.87.14 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Info-card titles dialled back 40%. v2.87.13's 2.4rem was overpowering relative to the body text below it. .dist-map-info-card-lbl font-size reduced to 1.44rem (-40%), still ~2.4× the pre-v2.87.13 baseline so the dep/arr headers still read as proper section labels — just no longer dominating the card.
v2.87.13 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Dep / Arr info card titles enlarged 300% and re-coloured. The "DEPARTURE ▶" / "ARRIVAL ▶" labels at the top of the two airport info cards (below the matrix) were a barely-visible 0.6rem muted-grey. .dist-map-info-card-lbl font-size bumped to 2.4rem (300% increase) for proper section-header prominence. Per-card colour overrides via the existing #distInfoDepCard / #distInfoArrCard IDs: DEPARTURE turns cyan (#00c8ff), ARRIVAL turns orange (#ff6a00). Matches the dep/arr convention used everywhere else on the screen. Also added line-height:1.0 so the larger glyphs don't over-pad the card header.
v2.87.12 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • "Departure" and "Arrival" labels now follow the convention colours. The two ICAO-field labels at the top of the main screen previously rendered in the muted default colour. They now inherit the cross-app dep/arr convention: Departure turns cyan (#00c8ff), Arrival turns orange (#ff6a00). Matches the same colours already applied to the Departure Airport Owner / Arrival Airport Owner labels, the Dep / Arr category filter labels, and the matrix 1:1 corner cell.
  • Aircraft range label re-coloured to the arrival convention. The ✈ X nm label at the east edge of the aircraft-range ring was rendered in the default gold of .rg-ring-lbl. Now uses the arrival-orange convention colour (#ff6a00) with the matching border tint — the ring's boundary represents how far the aircraft can reach from the departure, so it sits naturally with the rest of the arrival-side UI.
v2.87.11 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Map distance labels unified on a single font configuration. The various on-map distance tags had drifted apart: the region-line label (`.rg-nm-label`, the cyan "1,522 nm · 2,819 km" tag) was Courier New / 0.88rem, while the measurement labels and range-circle labels (`.rg-ring-lbl`, the yellow / orange chips) were Rajdhani / 0.82rem class default — but with inline 0.94rem overrides on the measurement label rendering. All map distance labels now share the same configuration: Rajdhani 0.94rem semibold + font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums + 0.02em letter-spacing. Tabular-nums forces every digit to occupy the same width slot, so values like 1,331 and 2,464 don't jitter relative to each other across redraws. Rajdhani matches the established convention for numbered labels across all SimFly apps (memory note: "use Rajdhani for any label containing digits"). The route-total badge (`.rg-route-total`) also picks up tabular-nums so its Σ figures line up with the per-leg chips.
v2.87.10 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Diagonal corner cell: line colour matches matrix cell borders. The diagonal divider was drawn at rgba(255,255,255,0.32); now uses var(--border) in the gradient so the line is visually continuous with the rest of the matrix's grid lines.
  • DEP / ARR labels increased 40%. Font size bumped from 0.78rem to 1.09rem on both .matrix-corner-diag-arr and .matrix-corner-diag-dep. Corner-offsets nudged from 3px/4px to 4px/6px so the bigger glyphs sit comfortably in their triangles. Applies to both the main matrix and Region Mode matrix via the shared CSS class.
v2.87.9 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Matrix 1:1 corner cells now use a diagonal-split design. Both the main distance matrix and the Region Mode matrix had a stacked two-line label (ARR → / DEP ↓) in cell 1:1. The new design divides the cell with a thin diagonal line drawn via a CSS linear-gradient background; ARR sits in the top-right triangle (orange #ff6a00) and DEP sits in the bottom-left triangle (cyan #00c8ff). The position itself conveys row/column orientation, so the explicit arrows were dropped — the diagonal does the same job more elegantly. New shared CSS class .matrix-corner-diag + two child label classes are reused by both matrices so future corner-cell tweaks happen in one place.
v2.87.8 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Owner-dropdown placeholder rows now use the dep/arr colour convention. Opening either the Departure or Arrival Airport Owner dropdown shows a first row labelled "— select owner —". That placeholder is now coloured: the departure dropdown's placeholder turns cyan (#00c8ff) and the arrival dropdown's turns orange (#ff6a00), matching the labels above each control. The render path branches on the existing `side` variable in the dropdown's population loop so the two placeholders pick up the right colour automatically.
v2.87.7 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Matrix corner cell now uses the dep/arr colour convention. The top-left ARR → / DEP ↓ label in cell 1:1 of the distance matrix had the row text and arrows wrapped in a single muted span. Each line is now split into its own coloured span: ARR → turns orange (#ff6a00) and DEP ↓ turns cyan (#00c8ff), matching the v2.87.6 changes to the Departure / Arrival owner labels and the Dep / Arr category-filter labels. Also removed the parent opacity:0.55 on .dist-corner-label — that was muting both lines and would have continued to mute the new convention colours; without it the colours now read at full brightness, identical to their usage elsewhere on the screen.
v2.87.6 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Departure / Arrival labels coloured to match the rest of the app. Recip Tracker and AA Region Mode both use cyan (#00c8ff) for departure and orange (#ff6a00) for arrival. Four main-screen labels now follow the same convention: Departure Airport Owner turns cyan, Arrival Airport Owner turns orange, and the Dep / Arr text next to the category-filter circles is recoloured accordingly. The category circles themselves keep their existing per-category colours — only the side-label changes. No layout / structural changes; pure colour pass.
v2.87.5 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • Bug fix — Alert History modal really does open now (z-index root cause). v2.87.2 added a "navigate Alerts → History" flow because the History modal wasn't showing on top of the Alerts modal; that diagnosis was wrong. The actual root cause: the Alerts modal carries a one-off #afkConfigModal { z-index:9500 } rule (set so it can sit above the map-layer elements at 9000–9700), but the History modal was using the default .modal-overlay { z-index:1000 } inherited from the base modal rule. With the Alerts modal closed, the History modal was opening AT z-index 1000 underneath the map's breadcrumbs / phase overlay / Region Mode infrastructure at 9000+, so the user saw "nothing but the map." Fixed by adding #afkHistoryModal { z-index:9510 } — slightly above the Alerts modal so the natural stacking is correct even in any future scenario where both are technically open. The v2.87.2 navigate-not-stack flow stays as-is because it's still cleaner UX (only one modal visible at a time, no half-faded background modal cluttering the view).
v2.87.4 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Disabled alarm cards faded further while keeping their controls usable. v2.87.3 applied opacity and filter:grayscale at the card level, which fades the enable checkbox and delete ✕ along with everything else (CSS opacity/filter on a parent cascades to descendants and can't be lifted back). v2.87.4 applies the fade per-child instead — grip handle, chevron, icon, description text, and editor body each get opacity:0.28 + grayscale(0.85), while the .afk-card-actions cluster (toggle + delete ✕) is deliberately excluded from the fade selectors and stays at full visibility. The disabled card now reads even more obviously as off, and the two controls a user actually needs to act on a disabled card (re-enable, delete) remain bright and clickable.
v2.87.3 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Disabled alarm cards now read unmistakably as inactive. Previously a disabled card was only opacity:0.55, which left the cyan accent, icon, and toggle bright enough that an off alarm could be mistaken for armed at a glance. Four cues now stack: opacity dropped to 0.50, a grayscale(0.75) filter drains the accent colour to muted grey, a 135° diagonal stripe pattern overlays the background (the classic "inactive" web convention), the border switches to a dashed muted grey, and the alarm's title + summary text get a thin strike-through line. The visual gap between "armed" and "off" is now obvious even from across the modal.
v2.87.2 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • Bug fix — Alert History button now opens the history modal. v2.87.0 introduced both modals at the same z-index (1000), each with its own backdrop-filter. Each modal's blur creates an independent stacking context, and on real browsers the Alerts modal's filter ended up rendering over the History modal even though History came later in the DOM. Result: clicking 📜 Alert History appeared to do nothing — the History modal was open underneath. Fixed by treating the button as navigation rather than stacking: clicking 📜 Alert History closes the Alerts modal and opens History; closing History (X or backdrop click) returns you to the Alerts modal. The transition is instant and the user always sees what they expect.
  • Second divider in the Alerts modal footer. The footer was grouped [Test Sound][Alert History] | [Save][Load][Disable All][Delete All] with one divider; the destructive Disable All / Delete All buttons now sit in their own visually-separated cluster: [Test Sound][Alert History] | [Save][Load] | [Disable All][Delete All].
v2.87.1 May 24, 2026 Fix
  • Bug fix — same-group alarms could both fire. Reported: a Geofence and a Descent Started alarm sharing the same Alert Group both tripped during one flight. The cascade contract is "one member fires, every sibling auto-disables", but the cascade only ran on user dismiss — so between the first trip and the dismissal, an enabled sibling whose own conditions later satisfied would fire independently. Cascade enforcement moved into _afkEvaluate: any alarm with a tripped sibling in its group returns false at evaluation time, blocking the second fire before it can happen. Also covers the same-tick race (two members satisfy on the same _afkTick — the trip loop calls trip() on the first one in list order, the second's eval then sees a tripped sibling and returns false). Mobile Alerts side-effect: duplicate push notifications for sibling group members can no longer occur.
  • Bug fix — Cruise Reached can no longer fire on the ground at any airport. v2.87.0 fixed the high-elevation-airport regression with a GS-based ground detector + wheels-up gate, but the protection was heuristic (could in principle be defeated by fast-taxi at gs > 55 sustained). v2.87.1 adds a structural gate: the climb → cruise phase transition now requires that we have actually observed peakClimbVS > +200 fpm at some point in this session. Sitting on the ground with sustained-level VS keeps peakClimbVS at 0, so the cruise transition is blocked regardless of how the rest of the phase detector behaves. Threshold is well below any real aircraft's initial climb (heavy jet at MTOW holds ~500 fpm, Cessna 152 holds ~700 fpm) and well above ground VS noise.
  • Bug fix — checking an enable box now always rearms the alarm. v2.87.0 fixed the alt-min auto-rearm via the ground-transition path, but the user-toggle path (clicking the enable checkbox after dismissing an alarm) only reset state.fired for cruise / descent / timer alarm types — alt-min, geofence, and waypoint silently kept their post-fire state. Re-enabling them produced a checked box that wouldn't actually fire on the next qualifying event. A single new _afkResetTransientState(alarm) helper is now the only sanctioned way to flip enabled from false to true; it lives next to _afkFreshState(alarm) which produces correct freshly-armed transient state for every alarm type. All four call sites (per-card toggle, group master toggle, file import, fresh alarm creation) go through the helper. The invariant is now: any time a visual checkbox is on, the alarm is genuinely ready to fire on the next qualifying event.
  • Load from File now activates every imported alarm. Previously an alarm's enabled state was taken from the saved file as-is, so loading a file produced disabled alarms if you'd saved them that way. v2.87.1 forces enabled = true on every imported alarm, runs the per-type state-reset helper so each one is freshly armed, and the alarm-history log is left untouched (it spans configurations).
  • Load from File while airborne now prompts a warning. Loading a fresh alarm configuration mid-flight can fire some alarms on the first or second tick if your current state already satisfies their conditions — e.g., a Cruise Reached with a target altitude below your current FL, or a Geofence centred on a destination you're already near. A warning modal now appears in this case (live source connected and aircraft moving / climbing / descending / wheels-up) explaining the risk and offering Cancel or Load Anyway. Quiet at the gate — the typical pre-pushback configuration workflow is unaffected.
  • Group cascade now visually disables siblings immediately. When one member of a group trips, every other enabled sibling is now flipped enabled = false in the same tick (in addition to being prevented from firing by the eval-time guard).
