The Alerts badge, alarm list, and any per-card visuals reflect the cascade state immediately rather than waiting for the user to dismiss the firing alarm.
  • Help modal updated — Alert Groups, Altitude alarm, Load from File, and Cruise Reached sections all clarified to match the v2.87.1 behaviour. The cascade contract paragraph now reads as a guarantee rather than an aspiration.
v2.87.0 May 24, 2026 Feature
  • 🔊 Test Sound button — new button at the bottom of the Alerts modal lets you preview alarm sounds without arming an alarm. Each click cycles to the next of the five built-in sounds (Digital Clock → Klaxon → Soft Chime → Urgent Beeper → Gentle Bell, then wraps) with a toast naming the one currently playing. If an alarm is actively tripped, the preview takes over briefly and the original sound resumes after the 2.5-second preview ends.
  • 📜 Alert History — persistent log of the last 100 alert events. New button at the bottom of the Alerts modal opens a dedicated history modal. Each row captures the local and UTC timestamp the alarm tripped, the alarm type and label, every configurable parameter, the eventual dismissal reason (user, duration timer, or group cascade), and a full aircraft telemetry snapshot at trip time (lat / lng, altitude, GS, IAS, heading, vertical speed, phase, source, aircraft ident). Per-row 📍 Pin to Map drops a labelled gold pin at the captured position so you can review where each alert fired; per-row ✕ deletes that single entry; Clear History in the footer wipes the whole log. Storage is FIFO at 100 events under localStorage key simfly_aa_afk_alert_history.
  • Group cascades are fully recorded in Alert History. When one alarm in a group fires, its own entry is logged with role group fired; each sibling that was also tripped is marked dismissed via group cascade; and each armed sibling that was auto-disabled by the cascade gets a synthetic entry noting it never actually fired but was auto-dismissed because a different alarm in the group did. The group name (if set) is shown on every related entry.
  • Visual divider in the Alerts modal footer. A thin vertical separator sits between the new Test Sound / Alert History buttons and the existing Save / Load / Disable / Delete buttons so the two functional groups read distinctly.
  • Bug fix — Cruise Reached no longer trips during taxi at high-elevation airports. The ground detector in the phase-state machine used to require MSL altitude < 1,000 ft for ground; at airports like MMAS (Aguascalientes, 6,112 ft) or El Alto (13,300 ft) this was never satisfied, so taxi looked airborne and after ~60 seconds the phase detector drifted to cruise and the Cruise Reached alarm fired on the ground. Ground detection now uses GS + VS instead: GS < 5 kt (near-stationary) OR GS < 55 kt AND |VS| < 200 fpm. The 55-kt ceiling accommodates fields that allow rapid taxi.
  • Defence-in-depth: Cruise Reached and Descent Started are now gated on confirmed wheels-up (sustained GS > 40 kt for 10 s, the same threshold that drives the ELAPSED timer). Even if the phase detector misclassifies something on the ground, these alarms cannot fire until takeoff actually happens.
  • Bug fix — Altitude alarms now auto-rearm between flights. The ground-transition rearm block previously only reset state.fired for Cruise Reached and Descent Started, so an Altitude alarm that fired on one flight silently never fired on the next unless the user manually toggled enabled off and on. Altitude is now included in that rearm sweep alongside cruise and descent.
  • Bug fix — landing detection no longer references MSL altitude. The old detector required alt < 500 ft alongside GS < 30 kt, which never fired at high-elevation airports and could leave _afkTakeoffAt set into the next flight. New rule: sustained valid GS < 30 kt for 60 seconds, with a guard against transient telemetry dropouts (null GS no longer collapses to zero for landing purposes).
  • Help modal updated. The Alerts help section now covers the new Test Sound and Alert History buttons, the wheels-up gate on Cruise / Descent, the Altitude auto-rearm, and the new high-elevation-airport ground guards.
v2.86.0 May 24, 2026 Improved
  • Route Map removed. The collapsible ROUTE MAP panel below the matrix — the small Leaflet map with dep / arr pins, distance lines, and a satellite toggle — has been retired. Its job (interactive exploration of dep / arr airport relationships on a map) is fully covered by ⊙ Map, which is faster, drag-resizable, and integrates with Region Mode, the Scenery Library, METAR overlays, measurement tools, and SimBrief plotting. Removing the secondary map deletes ~400 lines of duplicate code and ~150 lines of CSS, drops a Leaflet map instance from the main screen, and tightens the Flight Planner’s vertical layout so the matrix and aircraft selector sit closer to the airport-info cards.
  • Departure / Arrival info cards are now always visible whenever an endpoint is selected. The two info cards that previously appeared only when a hero route (both dep and arr highlighted) was active under the Route Map are now their own panel beneath the matrix — populated as soon as either a departure or arrival airport is set, by typing an ICAO, clicking a matrix row / column / cell, picking a dot in Region Mode, or via the SimBrief / URL handoff. Each card renders independently: the dep card hides on its own when the departure field is cleared, the arr card hides when the arrival field is cleared, and the whole panel collapses to nothing when both endpoints are empty. Cards still use the same shared _buildTooltipHtml renderer as the matrix header tooltips, so they show ICAO / IATA, city, owner, SimFly category, elevation, full runway list, aircraft-suitability verdict, and the cached METAR / weather block in one place — no hover required.
  • Help modal updated. The Route Map help section has been removed; a new Departure / Arrival Info Panel section now sits in the same slot describing the promoted cards.
  • Internal cleanup. Removed the route-map JS block (_dmRenderMap, _dmClearLayers, _dmFitAll, toggleDistMap, _dmToggleLayer, plus all _dm* state vars), the route-map CSS rules (.dist-map-panel, .dist-map-toggle-hdr, .dist-map-body, .dist-map-swap-row, .dist-map-status, #distMapContainer, .wx-map-legend, all .wx-legend-* dot styles), the now-dead _wxMapTooltipHtml, and the duplicate distMapSwapBtn sync. The shared Leaflet bootstrap (_dmLoadLeaflet, _DM_TILES) and the .dist-map-layer-btn styling are kept where Region Mode reuses them. A new _distRenderInfoPanel() helper replaces every former if (_dmMapOpen) _dmRenderMap(); call site, and a single hook at the top of _calcDistance() keeps the panel in sync on every entry path (typing, swap, matrix sync, URL params, weather refresh).
v2.85.2 May 23, 2026 Improved
  • Loading alarms from disk now runs the full Smart Load sweep. Two tightenings on ⤒ Load from File:
    • Clears every group before the import lands. The pre-import alarm wipe didn't touch the group registry, so ghost groups (zero members after the wipe) could linger and risk colliding with imported group ids during the remap step. Added _afkGroups._gc() right after the alarm-remove loop — every old group goes away with the alarms that filled it. Map visuals tied to the now-removed alarms (geofence rings + waypoint highlights) are also re-rendered once before the imports start landing so the canvas reads clean during the swap.
    • Smart Load fires automatically when a plan is already loaded. After the import toast, if _sbOfpLastData is set, _afkOnSbPlanLoaded() runs — same sweep that fires when a SimBrief plan plots. Plan-relative geofences and waypoint alarms in the imported set arm + draw immediately on the active plan instead of waiting for the next _afkTick (which never fires when no live source is connected). Confirms with the usual "🔔 Smart load: N DEP/ARR geofences + M waypoint alerts active" toast so you can see at a glance which imports went live.
v2.85.1 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Smart Load — plan-dependent alerts arm + draw the instant the SimBrief plan plots. Previously a dep/arr-anchored geofence or a waypoint alert only got its on-map visuals after the next _afkTick — which never fires when no live source is connected, so the rings stayed invisible until you started FSUIPC. v2.85.1 wires _sbOfpRender to a new _afkOnSbPlanLoaded() sweep that runs the moment the plan finishes plotting:
    • Drops stale Specific-waypoint picks when the new plan's origin/destination/fix-count differs from the previous plan (with a soft warn toast). Same logic as the existing tick path, just runs immediately.
    • Re-seeds dep/arr-anchored geofence state — wasInside is reset on every plan-anchored fence so the freshly-resolved centre doesn't risk a false ENTER trip on the very next tick.
    • Re-renders geofence + waypoint visuals immediately so all the rings, labels, and highlight markers appear in sync with the route polyline — no waiting for live aircraft data.
    • Smart-load summary toast — counts enabled plan-relevant alerts and reports "🔔 Smart load: 2 DEP/ARR geofences + 3 waypoint alerts active for this flight" so you know your pre-configured alerts armed themselves automatically.
    Also tightened _sbOfpClear — clearing the flight plan now removes plan-relative geofence circles + waypoint rings cleanly, resets wasInside on dep/arr fences so the next plan re-anchors them without stale state, and syncs _afkLastSbFingerprint so the next _afkTick doesn't think the plan "just changed" and re-toast. The localStorage-restore path passes silent:true so a page reload doesn't re-toast every load.
v2.85.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Mobile Alerts — push alarms to your phone. When an alarm trips while you’re away from the computer, the AA tab can now send a notification straight to your phone in addition to the in-tab sound + overlay. New 📱 Mobile Alerts… row at the bottom of the 🔌 Live pulldown opens a settings modal with six provider presets:
    • ntfy.sh — free, no account; install the ntfy app on your phone, subscribe to a topic name you pick (use something long and unguessable), and you’re live in under a minute.
    • Pushover — one-time US$5; more polished delivery with priorities, custom sounds, and emergency re-alerting via the Pushover app.
    • Discord webhook — paste any channel webhook URL; the alert lands as a message in that channel and the Discord mobile app handles the push.
    • Telegram bot — create a bot via @BotFather, grab your numeric Chat ID, and Telegram delivers the alert.
    • Slack webhook — paste an Incoming Webhook URL; alerts land in the configured channel and the Slack mobile app pushes them.
    • Custom webhook — any HTTP-POST endpoint with a configurable Content-Type and body template (use {{message}} as the placeholder). Covers self-hosted ntfy, Gotify, ifttt webhooks, Home Assistant, and anything else that accepts a POST.
    The modal shows a per-provider blurb explaining setup, validates required fields, and includes a ▶ Test button that fires a sample notification using the draft settings before you save — so you find out if the topic / token / URL is wrong while you’re still in the dialog, not on the next flight. Clear removes the settings entirely. Configuration is stored locally in localStorage under the key aa_mobile_alerts_v1 — tokens never leave your browser.
  • Per-alarm opt-in. Each alarm card in the 🔔 Alerts modal gains a Mobile alert row at the bottom of its editor with a toggle and the hint "Forward to phone when tripped". The row only appears when Mobile Alerts is configured — with no provider set, the alarm cards look exactly as they did in v2.84.x. Toggle is per-alarm so you can pick exactly which alarms warrant a phone buzz (e.g. Descent Started yes, Timer no). The toggle state persists with the alarm and travels through Save to File / Load from File. Clearing the provider doesn’t reset the per-alarm flags — they just go dormant until you configure a provider again.
  • Send happens after sound, never instead of it. The in-tab alarm sound, full-screen overlay, and tab-title flash all work exactly as before; the mobile alert is fire-and-forget on top. If the HTTP POST fails (offline, bad token, CORS), AA toasts a single soft warning containing the alarm name and the error reason — the alarm itself still trips normally. Each newly-tripped alarm fires its own notification, so dismissing one alarm doesn’t suppress later ones, and grouped alarms produce a buzz per member (matching the per-card opt-in granularity).
  • Save to File / Load from File now includes Mobile Alerts settings. The 🔔 Alerts modal's ⤓ Save to File and ⤒ Load from File buttons now bundle your Mobile Alerts provider configuration alongside the alarm queue, so a single JSON file carries everything needed to recreate the setup on another machine (or restore after clearing state). Export format bumped from v2 to v3 with a new mobileAlerts field containing the same { provider, fields } blob as localStorage. On import, if the file has a valid provider config that differs from your current one, a confirmation prompt warns before overwriting; files without a mobileAlerts field (older v2 saves, or files saved while Mobile Alerts was unconfigured) leave your existing settings untouched. Heads-up: exported files contain whatever tokens / webhook URLs / topic names you have configured — fine for personal backup, worth being careful when sharing the file.
v2.84.1 May 23, 2026 Improved
  • Two new rows on the 🔌 Live menu — one-click clearing for SimBrief plan and aircraft track. Below the existing FSUIPC / STKP / Follow A/C / Aircraft Icon rows, a thin separator now precedes:
    • ✕ Clear Flight Plan — removes the plotted SimBrief route from the map, wipes the local-storage cache so it won't restore on next page load, and re-renders any waypoint-alarm rings to drop alongside the route.
    • ✕ Clear Aircraft Track — erases the green breadcrumb trail (all three tiers) and wipes the local-storage track so the path stays gone after reload.
    Both rows toast a confirmation on success and a soft "nothing to clear" warning when the click hits an already-empty state. The same underlying handlers the existing toolbar buttons use are invoked, so nothing diverges from the established behaviour.
v2.84.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Geofences can now anchor to Departure or Arrival airport. Plan-relative geofences auto-relocate to wherever DEP / ARR sits in the currently-loaded SimBrief flight plan, so a single fence works across every route — no more re-drawing per flight.
    • Anchor picker on create — clicking Add → ⊙ Geofence now opens a small chooser first: Departure airport, Arrival airport, or Draw on map (the v2.69 click-and-drag flow). Dep / Arr picks skip the map-draw step entirely — you go straight to a config dialog that already knows the anchor and asks for radius + trigger + label + sound + duration.
    • Existing fences can switch anchors any time — the geofence alarm card editor gains an Anchor dropdown and an always-editable Radius input. Coords-anchored fences still display their captured lat/lng read-only; plan-relative fences show the live DEP / ARR ICAO the fence is currently following.
    • Auto re-anchor on plan change — whenever a new SimBrief plan loads, every dep / arr-anchored fence relocates and re-renders on the map, and its wasInside state resets so a "moved underneath the aircraft" centre swap doesn't immediately trip a false enter event.
    • Map label tag — plan-relative fences show @DEP KJFK / @ARR EGLL in the persistent map label so you can tell them apart at a glance from drawn-on-map fences.
    Data-model detail: alarm params gains an optional anchor field ('coords' | 'dep' | 'arr'); 'coords' is the default, preserving every v2.69 → v2.83 geofence with no migration. The runtime resolves the live centre through a new _afkGeofenceCenter(params) helper that's now the single source of truth for the eval path, the ETA computation, the NEXT ALARM click target, and the persistent map visuals.
v2.83.1 May 23, 2026 Improved
  • Alert groups get a master enable/disable checkbox. Each group's header now has a checkbox right next to the collapse chevron that drives every member's enable state at once: checked = all members on, unchecked = all off, indeterminate (browser-native style + amber accent) = mixed. Clicking the box does the natural thing — if any member is off, it enables all; if every member is on, it disables all. Re-enabling honours each alarm's per-type re-arm semantics (timer recomputes targetMs, cruise/descent clear state.fired + prevAlt) so each member arrives in the same just-armed state a single-card toggle click would produce. Useful with the v2.83.0 aggressive cascade: dismiss one member → group auto-disables → flip the master back on next flight to re-arm the whole bundle in one click.
v2.83.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Alert Groups — bundle related alarms so they fire and dismiss as one. A group is 2 or more alarms that logically belong together (e.g. TOD warning + FL250 crossing + 30-min-to-arrival timer). Dismissing ANY member of a group cascades to ALL members: every member's audio stops, the trip overlay rows clear, and every member is auto-disabled. One acknowledgement covers the whole bundle.
    • Three ways to create a group:
      • Drag-and-drop: drop one alarm onto the MIDDLE of another (a green "+ Group" indicator shows the drop target). The 60/40 top/bottom of each card still reorders as before — only the middle 40% triggers grouping.
      • Right-click any alarm: a context menu offers New group with… (pick any other ungrouped alarm), Add to existing group…, Remove from group, Rename group, and Ungroup.
      • Shift-click multi-select: click cards while holding Shift to highlight them, then click the ⛓ Group N Selected button that appears in the modal footer.
    • Optional name — click the group's name label at the top to type one inline (placeholder reads "Group of N alarms…"). Enter or click-away to commit; Escape to cancel.
    • Collapse the whole group with the chevron next to the name — all members fold into one stripe. Each card's own chevron still toggles just that card's editor body.
    • Auto-dissolve — if a group ever drops below 2 members (you remove a member, ungroup, or delete a member), the leftover lone alarm becomes a standalone alarm again automatically.
    • Drag out — drag a grouped alarm to a position outside its group (or to the top/bottom drop zones) and it leaves the group cleanly; the group dissolves if only one member remains.
    • Save/Load extended — the alarm JSON file format bumped to v: 2 and now bundles the group records too. Imports remap group ids onto fresh local ones so they never collide with existing groups in your queue.
v2.82.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Taxi-quality breadcrumb track — new ultra-fresh tier keeps the last ~5 nm at 0.01 nm resolution. The trail is now three tiers: ultra-fresh (500 pts @ 0.01 nm spacing — gate-to-runway lines stay smooth around turns instead of cutting through hangars), fresh (1500 pts @ 0.05 nm — existing mid-history detail), and archive (10 000 pts @ 1.0 nm — existing long-haul coverage). Points age down through the tiers with per-tier distance-based decimation. Persistence format bumped to v2; pre-v2.82 saved tracks still load (the ultra tier starts empty and fills from the next live fix).
  • Save / Load alarm settings to disk. Two new footer buttons in the Alerts modal: ⤓ Save to File downloads the current alarm queue as a timestamped JSON file (great for keeping per-aircraft or per-route alarm setups), and ⤒ Load from File replaces the current queue with a previously-saved file after a confirm. The local-storage autosave is unchanged — this is a parallel "named setups" channel.
  • Easier alarm drag/drop. Three fixes for the "needs super-precise placement" reorder feel: the per-card before/after split moved from 50/50 to 60/40 (upper 60% of any card lands above), and dedicated drop-at-top and drop-at-bottom zones appear above the first and below the last card while a drag is in progress so reordering to the start or end of the list is a single forgiving click. Top/bottom zones are invisible while idle (no visual cost when you're not dragging).
  • Map header polish:
    • The All / None / Invert mini-buttons on the Map category filter row are 25 % larger and centred vertically, bringing them in line with the surrounding 1-7 category dots. The dep/arr filter rows still use the original compact size.
    • The Live pulldown's Aircraft Icon… row uses an ✈ glyph instead of 🎨.
  • Aircraft Data Panel polish:
    • The ⏷ collapse toggle now stays pinned to the right of the panel when the panel is collapsed (used to drift left because the sections + gear button hide on collapse and there was nothing pushing the button rightward).
    • Alert-card chevron glyph now updates correctly when you click to expand a collapsed card (used to stay ▶ until the modal closed and reopened).
v2.81.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Map header refactored — 5 inline buttons collapsed into two pulldowns + a floating toolbar. The header was overflowing as more live-aircraft / SimBrief / measurement controls landed in successive releases. Three regrouping changes:
    • 🛠 Tools pulldown — absorbs 📐 Measurement and ✈ SimBrief Flight Plan. A small green dot on the trigger lights when either tool is currently active (measurement mode on, or a plan is plotted), so you know without opening the menu.
    • 🔌 Live pulldown — absorbs 🔌 FSUIPC Live, 🛩 STKP Live, 📍 Follow A/C, and a new 🎨 Aircraft Icon… row that opens the silhouette picker directly (in addition to the existing Shift-click on Follow A/C). Active dot lights when a live source is connected.
    • Floating measurement toolbar — the ◯ Distance Ring / ╱ Distance Line toggle and ✕ Clear All button no longer sit in the header. While measurement mode is active they float over the map (top-centre) alongside a new ✕ Exit button so all measurement controls live in one purpose-built strip.
    Buttons kept inline: region-mode controls (Select Regions / Undo / DONE / Clear Route) and 🔔 Alerts. The pulldown items are the SAME DOM nodes as the old inline buttons (same ids, same onclick handlers) just rehoused under a panel, so every existing state-setter — FSUIPC tracking label, SimBrief Clear-vs-Fetch button text, Follow A/C indicator — continues to work without modification. Clicking outside a pulldown closes it; opening one closes the other.
v2.80.4 May 23, 2026 Improved
  • Data-panel labels and values are 25% larger. Label font-size 0.7rem → 0.875rem, value font-size 1.02rem → 1.275rem. Per-field min-width values are still in em so columns scale proportionally and the layout stays steady at the bigger size.
  • Drag grips are hover-only again. v2.80.3 made them baseline-visible at 35% opacity; reverted to opacity:0 so the panel reads clean and the grips only fade in when you hover a field or spacer. The wider 16 px spacer hit zone and whole-row drag-handle behaviour from v2.80.3 are unchanged, so the easier grab is preserved without the visual clutter.
v2.80.3 May 23, 2026 Improved
  • Data-panel drag handles are much easier to grab now. Three changes addressing v2.80.0's small / hard-to-target drag targets:
    • Spacers got a 16 px hit zone. Each vertical-bar spacer used to be a 1 px-wide element (nearly impossible to click). The clickable element is now 16 px wide and 36 px tall; the visible 1 px bar is drawn via a ::before pseudo-element centred inside. Hovering the wider zone fattens the bar to 2 px and turns it accent-coloured so you can see exactly what you're about to drag.
    • Every field row is now itself a drag handle. .afk-dp-field picks up cursor:grab directly (was on the grip only); the cursor flips to grabbing on click. You can grab any pixel of the row — no need to aim at the small ⋮⋮ glyph above it.
    • Grips are baseline-visible. Opacity bumped from 0 → 0.35 so the glyph is always faintly drawn rather than only appearing on hover; font size up from 0.85rem → 1.0rem with 8 px of padding around the glyph so it's also a usable hit target if you prefer to aim at it.
v2.80.2 May 23, 2026 Fix
  • ⚙ gear menu now actually opens. v2.80.0's popover was positioned absolute inside #afkDataPanel, but the panel had no position:relative so the menu's anchor resolved against the document body and the popover landed off-screen. Added position:relative to the panel and corrected the bottom offset from calc(100% - 4px) (which placed the menu INSIDE the panel) to calc(100% + 6px) (a clean 6 px gap above the bar).
  • IND ALT removed. v2.79.1 tried type:'double', v2.80.1 tried type:'bytearray' + manual DataView.getFloat64(); neither produced a usable reading from FSUIPC offset 0x6020 through Paul Henty's WebSocket on real-world setups (the field stayed at —). Removed cleanly: registry entry, default config token, FSUIPC offset declaration, decode block, live-aircraft payload field, data-panel write, CSS rule, and help text. Existing saved layouts that included 'indAlt' get the entry silently filtered out by _afkDpGetConfig (no migration step needed). A SimVar-passthrough approach (INDICATED ALTITUDE via Paul Henty's simvars.declare channel) is the path forward if this is ever revisited.
v2.80.1 May 23, 2026 Fix
  • IND ALT now actually populates under FSUIPC. v2.79.1 declared FSUIPC offset 0x6020 as type:'double' — Paul Henty's WebSocket Server didn't honor that on this build (the field stayed at —). v2.80.1 declares the offset as type:'bytearray', size:8 instead and decodes the raw 8 bytes locally with DataView.getFloat64(0, true) (little-endian IEEE 754). Wrapped in try / catch + a ±1 000 000 ft sanity clamp so a malformed payload can never poison the data panel. Backward-compatible: if a future Paul Henty build does start passing a real JS number through, the decode path picks it up directly without re-declaring the offset.
v2.80.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft Data Panel is now fully customisable. Every field can be shown, hidden, reordered, or surrounded by vertical-bar spacers entirely under your control:
    • ⚙ gear button on the right of the panel opens a small popover menu with checkboxes for every available field, grouped by category (Alerts, Altitude, Distance, Position, Speed, Status, Time) and alphabetised within each group. Tick a box to show, untick to hide — the panel re-renders instantly.
    • Drag to reorder — hover any field and a small ⋮⋮ grip fades in above it; grab it and drag left/right to move the field anywhere else on the bar. An accent-coloured drop indicator shows where the item will land. Works the same on field rows and on vertical-bar spacers.
    • Vertical-bar spacers are now first-class addable elements. The vertical divider lines previously drawn between the three fixed sections are now individual spacer items you can add as many of as you want (one per click on + Add Spacer in the gear menu), drag to any position, and remove via the checkbox list in the menu. Default layout still shows the original two dividers in their familiar positions.
    • Configuration persists to localStorage (key simfly_aa_afk_dp_config) and restores whenever the map loads. Reset to Default in the gear menu wipes the saved layout and falls back to the original order.
    Internally driven by a new _AFK_DP_ELEMENTS registry that knows each field's id, label, group, target value-DOM-id, min-width, and optional tooltip text. The pre-v2.80.0 afkDpXxx DOM ids are preserved so the live-aircraft update path is null-safe but otherwise unchanged. Every direct-write in _afkUpdateDataPanel now goes through a null-tolerant helper (_afkDpSet) or guard, so hiding a field is a clean no-op instead of a throw.
v2.79.1 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • New IND ALT field on the Aircraft Data Panel. Shows the Kollsman-calibrated indicated altitude — what the cockpit altimeter actually reads given the current QNH setting — positioned immediately before ALT MSL so you can see at a glance how much the indicated reading deviates from true altitude. Sourced from FSUIPC offset 0x6020 (INDICATED ALTITUDE CALIBRATED, IEEE 754 double in feet) read via Paul Henty's WebSocket with type:'double'. STKP's public payload doesn't expose indicated altitude, so the field shows — under that source.
v2.79.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • New TRK MILES REM field on the Aircraft Data Panel — sums the actual track miles remaining along the SimBrief route. Walks the ordered fix list (origin, every navlog fix, destination), picks the leg the aircraft is currently on (the one whose endpoints' distances to the aircraft sum closest to the leg's own length — robust to small lateral deviations and route doglegs), then totals (current position → next un-passed fix) + every remaining leg through the destination. Falls back to the great-circle figure when the plan has no navlog fixes (origin-direct-to-destination).
  • Old DIST TO GO field renamed to DIST TO DEST. Same value as before — great-circle nautical miles straight from your current position to the destination airport, ignoring any planned dogleg. With TRK MILES REM sitting next to it, the two side-by-side now read cleanly: "how far in a straight line" vs "how far along the route I will actually fly".
  • REMAINING and ETA now use track miles instead of great-circle distance. A 350 nm SimBrief route that doglegs around a TMA used to under-report its remaining time using the 320 nm straight-line figure; the new estimate is based on the 350 nm number, so REMAINING / ETA LOCAL / ETA UTC match what you'll actually fly. Falls back to the great-circle figure if the plan has no navlog fixes.
v2.78.1 May 23, 2026 Improved
  • FSUIPC live-aircraft push cadence relaxed from 2 Hz to 1 Hz. _FSUIPC_INTERVAL bumped from 500 ms to 1000 ms, halving the per-second JS work on the position-render pipeline (JSON parse, marker reposition, breadcrumb-trail update with distance check, phase + alarm evaluation, data-panel rebuild). Real but small CPU saving on busy machines and lighter GC pressure; track quality unchanged because the breadcrumb is decimated by distance (0.05 nm fresh / 1.0 nm archive), not time. STKP's 10 s polling is untouched.
  • Follow A/C pan tween bumped from 0.4 s to 0.8 s to bridge the longer gap between 1 Hz fixes so the map glides continuously instead of stepping. STKP still animates cleanly inside the wider envelope.
v2.78.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Wheels-up time persists across browser reloads. The ELAPSED data-panel field now survives a browser close, crash, or refresh mid-flight — the takeoff timestamp is saved to localStorage (simfly_aa_afk_takeoff_at) the moment the detector confirms takeoff (GS > 40 kt sustained 10 s) and restored at page-load. The wheels-up timestamp resets cleanly when a new flight starts — reusing the existing detector (a SimBrief plan with a different time_generated loaded while the aircraft is on the ground) so mid-flight reloads preserve the timer and only the unambiguous "new plan + on-ground" combination zeroes it. Landing also clears it automatically (sustained low GS + low altitude after takeoff), so the next leg of a multi-leg session starts fresh.
  • Map layer (Satellite vs Street) persists across sessions. Toggling 🛰 Satellite / 🗺 Street in the map header now writes the choice to localStorage (simfly_aa_map_layer) and the map opens at the saved layer on every subsequent visit. Defaults to street on first use, matching prior behaviour.
  • Descent Started alarm now has a rough ETA-to-TOD estimate. The NEXT ALARM “IN” countdown for a Descent alarm fills in when a SimBrief flight plan is loaded and the aircraft is well above the destination, using the classic 3× rule: descent distance in nautical miles ≈ altitude-to-lose in thousands of feet × 3 (equivalent to a ~3° glide, with a 1,500 ft pattern-altitude buffer baked in). Works across light singles through heavy jets without per-aircraft tuning. The alarm itself still trips on the adaptive phase detector when sustained descent actually begins — the ETA is a heads-up, not a trigger.
v2.77.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Live-aircraft marker can now be one of four silhouettes. Pick between Airliner, Prop, Helicopter, or Business Jet — each is a hand-rolled inline SVG so it stays crisp at every zoom and rotates cleanly to the live heading. The Helicopter silhouette uses a rotor-disc circle (rotationally invariant) plus a tail boom for the heading cue, so it reads correctly at every angle. Open the picker by Shift-clicking the 📍 Follow A/C button — mirrors the existing Shift-click pattern on ✈ SimBrief Flight Plan. The grid shows a static preview of each icon; click to apply instantly (no Save button needed). Your choice persists to localStorage (simfly_aa_ac_icon) so the next session restores it automatically. Default remains the Airliner shape from prior versions.
v2.76.1 May 23, 2026 Fix
  • AGL field removed from the Aircraft Data Panel. The FSUIPC offset it relied on (0x0B4C — "Ground altitude metres × 256") frequently returns the elevation of the nearest airport reference point rather than the actual terrain directly under the aircraft, so AGL readings were off by hundreds-to-thousands of feet over hills, lakes, ridges, or anywhere away from a runway. Cleanly removed the DOM field, FSUIPC offset declaration, gndAlt plumbing through the live-aircraft pipeline, CSS column rule, and help text. The remaining ALT MSL stays accurate. A SimVar-based replacement (SimConnect PLANE ALT ABOVE GROUND, plumbed through Paul Henty's WebSocket as a named SimVar rather than a numeric offset) is a likely future direction but needs verification against your sim setup before shipping.
v2.76.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Cruise Reached alarm now accepts a target cruise altitude. New optional Cruise altitude input on the Cruise Reached card — accepts feet (35000 or 35,000) or flight levels (FL350, FL 200), same parser as the Altitude alarm. When set, the alarm fires on the climbing-through crossing (so an FL280 step-climb pause won't trip an FL350 alarm) AND the NEXT ALARM "IN" countdown shows live time-to-cruise computed from current altitude and vertical speed. The value persists to localStorage (simfly_aa_afk_cruise_alt_text) so the next new Cruise alarm pre-fills with your usual cruise altitude. Leave blank to keep the legacy adaptive phase detector.
  • Every alarm that fires is now auto-disabled on dismiss. Once an alarm sounds and you dismiss it (manually or via the seconds-mode auto-dismiss), its enable toggle flips off — re-enabling for the next use is now an explicit user action. Geofence rings + waypoint highlights disappear from the map alongside the toggle flip so you can see at a glance which alarms are still armed. The Alerts toolbar badge counts only enabled alarms, so it visibly decrements as each alarm goes off.
  • Collapsed alarm cards now show a settings-summary tooltip on hover. When a card is collapsed (chevron pointing right), the editor body is hidden, so hovering the card reveals a multi-line summary of every setting: type, alarm-specific params (trigger / scope / altitude / cruise altitude / waypoints), sound, and sound duration. Expanded cards keep the existing per-region tooltips.
  • Alarm card action layout reorganised. Drag handle ⋮⋮ stays first, but the expand/collapse chevron now sits immediately after it on the FAR LEFT of the card. The enable toggle and delete button stay on the right. The chevron also uses explicit ▶ (collapsed) / ▼ (expanded) glyphs per the SimFly disclosure-triangle convention instead of CSS rotation.
  • Aircraft track no longer leaves phantom copies on zoom. The breadcrumb canvas renderer's padding was bumped from the default 0.1 to 0.5 (canvas extends 50% beyond viewport on each axis) and the map's zoomend event now triggers polyline.redraw() on both the glow and core layers — together these wipe the ghost remnants Leaflet otherwise leaves when zooming a transformed canvas pane.
  • 📍 Follow A/C button collapses to just the pin at narrow widths. Added an early breakpoint at 1700 px so the Follow A/C label drops alongside Measurement — both buttons sit in a dense neighbourhood of icon-heavy controls and were the first to push the toolbar onto a second row on smaller monitors.
v2.75.1 May 23, 2026 Improved
  • Cruise & Descent alarms now auto-dismiss correctly when Sound for is set to a specific time. Previously the 5 s (or any seconds-mode) auto-dismiss fired on schedule, but the very next tick saw state.fired = false + phase still cruise and re-tripped immediately — so the alarm appeared to never turn off. Root cause: _afkAlarms.dismiss() reset state.fired for cruise/descent alarms, which broke their one-shot-per-flight contract. The reset is gone. Cruise/descent are now genuinely one-shot per flight; the next flight rearms them automatically when the aircraft returns to the ground phase (or you can manually rearm by toggling the alarm off/on, which now clears state.fired).
  • Aircraft breadcrumb trail now draws above the SimBrief route line and waypoint markers. New dedicated map pane liveAcTrailPane at z-index 670 — above routeLinePane (450), the default markerPane (600), and routeLabelPane (650). Geofence labels (afkLabelPane 680) still float above the trail since they're annotations. Both the glow and core polylines now use a dedicated canvas renderer (_liveAcGetTrailRenderer()) pinned to that pane.
  • Active geofences and waypoint-alarm rings re-appear automatically after a browser reload. Alarms themselves always persisted, but their map visuals (geofence rings + labels, waypoint highlight rings) didn't render on reload because the map wasn't initialised yet when _afkInit ran. Map init now explicitly calls _afkRenderGeofenceVisuals() and _afkRenderWaypointVisuals() right after the trail-restore step. Geofence rings appear immediately; waypoint rings restore as soon as the SimBrief flight plan is in memory (see next item).
  • SimBrief flight plan persists across browser reload. The most recently plotted plan is saved to localStorage (simfly_aa_sb_ofp_last) on every successful fetch. On the next page load the plan re-renders silently at map-init time — route line, end-rings, waypoint markers, AND waypoint-alarm rings all reappear without re-clicking SimBrief Flight Plan. The reload-restore path skips the success toast and the fitBounds reframe so it doesn't override your last viewport. Clicking ✕ Clear Flight Plan wipes the cache.
v2.75.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Follow A/C toolbar button — keep the map centred on the live aircraft. New 📍 Follow A/C button appears next to STKP Live / FSUIPC Live while either source is active. Click to lock the map onto the aircraft; every position update smooth-pans (Leaflet's animated panTo, 400 ms tween) so the plane stays centred without jitter on either FSUIPC's 100 ms cadence or STKP's 10 s polling. The map keeps its current zoom — only the centre changes. Manually dragging the map automatically turns Follow off, so panning around to look at something never fights the auto-centre. State persists across sessions in localStorage.
  • Aircraft Data Panel layout is now rock-stationary. Every field has a per-id min-width sized to its worst-case value (lat / lon prefixes, "fpm" arrows, ETA hours, etc.), so values centre under their label and adjacent columns never shift as readings update. The NEXT ALARM field also has a sensible max-width with ellipsis so a long geofence label can't push everything else off-screen.
  • NEXT ALARM "IN" countdown now shows H:MM:SS. Previously formatted as M:SS (so a two-hour countdown read as "120:42"). Now reads as 2:00:42 — hours, minutes, and seconds, always padded.
  • Clickable NEXT ALARM — jump straight to a geofence or waypoint alarm on the map. When the next-firing alarm is a Geofence or Waypoint, the alarm name in the Data Panel becomes a clickable link (dotted underline, accent-colour on hover). Clicking it smooth-flies the map to that alarm's location and turns Follow A/C off first so the recentre actually sticks (otherwise the next live update would snap the view back to the plane). Geofence clicks pick a zoom level that keeps the ring visible. Timer / altitude / phase-change alarms have no map location and remain plain text.
  • Aircraft breadcrumb track persists to localStorage. The green flight trail (fresh + archive tiers, up to ~10,000 nm coverage) now autosaves every 5 s while flying, so a browser close or crash mid-flight no longer loses the path. On reload, the trail polyline is rendered immediately at map-init time — no need to wait for the next live-aircraft update. Manually stopping STKP / FSUIPC no longer wipes the trail either (it used to clear on every source-stop); the trail survives source-switches and idle gaps for the same flight.
  • Track auto-resets when a new flight starts. "New flight" = a fresh SimBrief flight plan is loaded (time_generated differs from the last one we cleared against) AND the aircraft is on the ground (phase = ground, or live data shows GS < 5 kt, or GS < 30 kt with altitude < 1000 ft). The same plan re-fetched after a browser reload is a no-op — the trail is preserved. Loading a new plan mid-flight (e.g., diverting) also preserves the trail. Only the unambiguous "new plan + on-ground" combination wipes the breadcrumb.
v2.74.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • New ⏰ Timer alarm type. The most obvious alarm we'd been missing all along — a simple clock / countdown timer with two modes:
    • Countdown — three discrete inputs for hours, minutes, and seconds. The card readout shows the absolute target time AND the live remaining countdown ("Fires at 14:32:18 (in 5m 42s)"). Updates every second while the Alerts modal is open.
    • At specific time — native HTML time picker for the local clock. If you pick a time that's already passed today, it fires tomorrow at that time. Useful for "alarm me at the FAF altitude at 14:30 local".
    Independent of aircraft position — timers work without a live aircraft source connected. Uses setTimeout for primary firing (so they fire even with the tab in the background, modulo browser throttling) plus a tick-time evaluator as a belt-and-suspenders fallback. The Timer card is listed at the top of the Add alarm column in the Alerts modal.

    Has all the features of the other alarm types: sound picker, sound-duration picker (indefinite / specific seconds), priority via drag-to-reorder, persistence to localStorage. Last-used countdown hours/minutes/seconds AND last-used clock time are remembered between sessions, so creating a new Timer pre-fills with your most recent values. Dismissing a fired timer leaves it in a "spent" state (subtitle reads "✓ Already fired — toggle off/on to re-arm") so it doesn't re-fire from a past targetMs on the next page reload — toggling the alarm off then back on, or editing any of the time inputs, recomputes a fresh target.
  • Collapsible alarm cards in the Alerts modal. Every card now has a small chevron button in its top-right action bar that toggles the editor body (the part with the sound picker, duration, and type-specific inputs). The card header (icon, title, subtitle) stays visible, so you can keep an eye on every alarm's status while hiding the controls you don't need to see. Collapsed state is persisted per-card to localStorage (simfly_aa_afk_collapsed_cards) so it survives reloads. Newly-added alarms default to expanded so you see the configuration options immediately; you collapse them once you're done editing.
v2.73.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Set sound duration directly from the Geofence and Waypoint quick-add modals. Both modals now have a Sound for row matching the one on the alarm cards — pick Indefinite (until dismissed) or Set duration with a seconds input. Previously the duration could only be set after committing the alarm by opening the main Alerts modal and finding the card; now you can pick the duration once during the initial draw / right-click and never have to revisit. The seconds value seeds from your saved preference (or 10 if you haven't picked one yet); changing it writes back to localStorage as the new default, same as on the main cards. Mode always opens at Indefinite so you must explicitly opt-in to seconds — preserves the existing "new alarms default to indefinite" contract.
v2.72.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • "Altitude Minimum" alarm renamed to "Altitude" — now works in both directions. The alarm type formerly known as Altitude Minimum (which only fired when altitude dropped below a threshold) is now called simply Altitude and has a new Direction picker on the card: choose Descending through or Climbing through. Use cases this enables: "wake me when I climb through FL100" for tracking departures, "alarm me at the FAF altitude on approach", etc. Icon updated from ▼ to ↕ to convey bidirectional. Internal alarm type id stayed 'alt-min' so existing localStorage alarms migrate transparently — legacy alarms default to Descending through (their original behaviour).
  • Altitude alarm now requires an actual CROSSING — won't fire if armed on the wrong side of the threshold. Previously, arming a "trip below FL100" alarm while already at FL080 would fire immediately. This was the wrong behaviour — the user expectation is that an altitude alarm should fire when you break through the altitude in the chosen direction, not just because you happen to currently be on the alert side. The evaluator now tracks the previous-tick altitude (alarm.state.prevAlt) and fires ONLY when prev was on the start side AND current is on the other side — an actual crossing event. First tick after arming just seeds the prev-alt sample without firing. Switching the direction picker resets the crossing-detection state so the alarm needs fresh samples before it can fire. ETA calculator updated to honour direction (predicts climbing-through ETAs too).
v2.71.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Per-alarm sound duration — indefinite or auto-stop after N seconds. Every alarm card now has a new Sound for row: choose Indefinite (until dismissed) (the default for new alarms, preserves current behaviour) or Set duration which auto-stops the alarm sound and dismisses it from the trip overlay after a number of seconds you specify. The seconds value defaults to 10 the first time you switch into seconds mode; if you change it, your value is saved to localStorage (simfly_aa_afk_alarm_duration_sec) as the default for the next time you use seconds mode on a different alarm. New alarms always default to indefinite — only switching the mode picker to seconds applies your saved preference. Auto-dismiss timers are properly cleared when an alarm is manually dismissed, disabled, deleted, or when Disable All / Delete All runs — never a stale timer leaking past its alarm.
  • [Enter] = submit on the per-geofence and per-waypoint quick-add modals. After drawing a geofence or right-clicking a waypoint, you can now press Enter to commit the alarm without reaching for the Add Alarm button. Skips when focus is on a button (so Tab-to-Cancel + Enter still cancels). The main Alerts config modal is a list manager (no single primary action) so it's intentionally not bound — Enter still toggles dropdowns / commits text inputs there as normal.
  • Toolbar reorder: ✕ Clear route moved before ✈ SimBrief Flight Plan. The dot-routing Clear route button was previously buried at the end of the toolbar (after the Alerts button); moved up to sit immediately after 📐 Measurement for better logical grouping — route controls now cluster together near the start of the toolbar.
v2.70.1 May 23, 2026 Fix
  • Geofence map label wording updated. The persistent label hovering above each armed geofence now reads "Practice · 5.0 nm — Alert on Entry" instead of "Practice · 5.0 nm · ENTER". For exit-trigger fences it reads "Alert on Exit"; for both-direction fences it reads "Alert on Entry & Exit". The alarm-card subtitle in the Alerts modal got the same wording for consistency.
  • Geofence labels now float above SimBrief waypoint labels. Previously the geofence label marker lived in Leaflet's default markerPane (z-index 600), while SimBrief waypoint pill labels lived in the tooltipPane (z-index 650) — so the waypoint pills rendered over any geofence label that happened to fall behind them. New custom afkLabelPane at z-index 680 hosts both the persistent geofence labels and the live radius readout during a draw. Result: geofence labels are always visible regardless of waypoint label placement.
  • Right-click on a SimBrief waypoint now actually opens the quick-add modal. v2.70.0 wired the contextmenu handler on the waypoint marker (a tiny 8×8 px SVG circle), but users naturally right-click the much larger white pill label — and Leaflet tooltips ship with pointer-events: none, so right-clicks on the pill fell through to whatever was behind it. Three fixes: (1) the .sb-ofp-wpt-label pill now has pointer-events: auto plus a cursor: context-menu hint; (2) a contextmenu listener is attached directly to the tooltip's DOM element, so right-clicking the pill opens the quick-add modal for that fix; (3) the underlying waypoint dot radius bumped from 3.5 → 5 (visual difference is negligible, hit target is meaningfully easier).
v2.70.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Right-click any SimBrief waypoint to add a per-fix alarm. Each plotted waypoint dot now responds to a right-click by opening a small quick-add modal pre-populated with the waypoint's ident and lat/lng position. Pick a sound (with ▶ preview) and click Add Alarm — the app creates a scope: 'specific' waypoint alarm targeting just that fix and immediately renders its cyan ring overlay on the route. If a specific-waypoint alarm already covers that ident, an amber warning hint appears in the modal so you don't unintentionally double-arm. Suppresses the browser's native context menu so the right-click cleanly routes to the alarm UI.
  • Geofence draw mode — right-mouse drag now pans the map. Previously, attempting to right-drag during a geofence draw would either do nothing or cancel the in-progress draw (because the trailing contextmenu event fired on mouse release). Two fixes: (1) _afkGfOnDown now ignores any mousedown whose button isn't left (so right-mouse no longer initiates a draw at the click point), and (2) _afkGeofenceCancel honours the existing region-map _rgRightPanJustEnded sentinel so a right-drag release doesn't trigger the cancel path. Result: right-mouse drag pans freely while drawing a geofence circle, matching the rest of the map's right-pan affordance. Pure right-click still cancels the draw.
  • Geofence radius readout floats above all other map content. The live "X.XX nm" label that follows the cursor during a geofence draw was previously at the default zIndexOffset, so on busy maps with permanent waypoint labels or other AA overlays it could end up partially obscured. Bumped to zIndexOffset: 10000 so it always renders on top during a draw. Persistent post-commit geofence labels stay at the lower offset so they layer alongside the rest of the AA overlays.
v2.69.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Alerts — configurable cockpit alarms for long flights. New 🔔 Alerts button in the map header opens a configuration modal where five independent alarm types can be armed in any combination for a single flight:
    • ⊙ Geofence — pick an anchor first: Departure airport or Arrival airport (the fence auto-relocates to wherever DEP/ARR sits in the loaded SimBrief plan — reusable across every flight), or Draw on map for a fixed-coords circle (click-and-drag, radius shown live in nm). Choose ENTER, EXIT, or both as the trigger. Plan-relative fences re-anchor automatically whenever a new SimBrief plan loads; their map label shows @DEP KJFK / @ARR EGLL so they're obvious at a glance. Switch any existing fence between Coords / DEP / ARR via the Anchor dropdown on its alarm card.
    • 📍 Waypoint — alerts when the aircraft passes a SimBrief flight-plan waypoint, using closest-point-of-approach (CPA) detection so it fires even when your track is a few nm off the planned line. Skips on the ground, resets on sim restart, cascades tight clusters, and falls back to a "near-miss" check for STKP's coarse 10 s polling. Pick Every waypoint, TOC, TOD, or a specific multi-select. Selected fixes get a cyan ring overlay on the route plot.
    • ─ Cruise Reached and ↘ Descent Started — one-shot alarms using an adaptive detector that tracks the peak climb VS for this flight and treats cruise / descent thresholds as a fraction of it. Works for both a Cessna 152 and a Boeing 777 without per-aircraft tuning.
    • ▼ Altitude Minimum — trips when MSL altitude drops below a configurable threshold. Input accepts both plain feet (10000) and flight levels (FL350, FL 200); the hint next to the input echoes the parsed value as you type.
    Each alarm has its own enable toggle, sound selection (5 synthesized tones — Digital Clock, Klaxon, Soft Chime, Urgent Beeper, Gentle Bell; ▶ preview without arming), and a ⋮⋮ drag handle for reordering. Hover any alarm icon or title for a plain-English description of what it does and when it fires. List order is priority order — drag cards up or down to re-rank, and the highest-priority tripped alarm’s sound is what plays. When an alarm trips, a full-screen overlay covers the map with a pulsing red ALARM header and the looping sound continues until DISMISS ALL is clicked. The browser tab title also flashes ⚠ ALARM — SimFly as a cross-window cue. Only one sound ever plays at a time; dismissing the leading alarm seamlessly swaps audio to the next-highest still-tripped one. The modal footer offers Disable All (disarms everything, clears map visuals, stops audio — config preserved for individual re-enable) and Delete All (permanently removes every alarm from the queue). Alarms persist to localStorage; if the loaded SimBrief plan changes (different origin/destination/fix count), specific-waypoint picks are auto-cleared with a toast notification.
  • Aircraft track / trail. While a live source is active, AA draws a green breadcrumb polyline (outer glow + bright inner core) behind the aircraft showing the actual path flown. Intentionally a different colour from the SimBrief route line so deviations are obvious. Uses a two-tier sampling strategy: the last ~75 nm at fine detail (0.05 nm spacing, 1,500-point cap) for zoom-in inspection of recent turns / holds / patterns, plus an archive tier at coarser 1 nm spacing (10,000-point cap) for the rest of the flight. Older points are decimated when they age out — straight cruise legs collapse from thousands of fresh samples to far fewer archive points without losing the visible shape of the trail. Total capacity: 11,500 points (~184 KB) covering ~10,000 nm — enough for any non-stop commercial flight on Earth (KJFK→YSSY ~9,950 nm, SIN→EWR ~9,500 nm). Auto-clears on source-stop and on large position jumps (sim restart). Visible on both street and satellite map backgrounds.
  • SimBrief waypoint labels — always-readable design. Permanent waypoint labels along the plotted route now render as white pills with dark navy text instead of using the user's chosen route colour for the text. Previous behaviour failed when the user picked a light route colour (yellow, light cyan, etc.) — text became unreadable against light terrain or the light-mode UI. The pill shape, dark border, and drop shadow keep the labels legible on street maps, satellite imagery, and light-mode UIs alike. Route polyline still follows the user's colour choice.
  • Aircraft Data Panel — persistent live-flight readout at the bottom of the map. Three sections in Rajdhani tabular-numeric:
    • Position & motion — LAT, LON, HDG, ALT MSL, AGL, GS, IAS, VS (with directional arrow), PHASE. ALT MSL = altitude above mean sea level; AGL = altitude above ground level, computed from FSUIPC ground elevation (offset 0x0B4C, int32 m×256). STKP has no ground-elevation field, so AGL shows — under that source. VS is computed locally from a rolling altitude buffer (works uniformly for both sources).
    • Flight progress — ELAPSED (since takeoff, detected when GS > 40 kt sustained 10 s), REMAINING, DIST TO GO, ETA LOCAL, ETA UTC. SimBrief flight plan required for destination-relative fields; ELAPSED works without one.
    • Next alarm — the soonest-tripping armed alarm + countdown (M:SS), pulses red when under 60 s. Geofence, waypoint, TOC, TOD, and Altitude Minimum are ETA-predictable; cruise and descent depend on future events and are not.
    The panel collapses to a single-line strip via the ⏷ chevron; collapsed state persists across sessions. Refreshes at 1 Hz regardless of source cadence, so elapsed and countdown fields tick smoothly even between live-aircraft updates.
  • FSUIPC IAS offset added. Live-aircraft FSUIPC reader now subscribes to offset 0x02BC (uint, knots × 128) and exposes it as ias on the live-aircraft payload for the data panel. STKP’s public payload doesn’t expose IAS separately, so the IAS field shows — under that source.
  • Help modal updated with full sections covering AFK Alerts and the Aircraft Data Panel (per CLAUDE.md §3).
v2.68.1 May 23, 2026 Fix
  • Flight Plan Settings modal now opens above the map — v2.68.0 shipped the new settings modal at the standard .modal-overlay z-index (1000), but the region-map modal sits at z-index 9000, so shift-clicking the SimBrief Flight Plan button appeared to do nothing — the settings modal opened underneath the map and was only visible after closing the map. #fpSettingsModal is now pinned to z-index: 9500 so it stacks above the map overlay. No behaviour change beyond visibility.
v2.68.0 May 23, 2026 Feature
  • Flight Plan Settings modal — the SimBrief button is renamed from ✈ SimBrief OFP to ✈ SimBrief Flight Plan, and shift-clicking it now opens a settings modal where you can change three things, all persisted to localStorage:
    • SimBrief Pilot ID — previously the shift-click cleared the stored ID and re-prompted; it now goes through the modal’s text input. The first-click prompt for users without a stored ID is unchanged.
    • Display waypoint labels (default On) — when on, each waypoint’s ident is shown as a permanent label next to its dot along the route. When off, the original behaviour is preserved: idents and detail (altitude, via-airway, climb/cruise/descent stage) appear on hover only.
    • Route color (default magenta #ff3da8) — a colour picker plus a hex input. Applies to the polyline glow / core, waypoint dots, and origin / destination rings in one pass.
    If a flight plan is already plotted when you save the modal, AA re-renders it in place with the new settings — no re-fetch, no network round-trip. The new modal lives under #fpSettingsModal with toggle / colour-picker styling that matches the rest of the UI.
  • STKP Live button hidden — the SimToolkitPro live-aircraft button is hidden in this release. All STKP plumbing (poll loop, token storage, auth-handshake handling, mutex with FSUIPC) is intentionally preserved in the JS so the source can be re-enabled in a future release without rewriting the integration. The STKP help section is removed from the help modal; its full text is archived in Kyndra’s local memory at project_aa_stkp_help_text.md for restoration.
  • FSUIPC is now the primary Live Aircraft source. The help modal’s “Live Aircraft Tracking” section is rewritten to cover only the FSUIPC path (FSUIPC7 install, Paul Henty’s WebSocket Server add-on, free unregistered tier covers position reads).
v2.67.1 May 23, 2026 Fix
  • FSUIPC Live — marker now renders for parked / slow aircraft — v2.67.0 sent changesOnly: true on the FSUIPC subscription, which the Paul Henty server interprets as “only push when at least one offset value changes”. For users whose aircraft was parked or taxiing slowly at low precision, no fields changed between polls, so the server never pushed and the green plane icon never appeared on the map — even though the WebSocket was happily connected. changesOnly is now removed; the server pushes at every interval regardless. Polling interval also relaxed from 100 ms to 500 ms — still smooth (2 Hz) but lighter on the loopback. Bonus: incoming WS frames are now logged to the browser console (prefix [fsuipc]) when FSUIPC Live is active, so any future protocol surprises can be diagnosed without paste-test workarounds. Toggle _FSUIPC_DEBUG = false at the top of the FSUIPC module to silence the logging.
v2.67.0 May 22, 2026 Feature
  • Live aircraft — FSUIPC source added alongside SimToolkitPro — the map header now offers two live-aircraft sources. The existing 🛫 STKP Live button is unchanged (10 s cloud polling, requires the STKP desktop client + your STKP token). A new 🔌 FSUIPC Live button connects to FSUIPC7 + Paul Henty’s WebSocket Server running locally on the user’s PC (ws://localhost:2048/fsuipc/ with the fsuipc subprotocol). Updates push every 100 ms (100× faster than STKP polling), no token, no cloud round-trip, no Start-Flight handshake. Reads FSUIPC offsets 0x0560 (lat), 0x0568 (lon), 0x0574 (alt m), 0x0580 (true heading), 0x02B4 (ground speed) and applies the necessary unit conversions client-side. Free unregistered FSUIPC7 covers position reads — users do not need a paid FSUIPC licence for this feature. Only one source is active at a time — activating either stops the other. Both render through one shared marker manager (_liveAc*), so the rotating-plane icon, tooltip (callsign, aircraft type, route, phase, altitude, ground speed, heading, age, source label), stale-state grayout at 30 s, and first-fix recentre all behave identically. Auto-reconnect at 10 s while FSUIPC tracking is active, so MSFS restarts no longer require re-clicking the button. Mixed-content note: AA is served over HTTPS, but browsers exempt localhost from mixed-content blocking (W3C “potentially trustworthy” rule), so the ws:// connection works from a GitHub Pages page without TLS on the local server.
v2.66.1 May 22, 2026 Improved
  • Live Aircraft onboarding flags the STKP “Start Flight” requirement — SimToolkitPro’s public API only authorises your token after you click Start Flight in the STKP desktop app’s Flight Planning tab. If you launch STKP after you’re already airborne, the desktop map still tracks you locally but the web API returns Authfail until that handshake fires. The token-entry prompt, the help-modal section, and the Authfail error toast now all explain this gotcha and point at the fix (load any flight plan — even a dummy one — and click Start Flight; allow ~30 s for cloud sync). No code-level behaviour change; pure UX/documentation improvement.
v2.66.0 May 22, 2026 Feature
  • Live aircraft tracking via SimToolkitPro — a new 🛫 Live Aircraft button in the map header polls the public SimToolkitPro API every 10 seconds (their published rate-limit floor) for your own aircraft’s real-time position and draws a rotating green plane icon on the map at your reported lat/lon, oriented to true heading. Hover the plane to see callsign, aircraft type, dep→dest, flight phase, altitude, ground speed, heading, and how long ago the position was last updated. On first click the button prompts once for your personal SimToolkitPro API token (generate one at simtoolkitpro.co.uk/account/apikeys); the token is saved locally in localStorage and never leaves your browser. Shift-click the button to change the stored token; clicking while tracking stops polling. The icon grays out automatically if no new position arrives for 30 s (sim paused, MSFS exited, or STKP desktop client offline). The first successful fix re-centres the map so your plane is on-screen. Requirements: SimToolkitPro desktop client installed and running locally; AA users without STKP can ignore the button entirely. CORS, auth, and TOS all verified — no proxy, no Kyndra-hosted infrastructure, no local server.
v2.65.1 May 22, 2026 Feature
  • Plot SimBrief OFP — moved to the main map header — the ✈ SimBrief OFP button now lives in the interactive map’s header bar, immediately to the right of the 📐 Measurement tool, so it is reachable any time the map is open (not just while the Flight Planner’s Route Map panel is expanded). The polylines now use the dedicated canvas geometry renderer per the AA drawn-geometry convention, so long routes do not slow the map down at high zoom. Status feedback — fetching, success, and error — now surfaces through the standard bottom-right toast pill, and the route summary stays available by hovering the magenta origin or destination ring on the map.
v2.65.0 May 22, 2026 Feature
  • Plot SimBrief OFP on the map — a new ✈ SimBrief OFP button fetches your most recent SimBrief OFP via SimBrief’s public API and draws the full route — origin, every navlog waypoint, and destination — as a magenta polyline with waypoint dots that show ident, altitude (FL or ft), the via-airway, and the climb/cruise/descent stage on hover. The first click prompts once for your SimBrief Pilot ID (saved locally in localStorage) so subsequent clicks fetch instantly. Shift-click the button to change the stored Pilot ID; clicking the button while an OFP is plotted clears it.
v2.64.3 May 22, 2026 Fix
  • File on SimBrief now resolves short airport codes — airports that SimFly identifies with a short local code (e.g. 1G4) were passed to SimBrief verbatim, but SimBrief expects the ICAO form (K1G4) and couldn’t find them. The dispatch URL now resolves the departure and arrival codes through the same OurAirports lookup the rest of the app already uses, so the US (K), Canada (CY/C) and Australia (Y) prefix conventions are handled automatically — SimBrief receives a code it recognises. Codes with no clean ICAO equivalent are left untouched.
v2.64.2 May 21, 2026 Fix
  • Consistent cursor over measurement handles — hovering a measurement’s resize handle, move handle or close × now reliably shows the matching cursor (resize arrows, move hand, pointer). Previously the cursor could flicker to a plain pointer whenever an airport dot happened to sit beneath the handle — the dot layer’s hover cursor was winning. The handle cursors now take priority everywhere.
v2.64.1 May 21, 2026 Improved
  • Right-click to exit measurement mode — while measurement mode is active, a plain right-click on an empty area of the map now turns it off — a quick way out without reaching for the toolbar button. Right-click drag still pans the map, and right-clicking an airport dot still opens its Quick-Rate popover, so neither is affected.
v2.64.0 May 21, 2026 Feature
  • Distance display toggle — rings or lines — a new toggle in the map header (shown while measurement mode is active) switches between ◯ Distance Ring — the full radius circle — and ╱ Distance Line — just the connecting lines, origin pins and distance labels. The button label always shows the current mode. Hiding the rings keeps a busy multi-segment route readable; the setting also applies to measurements drawn afterwards.
  • Tick marks on the radius line — every measurement line now carries small perpendicular tick marks at its 25%, 50% and 75% points, so you can gauge intermediate distances along the line at a glance.
  • Close × moved onto the distance label — the per-measurement remove control is now a × badge on the corner of the distance label rather than a separate handle on the ring. It stays reachable even when the ring is hidden, and is positioned so it never overlaps the line, pin or resize handle.
  • Route total shows direct distance in kilometres — the route summary chip’s straight-line direct figure now appears in kilometres alongside nautical miles, matching the cumulative total.
  • Longer window to extend a route — the time allowed between finishing a measurement circle and re-clicking to continue the route has been increased to 0.8 seconds for a more relaxed gesture.
  • Steadier measurement cursor — in measurement mode the cursor now stays a crosshair everywhere except over a measurement’s move handle or resize handle, where it becomes a grab or resize cursor.
  • Uniform map header button heights — map header buttons previously varied by 1–2 px in height depending on whether their icon was an emoji or a plain text symbol. A fixed line-height now makes every header button render at exactly the same height.
v2.63.0 May 21, 2026 Feature
  • Continuous-path measurement — chain circles into a route — after you finish drawing a measurement circle, release the mouse and immediately click again to start a new circle that begins exactly where the last one ended. Each conjoined leg adds to a continuous route, so you can measure a multi-stop path leg by leg. A brief window after each release distinguishes “continue the route” from grabbing the resize handle — pause for a moment and the just-drawn circle’s handles work as normal.
  • Route total distance chip — once a route has two or more legs, a distinct accent-bordered chip appears at the route’s end showing the cumulative distance (Σ total, in both nautical miles and kilometres), the leg count, and the straight-line direct distance from the route’s start to its end — so you can see at a glance how far the path deviates from a direct great-circle line. Each individual leg still shows its own distance and bearing label.
  • Route stays conjoined when you edit it — dragging the centre or edge handle of any circle in a route moves the shared waypoint, so adjacent legs follow along and the path never pulls apart. The total chip updates live as you adjust. Standalone (non-route) measurements drag exactly as before.
  • Deleting a route leg heals the gap — removing a circle from the middle of a route no longer leaves a break: the following leg re-anchors onto the previous leg's endpoint, so the route stays one continuous chain with one fewer leg. Deleting the first or last leg simply trims that end.
  • Auto-scroll while measuring — when you draw a measurement circle and the cursor reaches the edge of the map, the view now auto-scrolls in that direction so the circle's radius can grow well beyond the visible area — the same edge-pan behaviour used when placing a region. Scrolling stops as soon as you release.
v2.62.9 May 20, 2026 Improved
  • Region distance label now shows kilometres — the departure-to-arrival distance on the line between the two region circles now displays kilometres alongside nautical miles (e.g. 4,096 nm · 7,586 km), matching the measurement ruler label.
v2.62.8 May 20, 2026 Fix
  • Aircraft range ring now drawn as a true geodesic circle — the dashed gold range ring on the region map used Leaflet’s built-in circle, which distorts at long range and breaks entirely for high-range aircraft (a 5,000+ nm ring runs off a pole). It now uses the same real great-circle drawing as the measurement ring, so it is accurate at any range.
  • Measurement range ring is shaded again when it does not span a pole — the v2.62.7 ring fix dropped the ring’s faint fill tint. The fill is back: the ring is a filled disc whenever it is an ordinary circle, and automatically switches to an unfilled outline only when it is large enough to wrap a pole or the antimeridian (where a filled shape can’t be drawn sensibly).
v2.62.7 May 20, 2026 Fix
  • Measurement range ring now drawn as a true geodesic circle — on long measurements the dashed range ring drifted away from the cursor, and the close ✕ and resize handles appeared detached from it. The ring was drawn with Leaflet’s built-in circle, which is an approximation that distorts badly at continental distances and breaks entirely once the ring is large enough to reach over a pole. It is now built from real great-circle points, so the ring always passes exactly through the line endpoint, the resize handle, and the close ✕ handle — at any distance.
v2.62.6 May 20, 2026 Improved
  • Distance matrix corner reordered to ARR / DEP — the top-left corner cell of the main distance matrix now shows ARR → on top and DEP ↓ below, swapping the previous order.
  • Region-map swap button now uses the standard orange pill — the ⇄ swap control between the route ICAO chips on the region map was a plain text glyph. It now uses the same orange-oval pill button as the Departure / Arrival owner swap on the main screen, so the swap control looks consistent everywhere.
v2.62.5 May 20, 2026 Fix
  • Duplicate weather block in region-map route tooltips fixed — hovering the departure or arrival airport chip on the region map sometimes showed the decoded METAR / weather section twice. The chip tooltip was appending its own weather block on top of the one the shared tooltip builder already includes; the redundant copy has been removed so weather now appears once.
  • Aircraft range now evaluated in region-map route tooltips — with an aircraft selected, hovering the arrival airport chip on the region map now shows the in-range / out-of-range check against the aircraft’s range, just like the main-screen matrix tooltips. Previously the chip tooltips did not know which endpoint was the departure, so the arrival’s range line was silently dropped. Each chip’s tooltip now shows the suitability verdict for its own role — takeoff for the departure, landing plus range for the arrival.
v2.62.4 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Filter breadcrumb strip nudged up — small vertical alignment tweak to the region-map filter chip row.
v2.62.3 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Larger filter breadcrumb chips — the at-a-glance filter status chips on the region map are now 25% bigger (font, padding, and ✕ control all scaled up) for easier reading and clicking.
  • Readable multi-item tooltips on the Countries / Regions / Continents chips — hovering a multi-item filter chip (e.g. 3 Regions) previously showed the list in the browser’s tiny native tooltip. It now uses the same styled popover as the N Owners Displayed chip — same Rajdhani font, size, and panel styling — so the names are properly legible.
  • Region map search placeholder is easier to read — the faint “Search ICAO, name, owner…” placeholder text was too dim against the dark search box; its contrast has been raised.
v2.62.2 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Edge auto-pan now works while aiming the arrival region — v2.62.1 added edge auto-pan for dragging a region circle; it now also applies during the ruler phase, after the departure region is locked and while you move the cursor to choose where the arrival region will go. Reaching any map edge smoothly pans the map so the arrival can be placed far from the departure.
  • Map re-zooms to fit both regions after an auto-panned move — when you release the mouse after a drag in which the map auto-panned, the map now re-fits both the departure and arrival region circles into view (the same fit used when the regions are first drawn). If a drag didn’t trigger any auto-pan, the view is left alone so small nudges don’t cause the map to jump.
v2.62.1 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Map auto-scrolls when you drag a region to the edge — dragging a departure or arrival region center near the edge of the map now smoothly pans the map in that direction, so regions can be moved farther apart than the visible area would otherwise allow. The pan speed ramps up the closer to the edge the cursor sits, and the region circle keeps tracking the cursor as the map slides underneath.
  • Tighter zoom when the cross-region matrix opens — the map now fits the two region circles with a 5% margin (was 10%) and allows a closer maximum zoom, so the regions fill more of the available map area instead of sitting small with wide empty borders.
  • Region matrix Sort menu spacing — when both axes are pivot-sorted, the dual label now has more breathing room around the divider — KSEA ▲   |   KPDX ▼.
v2.62.0 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Independent per-axis pivot sorting — rows and columns no longer jump — both the owner distance matrix and the cross-region matrix previously used a single pivot: clicking a ⊙ on one axis snapped the other axis back to alphabetical, so the row or column you just clicked could shift out from under the cursor. Each axis now has its own independent pivot. A departure-row ⊙ only ever reorders the arrival columns; an arrival-column ⊙ only ever reorders the departure rows. Because clicking a ⊙ never touches its own axis, the header you click physically cannot move. You can also now sort both axes at once — e.g. arrival columns by distance to one departure airport while departure rows are sorted by distance to one arrival airport.
  • Region matrix Sort menu shows both pivots — when a pivot is active, the Sort dropdown now displays each active pivot as an ICAO followed by a ▲ (nearest) or ▼ (farthest) glyph — the same arrows used on the matrix-header dots — e.g. KSEA ▲  |  KPDX ▼ when both axes are sorted, or just KSFO ▲ when one is. The menu was widened to fit the dual label. Choosing any plain sort option (A–Z, Group by owner, Group by category) clears both pivots.
v2.61.19 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Category clusters now sit directly above their filter buttons — the Dep / Arr category circle groups were centered on the full left/right halves of the block, but the filter buttons (capped narrower) sat slightly inboard of those half-centers, so the two rows didn't line up. The category row now uses the same geometry as the button row — matching gap and per-group max-width — so each cluster centers directly above its button. The thin vertical divider between the two groups was removed; it was redundant now that the groups are clearly separated and individually labeled.
v2.61.18 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Filter buttons centered as an equal-width pair — reverted the v2.61.17 per-half positioning. The Filter airports unsuitable… and Hide filtered airports buttons are once again centered together as a pair, and both now render at the same width — previously Hide filtered airports sized to its shorter label and looked noticeably narrower than its neighbour.
v2.61.17 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Filter buttons centered beneath their category clusters — the Filter airports unsuitable… and Hide filtered airports buttons were centered together as a pair in the middle of the row. Each button now sits in its own half of the filter block, centered within that half — so they line up neatly beneath the Dep and Arr category clusters above them. (When no aircraft is selected, only Hide filtered airports shows, centered in the right half beneath the Arr cluster.)
v2.61.16 May 19, 2026 Fix
  • Dep / Arr category clusters centered on their half of the screen — v2.61.15 spread the two category groups with space-between, which pushed them hard against the left and right margins on wide displays. Each group now takes an equal half of the filter block and centers its own contents, so the Dep cluster sits centered in the left half and the Arr cluster centered in the right half.
v2.61.15 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Distance filter block scales with the wider layout — after the v2.61.13 main-screen widening, the Distance slider, the Dep / Arr category filters, and the two filter buttons stayed at their old narrow widths, leaving them clustered in the middle of the screen. All three rows now share a common 1280px block width (filling 100% on narrower screens, capping on 4K): the distance slider widened from its fixed 750 px cap, the Dep and Arr category groups now spread to the block edges instead of huddling around the center separator, and the two filter buttons flex-grow to share the row width.
  • Help — Category Filter section corrected — the section still said the category circles sit “below the sort pills” (a control removed long ago) and described a single filter group.
