  • Rate popover — airport name HTML escaped — the Quick-Rate popover now escapes &, <, >, and " in the airport name before injecting it as innerHTML, preventing DOM corruption from names that include special characters.
  • Scenery Library modal focus — requestAnimationFrame instead of setTimeout — the search field inside the Scenery Library modal now acquires focus on the next paint frame rather than after a fixed 50 ms delay, making focus reliable even under CPU load while avoiding an arbitrary timer.
  • Measure NM label — null guard — _rgShowNmLabel now verifies both the label element and map container exist before reading getBoundingClientRect(), preventing a crash if the region map has not yet been initialised when a mouse event fires.
  • Scan cancel — optional chaining on search field — _slScanCancel now uses ?.value when reading the search field, so cancelling a scan before the DOM is fully ready can no longer throw a TypeError.
  • Owner lookup cache — skip rebuild when already warm — _rgBuildOwnerLookup() is now only called when _rgOwnerLookupCache is null; because refreshOwnerDropdowns already clears the cache on every data change (v2.35.2), redundant rebuilds during a single render pass are eliminated.
v2.35.3 May 9, 2026 Improved
  • Owner color palette expanded — 15 → 64 colors — the region map dot and legend palette now contains 64 hand-curated colors spanning the full hue wheel. With 15 colors, the birthday-problem odds of two pilots in a 6-person group sharing a color exceeded 65%; the 64-color palette reduces this to around 20% for the same group size. All colors are tuned for the dual-mode constraint: L 52–68% for most hues (readable on both the default CartoDB street tiles and satellite tiles), dropping to L 47–55% for yellows and yellow-greens which appear perceptually bright on light backgrounds. Note: because the palette size changed, all existing pilot-to-color assignments will shift — this is expected.
v2.35.2 May 9, 2026 Fix
  • Unresolvable pilot warning — when a pilot's airports are all absent from the airport database, a console.warn now fires once per cache rebuild (DevTools › Console) naming the pilot and their unresolvable ICAOs, instead of silently dropping them from the matrix with no indication.
  • Declination fetch — stale-write guard — if the user cancels a measurement while the NOAA declination API request is still in flight, the resolved value is now discarded rather than written to _rgMeasureDecl, preventing a stale result from corrupting the next measurement's magnetic bearing.
  • Matrix debounce — phase and null guard — the 30 ms yield inside _rgComputeAndRender now checks that _rgPhase === 'results' and that both circle centres are non-null before computing airport lists, preventing a dereference crash if state changes during the yield.
  • Owner lookup cache invalidated on re-import — calling refreshOwnerDropdowns (triggered by every KML re-import) now clears _rgOwnerLookupCache, so region-map tooltips immediately reflect the new owner data instead of showing stale associations until the next matrix render.
  • Region matrix HTML escaper — > added — _rgX() now escapes > in addition to &, ", and <, closing the last gap that could produce malformed HTML from names containing a right-angle bracket.
  • Measure handle drag — NaN index guard — the mousedown hit-test handler now validates the parsed index before accessing _rgMeasureSaved[idx]; a malformed hit string can no longer produce a NaN index that throws on the subsequent .origin.lat access.
v2.35.1 May 9, 2026 Fix
  • Excel export — CDN load guard — if the SheetJS library fails to load from its CDN, the export button now shows a clear error message instead of crashing silently with an unhandled ReferenceError.
  • Owner dropdown — HTML escaping — owner names are now fully escaped before being inserted into dropdown option and custom list HTML, preventing DOM corruption or script injection from names containing <, >, or & characters.
  • Measure mode — click-without-drag crash — fixed a TypeError thrown when the user clicked (mousedown then mouseup with no drag) while in measure mode; the position state is now null-checked in both mouse-up handlers before use.
v2.35.0 May 9, 2026 Feature
  • Pilot group launch from Recip Tracker — accepts a new pilots= URL parameter containing a comma-separated list of owner names. When present, the app opens directly to the region map with the Owner filter pre-set to exactly those pilots — all other owners are hidden. Triggered by the new 🗺 Send to AA → button in Recip Tracker's Balance Queue selection mode.
v2.34.6 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Payout filter — matrix now respects threshold — dragging the payout slider now also filters the route matrix, removing rows and columns for airports below the threshold. Toggling payout mode on or off also re-renders the matrix accordingly.
v2.34.5 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Region map toolbar — separator before Payout button — added a vertical divider between the Measure Distance group and the Payout button, matching the existing separator between Payout and Scenery.
v2.34.4 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Payout filter label — improved legibility — changed the "Hide < N%" text in the payout legend from dark orange to golden amber (#ffcc44) so it reads clearly against the dark panel background.
v2.34.3 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Quick-Rate popover — 50% larger — increased all font sizes, padding, spacing, and min-width across the right-click/long-press rating popover to match the resized hover tooltip. Interactive star size also scaled up via a scoped override so the rating stars remain proportionate.
v2.34.2 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Region map dot tooltips — 50% larger — increased font sizes, padding, and spacing across all tooltip elements (ICAO, category, name, owner, payout, runway, scenery badge, star rating) for easier reading at arm's length.
v2.34.1 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Payout filter slider no longer pans the map — dragging the slider was also triggering Leaflet’s map-pan handler. Fixed by stopping mouse and touch event propagation at the legend container so Leaflet never sees the drag-start.
  • Pilot Payout % restored to region map dot tooltips — region map dots use a separate tooltip template (_rgBindDotTip) that was never wired to payout data. Now shows a color-coded Pilot Payout: N% line (green ≥50%, gold ≥30%, orange >0%, grey for 0%) whenever payout data is loaded, for every airport including those at 0%.
v2.34.0 May 8, 2026 Feature
  • Payout legend — filter slider — the Payout mode legend now doubles as a live filter. Drag the slider on the gradient bar to hide airports whose pilot payout % falls below the selected threshold. A dark overlay fills the filtered zone of the bar, a white thumb shows the current cutoff, and an orange Hide < N% label appears below. Releasing payout mode resets the filter automatically. Scenery center dots remain visible at all times in payout mode.
  • Payout legend — 50% larger — increased padding, font sizes, circle indicator, and gradient bar dimensions by approximately 50% for easier reading at arm's length.
v2.33.3 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Payout map — more vibrant colors — raised saturation to 100% and lightness to 60% at the red end, 45% at the green end, producing vivid salmon→orange→yellow→lime→green dots that read clearly on both street and satellite tiles.
  • Zero-payout airports — visible grey instead of near-black — airports at 0% (or absent from the payout data) now render as mid-grey (#7a7a90) in payout mode; the previous charcoal color was indistinguishable from black on a dark map.
  • Payout map legend repositioned — legend moved to top-right below the satellite toggle button (z-index raised to 1001) to keep it clear of the map zoom controls and any bottom-left overlays.
  • Scenery center dots suppressed in payout mode — the small black center dot that marks airports in your Scenery Library is hidden while payout mode is active so it does not add unintended black pixels over vivid payout colors.
v2.33.2 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Payout map — vivid hue gradient — the Payout map mode now uses a full red→orange→yellow→green color sweep instead of a green-only lightness ramp, making low, mid, and high payout airports clearly distinguishable at a glance. Dot fill is fully opaque in payout mode so the hues read clearly against any map layer. Zero-payout airports render as charcoal (#3a3a52) rather than a light grey. The legend now shows the hue sweep with a charcoal dot marking 0%.
  • Payout % in all tooltips — when payout data is loaded every airport tooltip now shows a Pilot Payout line, including airports at 0% or absent from the payout data (previously these were silently omitted). Color coding: green ≥50%, gold ≥30%, orange >0%, muted grey for 0%.
v2.33.1 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Payout data loaded from Active Airports Gist — SimFly_Pilot_Payouts.json is now fetched from the shared Active Airports Gist on every load alongside categories and overrides. This makes payout data portable — any AA user sees the same payout percentages without needing the KML Generator or a local import step. The Gist is the authoritative source; localStorage is updated from it automatically and serves as a pre-load cache and offline fallback.
v2.33.0 May 8, 2026 Feature
  • Pilot Payout Map mode — a new 💰 Payout toggle button in the Region Mode header re-colors all airport dots on a green gradient based on pilot payout percentage: light green near 0%, deep forest green at 60%+, grey where no data is available. A legend in the lower-left corner of the map displays the scale while the mode is active. Click the button again to return to standard coloring.
  • Payout % in airport tooltips — when payout data is loaded, a color-coded Pilot Payout line appears in every airport tooltip directly after the owner name (deep green for ≥50%, lime for ≥30%, muted for lower values).
  • Payout data sourced from KML Generator — payout percentages are imported via the Pilot Payout Data panel in SimFly KML Generator (v2.20.3+), which writes them to localStorage under simfly_payout_db. Active Airports reads that key automatically on load — no manual transfer step required.
v2.32.2 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery Library — addon/sim text no longer disrupts star alignment — rows that had an addon or type value (e.g. MSFS, Orbx EU North) were rendering it as a chip between the rating badge and the delete button, which pushed those rows’ stars out of column alignment with their neighbours. The chip is now suppressed in the row layout so every row’s stars line up exactly. The data itself is still preserved on each entry — it round-trips through Export / Import JSON, and the search box still matches against it — it’s just no longer rendered as an inline chip in the list.
v2.32.1 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Quick-Rate — long-press fallback for touch devices — holding any airport dot for 600 ms now opens the Quick-Rate popover, in addition to the existing right-click gesture. This makes rating from the map work on iPad / iPhone and any other device without a working right-click. The subsequent click that fires when the finger or mouse button is released is suppressed (via a per-marker fired-flag) so it doesn’t also trigger route selection. Releasing early, dragging, panning, or moving the cursor off the dot all cancel the press cleanly.
v2.32.0 May 8, 2026 Feature
  • Quick-Rate popover — right-click any airport dot to rate it — right-clicking an airport dot on the region map now opens a small floating popover anchored to that dot. The popover shows the ICAO and airport name, the same big interactive 5-star widget used in the Scenery Library, and a one-line status indicating whether the airport is already in the library. Click a star to set or change the rating; click the same half again to clear. If the airport is not yet in your Scenery Library, setting a rating adds it (and auto-enables the Highlight overlay so the black scenery dot appears immediately, mirroring the existing shift-click flow). The popover stays open while you adjust — close with the × button, the Esc key, by clicking elsewhere on the map, or by zooming.
  • Bigger stars in the airport dot tooltip — the static rating shown in the dot tooltip is now rendered at 1.45 rem (was 1.05 rem) with a touch more horizontal padding, so half-star ratings are easy to read at a glance without leaning into the screen.
v2.31.2 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library — wider modal so fewer airport names get truncated — the modal’s max-width increased from 560 px to 820 px. The list rows now have substantially more horizontal space, so the airport name column (which has text-overflow:ellipsis) can show the full name for the vast majority of airports rather than cutting off after a few words.
v2.31.1 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library — sort and filter choices persist across sessions — the Show and Sort dropdowns are now saved to localStorage under simfly_aa_scenery_prefs. Reopening the modal — even after a full page reload or in a new browser session — restores your last selection (e.g. SimFly-owned only · Rating high to low). Saved values are validated on load, so a corrupted or unknown setting falls back to the safe default rather than breaking the list.
v2.31.0 May 8, 2026 Feature
  • Scenery Library — sort and filter the airport list — two new dropdowns above the list. Show filters the list to All airports, SimFly-owned only (the essential filter — keeps the list focused on airports that are still in the active community ownership data), Rated only, or Unrated only. Sort orders the list by ICAO (A–Z), Rating (high to low), or Airport name (A–Z). The text search box now also matches the airport's full name in addition to ICAO and addon. The footer count adapts to show X of Y airports whenever a filter is active, and the empty-state message tailors itself to the chosen filter.
v2.30.1 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery rating — bigger stars, easier half-star clicks, live hover preview — interactive stars in the Scenery Library list are now substantially larger (1.7 rem) with wider horizontal padding so the left/right halves are easy to target. Moving the cursor over the stars shows a live preview of the rating you are about to set — every star fills (or half-fills) up to the cursor position, and a numeric badge to the right of the row updates in real time (e.g. 3.5/5). Move the cursor away and the row reverts to the saved value. The static stars rendered in the airport dot tooltip are unchanged.
v2.30.0 May 8, 2026 Feature
  • Scenery rating — 0 to 5 stars per airport — every airport in your Scenery Library now has an interactive 5-star rating with half-star granularity. Click the left half of a star to set a half rating, the right half for a full rating; click the same half again to clear the rating back to zero. Ratings are saved as a rating field on each library entry, persisted to localStorage, and round-trip through Export JSON / Import JSON so you can move them between machines along with the rest of your library.
  • Star rating shown in airport dot tooltip — hovering an airport dot on the region map now displays its scenery rating below the existing “Detailed scenery” chip whenever a rating has been set. The same gold-filled star widget is used in both the library list and the tooltip for visual consistency.
v2.29.20 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Route ICAO chips now show full airport tooltips — hovering the selected departure or arrival ICAO in the region-mode header shows the same rich tooltip as the main screen: airport name, IATA code, city, owner (with profile link), SimFly category, elevation, runway details (length, width, surface, lighting), and — if weather has been fetched — the full decoded weather section (flight category, raw METAR, wind, visibility, sky, temp/dew, altimeter). The tooltip is built live at hover time so any weather fetched after the chips were displayed is always included.
  • Measurement drag release no longer fires dot selection — confirmed: moving or resizing a saved measurement ruler (center drag or edge drag) routes through _rgEndDrag(), which sets the 300 ms _rgDragJustEnded guard. Any airport dot under the cursor at release is suppressed identically to region-circle and measurement-draw drags.
  • Scenery split button matches adjacent button height — the ⬤ Scenery / Highlight / Only split button was shorter than the Measure Distance and Close buttons due to its smaller font-size. Fixed with align-self: stretch so it fills the header row's cross-axis height and aligns flush with all neighboring buttons.
v2.29.19 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Measurement circles are now movable and resizable — saved measurement rulers can be dragged and resized just like region circles. Grab the origin pin (center handle, 20 px tolerance) to translate the whole measurement — origin and endpoint move together, preserving direction and distance. Grab the edge handle (magenta resize handle placed at the 90° east point of the ring, 16 px tolerance) to resize the radius while keeping the bearing locked to the angle at the time the drag started. Cursor changes to a grab hand over the center and a resize arrow over the edge, consistent with region circle handles.
v2.29.18 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Drag release no longer triggers route selection — releasing the mouse after dragging a region circle handle, a measurement ruler line, or a range ring no longer fires the airport/route selection logic. The existing guard (_rgDragJustEnded) was already in place for the initial circle-draw drag but was missing from three other drag-end paths: circle handle resize/move, and ruler drag end (both the map-level and document-level mouseup handlers).
v2.29.17 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Header ICAO links only active for SimFly-owned airports — ICAOs in the route display are now clickable SimFly detail links only when the airport is owned by a SimFly player. Non-owned airports (clicked from the background OurAirports dot layer) render as plain text instead.
v2.29.16 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Header route ICAOs now centered and larger — the ICAO display expands to fill the available space in the left header column and centers itself within it, rather than being pushed against the category filter circles. Text size increased ~50%.
v2.29.15 May 8, 2026 Feature
  • Selected route ICAOs shown as links in the region mode header — when one or both airports are selected on the map, their ICAOs appear in the header between the Clear Route button and the category filter circles, colored by SimFly category. Each ICAO is a clickable link to the SimFly airport details page. Updates live with every dot click and rolling advance; clears with the ✕ Clear route button.
v2.29.14 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Unsaved-changes banner pulses inside the modal — the backup banner now pulses with the same yellow glow rhythm as the ⬤ Scenery button, animating its background, border, and inset shadow in sync.
v2.29.13 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Dirty glow triggers immediately on shift-click — the ⬤ Scenery button now starts glowing as soon as the first shift-click adds or removes an airport from the map. Previously the glow only activated the next time the Scenery Library modal was opened or closed.
v2.29.12 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery button dirty glow is now clearly visible — the pulsing yellow glow on the ⬤ Scenery button is much stronger: it starts with a clearly visible ring and pulses to a bright 22 px yellow halo with border-color animation, so the unsaved-changes reminder is hard to miss.
  • Unsaved-changes banner shows the exact change count — the backup banner now reads "N unsaved changes — download a backup before closing?" so you know precisely how many edits are pending. Count increments for every add, edit, delete, paste, or shift-click toggle.
v2.29.11 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • "Close anyway" no longer discards unsaved-changes state — dismissing the backup prompt with "Close anyway" now closes the modal without clearing the dirty flag. The glow on the ⬤ Scenery button persists, and re-opening the modal shows the backup banner again — until the user actually exports the library.
v2.29.10 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Unsaved-changes banner moved to top of Scenery Library modal — the banner now appears immediately below the modal header rather than at the bottom, so it is the first thing seen when the modal opens with pending changes.
  • X close button hidden while banner is visible — the close button is suppressed while the backup prompt is showing, requiring users to explicitly choose "Download & Close" or "Close anyway" rather than bypassing the prompt entirely.
  • Scenery button glows when library has unsaved changes — the ⬤ Scenery split button pulses with a yellow glow whenever the dirty flag is set, drawing attention to pending changes even when the modal is closed.
v2.29.9 May 8, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery edit mode banner is now conspicuous — the map paint mode bar is now bold amber-yellow with a slow pulsing glow, matching the scenery active color scheme, so it clearly signals that click-to-add mode is in effect. Previously it blended into the dark map background.
v2.29.8 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix restores all airports when Only mode is turned off — cycling from Only back to Highlight or Off now immediately rebuilds the matrix with the full airport set. Previously the matrix stayed filtered because the refresh was conditional on _rgOnlyScenery being true; it now fires on every scenery state change.
v2.29.7 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Only mode now filters the region matrix in Select Regions results — the region matrix rendered after drawing dep/arr circles was reading _rgDepAirports and _rgArrAirports without applying the scenery filter. Both arrays are now filtered to scenery-library airports when Only mode is active, matching the map. Mode changes and library edits also refresh the region matrix when results are visible.
v2.29.6 May 8, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery Library — backup banner shown after shift-click changes — opening the Scenery Library modal after adding or removing airports via shift-click now shows the unsaved-changes banner immediately, rather than silently clearing the dirty flag on open and never prompting to export.
v2.29.5 May 7, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery split button — dot now matches text color — the dot in Highlight/Only states now uses currentColor and renders yellow to match the active label, rather than the previous map-blue that was unrelated to the button's visual state.
  • Shift+click — black dot now appears on map immediately — if neither Highlight nor Only mode was active when shift-clicking to add an airport, the scenery dots were never drawn. Adding an airport via shift-click now automatically enables Highlight mode so the black centre dot appears on the airport right away.
  • Scenery Library — Addon field now fills available width — the Addon input in the Add Airport row was a fixed narrow width; it now expands to fill the remaining row space.
v2.29.4 May 7, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery split button — dot icon color and spacing corrected — the dot in Highlight/Only states now renders in the correct map-blue color with a black centre, matching the appearance of scenery airport dots on the map. Active-state yellow text color no longer bleeds into the dot. Spacing between the dot and the mode label is now consistent via flex gap.
v2.29.3 May 7, 2026 Fix
  • Only mode now filters the region distance matrix — airports hidden from the map in Only-scenery mode were still appearing as rows and columns in the distance matrix. The matrix now filters to scenery-library airports only, matching exactly what is visible on the map.
v2.29.2 May 7, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery split button — Highlight and Only states show map-style dot icon — the mode label now displays a CSS-rendered circle with a black center dot (matching the appearance of scenery airports on the map) instead of plain text characters when Highlight or Only mode is active.
v2.29.1 May 7, 2026 Fix
  • Shift+click — map now shows black dot immediately — the canvas layer was being torn down and rebuilt mid-click-event, which could prevent the canvas from fully invalidating. The map redraw is now deferred until the Leaflet event unwinds, so the scenery dot appears reliably.
  • Shift+click — unsaved-changes prompt now triggers correctly — the dirty flag was not being set when adding or removing airports via Shift+click or paint-mode clicks, so closing the library modal never offered the backup nudge. Fixed by marking dirty in the map-toggle path.
v2.29.0 May 7, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library — unsaved-changes backup nudge — closing the modal with unexported changes now shows a banner prompting you to download a backup before leaving. 💾 Download & Close exports the file and closes; Close anyway discards without saving. If you export manually via the overflow menu the flag clears automatically.
  • Scenery Library — drag-and-drop import — drag a .json backup, an X-Plane scenery_packs.ini, or an MSFS scenery.cfg onto the modal to import it directly, without opening the file picker.
  • Scenery Library — paste validation — the Import Pasted ICAOs action now validates every token against the airport database; unrecognised tokens are silently skipped, and a toast reports how many were added vs. skipped. Delimiter handling is also broadened to cover slashes, colons, pipes, and equals signs so pasted paths and route strings work cleanly.
  • Scenery Library — scenery config file import — new Import scenery config option in the ⋯ menu parses X-Plane scenery_packs.ini and MSFS/P3D scenery.cfg files, extracting airport codes from package names and presenting the same checkbox preview as the folder scan.
  • Scenery Library — airport list sorted by ICAO — the library list and all filter results now display in alphabetical ICAO order.
  • Scenery Library — onboarding card labels legible in both modes — fixed a missing CSS variable (--fg → --text) that made the three getting-started card titles invisible on dark backgrounds.
  • Scenery — single split button replaces three toolbar controls — the ⬤ Scenery, Highlight, and Only scenery buttons in the region map toolbar are consolidated into one split button. The left area cycles display modes (Highlight → Only → Off) and its label reflects the current state; the right ✏ icon always opens the library modal.
v2.28.0 May 7, 2026 Feature
  • Scenery Library — add airports directly from the map — two new ways to add or remove airports without opening the library panel: Shift+click any airport dot on the region map at any time to instantly toggle it in or out of your library; or click 📍 Add from map in the Scenery Library footer to enter paint mode, where normal clicks toggle scenery — a blue banner confirms the mode is active, and ✕ Done or Escape exits it.
  • Scenery Library — guided first-use onboarding — when your library is empty the panel now shows three clearly-labelled starting options (click on map, scan simulator folder, paste ICAO codes) instead of a blank list with unlabelled buttons.
  • Scenery Library — richer airport list — each entry now shows a category-colored dot, the full airport name alongside the ICAO, and the add-on name when set. The filter empty-state message is updated to distinguish "no filter match" from "library is empty".
  • Scenery Library — simplified footer — primary actions (Import pasted ICAOs, 📂 Scan folder, 📍 Add from map) are always visible; secondary tools (FS Addon Compare, Import JSON, Export JSON, Clear all) are tucked behind a ⋯ overflow menu.
v2.27.0 May 6, 2026 Feature
  • Scenery Library — FS Addon Compare import — click 📥 FS Addon Compare in the Scenery Library footer, select a .har file captured from the FS Addon Compare website, and the app extracts every airport product from your library. The same scan-preview flow applies: airports already in your library are unchecked, new ones are checked, and you confirm with Add selected. To capture a .har file: open FS Addon Compare in Chrome or Edge, go to My Library, open DevTools (F12) → Network tab, reload the page, then use the ⬇ Export HAR button in the Network toolbar.
v2.26.0 May 6, 2026 Feature
  • Scenery Library — folder scan — click 📂 Scan folder in the Scenery Library footer to point at your MSFS Community folder (or X-Plane Custom Scenery). The app reads each subfolder name, extracts any recognised ICAO codes, and presents a preview list with checkboxes. Airports already in your library are unchecked by default; new ones are checked. Hit Add selected to import or Cancel to go back. Requires Chrome or Edge 86+ (File System Access API).
v2.25.1 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Scenery Library — Clear all button — added a Clear all button to the Scenery Library footer that removes every airport from the library after a confirmation prompt.
v2.25.0 May 6, 2026 Feature
  • Scenery Library — track which airports you have high-detail scenery for. Click ⬤ Scenery in the Region Mode header to open the library. Add airports by ICAO (with autocomplete from the SimFly airport list), paste a bulk list of ICAOs, or import a JSON file. Airports in your library display a central black dot on their region map pin and show Detailed scenery in the hover tooltip. Use the Highlight toggle to show or hide the black dots, and Only scenery to filter the map to your scenery airports only. The library is stored in browser localStorage — use Export/Import JSON to move it between machines.
v2.24.7 May 6, 2026 Improved
  • Region Mode opens with Airport Owners filter pre-synced to main screen — when Departure or Arrival owners are selected on the main screen, opening Region Mode now immediately applies the same owner(s) to the Airport Owners map filter, so the map opens showing only those pilots' airports. If both owners are selected, both appear. If neither is selected, the filter shows all airports as before.
v2.24.6 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Map search results no longer truncate — the results dropdown was constrained to the 240 px input width and clipping long airport names. It now expands to at least 380 px and allows text to wrap, so names, cities, and owner labels are fully readable.
v2.24.5 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Map search placeholder updated — search box now reads "Search ICAO, name, owner…" to reflect that owner name is a first-class search field.
v2.24.4 May 6, 2026 Improved
  • Map search — owner filter zooms to fit all airports — activating an owner filter row now fits the map bounds to show all of that owner's airports. Previously the map stayed at the current view after filtering. Single-airport navigation rows continue to fly to that airport at a close zoom.
v2.24.3 May 6, 2026 Feature
  • Map search — owner filter rows — when a query matches an owner name, a ⊙ Filter map → row appears at the top of the results. Selecting it (click or Enter) immediately applies the Airport Owners map filter to that owner only, hiding all other owners' dots. Individual airports from that owner still appear as normal navigation rows below the filter row.
  • Map search — Enter fires the first result — pressing Enter with no row explicitly highlighted now activates the topmost result, whether that is an owner filter row or an airport navigation row. Previously Enter did nothing unless a row had been selected with the arrow keys.
v2.24.2 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Map search now covers all airports, not just owned ones — the search previously only matched airports from your loaded KML, making name and city fields nearly useless. It now searches the full OurAirports database so typing a city, region, or any ICAO navigates the map even for airports you don't own. Owned airports are ranked above non-owned matches. Name matching requires 2+ characters; city matching requires 3+ to keep short queries focused on ICAO results.
v2.24.1 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Map search — zoom buttons no longer overlap the search box — the Leaflet +/− zoom control is now placed at the bottom-left of the map, freeing the top-left corner for the search input.
  • Map search — owner name matching — searching by owner name now surfaces all of that owner's airports. The city and owner name both appear in each result row so it's clear why an airport matched.
v2.24.0 May 6, 2026 Feature
  • Region map airport search — a floating search box in the top-left corner of the region map lets you find any loaded airport by ICAO, name, or city. Type to filter; the dropdown shows up to 10 matches ranked by exactness. Click a result (or press ↑/↓/Enter to navigate and select) to fly the map to that airport. Press Esc or click ✕ to dismiss.
  • RESET clears region map — clicking the main-screen Reset button now also closes the Region Mode modal and resets all region state (circles, route line, matrix) so the planner returns fully to its initial state.
v2.23.11 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Airport Owners filter now applies to the matrix — when the Airport Owners filter was active and regions were drawn, the matrix showed airports from hidden owners while those dots were invisible on the map. The filter now gates both the map dots and the matrix rows/columns, so what is visible on the map exactly matches what appears in the matrix.
v2.23.10 May 6, 2026 Improved
  • Airport Owners filter — renamed and repositioned — the map-wide owner filter is renamed from "Map Owners" to "Airport Owners" for clarity. The button is now centered in the empty space between the category filter bubbles and the Measure Distance button using flex spacers, and the panel opens downward (was upward and clipped off-screen).
v2.23.9 May 6, 2026 Feature
  • Airport Owners filter — a new Airport Owners ▾ dropdown in the region map header bar filters which owners' airports appear as dots on the map. It is independent of the Dep/Arr Owners controls in the results bar — the map can be narrowed to specific pilots without affecting the matrix. Supports the same full interaction model as the dep/arr panels: type-ahead search, All/None buttons, arrow-key navigation, Space to toggle, Enter to clear search, and a live count badge on the button when a filter is active.
v2.23.8 May 6, 2026 Improved
  • Clear button renamed to "Clear route" — the ✕ Clear button in the Regions panel header is now labelled ✕ Clear route, making its purpose explicit at a glance.
v2.23.7 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Owner ribbon tooltip spacing — fixed missing space between "by" and the owner name in the region matrix owner stripe tooltip (was "Show airports owned byabc", now "Show airports owned by abc").
v2.23.6 May 6, 2026 Feature
  • Main screen route pre-loads into Region Mode on open — if both Departure and Arrival are filled on the main screen when ⊙ Regions is clicked, those ICAOs are pre-set as _rgDotSelDep/_rgDotSelArr before the map initialises. On first open the route is drawn immediately after the background dots render (120 ms); on subsequent opens it is drawn in the map-ready path before returning.
v2.23.5 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Route line persists when new regions are drawn — _rgRenderMatrix was still calling _rgClearRouteHL() when the matrix rendered after completing the arrival region draw, erasing the previously selected route. The route now survives the full region-draw cycle and is only cleared by the explicit ✕ Clear button.
  • Owner color bar tooltip wording — changed from "Filter airports owned by …" to "Show airports owned by …" to match the actual click behavior (filtering to just that owner's airports, not hiding all others).
v2.23.4 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Route line persists through Done and Select Regions — _rgClearRouteHL() was still called in both rgReset and rgExploreReturn, erasing the dep→arr route line even though the airport pair was now preserved. Both functions now skip the route clear so the line stays visible alongside the selection rings.
  • Region-draw mouseup no longer triggers dot-click routing — releasing the mouse over an airport dot at the end of an arrival (or departure) region drag was firing _rgMapDotClick as a false positive, immediately pairing that airport with any previously selected airport. A short-lived _rgDragJustEnded guard (300 ms) is now set when _rgLockArr or _rgLockDep finishes; _rgMapDotClick silently ignores any click that arrives while the guard is active.
  • ✕ Clear button shows correctly when selections are inside region circles — _rgUpdateClearDotBtn was only reached when at least one selected airport fell outside the region circles (triggering the overlay ring path). Moving the call to the top of _rgRefreshSelOverlay ensures it always runs, so the button appears and disappears correctly for all selection states.
v2.23.3 May 6, 2026 Improved
  • Done and Select Regions preserve the selected airport pair — clicking ⊙ DONE or ⊙ Select Regions no longer clears _rgDotSelDep/_rgDotSelArr; the selection rings stay visible on the map across both transitions. Matrix rebuild (drawing new regions) also preserves the pair so you can reference it while setting up a new region.
  • ✕ Clear button explicitly removes the selection — a new ✕ Clear button appears in the region header whenever an airport pair is selected and hides itself when the selection is empty, giving a deliberate path to clear the pair without having to close and reopen Region Mode.
v2.23.2 May 6, 2026 Improved
  • Region dot clicks integrate with the rolling chain — clicking an airport inside a departure or arrival region now goes through the same rolling chain logic as background dot clicks (_rgMapDotClick), so a region-circle airport can pair with an airport selected outside the region and vice versa. Previously, clicking inside a region only called the row/column highlight directly, bypassing the chain and ignoring any airport selected outside the region circles.
  • Cross-region routes work for any dot pair — route drawing in _rgRouteFromDots now falls back to lookupICAO when either airport is not in the current region arrays, so a route can be drawn between a background dot and a region dot or between two background dots without requiring both airports to be inside a region.
  • Owner color bar tooltip says "Show airports owned by…" — hovering the colored bar at the top of an arrival column or left edge of a departure row now shows a descriptive tooltip rather than just the owner name, making the click behavior self-documenting.
v2.23.1 May 6, 2026 Fix
  • Selection ring appears on background map dots — clicking an airport dot outside the region circles now shows the same weight-5 black ring as in-region highlighted dots, via a lightweight overlay layer (_rgSelOverlayLayer) that sits above the background dot layer. The overlay is cleared automatically when regions reset or the matrix rebuilds, and rebuilds in _rgHighlightDots() so it stays in sync with all selection paths.
v2.23.0 May 6, 2026 Feature
  • Map category filter syncs to main screen — toggling any of the category buttons in the Map filter at the top of the region screen now immediately updates both the departure and arrival category filters on the main screen to match, keeping both UIs in sync without manual re-entry.
  • Send to Flight Planner uses matrix dep/arr category filters — when a region has been set and the matrix dep/arr category filters differ from the global Map filter, clicking Send to Flight Planner applies those specific dep/arr category settings to the main screen rather than the Map-wide filter, so the matrix's filtering intent carries through.
  • Click airports on the region map to set routes — airport dots on the main region map (outside the matrix sub-UI) are now clickable for route selection. First click sets the departure; second click sets the arrival and draws the route. Third and subsequent clicks roll the chain forward: departure becomes the previous arrival and the new click becomes the new arrival. Works in both explore and results phases.
  • Region and matrix selections auto-update main screen — selecting a departure/arrival pair anywhere in the region UI (map dot click, matrix cell click, or highlighted dot click) now immediately writes those ICAOs to the main screen departure and arrival fields and triggers owner auto-selection, without requiring a separate Send to Flight Planner click.
v2.22.13 May 5, 2026 Fix
  • Sticky column and row headers no longer bleed cell content on hover — the generic [data-rg-tip]:hover rule applied a semi-transparent background to all tooltip-hosting elements, including the sticky matrix headers, making them see-through when hovered. Overriding that rule with a solid var(--panel) background on sticky matrix headers prevents scrolled cell text from bleeding through in both dark and light mode.
v2.22.12 May 5, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix cell click shows black-ring selection on map dots — clicking a distance cell in the matrix set the selected dep/arr ICAOs but did not call _rgHighlightDots(), so the dots kept their unselected appearance. Added the call at the end of _rgSelectCell so every selection path (matrix cell, row header, column header, dot click) consistently renders the weight-5 black ring.
v2.22.11 May 5, 2026 Improved
  • Selected airport indicators: solid black ring — the departure and arrival dot-selection highlights (formerly a thin cyan / yellow stroke) are now a 5 px solid black ring at radius 13, making the selected airport clearly visible at a glance regardless of map tile or category color.
v2.22.10 May 5, 2026 Fix
  • Category filters preserved across region selections — clicking Select Regions or Done was resetting all category filters (dep, arr, and global) back to "all active." Category filter state now persists for the duration of the session; only the per-filter toggle buttons change it.
v2.22.9 May 5, 2026 Improved
  • Max per region: added 500, 750, and 1000 options — the airport cap selector now goes up to 1000 airports per region for large-area analysis.
v2.22.8 May 5, 2026 Fix
  • Region dot click re-routes after matrix cell selection — clicking a matrix cell sets the active pair but did not update the dot-selection state (_rgDotSelDep / _rgDotSelArr), so a subsequent dot click found no companion and fell through to plain row/column highlighting. _rgSelectCell now also writes those two variables, so clicking any dot after a matrix selection immediately reroutes to the new airport.
v2.22.7 May 5, 2026 Improved
  • Region dot click immediately routes when companion is already selected — clicking an airport dot in the source or destination region now draws the route line and populates the selection panel immediately if a companion airport is already selected in the other region. Previously, the route only updated when the matrix cell existed and was found; clicks that fell through the matrix lookup (no matrix built, or airport filtered out of matrix rows/columns) merely highlighted the row/column without drawing a route.
v2.22.6 May 5, 2026 Fix
  • ICAO entry auto-selects owner in custom dropdown — typing a valid owned ICAO in the departure or arrival field was setting the hidden select value but not updating the visible dropdown button label. Added _distSyncOwnerBtn call after the auto-select so the button reflects the chosen owner immediately.
  • Distance measurements survive region reset — clicking Select Regions (and any dep-circle-too-small rejection) was wiping saved distance measurements because rgReset called _rgClearMeasure. Measure state is now independent of region selection reset; only the measure tool's own Clear All button clears measurements.
v2.22.5 May 5, 2026 Improved
  • Owner dropdowns: type-to-jump works from button focus (no click required) — when the owner button has keyboard focus (e.g. reached via Tab), typing any character immediately opens the panel and starts matching — no mouse click needed first. Arrow keys also open the panel from button focus. Keyboard state (buffer, highlighted row) is now module-level so it persists correctly across open/close cycles.
  • Done button now clears the region selection — clicking Done was leaving circles on the map instead of ending the selection. Done now clears all circles, collapses the matrix panel, expands the map, and returns to the Select Regions button — ready to start a new selection whenever you choose.
v2.22.4 May 5, 2026 Improved
  • Owner dropdowns: arrow-key navigation — ↓ / ↑ move the highlight through the owner list (matching the Region owner panel behaviour). ↑ past the first row clears the highlight. Space confirms the highlighted row in addition to Enter. Arrow movement resets the type-to-jump buffer so the two modes don't interfere.
v2.22.3 May 5, 2026 Fix
  • Owner dropdown type-to-jump: multi-character search — characters typed within 600 ms are accumulated before matching, so typing K Y N quickly finds Kyndra rather than jumping to K, then Y, then N separately. The buffer resets automatically after 600 ms of inactivity.
v2.22.2 May 5, 2026 Fix
  • Owner dropdowns: panel positioning fixed — the panel was appearing in the wrong position because the .panel CSS animation retains transform:translateY(0) which creates a containing block for position:fixed children. Switched the dropdown panel to position:absolute relative to its own wrapper, eliminating the offset. Opens downward by default and flips upward automatically when near the bottom of the viewport.
  • Owner dropdowns: type-to-jump restored — typing a character while the dropdown is open scrolls to and highlights the first owner whose name starts with that letter, replicating the native <select> behaviour. Press Enter to confirm or Escape to dismiss.
v2.22.1 May 5, 2026 Improved
  • Region map expands to full screen when distance matrix closes — closing the results panel (Done, Undo back past results, or Reset) now resets any manual split position and gives the map the full available height. A shared _rgCollapseMatrix() helper binds all three close paths so any future feature that hides the matrix gets this behaviour automatically.
v2.22.0 May 5, 2026 Feature
  • Region map: double-click airport to zoom in — double-clicking any airport dot on the region map zooms the view to approximately 3 NM width centered on that airport. A second double-click on the same airport restores the previous map view. Any manual pan or zoom resets the toggle, ready for the next double-click.
  • Main screen owner dropdowns: unified style — the Departure Owner and Arrival Owner selectors now use the same Rajdhani font and panel style as the Region owner filter menus. Each owner row shows the airport count right-justified at the far right of the row, matching the Region panel layout.
v2.21.15 May 5, 2026 Improved
  • Owner panel: airport count per owner — each name in the Dep Owners and Arr Owners dropdowns now shows the number of airports that owner has within the drawn region in parentheses, e.g. Pilot One (3). Count reflects only airports currently loaded for that side — it updates automatically when the region changes. The panel also expands horizontally to fit the widest entry rather than wrapping long names.
v2.21.14 May 5, 2026 Improved
  • Owner panel: arrow-key navigation and spacebar toggle — while a Dep Owners or Arr Owners panel is open, pressing ↓ moves a highlight down through the visible (filtered) rows; ↑ moves back up, and arrowing above the first row returns focus to the search box. Pressing Space toggles the highlighted row checked or unchecked. Space only acts as a toggle when arrow navigation is active — if no row is highlighted (the cursor is in the search box), Space types a space character as normal, allowing names with spaces to be searched correctly. Highlighting is preserved after each Space toggle so you can toggle, move, toggle without lifting your hands.
v2.21.13 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Owner panel: auto-focus filter as you type — while a Dep Owners or Arr Owners panel is open, any character you type is automatically routed to the Filter owners search box without requiring a manual click to focus it first. Backspace trims the last character; Enter clears the filter as before. Focus also returns to the search box after clicking a name row or pressing All / None, so the full type → select → Enter → type again workflow requires no extra clicks.
v2.21.12 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Crosshair cursor maintained throughout Measure Distance mode — the crosshair was set correctly when activating the tool, but mouse-move events in the results phase called _rgSetCursor on every tick, silently overriding it. The mouse-move handler now returns early whenever measure mode is active (not only while a drag is in progress), so the cursor stays as a crosshair for the full duration of the tool.
v2.21.11 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Owner search: ✕ clear button on the input — a small ✕ button appears at the right end of the Filter owners text box whenever search text is present. Clicking it clears the text and restores the full owner list, identical to pressing Enter, without leaving the keyboard.
v2.21.10 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Measure Distance: Undo and Clear All buttons — the ↩ Clear button is renamed ↩ Undo to better reflect that it removes only the last measurement. A new ✕ Clear All button appears to its right whenever two or more measurements are saved, and removes all of them at once. Both buttons auto-hide when there is nothing to act on.
v2.21.9 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Measure Distance tool: stuck-ring edge case fixed — a click with no drag (mousedown + immediate mouseup) left the origin point set. Subsequent mouse movement immediately drew a new measurement ring from that stale origin, and every further click repeated the cycle. Origin is now always cleared on mouseup, regardless of whether any dragging occurred. Additionally, calling Clear Live (e.g. toggling the tool off mid-drag) now re-enables map panning if dragging was disabled by the in-progress draw.
  • Done button collapses the matrix panel only — clicking ⊙ DONE now hides the distance matrix and results bar and returns to free map exploration, leaving the drawn region circles visible on the map. Previously it triggered a full reset, clearing the circles. Clicking ⊙ Select Regions still performs the full reset and clears old circles before a new selection begins.
  • Owner search All / None scoped to visible rows — while text is typed in the owner search box, the All and None buttons now apply only to the rows that match the search. Rows hidden by the filter keep their current checked/unchecked state. With no search text active, All and None continue to affect the complete list as before.
  • Enter clears owner search and restores full list — pressing Enter in a Dep Owners or Arr Owners search box clears the typed text and makes all owner rows visible again, preserving whatever was checked or unchecked during the filtered state.
v2.21.8 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Owner menus in region mode are now searchable — both the Dep Owners and Arr Owners dropdowns now include a text input at the top of the list. Typing letters instantly hides non-matching owner rows so you can find a specific pilot quickly in large regions. The search clears and the input is focused automatically each time the panel opens. The underlying checkbox filter, All/None buttons, and stripe-click behaviour are unaffected.
v2.21.7 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Weather dot vertical alignment on departure/arrival name lines — the flight-category dot next to the ICAO on the selected airport lines now sits 2 px higher, matching the alignment already applied to dots in the distance matrix.
v2.21.6 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix drag debounce set to 120 ms — reverted the adaptive debounce (which caused flickering on fast machines) in favour of a simple fixed 120 ms delay, down from the previous 160 ms.
v2.21.5 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Adaptive matrix debounce during drag — the delay before the matrix recomputes while dragging a region ring was a fixed 160 ms. It now scales automatically between 40 ms and 160 ms based on how long the previous compute actually took (delay = last compute time × 3, clamped). Small matrices with few airports refresh near-instantly; large matrices self-regulate to avoid jank. Worst case remains 160 ms, so performance is always the same or better than before.
v2.21.4 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Region dot opacity matches background map dots — highlight dots inside region rings were slightly more opaque (0.92) than background dots (0.75 street / 0.88 satellite), causing a visible pop when rings moved over airports. Both layers now use the same fill opacity, so dots look identical whether inside a ring or not. Selected dots remain fully opaque.
v2.21.3 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Consistent dot z-ordering between map and region rings — airports in both the background dot layer and the region highlight layer are now rendered in ascending category order (cat 1 at the bottom, cat 7 on top). Previously the two layers used different iteration orders, so overlapping dots could swap stacking positions when a ring moved over them, causing a visible color flicker. Both layers now use the same sort, eliminating the artefact.
v2.21.2 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Selected region dot ring is now clearly visible — the white ring on a clicked dep/arr dot was hard to read. Selected dep dots now show a bright cyan ring (#00ffff), selected arr dots show a bright yellow ring (#ffff44), and both grow slightly to radius 12 to further distinguish the active selection. Unselected dots remain radius 9.
v2.21.1 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Region airport dots same size as map dots — departure and arrival highlight dots inside region rings were previously rendered larger (radius 12, selected 14) than the standard background dots (radius 9). They now use the same radius 9, so the map has a uniform dot size throughout. Selected dots still show a bright white ring to indicate which row/column is active.
v2.21.0 May 4, 2026 Feature
  • Global map category filter in header bar — a new Map filter row (All / None / cat 1–7 / Invert) sits centred in the region modal header, giving you a map-wide category switch that hides background airport dots across the entire map regardless of region selection. Per-region Dep / Arr category filters continue to work independently and take precedence: if a region filter shows a category that the global filter has hidden, airports of that category still appear inside the region rings.
v2.20.1 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Distance slider rescales when Hide filtered airports is active — the slider's right (and left) edge now reflects only the distance range of airports still visible in the matrix. Previously the scale stayed anchored to the full dataset max even after hidden-category airports were removed by the Hide filtered toggle. Scale restores to the full range when Hide filtered is turned off.
  • File on SimBrief button text centred — the button label was left-aligned inside its full-width container; it is now horizontally centred.
v2.20.0 May 3, 2026 Feature
  • Minor version bump consolidating two weeks of region-mode and main-matrix improvements. Highlights below; granular per-version notes preserved in the entries that follow.
  • Region matrix sticky headers — column headers now stay pinned to the top during vertical scroll, row headers stay pinned to the left during horizontal scroll, and the corner cell stays anchored top-left in both directions. Multiple iterations led to a final root-cause fix: the column resize feature was setting inline position:relative on every <th> after render, silently overriding the CSS position:sticky rule. Sticky headers now work correctly in all configurations.
  • Pivot sort bullseye (⊙) — main distance matrix — the main matrix now has the same one-click pivot sort system as the region matrix. Click the ⊙ on any row or column header to sort the opposite axis by distance to that airport: ⊙ → ▲ nearest → ▼ farthest → ⊙ clear. Replaces the older two-click sort pills + padlock system.
  • Main matrix typography & styling parity — ICAO labels now use Rajdhani (replacing Courier) and pick up category colors, matching the region matrix. Owner stripes, click-to-filter behavior, and free-space header click highlights all carry over from region mode.
  • Category filter split into Dep / Arr groups — the main matrix category filter now shows two independent sets of category buttons side-by-side, one for departures and one for arrivals, allowing cross-axis filtering. Buttons are sized down for side-by-side fit, "Inv" operates per group, and breathing space around the filter section was increased for clarity.
  • Light mode legibility overhaul — the dark-mode neon ICAO category palette (yellow, lime, cyan, etc.) was nearly unreadable on the cream light background. Light mode now uses a darker accessible palette — crimson, burnt orange, dark gold, forest green, dark teal, royal blue, dark magenta — across the region matrix, main distance matrix, and region-mode map dot tooltips. Dark mode keeps the original neon palette unchanged.
  • Owner stripe click-to-filter — click any owner stripe in the region matrix to filter to that owner only on that axis; click again to restore. Works for both departures and arrivals.
  • Region map dot selection ring — clicking an airport dot on the region map now draws a white ring around it, providing immediate visual confirmation of which airport is highlighted in the matrix.
  • Top menu buttons 50% larger; route strip 50% larger; owner profile links extended to cover the full Pilot Directory; ruler magnetic heading now uses the NOAA WMM API for sub-degree accuracy with cached results.
  • Numerous polish iterations — sort dot positioning, weather dot alignment, tooltip restrictions to ICAO/owner text, header redesigns across phases, dropdown composedPath fix, immediate stats updates, and many small spacing tweaks.
v2.19.55 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Main matrix dep sort dot nudge — departure row sort dots shifted 2px up and 1px right for tighter optical alignment with the ICAO label.
v2.19.54 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Light-mode ICAO category color legibility — replaced the dark-mode neon palette (cat 3 yellow, cat 5 cyan, cat 4 lime, etc.) with a darker accessible palette in light mode. The bright neon colors washed out against the cream background and made airport ICAO codes nearly impossible to read in the region matrix and main distance matrix. Each of the seven categories now uses a darker tone — crimson, burnt orange, dark gold, forest green, dark teal, royal blue, dark magenta — with strong contrast against the light background. Dark mode still uses the original neon palette unchanged.
  • Main matrix departure sort dot — nudged up another 2px (now sits 5px above vertical center) for better optical alignment with the ICAO label.
v2.19.53 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Region matrix sticky headers — root-cause fix — the column resize feature was setting th.style.position = 'relative' on every header cell after the matrix rendered. That inline style overrode the CSS position:sticky rule, silently turning sticky headers into ordinary relatively-positioned cells that scroll away with the rest of the table. Removed the override; position:sticky already creates a containing block for the absolutely-positioned resize handle, so the override was unnecessary as well as actively breaking the feature. Sticky now works in all three configurations: column headers stick to the top during vertical scroll, row headers stick to the left during horizontal scroll, and the corner cell stays pinned to the top-left during both.
v2.19.52 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Category filter breathing space +25% — increased the gap above the Dep/Arr category filter section (from the distance slider), the gap between the bubble groups and the Hide button, and the margin-bottom beneath the distance slider — providing clearer visual separation between all three UI groups.
v2.19.51 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Dep sort dot nudged up 3px — main distance matrix departure row sort dots shifted up 3px from vertical center for better optical alignment with the ICAO label.
  • Weather dots nudged up 2px — wx condition dots in the main distance matrix headers shifted up 2px to sit more naturally alongside the ICAO text.
  • Category filter breathing space +20% — increased margin above the Dep/Arr category filter row and the gap between the filter groups and the action buttons below.
v2.19.50 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Split category filter into Dep / Arr groups — the distance matrix category filter now shows two independent sets of category buttons side-by-side, one for departure airports and one for arrivals. Filtering dep and arr independently lets you cross-filter by owner category on each axis without affecting the other.
  • No "Category" label — the group headers now read "Dep" and "Arr"; the word "Category" is omitted since the colored buttons make the purpose clear.
  • Smaller buttons, Inv shorthand — category buttons are slightly smaller to fit both groups side-by-side; the Invert button is shortened to "Inv" and each group's Inv operates on that group only.
v2.19.49 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Category colors in distance matrix headers — departure row labels and arrival column headers now use the same per-category color scheme as Region Mode (red through magenta for categories 1–7).
  • Rajdhani font for ICAO headers — departure and arrival ICAOs in the main distance matrix now use Rajdhani instead of Courier, matching the Region Mode matrix style.
  • Pivot sort bullseye (⊙) — each row and column header now shows a ⊙ sort dot. Click it to sort the opposite axis by distance to that airport — cycles nearest (▲), farthest (▼), then clears. Replaces the previous two-click sort pills and padlock system.
  • Dep sort dot nudge — Region Mode dep row sort indicator shifted one more pixel right.
v2.19.48 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Map dot selection ring — clicking a departure or arrival dot on the Region Mode map now shows a white ring around the selected dot, giving immediate visual confirmation of which airport is highlighted in the matrix.
  • Tooltip restricted to ICAO / owner text — the airport tooltip in the region matrix only appears when hovering the ICAO code or owner name text; moving the cursor to blank space in the same header cell hides it.
  • Sort dot nudge — dep row sort indicator shifted one pixel right.
v2.19.47 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Sticky headers — departure row and arrival column headers now stay pinned when scrolling the route matrix. The root cause was overflow:hidden on the modal acting as the sticky container; changing it to overflow:clip lets the inner scroll div take that role.
  • Route strip centered — the “{dep} → {arr} xxx nm” bar is now horizontally centered. Root cause: margin-left:auto on the Send button consumed all free space. Button is now position:absolute at the right edge.
v2.19.45 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Phase guidance text enlarged 50% — step badges and instruction text during region drawing are now 50% larger.
  • Owner profile tooltip gated — “Click to view SimFly profile” only appears in header tooltips when a URL is actually known for that owner.
  • ICAO click area tightened — ICAO link hit area is now text-width only; clicking blank space in a header cell fires the row/column highlight.
v2.19.44 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Header tooltip distinguishes ICAO vs owner — hovering the ICAO shows “Click to view SimFly details”; hovering the owner name shows “Click to view SimFly profile”. Updates live as the cursor moves between them.
  • Free-space click highlights row / column — clicking blank space in a departure row or arrival column header highlights that airport’s row or column, matching the map dot behavior.
  • Main panel category buttons adopt per-category colors — the 1–7 buttons in the Distance Matrix panel now use the same color scheme as Region Mode (red, orange, yellow, green, cyan, blue, magenta).
v2.19.43 May 3, 2026 Feature
  • Owner stripe click-to-filter — clicking the colored owner ribbon on any column or row instantly filters the matrix to that owner. A second click restores the previous state. Active stripe shows a white inset outline.
  • Filter-then-cap — owner and category filters are applied before the per-region airport cap, so filtering frees cap slots for airports you want to see.
  • Region Mode header redesigned — Select Regions, Undo, and DONE buttons appear only in the phases where they are relevant.
  • Selected route strip enlarged 50% — ICAO codes, distance, and Send button are all 50% larger when a route is selected.
  • Airport dot tooltips improved — immediate on hover, darker background, cyan border glow.
  • Distance Map renamed to Route Map.
  • Owner dropdown stays open during filtering — fixed a bug where clicking a checkbox in the Dep/Arr Owners panel immediately closed it.
  • Route summary updates on filter change — dep × arr × routes count recalculates on every owner or category filter change, not only on region move.
  • Filtered airports removed from base map layer — filtered-out airports no longer appear as smaller dots alongside the active set.
v2.19.42 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owner profile links cover full Pilot Directory — SimFly profile URLs from Recip Tracker’s Pilot Directory now appear on owner names throughout Active Airports, not just for directly tracked pilots.
v2.19.41 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Ruler magnetic heading uses NOAA WMM API — replaced the dipole approximation with a live NOAA World Magnetic Model call for sub-degree accuracy. Cached per origin; falls back to dipole with an ≈ prefix if unreachable.
v2.19.40 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Performance — owner data, canvas renderer, and tooltip HTML are all cached and reused across filter toggles and satellite switches, reducing GC pressure and redraw time.
v2.19.34–39 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Airport dot tooltips suppressed during region drawing — tooltips are correctly silent from Select Regions click through both region placements, then re-enabled once the matrix is visible. Required 6 iterations to fully neutralize Leaflet’s internal auto-open paths.
  • Max per region capped at 250 — removed the 500 and 1000 options to prevent out-of-memory crashes on large region overlap sets.
  • Owner filter preserves state on region resize — hidden owners stay hidden when the region is moved or resized; new owners entering the region appear visible by default.
v2.19.33 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owner filter persists across region moves and resizes — when at least one owner is hidden in the Dep or Arr owner filter, dragging a region or resizing its radius now preserves your filter: previously-hidden owners stay hidden, and any new owners that enter the region appear visible by default. If all owners are currently visible (no filter active), the owner list auto-refreshes as before. Makes it easy to visually scan the map for a specific person’s airports by moving the region without losing your selection.
v2.19.32 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Route line always draws above the region hero line — the selected-route line and its distance label were sometimes hidden behind the center-to-center hero line, especially after dragging a region (which re-stacks the hero layers). Fixed by placing the route line in dedicated Leaflet panes at higher z-indices — routeLinePane (450, above the default overlay pane at 400) and routeLabelPane (650, above the marker pane at 600) — so the route is always on top regardless of draw order.
v2.19.31 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Sort menu reflects active distance pivot — clicking a dep or arr bullseye (⊙) to sort by distance now updates the Sort dropdown to read Closest ICAO or Furthest ICAO instead of still showing the previous mode. Choosing any option from the Sort dropdown while a pivot is active clears the pivot and returns to the selected global sort mode.
v2.19.30 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Same-owner dimming removed — cells where the departure and arrival airport share the same owner were previously dimmed and non-clickable on the basis that same-owner flights have no reciprocation value. That assumption was wrong, so the dim and the click block are gone. All cells are now equally selectable.
  • Pilot profile links in arrival column headers — owner name links in the arrival column headers were inconsistently absent due to a gist-load timing race. The matrix now always renders owner names as plain text first, then upgrades to profile links in a single post-render pass, so both departure rows and arrival columns get links under the same code path with no race condition.
v2.19.29 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Split owner filters — Dep Owners and Arr Owners — the single Owners dropdown has been replaced with two independent menus: Dep Owners (left of the Dep cat buttons) and Arr Owners (right of the Arr cat buttons). Selecting owners in each menu filters only the rows or columns for that side of the matrix, so you can mix and match — for example, showing departures from any owner but arrivals at airports owned by a specific pilot.
v2.19.28 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Escape from region selection at any time — the header Reset button has been renamed Cancel and now exits region selection mode entirely, returning the map to free exploration. Previously it only restarted the selection process (back to Step 1 crosshair) with no way to cancel short of drawing both regions and then using Close Results. Undo is unchanged — it still steps back one phase within the selection flow.
v2.19.27 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Cat filter groups inset toward center — the Dep and Arr category filter groups each have 100 px of inward margin, creating a visual buffer between the filter buttons and the centered route count.
v2.19.26 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Results bar reorganized with centered route count — the filtering bar above the matrix is now arranged as: Owners · Sort on the left, Dep category filters just left of center, the X dep × Y arr = Z routes summary in the true center (flex-grows to fill), Arr category filters just right of center, and Max per region · Close on the right. Max per region and Sort are separated to either side of the center stat.
v2.19.25 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix column headers now sticky — the arrival column header row was rendered without a <thead> element, which caused Chrome to ignore position:sticky; top:0 on those cells. Headers now render inside a proper <thead> so both the column headers (arr airports, vertical scroll) and row headers (dep airports, horizontal scroll) stick correctly as the matrix is scrolled.
  • Owner name hyperlinks restored in matrix headers — when the pilot-URL gist finished loading after the matrix had already rendered, the owner name spans were never upgraded to anchor links. Added a post-load refresh that walks the matrix and replaces any plain <span> owner labels with clickable links once the URL data becomes available.
v2.19.24 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owner filter list scoped to current regions — the Owners dropdown now lists only pilots who own at least one airport in the departure or arrival region. Owners with no airports in either region are not shown. The list rebuilds automatically whenever the regions are recomputed (including handle drags and radius adjustments).
v2.19.23 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Map dot clicks accumulate dep + arr selection independently — clicking a departure airport dot highlights its matrix row without clearing any arrival column highlight, and vice versa. Each side remembers its last selection.
  • Intersection fires full cell selection — once both a departure and an arrival dot have been clicked, the matrix highlights the intersection cell, draws the route line, and shows the distance — identical to clicking that cell directly.
v2.19.22 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owners panel 50% larger — panel width, padding, font sizes, row height, color swatches, and All/None buttons all scaled up for easier readability and interaction.
  • Checked/unchecked state is now visually unambiguous — active owners show a filled cyan checkbox with a dark check mark; inactive owners show an empty box with a subtle border. No more relying on color alone to distinguish state.
v2.19.21 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Aircraft range ring is now fully non-interactive — the yellow range ring no longer captures mouse events, grabs focus, or shows a tooltip. It is display-only and no longer blocks map interaction.
  • Clicking a highlighted airport dot scrolls to its matrix row or column — clicking a dep airport dot highlights and scrolls to that departure row; clicking an arr airport dot highlights and scrolls to that arrival column.
  • Close Results button on results bar — a ✕ Close Results button on the right side of the results bar clears all selections and returns the map to free-explore mode, as if the modal had just opened.
v2.19.20 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Undo and Reset moved to upper-left header — both buttons are now in the region-controls group (left side), consistent with their role in the selection workflow rather than global controls.
  • Undo and Reset hidden until selection starts — buttons are not visible in the initial explore phase; they appear only after clicking Select Regions, so the header stays uncluttered until the region-drawing workflow begins.
v2.19.19 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Sort pivot (⊙) moved to top-right corner of header cells — was bottom-right, which caused overlap with owner name text. Increased font size by 50% (0.68 → 1.02 rem). Added right padding to all header cells to keep ICAO/owner text clear of the button.
  • Range ring tooltip no longer follows pointer — removed sticky:true so the tooltip only appears when hovering directly on the ring line; the permanent east-edge label is the primary distance indicator.
  • Header layout: region controls left, measurement controls right — Select Regions button and phase text are now left-justified; Ruler and ↩ Clear buttons are right-justified with a separator before Undo / Reset / Close.
  • Ruler label font 25% larger — measurement distance/bearing text increased from 0.75 to 0.94 rem for easier reading.
  • ↩ Clear removes one measurement at a time — each click undoes the most recently drawn measurement; repeated clicks walk back through the stack. The button disappears when all measurements have been removed.
v2.19.18 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Airport dots 3× larger — base dot radius increased from 3 px to 9 px, highlighted (in-region) dots from 4 px to 12 px, for easier visibility at typical zoom levels.
  • Ruler measurements persist until cleared — drag-releasing a measurement now freezes the line and circle in place. Each subsequent drag adds a new measurement overlay; all remain visible simultaneously. A ✕ Clear button appears in the header when any measurements exist and removes them all at once.
  • Ruler line contrast improved — changed color from washed-out light green to vivid purple (#cc44ff), which reads clearly on both light street maps and dark satellite imagery. Label background darkened with a matching border.
v2.19.17 May 2, 2026 Feature
  • Free map exploration on open — Region Mode now opens in a neutral “explore” state so you can pan and zoom freely before committing to a selection. A new Select Regions button in the header initiates the departure/arrival drag flow when you’re ready.
  • Measurement ruler tool — a new Ruler toggle button in the header activates a live measurement overlay. Click anywhere on the map to anchor the origin; as you move the cursor, a radius spoke and range circle appear showing distance in nm, true bearing, and approximate magnetic bearing (simplified dipole model, accurate to ±5° for most regions). Click again to move the origin; click the button again to clear.
  • Per-column sort in the region matrix — every departure and arrival header cell now has a small ⊙ button in its corner. Clicking it sorts the opposite axis by distance to that airport: first click = nearest first (▲), second = farthest first (▼), third = clear (back to global sort). Active pivot is highlighted in the header.
v2.19.16 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Owners dropdown no longer hidden behind the map — the dropdown panel was being clipped by the modal’s overflow:hidden container. Changed to position:fixed with viewport-relative coordinates computed at open time, so the panel always renders above all Leaflet layers.
  • Range ring label no longer shows as a black box — the L.divIcon wrapper was producing a misaligned Leaflet container. Replaced with the same position:absolute;transform:translate() wrapper pattern used by the hero route badge, which renders cleanly with no background artifact.
  • Stale range ring glow halos removed on update — the soft glow halo circle under the range ring was not stored in a variable, so it was never removed when the ring updated (e.g., changing fuel or payload). The glow is now tracked in _rgRangeGlow and cleaned up on every ring update and on Undo/Reset.
v2.19.15 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owner filter in Region Mode — a compact Owners ▾ dropdown in the results bar lets you show only selected owners’ airports in both the matrix and the map dots. Supports multi-select with checkboxes; All and None one-click accelerators; the button label changes to show the active count (e.g., “Owners: 3 / 7”) when filtering is active.
  • Matrix auto-expands with panel height — the distance matrix now fills all remaining space below the results bar rather than being capped at a fixed 38 vh, so dragging the split handle to grow the panel gives the matrix more visible rows.
  • Range ring visibility and label — the aircraft range ring is now thicker (3 px) with a soft gold glow halo underneath for better contrast. A permanent label at the ring’s east edge displays the range in nm, and hovering the ring shows a tooltip with the aircraft name.
v2.19.14 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Resizable map/matrix split — a thin drag handle now separates the Region Mode map from the results area. Drag it up or down to give more space to either the map or the matrix. The map redraws correctly after resize.
  • Global category colors on all airport dots — every airport dot on the Region Mode map now uses its SimFly category color (not just the highlighted region results). Airports with no category entry fall back to the previous blue/cyan tones. All dots carry the dark outline for satellite-imagery legibility.
  • Richer airport tooltips — dot tooltips now include the longest runway’s identifier pair, length in feet, surface type, and whether it is lighted, in addition to the existing ICAO, category, name, and owner fields.
  • Tooltip accessibility fix — the dark tooltip background was being overridden by Leaflet’s default white style. Fixed by strengthening the CSS selector specificity so the dark theme is applied consistently.
  • Consolidated filter row + All/None accelerators — the Dep and Arr category filter groups are now inline within the single results/controls bar instead of sitting in two separate rows below it. Each group gains All and None one-click buttons to select or clear all categories in that group at once.
v2.19.13 May 2, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft range ring in Region Mode — when an aircraft is selected in the main UI, placing the departure region center draws a dashed gold ring at that point showing the aircraft’s effective range in nm (matching the adjusted value shown in the aircraft panel — accounting for current fuel and payload settings). The ring updates live when the aircraft is changed or the fuel/payload sliders are moved, and clears on Undo or Reset.
v2.19.12 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Region map dot tooltips — hovering any airport dot (both the background layer and the highlighted results layer) shows a tooltip after a 500 ms delay with the ICAO in its category color, airport name, and owner name.
