  • Aircraft card text truncation fixed — after the 40% font-size increase the cards were too narrow to show the spec line. Grid minimum card width increased from 120 px to 160 px; spec line now wraps rather than clipping; minimum card height increased slightly to accommodate the taller content.
v2.6.4 Apr 14, 2026 Improved
  • Aircraft panel fonts enlarged 40% — all font sizes in the aircraft selector panel (category tabs, chip cards, ICAO code, aircraft name, spec line, spec strip) increased by 1.4× for easier legibility.
v2.6.3 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Matrix ICAO header clicks populate dep/arr fields — clicking a departure row label immediately sets the Departure ICAO field and triggers recalculation; clicking an arrival column header sets the Arrival ICAO field. Either works independently — both fields are updated on every click path including first highlight, sticky-on, and sort clicks.
v2.6.2 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Out-of-range warning in matrix arrival tooltips — _getDepIcaoRec() now checks three sources in priority order: (1) ICAO in the Departure field, (2) pinned departure from the lookup button, (3) highlighted/pivot row in the matrix. Arrival column tooltips now show the in-range or out-of-range line whenever any departure context is available.
v2.6.1 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Range marker layout corrected — arrow now points up (▲) toward the track, label sits below it. The slider row has bottom padding so the marker no longer collides with the sort pills and filter buttons beneath it. overflow: visible added to the range wrap so the marker is not clipped by the 16 px track container.
v2.6.0 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Range marker moved below slider bar; text enlarged — the aircraft max-range marker now sits below the distance track instead of above it, avoiding collision with the Distance label. Label font increased from 0.6 rem to 0.9 rem and arrow from 0.7 rem to 1.05 rem (both ~50% larger).
v2.5.9 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Range marker label above arrow — the distance text and downward arrow on the slider range marker are swapped so the label appears on top and the arrow points down toward the track position.
v2.5.8 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Light mode readability and range marker fixes — (1) Range marker was never drawn: its update call landed outside _distApplyFilter()’s closing brace; moved inside. (2) Green ticks and amber warnings in tooltips are near-invisible on white; light-mode overrides use dark green (#006e3a) and dark amber (#7a5200). (3) dist-best yellow (#ffe94d) overridden to dark gold. (4) Range marker category colors use a dark-accessible palette in light mode.
v2.5.7 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft max range marker on distance slider — when an aircraft with range data is selected and its max range falls within the slider’s current min/max scale, a downward-pointing arrow and label appear above the track at the exact range position, colored in the aircraft’s SimFly category color. No aircraft selected, or range outside the slider scale, hides the marker. The position uses the same pixel formula as the track gradient for pixel-accurate alignment across all browsers.
v2.5.6 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Range check uses departure ICAO textbox — the arrival tooltip range warning now computes distance from the specific airport typed in the Departure ICAO field rather than the nearest airport in the departure owner’s list. If the departure field is empty or unresolved, the range line is omitted.
v2.5.5 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Range warning in arrival airport tooltips — when an aircraft with max range data is selected, hovering any arrival ICAO label in the matrix shows whether the airport is within range. The check computes the actual great-circle distance from the nearest departure airport (current dep owner) to this arrival and compares it against the aircraft’s max range. A green tick confirms the airport is reachable; a red cross shows it is out of range with the nearest-dep distance and max range figures. Departure ICAO tooltips are unaffected.
v2.5.4 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Aircraft range parsing made robust — the Max_Range CSV column is now parsed with parseFloat + Math.round instead of parseInt, correctly handling decimal values (e.g. 465.0). Comma-formatted numbers (e.g. 1,234) are also stripped before parsing. These were the likely cause of some aircraft showing no slider adjustment while others worked correctly.
v2.5.3 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Aircraft range column name corrected — v2.5.2 looked for Max_Range_NM in the aircraft Gist CSV; the actual column is named Max_Range. Range data now loads correctly.
v2.5.2 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft max range integrated — the Max_Range column from the aircraft Gist is parsed and stored on each aircraft entry. Selecting an aircraft automatically sets the distance filter’s right handle to the aircraft’s max range, limiting the matrix to reachable routes. Deselecting restores the full range. Range is displayed in the card spec line, the spec strip, and the hover tooltip.
v2.5.1 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Dimmed matrix cells and ICAO labels invisible in light mode — all dim classes (dist-dim, dist-cat-dim, dist-ac-dim, dist-label-dim, dist-ac-label-dim) used rgba(200,223,245,0.15) — a pale blue-white at 15% alpha that is near-invisible against a dark background and completely invisible on white. Light mode now overrides all dim rules to rgba(15,30,46,0.18) — a dark navy at 18% alpha that reads clearly as dimmed without disappearing.
v2.5.0 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Light / dark theme toggle — a fixed toggle button in the top-right corner (matching the Recip Tracker position) switches between dark and light mode. Light mode uses a clean blue-on-white palette with the same CSS variable overrides as the Recip Tracker. Matrix sticky cells are re-composited against the light panel background to prevent scroll bleed. Preference is persisted to localStorage under simfly_aa_theme and restored on page load.
v2.4.0 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft cards redesigned — data-first with category color accents — SVG silhouettes replaced with clean data cards. Each card shows the ICAO type code in Orbitron, the full aircraft name, and a compact TO/LDG spec line. A colored top bar encodes the SimFly category: Cat 1 green, Cat 2 teal, Cat 3 cyan, Cat 4 sky blue, Cat 5 violet, Cat 6 orange, Cat 7 red. Category tab pills adopt the same color when active. The selected card is outlined in its category color.
v2.3.2 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Aircraft silhouettes redrawn — all 7 SVG side-profile silhouettes were redrawn with correct aircraft proportions. Each category now shows a recognisable profile: Cat 1 high-wing single with prop disc, Cat 2 low-wing twin with two props, Cat 3 T-tail turboprop single, Cat 4 high-wing twin turboprop (ATR style), Cat 5 rear-engine T-tail business jet, Cat 6 narrowbody with under-wing engines, Cat 7 widebody with large under-wing engines.
v2.3.1 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Aircraft selector panel toggle broken — _acTogglePanel() was accidentally omitted from the JS block rewritten in v2.3.0. Clicking the Aircraft header did nothing. Function restored.
v2.3.0 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft selector redesigned with SimFly category tabs and card layout — the category tabs now show SimFly categories 1–7 (matching the airport/aircraft compatibility rules) rather than generic type-class groups. Each tab shows a count badge. Aircraft are displayed as cards showing an SVG side-profile silhouette unique to each SimFly category, the full aircraft name, and the ICAO type code. Hovering a card shows a tooltip with takeoff distance, landing distance, and surface requirement.
v2.2.3 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Aircraft chip labels blank — the ICAO code label was missing from each aircraft chip in the selector grid, leaving empty styled boxes. The chip HTML builder lost the code + '</button>' line during a previous edit, so buttons rendered with no text content. Fixed; chips now show ICAO code labels and hover tooltips with full performance specs.
v2.2.2 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Aircraft selector CSS missing; panel starts collapsed — the aircraft selector CSS (panel border, Orbitron title, category tabs, chip grid, spec strip) was never inserted into the stylesheet, leaving the panel completely unstyled. Fixed. The panel body now starts collapsed so it does not dominate the layout before an aircraft is selected; clicking the header or selecting an aircraft opens it. The chevron correctly reflects the open/closed state.
v2.2.1 Apr 14, 2026 Fix
  • Aircraft selector JS errors fixed — three bugs introduced during the v2.2.0 build process. (1) The category tab onclick handler used broken string escaping that caused a JS syntax error, preventing all aircraft selector code from executing — fixed by passing this to _acSetCategory and reading btn.textContent for the category name. (2) Aircraft chip onclick used the same broken pattern — fixed by storing the ICAO code in a data-code attribute and reading this.dataset.code in the handler. (3) _distShowAcFilterBtn was defined twice; the stub (which only set display without checking window._urlAc) was removed, leaving only the correct implementation.
v2.2.0 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Native aircraft selector — a new Aircraft panel sits above the owner dropdowns in the Flight Planner. Aircraft data is loaded at startup from the same SimFly_Aircraft.csv Gist used by the Recip Tracker, so the database is always in sync. The panel shows category tabs (GA Piston, Turboprop, Business Jet, Regional Jet, Narrowbody, Widebody); clicking a tab displays ICAO-code chips for all aircraft in that category. Selecting a chip instantly activates the aircraft for suitability filtering and tooltip verdicts. A spec strip below the chips shows the selected aircraft’s full name, takeoff distance, landing distance, SimFly category, and surface requirement. The selector header shows the active selection at a glance and collapses to save space. When launched from the Recip Tracker with acCode URL parameters, the correct aircraft is auto-selected and its category tab navigated to automatically. The Filter airports unsuitable for aircraft button appears whenever any aircraft is selected and disappears when None is chosen.
v2.1.0 Apr 14, 2026 Improved
  • Tooltip font sizes reduced 20% — all text inside the airport info tooltip is 20% smaller, allowing more runway and aircraft suitability detail to fit without scrolling. Container base reduced from 1.31 rem to 1.05 rem; airport name heading from 1.38 rem to 1.10 rem; SimFly link and sort hint from 1.05 rem to 0.84 rem; aircraft section label from 0.72 rem to 0.58 rem.
v2.0.9 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft suitability in tooltips — when launched with aircraft data from the Recip Tracker, every ICAO tooltip in the matrix and point-to-point fields gains an aircraft section showing the verdict for that airport’s role. Matrix row labels (departure role) show takeoff-distance and paved-surface checks; column headers (arrival role) show landing-distance and paved-surface checks. The point-to-point departure field shows the takeoff assessment and the arrival field shows the landing assessment. Each check reports the specific reason for failure: runway too short (with available vs required figures), no paved runway at sufficient length, or Cat 1 airport blocking the aircraft category. A green tick indicates the check passes; a red ❌ marks a failure; an amber warning appears when runway data is absent. The section is suppressed entirely when the app is opened standalone.
v2.0.8 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft suitability filter — when launched from the Recip Tracker with aircraft performance data (acMtow, acLand, acCat, acPaved), a new Filter airports unsuitable for aircraft button appears in the category filter row. Activating it dims any departure airport whose longest runway is shorter than the aircraft’s takeoff distance requirement, and any arrival airport whose longest runway is shorter than the landing distance requirement. Paved-only aircraft additionally require at least one qualifying-length paved runway. SimFly Cat 1 airports block aircraft with SimFly category 5–7 on both roles. Label cells for unsuitable airports dim in the matrix row and column headers. The button is hidden when the app is opened standalone (no acCode URL parameter).
v2.0.7 Apr 14, 2026 Feature
  • Recip Tracker deep-link support — the app now reads URL parameters passed by the SimFly Recip Tracker’s Plan Flight modal. dep and arr pre-fill the departure and arrival ICAO inputs; depOwner and arrOwner pre-select the owner dropdowns and render the distance matrix automatically. When an aircraft is included (acCode, acLabel, acBrand, acMtow, acLand, acCat, acPaved), a slim aircraft context banner appears at the top of the Flight Planner panel showing the aircraft code, model, takeoff/landing distances, SimFly category, and surface requirement. The banner can be dismissed with ×, and the aircraft data is stored on window._urlAc for future aircraft-aware filtering.
v2.0.6 Apr 12, 2026 Feature
  • DB_LOOKUP cross-mapping support — airports whose SimFly code does not match any gps_code in OurAirports (e.g. EGON, stored as GB-0464 because its gps_code field is blank) now resolve correctly. The optional third column DB_LOOKUP in SimFly_Airport_Categories.csv is parsed into a new dbLookupDB map. lookupICAO() consults this map as a final fallback, so EGON-class airports appear in the distance matrix, resolve in the point-to-point calculator, and display full tooltips including runway data. Runway lookups in the tooltip now use rec.icao (the OurAirports ident) rather than the SimFly code, so cross-mapped airports show correct runway information. lookupICAO() also gains national-prefix matching (US K, Canadian CY/C, Australian Y) for additional coverage. Mappings are managed in the KML Generator’s New Airport tab and written to the Gist; this app consumes them read-only.
v2.0.5 Apr 12, 2026 Fix
  • Distance slider track alignment fixed on Edge/Mac — the colored fill between the two handles used CSS calc() expressions that multiply two percentage values (rawPct% × (100% − 18px) / 100%). Percentage × percentage is invalid CSS; browsers handle it inconsistently, causing the filled bar to drift progressively to the right of the thumb centres on Edge for macOS. Gradient stop positions are now computed in JavaScript as pixel values using track.offsetWidth, matching the formula already used by the drag-to-pan logic and producing pixel-accurate alignment on all browsers.
v2.0.4 Apr 9, 2026 Feature
  • Typing an ICAO highlights the matching row or column — when a valid owned ICAO is typed in the Departure or Arrival field, the corresponding matrix row or column is highlighted automatically, the owner dropdown is populated, and tooltips update to reflect the highlighted state. If the matrix is not yet visible (e.g. only one field is filled), the highlight is deferred and applied the moment the matrix renders. Sticky sort is honoured: if sticky is active when an ICAO is typed, the sort pivot is set and the matrix re-sorts immediately, exactly as if the label had been clicked. Clicking a header manually clears any pending auto-highlight so user intent always takes precedence.
v2.0.3 Apr 9, 2026 Feature
  • ICAO pin overrides owner dropdown for matrix axis — clicking 🔍 now pins the typed ICAO as the sole airport for its matrix axis, bypassing the owner dropdown entirely. This allows any airport in the database (including non-SimFly-owned airports such as KSEA) to be used as a departure or arrival against a full owner fleet. The pinned dropdown dims and becomes non-interactive. The button switches to ✕; clicking it clears the pin and restores the dropdown. The pin is also cleared automatically when the ICAO field is edited. The ⇄ Swap button correctly crosses both pins and inputs. If the airport is also SimFly-owned, its owner is auto-selected in the (now dimmed) dropdown as a convenience.
v2.0.2 Apr 9, 2026 Feature
  • Owner lookup buttons on Departure and Arrival fields — a 🔍 button now sits to the right of each ICAO input. When the typed ICAO belongs to an owner in the loaded KML, the button enables; clicking it auto-selects that owner in the corresponding Departure or Arrival dropdown and renders the matrix. The button is disabled whenever the field is empty, contains an unresolvable ICAO, or the airport belongs to no loaded owner. Button state refreshes on every keystroke (onDistInput), on KML load/clear (refreshOwnerDropdowns), and on distance clear (clearDist).
v2.0.1 Apr 8, 2026 Improved
  • Tooltip delay on click now matches hover delay — after clicking a row or column label to highlight it, the updated tooltip (Click again to sort…) now waits the full 1000 ms before appearing, consistent with any other hover. Previously refreshCurrent() rewrote the visible tooltip HTML instantly when the tip was already open. Fixed by hiding the tip immediately on click and re-arming a TIP_DELAY_MS timer using the last tracked cursor position (_lastMx/_lastMy). The pending-timer path (user clicked before the tip appeared) was already correct and is unchanged.
v2.0.0 Apr 8, 2026 Improved
  • Tooltip hover delay increased to 1 second — TIP_DELAY_MS raised from 750 ms to 1000 ms for matrix header and row label tooltips.
v1.9.9 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Tooltip updates in place on first click — after clicking to highlight a row or column, the tooltip now immediately refreshes to Click again to sort… without requiring the user to move the mouse away and back. Previously the tooltip was hidden on click (v1.9.8), but no mouseover event fires while the cursor stays still, so the updated message never appeared until the user re-entered the element. The fix adds a refreshCurrent() method to the tooltip API that rewrites the visible popup's content in place; if only a pending timer is active, the timer callback already reads the latest tip store content at fire time.
v1.9.8 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Tooltip dismisses on first click so updated message shows immediately — after a first click highlights a row or column, the tooltip popup is now explicitly dismissed. On the next hover the tip store has already been updated to show Click again to sort…, so the correct message appears straight away rather than the stale Click to highlight from the pre-click hover.
v1.9.7 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Tooltip correctly predicts A–Z step — after sorting Farthest the tooltip now reads Click again to sort arrival airports: A–Z as expected. The previous fix used the effective mode (which maps alpha→Nearest) as input to the next-mode calculation, causing alpha to be skipped and "Nearest" to be shown instead of "A–Z". The raw sort mode is now used as input so the full cycle — Nearest → Farthest → A–Z → Nearest — is reflected correctly in all tooltips.
v1.9.6 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Tooltip predicts correct next sort — on a highlighted label that already has an active sort pivot, the tooltip now shows what the next click will actually apply (the cycled mode), not the current mode. For example, after sorting Nearest the tooltip reads Click again to sort arrival airports farthest from X, correctly predicting the Farthest sort that the next click will trigger. First-sort labels (pivot not yet set) continue to show the current mode.
v1.9.5 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Tooltips stay accurate when highlight moves — when a first click moves the highlight from one label to another, the previously-highlighted label's tooltip now resets to Click to highlight row/column. Previously it remained stuck on Click again to sort… even after the highlight had moved away. The fix uses in-place tip store updates (no re-render) alongside the existing update for the newly-highlighted label.
v1.9.4 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Repeated clicks on highlighted label now cycle through sort modes — the 2nd click on a highlighted label applies the current sort mode (Nearest). The 3rd click detects that this label is already the active pivot and advances the mode before sorting (Farthest). The 4th click advances again (A–Z / back to Nearest). Previously every click after the 2nd re-applied Nearest without advancing.
v1.9.3 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • First click never re-orders the table — the root cause was that renderDistMatrix always rebuilds the table from scratch, so any call to it — even with unchanged sort state — re-sorts the columns. First clicks now bypass renderDistMatrix entirely: the highlight is moved in place, the tooltip for the newly-highlighted label is updated directly in the tip store (switching it from Click to highlight to Click again to sort), and the distance inputs are synced. No rebuild, no re-order, no scroll side-effects.
v1.9.2 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • First click after a sort no longer re-sorts — after a 2nd-click sort, clicking any other unhighlighted row or column label now correctly highlights only, with no table re-order. The previous fix cleared the pivot but left the sort mode (e.g. Nearest) intact; on the re-render without a pivot the table reverted to A–Z, which was itself a visible re-sort. The sort mode is now also reset to A–Z alongside the pivot when a first click moves the highlight to a new label.
v1.9.1 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Sort pill stays in sync with applied sort — the Dep/Arr pill now always shows the mode that is actually applied to the table. Previously, clicking a highlighted label would sort correctly but then advance the pill to the next mode, showing Farthest when Nearest had just been applied. The automatic post-sort pill advance has been removed — the pill only changes when explicitly cycled via the pill body click or when the label is clicked again.
  • First click no longer re-sorts when a stale pivot exists — clicking an unhighlighted row or column label now clears the pivot for that axis before re-rendering. Previously, if a pivot from a previous second-click was still in state, a first click on a new label would trigger a re-sort of the perpendicular axis using the old pivot.
v1.9.0 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Help text updated for accuracy — the Row & Column Sorting section now accurately describes the v1.8.9 click model: second click applies the mode shown on the pill, third click applies the next mode. Sticky sort (padlock) behaviour is now documented in the help modal.
v1.8.9 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Sort button and click behaviour now in sync — clicking an already-highlighted row label or column header now sorts by whatever the Arr or Dep pill currently shows, then advances the pill to preview the next step. Previously the code cycled the mode before sorting, so clicking when the pill showed Nearest would actually sort Farthest. Tooltips on highlighted labels also now describe the current mode that will be applied, not the post-cycle value.
v1.8.8 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • First click no longer triggers cross-axis re-sort — clicking a row label or column header for the first time (sticky off) was inadvertently updating the sort pivot, causing the perpendicular axis to immediately re-sort if a non-alpha sort was already active. For example, clicking a new column header while row sort was set to Nearest would instantly reorder the rows around the newly-highlighted column — on what should be a highlight-only click. The pivot is now only assigned on the second click (or on any click in sticky mode), matching the intended two-click behaviour exactly.
v1.8.7 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Tooltip updates immediately after first click — clicking a row label or column header now always triggers a re-render, so the tooltip on the newly-highlighted label immediately changes from Click to highlight to Click again to sort…. Previously, when sort mode was A–Z and no sort was active, the first click skipped the re-render and the tooltip remained stale until the next interaction.
v1.8.6 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Tooltip for unhighlighted labels corrected — hovering a row label or column header that is not yet highlighted now shows Click to highlight row or Click to highlight column rather than describing the sort action, which only applies on the second click. The sort description still appears on already-highlighted labels.
v1.8.5 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Sort hint tooltips reworded — row label and column header tooltips now use the format Click to sort arrival airports nearest to KLAX or Click again to sort departure airports farthest from EGLL. A–Z mode omits the direction phrase and ICAO. Locked sticky sort appends (locked).
v1.8.4 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix scroll flash fixed — the hide/show row and column logic was unconditionally writing style.display on every _distApplyFilter call — including during slider drags — even when the value wasn't changing. Every such write triggers a layout reflow on the scroll container and causes the viewport to reset. All display writes are now gated behind a change check and only fire when the value actually changes.
v1.8.3 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Category filter allows all-off state — individual category buttons can now all be toggled off simultaneously. Previously, clicking the last active button would reset all seven to active. The Invert button also no longer resets to all-active when toggling from all-on to all-off — it now correctly produces an all-off state. Use ✕ Reset to restore all categories at any time.
v1.8.2 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Hide filtered — combined category + range edge cases fixed — the v1.8.1 row/column hide check used the dist-dim CSS class to detect range-dimmed cells, but that class is intentionally not applied when a cell is also category-dimmed (to avoid double-styling). This meant cells that were simultaneously outside the distance range and in a hidden category weren't counted, leaving rows/columns visible when every cell was actually invisible. The check now reads data-nm, data-dep-cat, and data-arr-cat attributes directly — covering all eight combinations of range and category state — making it immune to CSS class application order.
v1.8.1 Apr 7, 2026 Feature
  • Hide filtered extends to range-dimmed rows and columns — when Hide filtered is active, any row or column where every route falls outside the active distance filter range is now removed from the matrix entirely, in addition to category-filtered airports. Highlights and pivot state on hidden rows/columns are cleared automatically.
v1.8.0 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Owner shown in airport tooltips — all airport info popups now include an Owner line positioned between the city and category rows. Applies to matrix row labels, column headers, and the point-to-point name lines below the distance inputs.
  • SimFly link cue includes ICAO — the action prompt at the bottom of owned-airport tooltips now reads Click to view SimFly details for {ICAO}.
  • Sort pill labels renamed — Rows renamed to Dep and Cols renamed to Arr to match departure/arrival terminology.
  • Sort requires a pivot — clicking a sort pill while no label is highlighted no longer re-orders the matrix using average distances. The table stays alphabetical until a row or column label is clicked to set the pivot.
  • Tooltip delay set to 750 ms — adjusted from 1000 ms after iterating through 500 ms.
  • Sort hint tooltips updated — hints now read Click to sort departure/arrival airports: Nearest first. A–Z mode omits the word "first". Locked sticky sort reads (locked) instead of (sticky).
v1.7.9 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Sort pill labels renamed — Rows is now Dep and Cols is now Arr, matching the departure/arrival terminology used throughout the rest of the interface. Help text updated accordingly.
v1.7.8 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Sort requires a pivot to take effect — clicking a Rows or Cols sort pill while no row or column label is highlighted no longer re-orders the matrix. The pill shows the selected mode (ready to apply), but the table stays in A–Z order until a label is clicked to provide a pivot. Previously, sort without a pivot used the average distance across all airports on the opposite axis as a fallback.
v1.7.7 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Tab order regression fixed — the airport name links (SimFly details) and external icon links (Flightsim.to, FS Addon Compare) generated by the distance calculator were entering the tab cycle, intercepting focus between the ICAO inputs and the owner dropdowns. All dynamically generated anchors in the distance result area now carry tabindex="-1".
v1.7.6 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix scroll flash resolved — removed tabindex="-1" from the matrix scroll container. The attribute was causing focus-triggered scroll resets on every re-render. The matrix wrapper receiving a tab stop is the accepted trade-off; the scroll flash is not.
  • Sort pill label centred — the mode text on the left section of each sort pill is now centred rather than left-justified.
v1.7.5 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix scroll flash regression fixed — the tabindex="-1" added to the matrix scroll container in v1.7.3 caused the browser to trigger focus-initiated scroll resets on re-render. The container now uses a focus listener that immediately redirects focus to the first trap element using preventScroll: true, and the Tab trap was extended to also catch focus landing anywhere outside the four designated controls.
  • Padlock zone fixed width — the right side of the sort pill (separator + padlock) was growing to fill leftover space when the mode label was short (A–Z). It is now a fixed 2.4rem wide so the padlock stays consistently positioned across all three modes.
v1.7.4 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Focus stays within the app — Tab no longer escapes to browser chrome (address bar, browser tabs, etc.). A focus trap intercepts Tab on the last control and Shift+Tab on the first, cycling back within the four designated fields. Owner dropdowns are skipped when hidden. The trap is suspended while Help or Release Notes modals are open.
  • Matrix scroll container excluded from tab order — browsers automatically make overflow scroll containers keyboard-focusable; the matrix wrapper now carries tabindex="-1" to prevent it appearing in the cycle.
v1.7.3 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Tab order scoped to four controls — only the Departure ICAO, Arrival ICAO, Departure Airport Owner, and Arrival Airport Owner fields participate in the tab order. All other interactive elements (buttons, sliders, category filters, file input, footer link) are excluded with tabindex="-1".
v1.7.2 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Full right-zone sticky toggle — the pill is now split into two independent click zones: the left side (label and sort mode) cycles the sort order; the right side (separator bar through the right edge) toggles sticky sort. Clicking anywhere to the right of the bar registers, not just the padlock icon itself. The padlock is centered in the right zone and no longer shifts position between modes.
v1.7.1 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Larger sticky toggle hit area — the separator bar and padlock icon are now a single clickable zone. Clicking anywhere to the right of the bar toggles sticky sort, rather than requiring a precise click on the padlock symbol itself.
v1.7.0 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Sort pill padlock enlarged and separated — the padlock icon on each sort pill is now ~50% larger, and a thin vertical rule separates it from the sort mode label for visual clarity.
v1.6.9 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Tooltip delay restored — matrix label and airport name tooltips now wait 1 second before appearing, matching the pre-v1.6.8 behaviour. The sticky sort feature introduced in v1.6.8 inadvertently removed the delay. Pending timers are correctly cancelled on mouseout and scroll.
v1.6.8 Apr 7, 2026 Feature
  • Sticky sort — each sort pill now has a padlock icon on its right edge. Click the padlock to enable sticky sort for that axis: the pill turns orange and the padlock closes. With sticky on, the first click on any row label or column header highlights it and immediately applies the sort — no second click required. Click the padlock again to unlock and return to the two-click behaviour. Sticky state is per-axis (Rows and Cols are independent) and is reset by the ✕ Reset button.
  • Sticky auto-advances from A–Z — enabling sticky sort while the mode is A–Z automatically advances to Nearest, since an alphabetical sort has no pivot and would be a no-op on first click. Cycling the mode back to A–Z while sticky is on also skips it for the same reason.
  • Tooltip reflects sticky state — row label and column header tooltips now read Click to sort: Nearest (sticky) when sticky is on, making the one-click behaviour immediately discoverable.
v1.6.7 Apr 7, 2026 Improved
  • Sort hint visible on first hover — the tooltip on every row label and column header now always shows what the next click will do. Un-highlighted labels read Click to sort columns/rows: Nearest; already-highlighted labels read Click again to sort columns/rows: Nearest. Previously the hint only appeared after the label was already highlighted.
v1.6.6 Apr 7, 2026 Fix
  • Same-owner matrix indicator — when the same owner is selected in both Departure and Arrival dropdowns, a small same owner label now appears in the corner cell so the intra-fleet context is immediately clear.
  • Owner swap always swaps distance inputs — previously the Swap button only swapped the departure and arrival ICAO inputs when both a row and a column were highlighted simultaneously. Now the inputs are always swapped, matching the intent of reversing the route regardless of highlight state.
  • ICAO extraction regex tightened — the dash-prefix pattern in extractICAOFromName now allows 3–5 characters (matching the other patterns), rather than the previous 3–7 which could match long non-ICAO strings.
  • CSV parser handles escaped quotes — parseCSVLine now correctly processes RFC 4180 double-quote escaping ("" inside a quoted field becomes a literal "). Previously, the toggle-on-every-quote logic would corrupt field boundaries for airport names containing embedded quotes.
  • Download filename uses date, not app version — the exported .txt file is now named simfly-airports-YYYY-MM-DD.txt so repeated downloads on the same day or across versions produce distinct, meaningful filenames.
  • ICAO input sanitisation simplified — onDistInput previously computed the cleaned value twice and used a compound condition that always triggered on lowercase input regardless of whether any change was needed. Simplified to a single clean-and-compare.
  • Category filter visually reset immediately on ✕ Reset — previously the category button states were reset but _distApplyFilter was not called, so dimmed cells remained visually dimmed until the next dropdown change. Reset now calls _distApplyFilter to immediately un-dim the matrix.
  • All ICAO reads from matrix DOM use data-icao — renderDistMatrix (highlight capture and highlight restore) and _distSwapOwners now read ICAOs from data-icao attributes rather than textContent, consistent with the fix applied to _distSyncInputsFromSelection in v1.6.5.
v1.6.5 Apr 6, 2026 Fix
  • Sort hint shows next action, not current state — the tooltip on a highlighted row label or column header now reads Click again to sort columns/rows: Nearest / Farthest / A–Z, describing what the next click will do rather than the current sort mode. Unhighlighted labels show no sort hint at all, correctly reflecting that the first click only highlights.
  • Stale sort pivot cleared on owner change — if the Departure or Arrival owner dropdown changes and the previously-highlighted airport is no longer in the new owner's set, the sort pivot is now cleared. Previously the sort would silently fall back to average-distance mode while the Nearest/Farthest pill remained lit, implying a specific pivot was in use.
  • Category filter and hide mode reset on ✕ Reset — clicking the Reset button now restores all category filter buttons (1–7) to active and turns off Hide filtered, in addition to clearing sort state and distance inputs.
  • window variable name collision resolved — the drag-to-pan handler declared a local variable named window (holding the pan range width), which shadowed the global. Renamed to panWidth.
  • Row/column ICAO reads use data-icao attribute — _distSyncInputsFromSelection now reads the ICAO from the element's data-icao attribute rather than textContent, making it robust against any future content added inside label cells.
  • Auto-load parse errors are caught — if a ?kml= URL points to a file that parses as invalid KML, the error is now caught and logged rather than propagating as an unhandled rejection.
  • Null guard on category hide button — _distApplyFilter now guards the distCatHideBtn element lookup, preventing a throw if the element is ever absent.
v1.6.4 Apr 6, 2026 Feature
  • Progressive sort on row/column click — clicking a row label (departure ICAO) or column header (arrival ICAO) now has two distinct actions: the first click highlights that row or column only, with no sort change. A second click on the same label cycles the sort for the perpendicular axis — Nearest → Farthest → A–Z — using the highlighted airport as the pivot. The Rows/Cols sort pills update to reflect the active sort. Clicking a different label always resets to the first-click highlight state.
v1.6.3 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Shift-click tip added to help — the Airport Info & External Links section now notes that holding Shift opens external links in the background.
  • Footer link color — the Active Player-Owned Airports Map link now uses the primary text color instead of cyan accent.
v1.6.2 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Help modal updated — all sections rewritten to reflect current functionality. Added dedicated sections for Row & Column Sorting and Category Filter. Airport Info and external links (SimFly, Flightsim.to, FS Addon Compare) consolidated into one section. Distance filter description updated to mention bar dragging and the yellow best-route highlight. Swap pill and cell-click behaviour moved into the Owner Distance Matrix section.
v1.6.1 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Distance readout color unified — the large nm distance number now uses the same muted color as the Departure and Arrival labels, replacing the previous cyan accent.
v1.6.0 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Airport name line spacing — gap between the ICAO input fields and the first airport name line increased by 30% (0.5 rem → 0.65 rem); gap between the departure and arrival name lines increased by 15% (0.25 rem added via adjacent-sibling margin).
v1.5.9 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Distance slider width +25% — max-width increased from 600 px to 750 px.
  • Min-nm readout no longer shifts — the minimum distance value is now right-aligned in a fixed-width column (110 px), so the slider stays anchored as digit count changes.
  • Pill and category text color unified — the Rows / Cols sort pill text and the 1–7 category circle numbers now use the same muted color as the Distance label.
v1.5.8 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Distance label and slider centred — “Distance” is now centred above the slider row; the slider and nm readouts are centred beneath it as a unit.
v1.5.7 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Distance slider color unified — the slider track fill, thumb handles, and nm readout values now use the same muted color as the Distance label, replacing the previous cyan accent.
v1.5.6 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Label size scaling — Distance +30% (1.268 rem); Departure and Arrival +50% (1.463 rem); Departure Airport Owner and Arrival Airport Owner +70% (1.658 rem). All relative to the unified 0.975 rem base established in v1.5.5.
v1.5.5 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Distance slider height +30% — track height increased from 12 px to 16 px; nm readout font size increased from 1.0 rem to 1.3 rem.
  • Label font unified — the labels Distance, Departure, Arrival, Departure Airport Owner, and Arrival Airport Owner now use the same system font as Category, replacing the previous Orbitron uppercase style.
v1.5.4 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Matrix scrollbar spacing — increased the gap between the matrix horizontal scrollbar and the Distance filter slider by 30% (0.9 rem → 1.17 rem).
v1.5.3 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Category filter controls reorganised — the category pills row now has an Invert button on its right end that flips the current selection (active ↔ inactive). Hide filtered moves to a second row below the pills. Inverting an all-active state to nothing active resets all to active instead.
v1.5.2 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Category filter repositioned and restyled — the category filter row now sits below the Rows / Cols sort pills rather than above them, with extra vertical breathing room. The 1–7 selector boxes have been redesigned as circular pills matching the sort pill aesthetic (rounded, cyan tint, same font and hover behaviour). The Hide filtered button uses the same pill shape with an amber tint. The “Category” label switches from Orbitron uppercase to the same system font used by the sort pills.
v1.5.1 Apr 5, 2026 Feature
  • Hide filtered categories — a Hide filtered toggle button sits to the right of the category pills. When active, airports whose category is toggled off are removed from the matrix entirely rather than dimmed — their rows and columns disappear. Row and column highlights on hidden airports are cleared automatically to prevent stale selection state. Turning the toggle off restores all rows and columns instantly. The distance filter slider range is unaffected by hiding.
v1.5.0 Apr 5, 2026 Feature
  • Category filter for the distance matrix — a row of seven toggle buttons (labelled 1–7) appears below the distance filter slider whenever a matrix is visible. All categories are enabled by default. Clicking a button toggles that SimFly category off or on; airports whose category is hidden are faded in both their ICAO label and their distance cells, using the same dim style as the distance range filter. Clicking the last active button resets all seven to active. Airports with no category assigned in the database are unaffected by the filter.
v1.4.39 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Footer stats omit unresolved count — the database statistics line now shows only owner and airport counts; the unresolved ICAO count is no longer displayed.
v1.4.38 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Footer database statistics — owner count, airport count, and unresolved ICAO count are now displayed beneath the Active Player-Owned Airports Map link in the footer, using the same Courier New muted-text style as the airport/country name lines.
v1.4.37 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Unowned airport tooltip shows explanatory message — instead of omitting the orange footer line entirely for airports with no SimFly owner, the tooltip now reads “Airport {ICAO} is not owned by a SimFly player”. Owned airports continue to show “Click to view SimFly details” as before.
v1.4.36 Apr 5, 2026 Fix
  • SimFly link only shown for player-owned airports — the SimFly airport details URL only resolves for airports that have an owner in the active database. _calcDistance now checks whether each ICAO appears in the owned-airport set (derived from _getOwnerData()). Owned airports render the name/country as a clickable SimFly link as before. Unowned airports render the name/country as plain non-linked text. The “Click to view SimFly details” line in the airport info tooltip is also suppressed for unowned airports. The two addon search icons appear for all airports regardless of ownership.
v1.4.35 Apr 5, 2026 Fix
  • Airport name link restored to SimFly details page — the name/country text anchor had been pointing to flightsim.to since v1.4.27 when the addon search icons were first introduced. Restored to https://simfly.io/assets/airport/{ICAO}/details. The two icon anchors continue to link to Flightsim.to and FS Addon Compare respectively.
v1.4.34 Apr 5, 2026 Improved
  • Icon label popup text white; icons larger with more spacing — icon label popup text changed from muted to white for easier reading. Ext-link icons enlarged 20% (11 px → 13 px). Gap between country text and first icon, and between the two icons, increased 20% (0.4em → 0.48em).
v1.4.33 Apr 5, 2026 Fix
  • SyntaxError crash fixed — _haversineNm declaration restored — the str_replace edit in v1.4.31 that inserted the icon label popup helpers accidentally consumed the function _haversineNm declaration from its match target, leaving only the parameter list (lat1, lon1, lat2, lon2) { in the source. This is a parse-time syntax error that prevents the entire script block from loading. Verified clean with node --check.
v1.4.32 Apr 5, 2026 Fix
  • SyntaxError on inline event handlers fixed — && inside HTML attribute values is parsed by the browser as an HTML entity sequence before being handed to the JS engine, producing Unexpected token ‘{’. Replaced the short-circuit window._tipAPI&&_tipAPI.showById(...) pattern with an explicit if(window._tipAPI){...} guard, which contains no bare ampersands. Also replaced &mdash; HTML entities inside showIconLabel() string arguments with the literal Unicode em dash so they pass through the HTML and JS parsers cleanly.
v1.4.31 Apr 5, 2026 Fix
  • Tooltip reliability rewritten — direct event attachment replaces delegation — document-level mouseover/mouseout delegation was inherently fragile for the name lines: bubbling order, SVG child elements inside icons, and relatedTarget ambiguity caused ~60% miss rate. Replaced with inline onmouseenter/onmouseleave attached directly to each element at render time. The airport info popup on name text calls into a small public _tipAPI. Icon hover now shows a separate lightweight custom label popup (#iconLabelPopup) via showIconLabel/hideIconLabel — these events fire exactly once at the element boundary with no bubbling. Matrix label tooltips continue to use the existing delegation path unchanged.
v1.4.30 Apr 5, 2026 Fix
  • Airport popup no longer fires on icon hover; tooltip hide boundary corrected — two bugs fixed. (1) Icons were inside the .dist-tooltip-wrap span, so closest() matched them and showed the airport popup on icon hover. Fixed by moving icons back to sibling position and marking them data-no-tip; the mouseover handler now hides and bails immediately when entering any [data-no-tip] element. (2) mouseout from the name text to a sibling icon was hiding the popup because the icon was outside the wrap. Fixed by wrapping each line in a .dist-names-line container and keying the hide decision off that boundary — the tooltip stays live while the cursor is anywhere inside the line (text or icons) and only hides when leaving the line entirely. Matrix label tooltips are unaffected (no .dist-names-line ancestor).
v1.4.29 Apr 5, 2026 Fix
  • Airport tooltip fires reliably over icons — the icons were sibling elements outside the .dist-tooltip-wrap anchor, so mousing onto an icon triggered mouseout on the wrap (hiding the tooltip) and mouseover on the icon (which found no wrap ancestor and did nothing). Fixed by restructuring each name line as a <span class="dist-tooltip-wrap"> that contains both the name link and both icon links. The tooltip now stays live across the entire line regardless of which part the cursor is over.
v1.4.28 Apr 5, 2026 Fix
  • Airport info tooltip now shows reliably — with zero delay, the previous setTimeout(..., 0) path was still asynchronous. A mouseout event fired by moving from the name text to a sibling icon would cancel the pending show before the callback ran, causing the tooltip to silently not appear. Fixed by calling _showTip synchronously in the mouseover handler when TIP_DELAY_MS === 0, eliminating the race entirely.
  • Icon spacing increased — gap between country text and the first icon, and between the two icons, increased by ~33% (0.3em → 0.4em).
v1.4.27 Apr 5, 2026 Feature
  • Addon search icons on airport name lines — each airport name line now shows two external-link icons to the right of the name/country text. Icon 1 searches Flightsim.to for the ICAO code; icon 2 searches FS Addon Compare. The destination is identified by the native browser tooltip on each icon. The previous SimFly details link on the name text has been replaced by the Flightsim.to search link.
  • Airport info tooltip shows instantly — removed the 1-second hover delay. The popup now appears immediately on mouseover.
v1.4.26 Apr 5, 2026 Feature
  • SimFly category shown in airport tooltips — a fourth parallel fetch now loads the community-maintained SimFly_Airport_Categories.csv Gist on startup alongside airports, regions, and runways. When a category is assigned for an airport, the tooltip displays a Category: N line immediately below the city / region / country row. Airports not yet in the list show no category line. The fetch is fire-and-forget and adds no latency to the existing data load.
v1.4.25 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Distance filter shows full-width bar when all routes are the same distance — a zero-width scale (single airport pair, or all routes equidistant) previously collapsed both thumbs to the far left. The scale is now expanded by ±1 nm around the single value so the bar renders full-width with handles at each end. The actual distance still falls inside the range so no routes are filtered out.
v1.4.24 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Distance filter slider z-index edge cases fixed — two stuck-handle scenarios addressed. (1) When both handles coincide at the left extreme, the previous logic incorrectly raised the min handle — but at the left extreme you need max on top so it can be dragged right to open the range. The thumb-top class is now suppressed when bothAtMin. (2) When both handles coincide at any middle position (not just the extremes), min was left underneath max with no way to drag it left. The raise condition is now curMin === curMax && !bothAtMin, covering coincidence anywhere on the track.
v1.4.23 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Sort hint grammar corrected — “nearest from” changed to “nearest to”. Farthest continues to use “farthest from”.
v1.4.22 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Sort hint includes ICAO — the orange action line at the bottom of label tooltips now reads “Click to sort rows/columns by airports nearest/farthest from {ICAO}”, naming the specific airport the sort will pivot on.
v1.4.21 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Release notes scrollbar widened — modal body scrollbar increased from 6 px to 9 px (50% wider). Well within the range that avoids the repaint-on-scroll overhead that caused the original performance issue at 20 px.
v1.4.20 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Release notes filter buttons: clearer on/off contrast — inactive filters now render as near-invisible grey (no color, low opacity) rather than a dimmed version of their active color. Active filters show their full color as before. The gap between states is now immediately obvious rather than requiring careful comparison.
v1.4.19 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • External-link icon has its own hover tooltip — the icon is now a separate <a> element outside the airport-name link, so hovering it shows a native browser tooltip “Open SimFly details page for {ICAO}” without triggering the airport info popup. Hovering the name text still shows the full airport info popup as before.
v1.4.18 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • External-link icon updated to SVG — replaced the Unicode arrow character with an inline SVG rendering of the conventional “box with arrow” external-link icon. Uses currentColor stroke so it inherits the text color; muted at rest, brightens on hover.
v1.4.17 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • External-link icon on airport name lines — a small superscript ↗ appears after each airport name/country line, indicating the line is a clickable link that opens a new tab. The icon is muted at rest and brightens on hover.
v1.4.16 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Sort action hint in label tooltips — when a sort pill is active, the airport info tooltip on each label now shows an orange action line at the bottom: row labels show “Click to sort columns by nearest/farthest distance” when the Cols pill is active; column headers show “Click to sort rows by nearest/farthest distance” when the Rows pill is active. No hint is shown when all pills are on A–Z.
v1.4.15 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Blank-flash on scroll after re-sort fixed — when a label click triggered a re-sort, replacing tbl.innerHTML caused the browser to reset scrollTop/scrollLeft to 0 mid-paint, producing a momentary jump to the empty top of the table before the visible area re-rendered. The wrap's scroll position is now saved immediately before the innerHTML replacement and restored immediately after, so the viewport never visibly moves.
v1.4.14 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Label clicks no longer force-activate sort pills — clicking a row or column ICAO label now honours the sort pill that is already set rather than silently switching it to Nearest. If a sort is active the pivot updates and the table re-sorts immediately; if all pills are on A–Z the table stays alphabetical and only the distance inputs update. The v1.4.9 auto-activate behaviour has been reverted.
v1.4.13 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Highlights follow ICAOs through swap — pressing ⇄ Swap now preserves the highlighted row and column by crossing them: the previously highlighted column ICAO becomes the highlighted row, and the previously highlighted row ICAO becomes the highlighted column. Previously both highlights were lost because the restore logic looked for each ICAO on the same axis it occupied before the swap, where it no longer exists.
v1.4.12 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Tooltip hover delay edge cases fixed — three failure modes addressed: (1) Re-entry after hide swallowed — the mouseover guard was checking id === _currentTipId regardless of whether the tip was visible or merely pending; moving off and back onto the same element within the delay window silently dropped the re-entry. Guard now requires _tipVisible && id === _currentTipId. (2) Scroll left timer running — the scroll handler called _hideTip but not _cancelPending, so a tooltip could still fire after the table had scrolled away from the cursor. Scroll now cancels the pending timer. (3) _currentTipId not reset on hide — _hideTip was clearing _currentTipId, which meant a legitimate re-entry to the same element after a full hide was blocked. Split into _hideTip (hides only) and _hideTipAndReset (hides + clears ID); all external callers use the reset variant.
v1.4.11 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Tooltip hover delay reduced to 1 second — trimmed from 1.5 s to 1 s.
v1.4.10 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Airport tooltip hover delay — tooltips now appear only after 1.5 seconds of continuous hover, eliminating accidental popups when moving the cursor across the matrix or airport name lines. Moving off the element before the delay expires cancels the pending tooltip cleanly.
v1.4.9 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Label clicks now cross-activate sort on the opposite axis — clicking a departure row label now immediately sorts the arrival columns by distance from that airport (auto-activates Cols Nearest if idle). Clicking an arrival column header immediately sorts the departure rows by distance to that airport (auto-activates Rows Nearest if idle). This matches the natural reading: "sort the columns relative to this row" and "sort the rows relative to this column".
v1.4.8 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Swap button redesigned as a pill — the DEP\ARR corner cell now renders as an ⇄ Swap pill consistent with the Rows / Cols sort pills, using the orange accent color to visually distinguish it from the cyan sort controls.
  • Cell clicks no longer set sort pivot — clicking a distance cell populates the departure and arrival inputs and highlights the row and column, but does not change the sort pivot. Sort pivot is now controlled exclusively by clicking row and column ICAO labels, keeping sort and selection concerns cleanly separated.
  • Help text updated — the Click to Highlight & Select section now accurately describes the new interaction model: label clicks control pivot/sort, cell clicks select route.
v1.4.7 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Release notes scroll performance fixed — the global ::webkit-scrollbar rule was applying 20 px fat scrollbars to every scrollable element including the modal, causing the browser to repaint a large scrollbar thumb on every scroll tick. Narrowed the global scrollbar to 8 px; the modal body now gets its own slim 6 px scrollbar. Added will-change: transform to the modal body to promote it to a compositor layer, and overflow-anchor: none to prevent scroll-anchor jump.
v1.4.6 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Cell click updates departure/arrival inputs again — when a sort was active, clicking a cell called renderDistMatrix() which ends with _calcDistance() — but the input boxes had not yet been set, so _calcDistance() read stale values and the inputs appeared unchanged. Fixed by setting both inputs and calling _calcDistance() directly in _distClickCell before the re-render, so the values are always current regardless of sort state.
v1.4.5 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Airport name hover tooltip restored — renderDistMatrix() calls _tipClearStore() at the start of every rebuild, wiping the tooltip IDs that _calcDistance() had registered for the departure/arrival name links. Fixed by calling _calcDistance() at the end of renderDistMatrix() so name-link IDs are always re-registered after the store is cleared.
v1.4.4 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Airport info tooltip restored on departure/arrival name links — _tipClearStore() was resetting the tip ID counter to zero on every matrix re-render, causing the IDs registered for the airport name links (which are written after the matrix rebuild) to collide with or be wiped by the next re-render. Fixed by clearing only the Map contents while leaving the counter monotonically increasing, so previously registered IDs are never reused.
v1.4.3 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Cell centre aligns with cursor after re-sort — the scroll anchor target is now offset by half the cell’s width and height, so the re-sorted cell’s centre lands under the mouse rather than its top-left corner.
v1.4.2 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Highlighted cell scrolls to the mouse cursor after re-sort — previously the scroll anchor targeted the cell's pre-render viewport position; after re-sort the cell could land nearby but not exactly where clicked. The anchor now records the mouse cursor coordinates (event.clientX/Y) as the target. After the re-render the re-sorted cell's top-left corner is scrolled to sit at the cursor position, so the highlight intersection appears right where the user clicked.
v1.4.1 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Highlighted intersection stays at the clicked position after re-sort — when clicking a cell triggers a sort re-render, the table now scrolls to keep the clicked cell at the same viewport position it occupied when clicked. Before the rebuild, the cell's offset from the visible scroll-container edge is recorded; after the rebuild the same cell (found by its departure/arrival ICAO pair) is located in the re-sorted table and the scroll position is adjusted by the difference, so the intersection highlight appears right where the user clicked.
v1.4.0 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Reverted cell-click re-render change — the v1.3.9 behaviour (cell click sets pivot and immediately re-sorts) was more correct than the v1.4.0 experiment of deferring re-sort to pill clicks. Restored to v1.3.9 logic.
v1.3.9 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Cell click always highlights the correct row and column — previously, distance cells carried data-row / data-col index attributes that reflected their position at render time. After a sort re-render those indices were stale, so clicking a cell would highlight the wrong row or column. All cell-click and column-highlight logic is now ICAO-based: each data cell carries data-dep and data-arr ICAO attributes, and selection always finds the matching row/column by ICAO regardless of current sort order. The same fix was applied to column-highlight restoration after re-render.
v1.3.8 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Sort pivot now works correctly — three bugs fixed: (1) clicking a distance cell highlighted both row and column but never set pivotRow/pivotCol, so the sort had no pivot to use. (2) _distToggleRow and _distToggleCol only triggered a re-render when the sort on their own axis was active — they now re-render whenever any non-alpha sort is active, so changing the highlight always immediately re-sorts. (3) Swapping owners via the DEP\ARR corner cell now also swaps the two pivot ICAOs alongside the sort axes.
v1.3.7 Apr 4, 2026 Feature
  • Sort uses highlighted row/column as pivot — when a row (departure) is highlighted and a Nearest/Farthest column sort is active, columns are ordered by distance from that specific departure airport rather than by average across all departures. Similarly, highlighting a column (arrival) pivots row sort on distances to that arrival. Changing the highlight re-sorts immediately if a non-alpha sort is active.
  • Draggable distance bar — the colored section of the distance filter track can now be dragged left or right to pan both handles in unison, preserving the window width. Clamping is honoured at both ends of the scale.
  • 1 px data bleed resolved & sticky borders always visible — switched the table from border-collapse: collapse to border-collapse: separate; border-spacing: 0. Each cell now owns its borders independently, so the sticky header and sticky first-column carry their thicker divider borders with them at all scroll positions — always visible, never merged with adjacent data cells.
v1.3.6 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Distance filter remembers user intent across owner changes — the slider scale (endpoints) always reflects the actual min/max of the current table. Handle positions are restored from the last values the user dragged to, clamped to the new scale. For example: set the max handle to 8,000 nm, switch to an owner pair whose furthest route is 5,000 nm — the max handle sits at 5,000 nm (the new scale maximum). Switch back to a pair with routes beyond 8,000 nm and the max handle returns to 8,000 nm. Intent is cleared by the Reset button.
v1.3.5 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Distance scale always reflects current table data — the slider scale (min/max endpoints) now always resets to the actual minimum and maximum distances in the table whenever the table is rendered. The previous “preserve range” logic that attempted to retain handle positions across owner changes was the source of multiple slider bugs and has been removed.
  • Track bar alignment fixed — the colored fill between the two handles was bleeding slightly beyond them because gradient stop positions were calculated as percentages of the full track width, while the thumb centres are inset by one thumb-radius (9 px) from each edge. Gradient stops now use calc() expressions that account for this inset, so the filled region begins and ends exactly at the thumb centres at all positions.
v1.3.4 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Scroll blank flash resolved — contain: layout paint was instructing the browser to defer painting off-screen table rows, producing the blank-then-render chunk on fast scroll. Replaced with isolation: isolate which preserves the compositor-layer benefit of will-change: transform without suppressing off-screen paint.
  • Sort pill size is now constant — pills have a fixed min-width matching the widest label (“Farthest”) with centred content, so cycling between A–Z / Nearest / Farthest no longer resizes the pill.
  • Distance filter slider overlap fixed — when owner changes caused both handles to land at the same position (observed at the left extreme), the min handle was hidden behind the max handle and could not be dragged. The thumb-top z-index raise now applies at both the left and right extremes. Additionally, a degenerate preserved-range case (both handles at the new minimum) now resets to the full range instead of restoring the stuck position.
v1.3.3 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Sort pills are now the click target for sort cycling — the Rows and Cols pills below the matrix cycle their respective sort orders (A–Z → Nearest → Farthest) when clicked. Pills have a hover state and pointer cursor. Row and column labels now only highlight on click; they no longer change sort order.
  • Airport info tooltip restored on name links — the popup on the departure/arrival airport name lines was being dismissed when the mouse moved within the link due to an overly aggressive mouseover hide call. Fixed: the tooltip is now hidden only via mouseout, not on entering any non-trigger element.
  • Sort pills 25% larger and centred — pill font size increased from 0.82rem to 1.025rem with proportionally larger padding; the hint bar is now centred below the matrix.
  • Row/column label hover highlight — mousing over a row label or column header now immediately shows a hover tint. Previously the tint appeared only after moving away from and back to the cell.
  • 1 px bleed masked — sticky row-label column and header row now use a 2 px solid border on the masking edge, fully covering the sub-pixel overlap of adjacent data cells during scroll.
  • Scroll blank-flash mitigation — added overflow-anchor: none to the table scroll container to prevent the browser jump-to-anchor behaviour that contributed to the visible blank-then-render chunk on rapid scrolling.
v1.3.2 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Table scrolling performance fix (second pass) — three root causes addressed: (1) the mousemove handler was reading offsetWidth / offsetHeight on every pixel of mouse movement, forcing a layout reflow each time; dimensions are now cached once when the tooltip is shown and reused for all subsequent position calculations. (2) The mousemove listener is now registered as passive, telling the browser it can scroll immediately without waiting for the handler to complete. (3) The scroll container now carries will-change: transform and contain: layout paint, promoting it to its own compositor layer so scroll repaints are isolated from the rest of the page — particularly important given the sticky header and column cells with box-shadow.
v1.3.1 Apr 4, 2026 Fix
  • Slow table scrolling resolved — tooltip HTML is now stored in a JavaScript Map keyed by a short integer ID. Matrix cells carry only a lightweight data-tip-id attribute instead of kilobytes of URL-encoded HTML, eliminating the attribute-data processing overhead that was causing sluggish scroll performance.
  • Sort indicator redesigned — replaced plain monospace text with compact pill badges (Rows / Cols) that highlight in accent color when a non-default sort is active. Instructional text removed from the hint bar; sort and interaction guidance moved to the Help modal.
  • First click highlights, second click sorts — clicking a row label or column header for the first time now only highlights it. A second click on the already-highlighted item cycles the sort order (A–Z → Nearest → Farthest).
  • “Click to view SimFly details” scoped correctly — this cue now appears only in tooltips on the airport name lines below the distance inputs (which are clickable links), not on matrix row/column labels (which trigger sorting).
v1.3.0 Apr 4, 2026 Feature
  • Matrix column sorting — clicking a row label (departure ICAO) now cycles how columns are ordered: A–Z → Nearest (ascending average distance) → Farthest (descending) → A–Z. The current sort order is shown in the hint bar below the matrix.
  • Matrix row sorting — clicking a column header (arrival ICAO) cycles how rows are ordered by the same three modes.
  • Swap owners — clicking the DEP\ARR corner cell swaps the Departure and Arrival owner dropdowns. If the distance inputs currently reflect the highlighted cell, they are also swapped.
  • Highlighting no longer toggles off — clicking a row label, column header, or cell always applies the highlight; clicking again no longer deselects it. Use Reset to clear.
  • Highlight persists on hover — mousing over a highlighted row or column no longer overrides the highlight color.
  • Preserve distance filter on owner change — changing the Departure or Arrival owner dropdown now keeps the current min/max slider values if they still fit within the new table's range.
  • SimFly details tooltip cue — removed the browser-native tooltip on airport name links; the airport info popup now includes an orange "Click to view SimFly details" line at the bottom.
  • Sticky header / column border fix — replaced border-collapse borders on sticky cells with box-shadow rules so the header row and first column no longer show a 1 px underlap or allow scrolled content to bleed through.
v1.2.5 Apr 4, 2026 Improved
  • Gist data source switched to input format — the auto-loaded Gist file is now SimFlyActivePlayerOwnedAirports.txt containing pilot input format (PilotName: ICAO1 ICAO2 …) instead of a full KML file. This is dramatically smaller (typically 95%+ reduction) and loads faster. The text format is read directly into the airport data model without any KML parsing step. Custom ?kml= URL overrides that point to .kml files continue to work as before.
v1.2.4 Apr 3, 2026 Fix
  • Comprehensive surface type translation — replaced the static lookup table with a two-stage parser that handles every OurAirports surface code format. FAA condition suffixes (ASPH-G, CONC-F, GRVL-P) are now decoded as e.g. "Asphalt (Good condition)", "Concrete (Fair condition)". Two-material combinations (ASPH/CONC, CONC/GRVL) are rendered as "Asphalt / Concrete". Added coverage for PEM, PCC, SOD, CALICHE, COP (Coral) and other codes present in the OurAirports dataset. Unknown codes fall back to title-cased display rather than showing raw text.
v1.2.3 Apr 3, 2026 Fix
  • Release notes filter bar — tag filter buttons (Feature / Improved / Fix / Initial) added to the release notes modal, matching the companion KML Generator app. Click a tag to toggle it; clicking the last active tag reselects all.
  • Surface type labels — airport info tooltips now show human-readable surface names (e.g. "Asphalt", "Concrete", "Turf / Grass", "Gravel") instead of raw OurAirports codes (e.g. ASPH, CONC, GRS). Unmapped codes fall back to title-cased display.
  • Runway spacing — each runway entry in the airport info tooltip is now separated by a visible divider line with padding above and below, making multi-runway airports much easier to read.
v1.2.2 Apr 3, 2026 Fix
  • Release notes close button fixed — the ✕ button was using e.currentTarget (always the overlay element) instead of e.target.closest('.modal-close') to detect a button click, so it never matched and the modal could not be dismissed.
v1.2.1 Apr 3, 2026 Fix
  • Static panel height — the distance result (NM readout) and airport name lines now always occupy the same vertical space regardless of whether ICAOs have been entered. The panel no longer jumps in size when valid airports are resolved. Achieved by switching from display:none/flex to visibility:hidden/visible so the elements always hold their layout footprint.
v1.2.0 Apr 3, 2026 Improved
  • App renamed — title changed to "SimFly Active Airports" and subtitle updated to "Route Distances Between Active Player-Owned Airports".
  • Help button — new ? Help button in the panel header opens a modal explaining all key features: point-to-point distance, SimBrief filing, the owner distance matrix, click-to-highlight interaction, airport info tooltips, SimFly details page links, and the distance filter slider.
  • Show/Hide button removed — the Flight Planner panel is always visible; the collapse toggle was unnecessary.
  • Clear renamed Reset — the clear button in the panel header is now labelled "Reset" for clarity.
  • Footer link updated — "SimFly Airports" link now reads "Active Player-Owned Airports Map".
v1.1.0 Apr 3, 2026 Feature
  • Auto-load KML on startup — the app now fetches the SimFly active-player airport KML from GitHub Gist automatically when the page loads, so no manual file selection is needed. The airport database (OurAirports) and the KML are fetched in parallel; owner dropdowns populate as soon as both are ready.
  • ?kml= query parameter — the default KML URL can be overridden at runtime by appending ?kml=<raw-url> to the page URL, enabling bookmarked links to alternative KML sources without changing the app file.
  • Import panel auto-collapses — after a successful auto-load the Load KML panel collapses so the Flight Planner is immediately visible. The panel can be re-expanded at any time to load a different file.
  • Silent failure — if the auto-load fetch fails (offline, CORS, bad URL) the drop zone restores to its default state and the user can load a file manually as before.
v1.0.0 Apr 3, 2026 Initial
  • Initial release — standalone Flight Planner spun off from SimFly KML Generator v2.9.81. Retains the full Flight Planner panel and all dependencies; strips the KML generation pipeline, color/shape picker, label scale, pilot text input, and output panel.
  • KML file loading — drag-and-drop or click-to-browse .kml file input (20 MB max); paste-direct KML textarea as an alternative entry method. Both feed the owner dropdowns automatically with no separate Apply step.
  • Owner stats bar — shows owner count, airport count, and unresolved ICAO count below the load section.
  • Flight Planner panel — point-to-point distance calculator, SimBrief filing button, owner-to-owner distance matrix, distance filter dual-handle slider, airport info tooltips, and matrix row/column highlight interaction. All features carried over verbatim from v2.9.81.
  • OurAirports database — three parallel CSV fetches (airports, regions, runways) on page load with shared cached promise; runway DB filters closed runways at parse time.
⬤  Scenery Library

    Get started — how do you want to add airports?

    Paste ICAOs

    Select all None
      No airports found in folder names.
      ⚙  Scenery Developers
      Per-developer summary of your Scenery Library. Average rating is computed across rated airports only — unrated entries do not pull the average down. Click a developer row to expand or collapse their airport list. Click the 🔍 button next to a developer's name to filter the Scenery Library to that developer (closes this modal).
      Developer Airports Rated Avg Rating
      No developers with rated scenery yet. Rate an airport in the Scenery Library to see it appear here.
      🛠  Manage Store & Developer Values
      Bulk-edit the Store and Developer values used across your Scenery Library. Edit the value and click Save to rename it on every airport that uses it (handy for fixing typos or merging duplicates — rename to the exact spelling of an existing value to merge). ✕ clears the value from every airport that uses it. All changes are applied immediately to the library, the region map, and the Developers modal.
      Stores
      ValueAirportsActions
      Developers
      ValueAirportsActions
      🌐  Import from FlightSim.to
      Pull every airport you've downloaded from FlightSim.to into your Scenery Library — whether or not a SimFly player owns it. It's a two-step copy-and-load: run a small snippet in your browser on FlightSim.to to generate a JSON file, then load that file back here.
      1. 1 Generate the JSON on FlightSim.to
        Sign in at flightsim.to, open your browser's developer console (F12 → Console), paste the snippet below, and press Enter. It scans your FlightSim.to downloads and saves simfly-flightsimto-scenery.json to your Downloads folder. (The console may warn about pasting code — type allow pasting if prompted.)
      2. 2 Load the downloaded file
        Back here, load simfly-flightsimto-scenery.json from your Downloads folder. New airports are added in full; for airports already in your library, only blank fields are filled — your existing ratings, stores, and developers are never overwritten. Imported airports float to the top of the list so you can review them, and you'll be nudged to Save a fresh backup.
      📱  Mobile Alerts
      Send a notification to your phone whenever an alarm trips. Pick a provider, fill in the fields, then enable the Mobile alert toggle on each alarm card you want to forward. The alarm sound and on-screen overlay still work as normal — this just adds a phone buzz on top.
      Provider
      🔔  Alerts No alerts configured
      Add Alert (or drag →)
      No alarms configured. Pick a type on the left to add one.

      —
      Log holds the most-recent 100 events on this device; oldest are removed first.

      ⚠ A specific-waypoint alarm already covers this fix. Adding another will create a duplicate.
      ⊙  New Geofence — where should it live?
      Pick how the fence should anchor. A Departure or Arrival anchor auto-follows the active SimBrief flight plan, so the fence works across every route without re-drawing. Draw on map pins the fence to a fixed lat/lng (the v2.69 behaviour).

        ⠿RENTAL PALETTE
        Set 2–10 color stops at % payout positions. Drag a tick along the bar to reposition; right-click a tick to delete. The 0% color is fixed grey. Saved automatically.
        📍 Scenery edit mode — click any airport dot to add or remove it · Shift+click works anytime without this mode
        ⠿PAYOUT PALETTE
        Set 2–10 stops with colors and % positions. Drag a swatch onto the gradient bar to set its position, OR drag a tick bar directly along the gradient. Rows auto-sort low → high after every edit. The 0% no-data color is fixed grey. Your settings will be saved automatically.
